However, Marauders cause Paradox in everyone around them: Sleepers caught in a Marauder's presence may begin taking Quiet damage, and other mages find their Paradox Pools growing faster in a Marauder's vicinity (the Storyguide may add 1 extra die per scene of exposure).

Fighting a Marauder is mechanically possible but narratively complex. Their reality is not the same as yours, and effects they create may not be dispellable through normal means — their "magic" is not magic in the traditional sense; it is the local reality being rewritten by their fixed delusion.

## Worked Example: A Full Paradox Sequence

> Scene: Kaito (NWO, Mind 3) has been pushed into a corner by three armed Sleeper thugs in a diner. He has 4 dice in his Paradox Pool already.
> 
> Kaito uses Mind 3 to Vulgarly blast all three with paralysis — a visible shimmer in the air, their muscles locking, their eyes rolling back. Fifteen Sleeper witnesses in the diner see everything.
> 
> PARADOX ADDITION: Vulgar with Witnesses. Highest Sphere used = Mind 3. +2 dice × 3 levels = +6 dice.
> Pool goes from 4 to 10 dice. That is above 7 — triggers immediately.
> 
> PARADOX ROLL: Roll 10 dice. Results: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 5.
> 
> Reading the dice:
>   1 — The Unraveling: One of the three thugs shakes off the paralysis partially — Kaito's hold on him falters. The thug can move one limb.
>   2 — Quiet Whisper: +1 Quiet box for Kaito. (He now has 1 box: Static level — he starts seeing number overlays on everything.)
>   3 — Backlash Wound: 1 Bashing damage. Kaito winces as reality bites back.
>   4 — Sensory Bleed: For this scene, Kaito sees everyone's biometric data overlaid on their faces — heart rate, stress markers, micro-expressions. -1 die to all non-magical actions this scene (the data is overwhelming).
>   5 — Paradox Flare: His next magical effect is automatically Vulgar and adds +1 die to the pool.
>   6 — Backwash: The magic worked, but the fluorescent lights in the diner explode. Shards of glass: every Sleeper in the room takes 1 Bashing. Chaos.
>   7 — Another Backwash: A second unexpected effect — the paralysis is not just physical. The three thugs will remember this experience vividly and permanently. They will never again be able to rationalize what they saw. They become mildly Awakening-aware (a small narrative hook).
>   8 — Paradox Drain: Remove 2 dice from pool. (Pool: 10 - 2 = 8).
>   9 — Spirit Touch: A Paradox Spirit has been drawn. It will manifest within 2 scenes.
>   10 — Catastrophe: Storyguide chooses: Kaito suffers 2 Aggravated damage. The energy of the blast tears through him.
> 
> Post-roll: Dice consumed by results 6 and 7 (Backwash) = 2 consumed. Dice drained by result 8 = 2 drained. Pool: 10 - 2 - 2 = 6.
> 
> Kaito is standing in a diner full of terrified, wounded Sleepers, leaking blood from mystical tears in his side, seeing everyone's biometric data, with a Paradox Spirit en route, and his next spell will make everything worse.
> "The math is not good," Kaito admits, finally.

# Chapter Six: The Nine Spheres

The Nine Spheres are the fundamental categories of magical understanding. Each Sphere represents a domain of reality that a mage can perceive, understand, and ultimately reshape. A mage's Sphere ratings are their magical vocabulary — the higher the rating, the more complex and far-reaching their statements in that domain become.

Each Sphere chapter below follows the same structure: a philosophical introduction to what the Sphere represents, the perceptual awareness each dot level provides (passive), the active effects each dot level enables, and the Sphere Tricks available at each level. Examples are woven throughout.

For a full catalogue of recorded Rotes organized by Sphere and dot level, see Appendix N: Master Rotes. Each Sphere section below calls out the minimum dot requirements for effects; Appendix N provides named, ready-to-use Rotes at each threshold. Sphere Tricks are informal techniques and do not require a Rote -- they can be performed freeform.

## Correspondence — The Sphere of Space and Connection

Space is an illusion. Two points in space are not fundamentally separate; they are simply differently-labeled coordinates in a continuous field. The mage who masters Correspondence learns to see through the illusion of distance and then to dismiss it entirely.

Tradition Affinity: Virtual Adepts, Akashic Brotherhood. Technocracy Affinity: Void Engineers (who call it "Dimensional Science").

## Correspondence Dot Levels

### Correspondence 1 — Spatial Awareness

At this level, the mage perceives connections between spaces: they can sense when two locations are linked, detect portals or gates, feel when something that belongs here is there, or sense gross distance-relationships. They cannot yet affect space — only perceive its geometry.

**Passive Sense: **

The mage can always feel the rough direction and distance of any location they have visited before, without needing GPS or maps. They also sense active Correspondence effects within their immediate vicinity.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Sense whether a specific person or object is within a defined area.
- Detect active portals, gates, or Horizon Realms nearby.
- Know the rough relative position of a target when given a strong sympathetic link.

#### Sphere Tricks — Correspondence 1

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Dead Reckoning (1) | On a successful Correspondence awareness roll, you know the exact coordinates of a previously-visited location, accurate to within 1 meter. |
| Link Sense (1) | You detect all active sympathetic connections involving a target you can see. You do not know their destinations, only that they exist. |
| Gauntlet Probe (2) | You sense the thickness of the Gauntlet (the barrier to the spirit world) at your current location. See Spirit Sphere for Gauntlet rules. |

### Correspondence 2 — Correspondence Sensing

The mage can now project their senses across space. They cannot physically be in two places, but they can see, hear, and smell through a point in space they have visited — like a magical surveillance camera with no hardware.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Project sight or hearing to a location you have previously visited.
- Communicate with a willing target anywhere in the world (using their image as a focus or a sympathetic link).
- Determine the exact location of an object you have a strong sympathetic link to.

#### Sphere Tricks — Correspondence 2

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| The Floating Eye (1) | Your senses project to a location you've visited. You can see and hear for up to one scene. You are blind and deaf to your actual surroundings during this time (-2 dice to local awareness). |
| Whisper Across (1) | Send a one-way message to a known target anywhere on Earth. They hear your voice clearly. |
| Find the Thread (2) | You instantly know the location of a person or object you have a sympathetic link to, regardless of where on Earth they are. |
| Sense the Wards (2) | Detect all magical wards, barriers, and Correspondence-based protections within a building or defined area. |

> Example: Vikram (Virtual Adept, Correspondence 2) wants to surveil a Technocracy safehouse across town.
> He visited it once during a stakeout. He focuses, pushing his senses through the digital-spatial network.
> Pool: Correspondence (2) + Cunning (3) = 5 dice. Difficulty 1. Coincidental (no one sees him).
> Result: 3 hits. Success. For the next scene, he can see and hear everything inside the building.
> He spends 1 Trick Hit on "The Floating Eye" — the effect is formalized and sustained for the full scene.
> "There are four agents, two in the eastern room, one at the door, one in the kitchen. I see a server rack — interesting."

### Correspondence 3 — Spatial Manipulation

Space bends to the mage's will. They can compress distances, extend them, create short-range portals, and link locations in complex ways. This is the level at which Correspondence becomes truly terrifying in combat.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Teleport yourself (or a willing subject) to a location you have visited. Vulgar.
- Redirect an attack mid-flight (redirect a bullet, a thrown object, a missile) by shifting its path through space.
- Create a short-duration portal between two locations you have visited.
- Compress space to make a long journey short (crossing a room-length in a single step).

#### Sphere Tricks — Correspondence 3

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Blink (1) | You teleport up to Short range (up to 20m) to a location you can see. Vulgar without witnesses; Coincidental if the area is empty. |
| Redirect (2) | When an attack is made against you, you can spend 2 Trick Hits on a Correspondence casting to make the attack arrive from a different angle — imposing +1 Difficulty on the attacker's next attack. |
| Pocket Space (2) | You create a small extra-dimensional container (~1 cubic meter) accessible only to you. It exists until you dismiss it or it is dispelled. |
| Gateway (3) | You open a stable portal between two locations you have visited. The portal lasts for up to one scene; up to 6 people can pass through. |

### Correspondence 4 — Correspondence Mastery

Distance becomes a concept the mage applies selectively. They can affect anything they can sense through their Correspondence perception as if it were adjacent to them. They can banish things to distant locations, pull objects from across the world, and create permanent spatial connections.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Touch, grab, or attack a target at any range if you can sense them via Correspondence.
- Create a permanent portal (requires an extended ritual, Threshold 10).
- Banish a target to a distant location (Vulgar; opposed by target's Resolve + Stamina).
- Create a spatial ward that alters the geometry of an area — making a room larger on the inside, hiding a corridor from non-magical perception.

#### Sphere Tricks — Correspondence 4

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Long Arm (1) | You physically interact with an object or person at any distance you can sense, as if adjacent. This can be used for attacks. |
| Exile (3) | You teleport an unwilling target to a location you specify. Opposed roll (your Correspondence + Cunning vs. their Stamina + Resolve). Difficulty 3. Vulgar. |
| Spatial Anchor (2) | Lock a location's spatial properties for one scene — no teleportation in or out, no Correspondence sensing possible. |

### Correspondence 5 — Spatial Transcendence

Space itself is a canvas. The mage can be in two places simultaneously, reshape geography on a large scale, and link vast distances as trivial connections. At this level, Correspondence effects are always considered Vulgar except in spaces that have already been fundamentally reshaped by Correspondence magic.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Be in two places at once (bilocate) for a scene. Both instances are fully real.
- Reshape the geography of a building or small region permanently.
- Create a Horizon Realm (a pocket dimension) — requires an extended ritual, Threshold 20, and Prime 4.

#### Sphere Tricks — Correspondence 5

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Omnipresence (3) | You exist at two locations simultaneously for one scene. You have full sensory awareness and can act from either location. Paradox +3 (always Vulgar). |
| Spatial Erasure (3) | Remove a location from normal spatial connectivity — it becomes impossible to reach by mundane means, effectively hidden from any direction. Duration: one day per hit. Paradox +3. |
| Sympathetic Cascade (2) | Through a strong sympathetic link, you extend any single Effect at range as if you were physically present at the linked location. The Effect must already be within your Sphere capability. |
| Geography Shift (3) | Permanently alter the physical layout of a location up to a building complex in size — corridors reroute, walls shift, exits relocate. Requires Matter 3 to affect solid structures. Paradox +4. |
| The Everywhere Web (4) | For one scene, you can perceive and reach any location you have ever visited as if you were physically present there. Essentially, you have no meaningful range limit. Paradox +4; use sparingly. |

### Worked Examples — Correspondence

> CORRESPONDENCE ● ROTE — SPATIAL SENSE (Order of Hermes)
> 
> Kezia (Order of Hermes, Correspondence ●●●, Intellect ●●●) wants to know whether anyone is waiting around the corner before she steps into the alley.
> 
> Pool: Correspondence ● (1) + Intellect ●●● (3) = 4 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: highest dot 1 − 1 = 0, minimum 1).  Difficulty: 1.
> Roll: [8, 6, 3, 1] = 2 hits, 1 net.  Kezia's spatial awareness pings the alley — two figures crouching behind a dumpster, roughly 12m away.
> Result: Awareness of the location and rough number of living beings within Short range (up to 20m), lasting one turn.
> COINCIDENTAL — reads as exceptional spatial memory or heightened situational awareness.  A Sleeper bystander would chalk it up to "she seems very alert."
> 
> SF 1 means the Storyguide draws only one card.  Even on a weak hand, the effect is subtle enough that Paradox buildup is negligible.

> CORRESPONDENCE ●● ROTE — SPATIAL WHISPER (Order of Hermes)
> 
> Kezia (Order of Hermes, Correspondence ●●●, Intellect ●●●) needs to locate a hidden courier inside a museum before the Technocracy does.
> 
> Pool: Correspondence ●● (2) + Intellect ●●● (3) = 5 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: highest dot 2 − 1 = 1).  Difficulty: 2.
> Roll: [9, 8, 6, 4, 1] = 3 hits, 1 net.  Kezia senses the spatial geometry of the building — the courier is hiding in the service corridor behind Exhibit C.
> Result: Precise location of one known target within Medium range (up to 60m), lasting the scene.
> COINCIDENTAL — reads as heightened spatial awareness or a mnemonic training technique.

> CORRESPONDENCE ●●● FREEFORM — BLINK STEP
> 
> Kezia must cross 20m of open floor in an instant to intercept an escaping NWO operative.
> 
> Pool: Correspondence ●●● (3) + Intellect ●●● (3) = 6 dice.  SF: 3 (freeform = sum of Sphere dots).  Difficulty: 3.
> Roll: [10, 9, 7, 5, 3, 1] = 4 hits (10 = 2), 1 net hit.  She folds the 20m gap and steps through.
> Result: Teleportation to any visible point within Medium range (up to 60m).  Default range for Correspondence ●●● = Medium.
> VULGAR — the spatial fold is briefly visible as a shimmer in the air.  With no Sleepers present, Paradox risk is minimal.
> FREE HIT SPEND: Kezia spends her 1 free hit to extend range to Long (up to 1,000m), reaching the emergency stairwell exit instead.

> CORRESPONDENCE ●●●● FREEFORM — REMOTE SENSES
> 
> Kezia needs to eavesdrop on a Syndicate board meeting happening in a penthouse across the city.  She sits in her parked car three kilometres away and extends her senses through folded space.
> 
> Pool: Correspondence ●●●● (4) + Intellect ●●● (3) = 7 dice.  SF: 4 (freeform = sum of Sphere dots used).  Difficulty: 4.
> Roll: [10, 9, 8, 6, 4, 2, 1] = 5 hits (10 = 2), 1 net hit.  Kezia establishes a stable sensory window inside the boardroom — she can see and hear everything for the scene.
> Result: Full sight and hearing at any known location within Extreme range (1,000+ meters).  Default range for Correspondence ●●●● = Extreme.
> VULGAR — the boardroom gains a faint spatial distortion visible to Awakened eyes.  With only Sleepers present, Paradox risk is moderate (SF 4).  Kezia keeps her focus shallow and closes the window the moment the meeting ends.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: She uses her 1 free hit to add a third sense — touch — letting her feel the papers being shuffled so she can deduce what documents are on the table.

> CORRESPONDENCE ●●●●● FREEFORM — STABLE PORTAL
> 
> The cabal needs an extraction route from the Technocracy's underground facility.  Kezia will open a walkable portal between the server room and the Chantry basement three kilometres away — large enough for a person with gear to step through upright.
> 
> Pool: Correspondence ●●●●● (5) + Intellect ●●● (3) = 8 dice.  SF: 5 (freeform).  Difficulty: 5.
> Roll: [10, 9, 8, 7, 6, 4, 2, 1] = 6 hits (10 = 2), 1 net hit.  A rippling vertical aperture, roughly 2m tall, opens between the two points.  It holds for the full scene (roughly 10 minutes).
> Result: A two-way, traversable portal between two known locations within any range.  Both endpoints are anchored spatially — anyone or anything that passes through arrives at the other location instantly.
> HIGHLY VULGAR — the portal is unmistakably magical.  Any Sleepers who witness it must either be Dazzled (Mind) or controlled otherwise.  SF 5 means the Storyguide draws five cards; a bad hand here could mean immediate Paradox backlash.  Kezia has salted the extraction route — the moment the last person is through, she collapses the portal and lets the spatial fold snap shut.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: The 1 free hit is used to make the portal one-way (inbound only from the facility side), preventing Technocracy agents from following through even if they reach the aperture in time.

## Entropy — The Sphere of Chaos, Fate, and Decay

Everything ends. The mountain erodes, the empire collapses, the heart stops. Entropy mages do not worship death; they understand it. They see the currents of probability, the way fortune flows, and the built-in tendency of complex systems toward eventual disorder. With that understanding comes a kind of power that looks, from the outside, like the most frightening kind of luck.

Tradition Affinity: Euthanatos. Technocracy Affinity: Syndicate (who call it "Primal Utility Theory" — the math of what survives).

## Entropy Dot Levels

### Entropy 1 — Sense Corruption and Chance

The mage perceives entropy flows: they see the fault lines in a wall, the weakness in a chain, the lie hidden in a perfect story. They also perceive fate-lines — the probable futures branching from any given moment. They cannot yet alter them.

**Passive Sense:**

The mage automatically senses structural weaknesses in objects and lies in statements. They can tell when an NPC is hiding something.

#### Sphere Tricks — Entropy 1

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Read the Thread (1) | On any Empathy or Enigmas roll, spend 1 Trick Hit to sense whether the subject of your inquiry is telling the truth, even about things you did not ask. |
| Spot the Crack (1) | Identify the single weakest point in a structure, lock, or physical barrier. The next attack or attempt to break that object gains Enhancement +2. |
| Fate Sense (2) | Ask one yes/no question about the probable outcome of an action you are about to take. The Storyguide answers honestly based on current probabilities — this is not omniscience, just a strong read on the currents. |

### Entropy 2 — Control Probability

The mage can nudge fate. They can tilt the odds of mundane events — a coin flip, a die roll, a chance encounter — and begin to accelerate or slow simple physical decay.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Cause a mundane system to fail (a lock jams, an engine stutters, a rope frays at a critical moment). Coincidental if subtle.
- Grant or deny another character a moment of fortune (a stumble at the wrong time, a sudden insight).
- Accelerate the natural decay of an object (rust, rot, tarnish — over hours rather than years). Coincidental.

#### Sphere Tricks — Entropy 2

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| The Unlucky Strike (1) | On a successful Entropy casting against a target, their next roll is made at +1 Difficulty (misfortune dogs them). |
| Jam (1) | One device, vehicle, or weapon of your choice fails at a dramatically appropriate moment this scene. Must be mechanical or electronic; magic items resist (Resolve 4 + relevant Sphere). |
| Hasten Decay (2) | An object you touch ages by up to 10 years in minutes. Paper crumbles, metal rusts, food rots. Coincidental for gradual deterioration; Vulgar if instantaneous. |
| Fortunate Moment (2) | An ally gains +1 die on their next roll, as chance seems to break in their favor. |

### Entropy 3 — Affect Living Systems

Entropy 3 extends the mage's influence to living beings and complex organic systems. They can nudge a person's health, curse them with ill fortune on a deeper level, or bless someone with uncanny survival instincts.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Inflict or cure disease (over time, not instantly — requires extended casting). Vulgar if instantaneous.
- Curse a target with persistent misfortune: every roll they make has +1 Difficulty for [hits] scenes.
- Bless a target with uncanny survival: once per scene, they may reroll one failed roll.
- Sense the exact moment a living being will die if their current trajectory continues (within a margin of hours to days).

#### Sphere Tricks — Entropy 3

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| The Evil Eye (1) | Target suffers -1 die to all rolls for one scene. This looks like mundane bad luck — stubbed toes, dropped keys, misfiring weapons. |
| Touch of Dust (2) | You inflict a disease on a touched target. The disease progresses naturally but is guaranteed to manifest. Medicine + Intellect (Difficulty 3) or Life 2 magic required to cure. |
| Euthanatos Mercy (2) | You ease the passage of a dying creature, ensuring they die quickly and painlessly. No further healing magic can be applied after this — the death thread is sealed. |
| Pattern Rot (3) | You inflict a permanent flaw in a target's body — a weak knee, a tendency to headaches, a subtle cardiac irregularity. Lasts until removed by Life 3 magic. |

### Entropy 4 — Unravel Patterns

At this level, Entropy can disassemble the fundamental patterns of things — unmaking objects entirely, dissolving magical effects, and tearing the probability-thread of a person's life so badly that they cannot form coherent plans.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Destroy an object by accelerating its entropy to completion. Vulgar. The object crumbles, rusts, or rots into nothing.
- Dispel any magical effect by unraveling its pattern (replaces normal dispelling; add +2 to the Dispel roll).
- Impose a "doom" on a target — a significant guaranteed bad outcome within the next 24 hours. The Storyguide must narratively fulfill this.

#### Sphere Tricks — Entropy 4

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Unmake (2) | One non-magical object crumbles to dust. Takes one full round. Vulgar. |
| Doom (3) | You place a doom on a target. Within 24 hours, something significantly bad happens to them — their car crashes, their backup fails, their ally betrays them. Vulgar; the doom is always proportional to the target's current precarity. |
| Pattern Shred (3) | Target suffers 2 Aggravated damage as their body's entropy accelerates momentarily. Vulgar. Medical examination shows rapid cellular degradation. |

### Entropy 5 — Entropy Mastery

At Entropy 5, the mage sees and can manipulate the grand sweep of fate and decay on a civilizational level. They can decree that a building will fall (and it will, within days, for mundane reasons), that a political movement will collapse, or that a person's luck turns permanently — for good or ill.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Permanently alter an organization's fate-thread (a company goes bankrupt, a political party splinters, a criminal gang implodes). Requires an extended ritual.
- Grant a person permanent immunity to a category of misfortune (bullets that always miss, fires that always spare them).
- Sense all deaths within Extreme range (1km) as they occur.

#### Sphere Tricks — Entropy 5

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Civilizational Doom (4) | You decree that an institution — a government, a corporation, a building complex — will fall. Within [hits] months, it does, through entirely mundane cascading failures. Cannot target people directly. Extremely Vulgar; Paradox +4. |
| Probability Field (3) | Create a persistent zone (up to one city block) where probability is fundamentally warped for one scene. All rolls made in the zone are at Difficulty +2 or -2 (your choice at casting). Paradox +3. |
| True Dissolution (4) | Unmake a non-living Pattern entirely. An object or structure is erased from existence — no debris, no residue. Paradox +4. Requires Prime 3 to handle the released Quintessence safely. |
| The Long Doom (3) | Imprint a slow entropic curse on a target that manifests over months: their health gradually fails, their luck steadily worsens, their plans unravel. Opposed by the target's Resolve + Composure monthly. |
| Entropy Immunity (3) | For one scene, you become immune to the effects of entropy — disease cannot progress in your body, your equipment cannot malfunction or break, your spells cannot unravel from age or Paradox erosion. |

### Worked Examples — Entropy

> ENTROPY ● ROTE — READ THE THREAD (Cult of Ecstasy)
> 
> Vivienne (Cult of Ecstasy, Entropy ●●●, Cunning ●●●) wants to know if the used car she's about to buy is going to fall apart within the month.  She runs her fingers along the chassis and reads the probability threads.
> 
> Pool: Entropy ● (1) + Cunning ●●● (3) = 4 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: highest dot 1 − 1 = 0, minimum 1).  Difficulty: 1.
> Roll: [9, 7, 3, 2] = 2 hits, 1 net.  The threads tell her: the engine is sound, but the brake line has a hairline fracture — it will fail within two weeks of normal use.
> Result: Reads the most probable near-future failure point of any non-living object she touches.  Result is honest but narrow — one failure mode, clearly identified.
> COINCIDENTAL — reads as an expert mechanic's intuition.  No Paradox risk.
> 
> At ● Entropy, Vivienne can't alter probabilities — only read them.  This is the sphere's most basic application: the Mage as a living diagnostic tool.

> ENTROPY ●● ROTE — UNRAVEL MECHANISM (Cult of Ecstasy)
> 
> Vivienne (Cult of Ecstasy, Entropy ●●●, Cunning ●●●) needs an enemy's pistol to jam at a critical moment.
> 
> Pool: Entropy ●● (2) + Cunning ●●● (3) = 5 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: 2 − 1 = 1).  Difficulty: 2.
> Roll: [8, 6, 5, 3, 1] = 2 hits, 0 net above Difficulty.  Exactly meets Difficulty — bare success.
> Result: The pistol jams on its next shot.  The probability threads of mechanical failure were already present; Vivienne just gave them a gentle push.
> COINCIDENTAL — a gun jam is a normal mechanical event.  No Paradox.

> ENTROPY ●●● FREEFORM — READ THE FLOW
> 
> Vivienne wants to read the probability currents around an enemy cabal to find their weakest link.
> 
> Pool: Entropy ●●● (3) + Cunning ●●● (3) = 6 dice.  SF: 3.  Difficulty: 3.
> Roll: [9, 8, 7, 6, 4, 2] = 4 hits, 1 net hit.  The web of fate becomes momentarily legible.
> Result: The Storyguide reveals one hidden vulnerability — the cabal's lookout leaves his post at the same time each evening.
> COINCIDENTAL — framed as uncanny perceptiveness and pattern recognition.  No Paradox unless overseen by Sleepers with supernatural awareness.

> ENTROPY ●●●● FREEFORM — PROBABILITY COLLAPSE
> 
> The NWO handler across the table is about to run a long con — a carefully constructed narrative that will take weeks to unravel.  Vivienne decides to collapse the probability threads holding his plan together before a single thread is spun.
> 
> Pool: Entropy ●●●● (4) + Cunning ●●● (3) = 7 dice.  SF: 4 (freeform).  Difficulty: 4.
> Roll: [10, 9, 8, 5, 3, 2, 1] = 5 hits (10 = 2), 1 net hit.  Vivienne threads entropy through the handler's web of contacts and contingencies.  For the remainder of the scene, any Extended action he initiates gains an automatic complication on each roll — phones go to voicemail, cab drivers take wrong turns, passwords stop working.
> Result: Applies the [Jinxed] tag to one target for the scene.  All Extended actions the target attempts accrue one automatic Complication per roll regardless of successes.
> VULGAR if performed overtly; COINCIDENTAL if Vivienne simply sits back and watches.  With SF 4, Paradox risk is real.  She keeps her face neutral and sips her wine.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: The 1 free hit extends the effect beyond the scene — [Jinxed] lasts until the target's next milestone (roughly one session), meaning his plan continues to unravel even after Vivienne has left the building.

> ENTROPY ●●●●● FREEFORM — CASCADE
> 
> The Syndicate's eastern distribution hub — a converted warehouse coordinating financial instruments, shipping manifests, and corporate cover identities — represents years of careful construction.  Vivienne intends to pull a single thread and watch all of it unravel.
> 
> Pool: Entropy ●●●●● (5) + Cunning ●●● (3) = 8 dice.  SF: 5 (freeform).  Difficulty: 5.
> Roll: [10, 9, 9, 7, 5, 3, 2, 1] = 6 hits (10 = 2), 1 net hit.  Vivienne collapses the probability architecture of the entire hub.  Over the next 24 hours: shipping routes flag customs inspections, cover identities trigger fraud alerts, financial transfers are frozen by automated compliance systems.  None of it is supernatural — every failure is technically mundane.  But every failure is inevitable.
> Result: Triggers a cascading entropy event through a connected system (a corporation, a supply chain, a criminal network).  The effects unfold over hours or days as coincidences stack.  No single event is traceable to magic.
> HIGHLY VULGAR in perception — any Awakened observing the aftermath will sense the entropy signature.  To Sleepers, it looks like spectacularly bad luck.  SF 5 means substantial Paradox risk; Vivienne has spent weeks building coincidental cover for this moment.  She is not in the building when it falls apart.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: The net free hit is used to ensure the cascade is self-sustaining — she doesn't have to maintain concentration.  The entropy propagates on its own thermodynamic momentum once she releases it.

## Forces — The Sphere of Energy and Physical Law

Fire, electricity, kinetic force, light, gravity, magnetism, radiation — all of these are expressions of a single underlying truth: reality runs on energy, and energy can be directed. Forces mages are perhaps the most immediately dramatic; their magic is loud, obvious, and destructive. They also have the highest Paradox risk of any straightforward combat Sphere.

Tradition Affinity: Sons of Ether. Technocracy Affinity: Iteration X. Verbena can access Forces through elemental connections to fire, storm, and earth.

## Forces Dot Levels

### Forces 1 — Perceive Energy

The mage perceives energy flows directly. They see electricity flowing through walls, feel heat sources through a room, sense the direction and strength of electromagnetic fields. They also perceive magical energy (Quintessence) — this partially overlaps with Prime 1 but is focused on the physical energy expression of magic rather than its raw pattern.

**Passive Sense:**

The mage can always sense the location of significant energy sources: electrical lines in walls, heat signatures, approaching storms, active explosives. This gives +1 die to any Survival or Athletics roll that would benefit from sensing nearby energy.

#### Sphere Tricks — Forces 1

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Energy Map (1) | You instantly know the location and output of all significant energy sources within Medium range (50m). Useful for finding generators, live wires, fuel tanks. |
| Darklight (1) | You see in complete darkness using heat signatures. Effect lasts one scene. |
| Static Sense (2) | Detect all active electronic surveillance (cameras, microphones, tracking devices) within Short-to-Medium range (20m). |

### Forces 2 — Control Minor Forces

The mage can now redirect and reshape small energy expressions — turning a torch's flame, redirecting a bullet's kinetic vector slightly, dimming or brightening lights, creating small electrical discharges. These effects are often Coincidental if subtle.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Redirect a small projectile by 1-2 degrees (effectively causing it to miss by inches). Coincidental.
- Generate a brief electrical discharge (1 Bashing, touch range). Coincidental.
- Control ambient light and heat in a room — dim lights, lower temperature by 10 degrees. Coincidental.
- Set something flammable alight without apparent cause (a candle, a newspaper). Borderline — Coincidental if there is a nearby ignition source, Vulgar if there is not.

#### Sphere Tricks — Forces 2

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Deflect (1) | One ranged attack against you is deflected. The attacker rerolls and must accept the new result. Coincidental if framed as air currents or vibration; Vulgar if the deflection is visible. |
| Surge (1) | All electronics within Short range (up to 20m) experience a power surge — lights blow, computers restart, phones die. Coincidental (power fluctuation). Very useful for disabling surveillance. |
| Static Field (2) | You create a localized field that disrupts electronic communications within Short range (15m) for one scene. Coincidental. |
| Kindle (2) | You ignite a flammable target at Short-to-Medium range (up to 20m). If visible to Sleepers: Vulgar. Otherwise: Coincidental. Damage: 1 Lethal per round while burning. |

### Forces 3 — Command Significant Forces

Now the mage can throw lightning, call fire, reshape gravitational pull locally, walk unharmed through flames, and stop bullets in mid-air. This is the level at which Forces magic becomes unambiguously cinematic — and therefore consistently Vulgar.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Hurl a bolt of electricity, fire, or kinetic force: Range Medium (30m), Damage 3 Lethal, Vulgar.
- Create a wall of force (kinetic field) that blocks passage and attacks. Health 6, Armor 3.
- Walk through fire unharmed (immunity to mundane flames for one scene).
- Redirect gravity locally — make one target fall sideways, float, or be pinned to the ceiling.

#### Sphere Tricks — Forces 3

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Lightning Strike (1) | Your Forces attack deals an additional 1 Lethal damage and stuns the target (loses next Minor Action) if they are wearing or touching metal. |
| Shaped Charge (1) | You direct your Forces attack to affect a precise area, sparing designated targets within the blast radius. |
| Gravity Well (2) | One target within Medium range (up to 60m) is pinned to a surface (floor, wall, ceiling) by increased local gravity. Immobilized status. Resist: Might + Stamina, Difficulty 3, as a Major Action. |
| Barrier (2) | You raise a kinetic force wall. It has Health 8, Armor 4, and lasts until destroyed or you dismiss it. Anyone passing through it takes 2 Bashing. |
| Ride the Wave (2) | You move at 3x normal Speed for one round, carried on a wave of directed kinetic force. |

> Combat Example — Forces 3 in action:
> 
> Setup: Silas (Son of Ether, Forces 3) is in an industrial warehouse facing two Iteration X cyborgs. The cyborgs are armed with submachine guns and have Armor 3. There are Sleeper workers on the far side of the building.
> 
> Round 1: Silas raises a kinetic barrier between himself and the cyborgs.
> Casting Roll: Forces (3) + Might (3) = 6 dice. Difficulty 1. Coincidental? No — the shimmering wall of force is very visible. Vulgar without Witnesses (the Sleepers are too far away to clearly see). +3 dice to Paradox Pool (Forces 3).
> He rolls: 9, 8, 4, 3, 7, 10 = 4 hits. 3 Trick Hits. He spends 2 on "Barrier" — wall goes up (Health 8, Armor 4).
> 
> Round 2: Cyborg 1 fires through the barrier. The barrier takes 4 Lethal (reduced to Health 4).
> 
> Round 3: Silas fires a lightning strike at Cyborg 1 over the top of the barrier.
> Casting Roll: Forces (3) + Dexterity (3) = 6 dice. Difficulty 2 (targeting over the barrier). Vulgar with Witnesses (the Sleeper workers see lightning). +6 dice Paradox (Forces 3, Witnesses = 2×3).
> Rolls: 10, 9, 8, 3, 5, 2 = 5 hits (the 10 counts as 2). 3 Trick Hits above Difficulty.
> Damage: 3 Lethal base. Cyborg 1 has Armor 3: 3 - 3 = 0 Lethal. The lightning hits but bounces off the armor!
> Silas spends 2 Trick Hits on "Lightning Strike" — additional 1 Lethal damage AND the metal armor is a conductor: stun effect triggers. Cyborg 1 takes 1 Lethal through armor (the trick bypasses the armor calculation) and loses their next Minor Action.
> 
> Silas's Paradox Pool has grown significantly. He needs to finish this quickly.

### Forces 4 — Reshape Major Forces

Gravity, weather patterns, sustained energy fields, and broad kinetic effects bend to the mage's design. They can fly under their own power, create localized weather phenomena, and channel enough electricity to destroy vehicles.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Fly at Speed 15 (requires sustained Forces 4 concentration; breaks if the mage is Stunned or Confused).
- Create a localized storm (Large Group area (50m radius): wind, lightning, hail. 1 Bashing per round to everyone in the area not protected.
- Destroy a vehicle with a concentrated energy burst. Damage 6 Lethal to the vehicle.
- Create sustained energy constructs (a ladder of magnetism, a bridge of compressed air) that others can physically use.

#### Sphere Tricks — Forces 4

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Tempest (2) | You call a localized storm. All targets in a Group area (50m radius) take 1 Bashing per round. Lightning strikes can be directed: +3 Lethal to one target per round as a Minor Action. |
| Flight (1) | You fly for one scene. Speed 15 in open air. If combined with Correspondence 2, range is unlimited. |
| EMP (3) | Electromagnetic pulse. All electronics within Long range (up to 1,000m) are destroyed. Coincidental if framed as a natural surge; Vulgar if visible and instantaneous. |

### Forces 5 — Command Primal Forces

At this level, the mage can reshape energy on a civilizational scale. Earthquakes. Solar flares redirected. Gravity reversed over a city block. These workings are always Vulgar, always expensive in Paradox, and are rarely done casually.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Create a localized earthquake (Extreme range — 1km radius). Damage 3 Lethal to structures; 2 Lethal to individuals caught in collapse.
- Create a sustained field of altered gravity, light, or electromagnetic force over a neighborhood.
- Absorb and redirect any energy attack directed at you or nearby allies as a reflexive action.

#### Sphere Tricks — Forces 5

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Earthquake (4) | Trigger or suppress a significant seismic event within Extreme range (10km). The mage does not control the exact damage — they set the epicenter and magnitude. Paradox +5; always Vulgar. |
| Weather Mastery (3) | Completely control the weather in a region up to regional scale (50km across) for one day. Summon or dissipate storms, redirect lightning, freeze or thaw. Paradox +3. |
| Force Dominion (3) | One type of energy (fire, electricity, gravity, light) does not affect you for one scene. Bullets stop in the air; flames part around you; falls become gentle glides. Paradox +2. |
| Solar Redirect (5) | Redirect a focused beam of solar radiation at a point. Area effect: 10-meter radius, Aggravated damage to all in the area every round for [hits] rounds. Paradox +5; chronicle-event level. |
| Primal Stillness (3) | Neutralize all energy in an area up to Medium range (up to 60m) across — lights go out, fires extinguish, electricity stops, sound cannot propagate, kinetic energy halts. Duration: one scene. Paradox +3. |

### Worked Examples — Forces

> FORCES ● ROTE — KINETIC TOUCH (Akashic Brotherhood)
> 
> Sasha (Akashic Brotherhood, Forces ●●, Resolve ●●●●) needs to silently slide a door bolt open from the wrong side.  He places two fingers against the metal and channels a precise kinetic pulse through it.
> 
> Pool: Forces ● (1) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 5 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: highest dot 1 − 1 = 0, minimum 1).  Difficulty: 1.
> Roll: [9, 8, 6, 3, 2] = 3 hits, 2 net.  The bolt slides back cleanly.  No sound.  No visible force.
> Result: Applies precise kinetic force to any small object within Close range and actual physical contact — enough to open a bolt, press a button, or nudge a key. This is a [Touch]-tagged effect: Sasha must be at Close range and actually touching the mechanism. Cannot harm living targets at ●.
> COINCIDENTAL — reads as a careful mechanical manipulation.  Zero Paradox risk.
> 
> At ● Forces, Sasha can nudge the physical world but not strike it.  This is a tool, not a weapon — the sphere's most basic expression: directed force at the smallest scale.

> FORCES ●● ROTE — THUNDER PALM (Akashic Brotherhood)
> 
> Sasha (Akashic Brotherhood, Forces ●●, Resolve ●●●●) channels chi through a palm strike, adding kinetic force to the blow.
> 
> Pool: Forces ●● (2) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 6 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: 2 − 1 = 1).  Difficulty: 2.
> Roll: [9, 7, 5, 4, 3, 1] = 3 hits, 1 net.  The palm strike lands with focused force.
> Result: +3 damage to the attack, [Stun] tag applied if 3+ net hits total.
> COINCIDENTAL — looks like an exceptionally powerful martial arts strike.  Standard chi enhancement; no Paradox.

> FORCES ●●● FREEFORM — LOCALIZED BLACKOUT
> 
> Elias (Order of Hermes, Forces ●●●, Resolve ●●●●) severs power to a section of the building to create cover.
> 
> Pool: Forces ●●● (3) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 7 dice.  SF: 3.  Difficulty: 3.  Default area: Medium (30m radius).
> Roll: [9, 8, 8, 6, 5, 3, 1] = 5 hits, 2 net hits.  Power goes out across the target zone.
> Result: [Low Visibility] environmental tag applied to Medium (30m radius).  Lasts the scene unless power is restored.
> FREE HIT SPEND: Elias spends both free hits to extend area to Large Group (building-sized) instead of Medium.
> COINCIDENTAL — framed as an electrical fault from equipment overload.  Coincidental rating holds if no direct witness can disprove it.

> FORCES ●●●● FREEFORM — LIGHTNING LANCE
> 
> Elias (Virtual Adepts, Forces ●●●●, Resolve ●●●●) is on the roof of a parking structure.  A Void Engineer drop-squad is rappelling down the facing building.  He needs to take them off the ropes without touching the lines himself.
> 
> Pool: Forces ●●●● (4) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 8 dice.  SF: 4 (freeform).  Difficulty: 4.
> Roll: [10, 9, 8, 7, 5, 3, 2, 1] = 6 hits (10 = 2), 2 net hits.  A focused bolt of electrical force leaps from Elias's outstretched hand.  Base damage: Forces ●●●● = 4 Lethal.  Each net hit adds +1.  Total: 6 Lethal against the lead agent.
> Result: Ranged electrical attack.  Default range for Forces ●●●● = Long (61–1,000 meters, line of sight).  The bolt cauterizes as it burns — the agent's rappel harness fuses and he can't move his right arm.
> VULGAR — a lightning bolt from a clear sky at night is not easily explained.  SF 4 means the Storyguide draws four cards; Paradox backlash is possible.  Elias has a Faraday suit prop and three Technocracy-style gadgets on his person; if challenged, he claims directed-energy prototype.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: 2 free hits.  First hit extends damage to the second agent on the same rope (splash arc — 3 Lethal).  Second hit applies the [Stunned] tag, dropping the lead agent's actions for one turn.

> FORCES ●●●●● FREEFORM — WEATHER MASTERY
> 
> The cabal is defending a mountain chantry.  A Technocracy strike team is approaching through a forest valley below.  Elias (Virtual Adepts, Forces ●●●●●, Resolve ●●●●) reaches into the atmosphere and reshapes the local weather pattern.  A cold front is already building to the north — he accelerates it by three hours.
> 
> Pool: Forces ●●●●● (5) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 9 dice.  SF: 5 (freeform).  Difficulty: 5.
> Roll: [10, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 3, 2, 1] = 7 hits (10 = 2), 2 net hits.  A storm rolls in within ten minutes instead of three hours.  Visibility drops to 20m.  Wind gusts exceed 60 kph.  The forest valley becomes a kill zone for anything not protected from the elements.
> Result: Alters local weather patterns over a 5-km radius for the scene (or longer with sustained concentration).  Can create storms, fog banks, electrical activity, temperature extremes, or conversely calm existing weather.  All environmental modifiers from the storm (reduced visibility, wind interference with ranged attacks, slippery ground) apply to everyone in the area.
> COINCIDENTAL if a weather system already exists in the region (he accelerated something real).  VULGAR if he conjures weather from nothing — a thunderstorm from a clear sky in the desert, for instance, has no natural explanation.  SF 5 with the coincidental cover of the existing cold front keeps Paradox risk manageable.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: 2 free hits.  First hit targets the storm's electrical activity — lightning strikes the valley three times per round, each strike requiring exposed targets to make a Difficulty 3 Resolve roll or take 2 Lethal from a near-miss.  Second hit extends duration to the full night, not just the scene.

## Life — The Sphere of Living Patterns

Life is the most intimate Sphere. It deals not with the abstract laws of physics or the cold distances of space, but with the warm, wet, breathing miracle of biology. A Life mage reads bodies like books and edits them like manuscripts. They are surgeons without instruments, viral engineers without laboratories, shapeshifters without the need for a sympathetic wolf.

Tradition Affinity: Verbena. Technocracy Affinity: Progenitors. Order of Hermes approaches Life through alchemical analogy.

## Life Dot Levels

### Life 1 — Sense Life

The mage perceives living patterns directly: they sense the health, emotional state, and general biology of any living creature they examine. They can feel disease, spot structural injuries, and sense the presence of life through walls or in darkness.

**Passive Sense:**

The mage can always tell if a creature is alive and roughly how healthy they are. This gives +1 die to Medicine rolls when treating someone they have examined with this sense.

#### Sphere Tricks — Life 1

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Diagnose (1) | You instantly know the nature, severity, and probable course of any natural illness or injury affecting a target you touch. No Medicine roll required. |
| Life Sense (1) | Detect all living beings within Medium range (up to 60m), including through walls. You know their rough size and vitality. |
| Fertility Sense (2) | Determine the exact reproductive state and health of any plant or animal. Used for agricultural magic, tracking cycles, or identifying supernatural creatures by biology. |

### Life 2 — Affect Simple Life

The mage can now alter simple life — plants and animals below complex vertebrate cognition. They can heal wounds in animals, accelerate plant growth, command insects, or cause plants to move and grasp. Complex organisms (mammals, birds, humans) can be healed of minor injuries.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Heal [hits] boxes of Bashing or Lethal damage on a human or animal (Rosamund's core healing). Coincidental.
- Accelerate plant growth (a vine grows to Short range (up to 20m) in seconds). Vulgar.
- Command a small animal to perform a simple task. Coincidental if the animal might plausibly do it anyway.
- Cause a plant or fungus to release chemical compounds (pollen, toxins). Coincidental.

#### Sphere Tricks — Life 2

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Heal (1) | [Hits] boxes of Bashing or Lethal damage are healed on a touched target. Coincidental. |
| Fortify (1) | Target gains Armor 1 (natural toughness) for one scene. Vulgar — their skin subtly thickens. |
| Entangle (2) | Plants (or flesh-like growth) grapple one target. Grappled status until the target breaks free (Might + Athletics, Difficulty 2) or the plants are destroyed (Health 4). |
| Purge Toxin (2) | Remove one poison or disease from a target. Coincidental for disease; Vulgar for instantaneous poison removal. |

### Life 3 — Reshape Life

The mage can alter the basic form of living creatures: enhancing attributes, granting new capabilities, altering appearance. They can also cause significant harm by attacking a target's biology directly.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Grant or remove a physical capability (night vision, enhanced strength, webbed fingers). Vulgar.
- Heal Aggravated damage. Coincidental if slow; Vulgar if rapid.
- Cause a target's body to attack itself (anaphylaxis, autoimmune response, cardiac arrhythmia). Vulgar.
- Transform a simple animal into a different simple animal.

#### Sphere Tricks — Life 3

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Enhance Attribute (2) | Target's chosen Attribute increases by 1 for [hits] scenes. Cannot exceed 5. Vulgar. |
| Bio-Attack (2) | You attack a target's biology. 2 Lethal damage, bypasses mundane Armor. Vulgar — the target clutches their chest/stomach/head with no visible cause. |
| Heal Aggravated (2) | 1 box of Aggravated damage healed on a touched target. Vulgar and slow — requires a full scene of sustained effort. |
| Shapeshifting (3) | You reshape the target's physical form substantially — changing apparent species, major structural features, voice, fingerprints. Duration: [hits] hours. Vulgar. |

### Life 4 — Control Complex Life

The mage has full control over any living organism's biology. They can rebuild bodies from near-death, create new life, command any living creature, and turn their own body into a weapon of bewildering capability.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Stabilize any injury, no matter how severe. Prevent death even without resources.
- Transform yourself or another into any biological creature of similar mass. Vulgar.
- Command a living creature with complete authority — it obeys as if it were a trained animal, and complex commands can be given. Vulgar.
- Create new organisms (small, simple ones at this level; complex ones require Life 5).

#### Sphere Tricks — Life 4

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Apex Predator (2) | You transform into a large, dangerous animal (lion, bear, shark) for a scene. Your physical attributes become: Might 5, Dexterity 4, Stamina 5. You retain your mind and Sphere access. |
| Puppetmaster (3) | You seize direct control of a living target's motor functions. They are Immobilized and move at your command (Major Action to command). Resist: Stamina + Resolve, Difficulty 3. Vulgar. |
| Regeneration (2) | Target heals 1 box of any damage type at the start of each of their turns for [hits] rounds. |

### Life 5 — Life Mastery

The mage is a god of biology. They create life at will, can bring the dead back if death occurred within the last scene, reshape ecosystems, and transform living creatures permanently. At this level, the mage's own body can be maintained indefinitely — they do not age while sustaining a Life 5 self-affecting working.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Create complex organisms from raw biological material.
- Permanently transform any living creature into any other.
- Restore the recently dead (within 1 scene; beyond that is Spirit territory, and then only if the soul cooperates).

#### Sphere Tricks — Life 5

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Create Life (4) | Create a new living organism from raw biological material. Simple organisms (microbes, insects) require Threshold 5; complex animals require Threshold 15+; sapient life is a chronicle-defining act requiring Prime 4. |
| Death Reversal (4) | Restore life to a creature that died within the current scene, healing all damage. The creature returns exactly as they were. Beyond one scene, additional conditions apply (Threshold 20 + ritual requirements). Paradox +3. |
| Ecosystem Reshaping (3) | Permanently alter the biology of a local ecosystem — change what plants grow in an area, introduce or remove a species' presence, alter migration patterns. Duration: permanent within the affected zone. |
| Perfect Body (3) | Permanently alter your own biology to achieve any physical quality: eliminate all disease and toxin susceptibility, achieve biological immortality (aging stops), enhance all physical Attributes by 1. Paradox +2 per permanent alteration. |
| Plague Mastery (4) | Create or annihilate a disease at the biological-pattern level. You can engineer a pathogen with specific targets (affecting only beings with a specific genetic marker) or completely purge an existing infection from a region. Paradox +3 to create; +1 to purge. |
| Death Touch (3) [Touch] | A single touch kills any living organism up to the size of a horse. Requires Close range and actual physical contact with the target. Larger organisms suffer [hits] levels of Aggravated damage instead. Resisted: Stamina + Fortitude vs. Difficulty 4. Always Vulgar. |

### Worked Examples — Life

> LIFE ● ROTE — SENSE LIFE (Akashic Brotherhood)
> 
> Sasha (Life ●●, Resolve ●●●●) presses his palm flat on a warehouse floor and extends his biological awareness through the building.  He wants to know whether the room above is occupied before he climbs the stairs.
> 
> Pool: Life ● (1) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 5 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: highest dot 1 − 1 = 0, minimum 1).  Difficulty: 1.
> Roll: [9, 8, 6, 2, 1] = 3 hits, 2 net.  Sasha senses three living beings above: two with elevated heart rates (alert, nervous), one calmer and seated.  None are animals — all human-level biological complexity.
> Result: Detects number, rough size, and emotional state (via biochemistry) of all living beings within Short range (up to 20m).  Cannot identify species beyond "mammal / non-mammal" at ●.
> COINCIDENTAL — reads as exceptional situational awareness or a trained meditation technique.  The Akashic tradition teaches this rote as a basic combat-awareness kata.

> LIFE ●● ROTE — IRON SKIN (Akashic Brotherhood)
> 
> Sasha (Life ●●, Resolve ●●●●) hardens his body with focused chi before combat begins.
> 
> Pool: Life ●● (2) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 6 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: 2 − 1 = 1).  Difficulty: 1 (low-stress environment).
> Roll: [8, 6, 5, 4, 3, 1] = 3 hits.  Iron Skin settles over him.
> Result: [Armored 2] tag for the scene.  Each incoming hit has 2 damage absorbed.
> COINCIDENTAL — peak physical conditioning is a natural explanation for unusual resilience.
> NOTE: Iron Skin declared in Phase 1 resolves in Phase 2 (SF 1).  It does NOT protect Sasha from attacks in Phase 1.

> LIFE ●●● FREEFORM — ACCELERATED HEALING
> 
> Rosamund (Verbena, Life ●●●, Resolve ●●●●) accelerates a wounded cabal-mate's recovery between scenes.
> 
> Pool: Life ●●● (3) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 7 dice.  SF: 3.  Difficulty: 2 (healing a willing, accessible target).
> Roll: [9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 2] = 5 hits, 3 net.  Cellular repair accelerates.
> Result: Target heals 3 boxes of Bashing damage or 1 box of Lethal damage immediately.
> COINCIDENTAL for Bashing — fast recovery reads as natural resilience.  Healing Lethal at visible speed is BORDERLINE VULGAR.
> If Sleepers observe Lethal wounds visibly closing, Storyguide should call it Vulgar.

> LIFE ●●●● FREEFORM — ACCELERATED HEALING (AGGRAVATED)
> 
> Rosamund (Verbena, Life ●●●●, Resolve ●●●●) is treating a fellow mage whose arm was burned by a Technocracy laser — Aggravated damage, the worst kind.  She places her hands on the wound and works for a full minute of ritual chanting, drawing life-force from a fresh-cut branch she carries as a focus.
> 
> Pool: Life ●●●● (4) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 8 dice.  SF: 4 (freeform).  Difficulty: 4.
> Roll: [10, 9, 8, 7, 5, 3, 2, 1] = 6 hits (10 = 2), 2 net hits.  Rosamund heals 2 boxes of Aggravated damage — the burned flesh knits back to healthy skin over about 30 seconds.  The tissue is fully regenerated, not merely closed.
> Result: Heals Aggravated damage in another living being — 1 box per hit, minimum 1.  This requires physical contact and takes one full minute of concentration (cannot be rushed without an additional Resolve roll).
> VULGAR — the healing is visible and undeniable.  Skin grows back in real time.  Any Sleeper witnessing this is immediately aware something impossible has happened.  Rosamund's ritual aesthetic (chanting, herbal focus) makes the magic look like folklore rather than obvious power — some Storyguides may rule this reduces the Paradox pool by one die.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: 2 free hits.  First hit clears all scar tissue — the healed arm is structurally perfect, not just functional.  Second hit extends the healing to also seal a secondary wound (1 Lethal) at no additional cost.

> LIFE ●●●●● FREEFORM — APEX PREDATOR
> 
> Rosamund (Verbena, Life ●●●●●, Resolve ●●●●) needs to cross hostile territory — a forest controlled by a rival Tradition's territorial spirits who will respond to magic but not to animals.  She fully transforms into a large grey wolf, retaining her intelligence, memories, and Sphere access.
> 
> Pool: Life ●●●●● (5) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 9 dice.  SF: 5 (freeform).  Difficulty: 5.
> Roll: [10, 10, 9, 8, 6, 4, 2, 1, 1] = 7 hits (each 10 = 2), 2 net hits.  Rosamund's body reshapes over about 3 seconds — bones remodel, mass redistributes, sensory organs transform.  She is now a 45-kilogram grey wolf with full human consciousness.
> Result: Complete physical transformation into any natural animal form up to twice the caster's original mass.  The character retains full mental capacity and may still cast Sphere effects.  Physical attributes shift to match the animal form (Storyguide assigns appropriate values).  Duration: scene, or until voluntarily released.
> HIGHLY VULGAR — full shapeshifting is unmistakably magical.  In front of Sleepers, this is a category-one Paradox event.  Rosamund performs the transformation inside a ring of standing stones on a moonlit night — Verbena ritual aesthetics that her Avatar uses as a focus.  Even so, SF 5 draws five cards from the Storyguide.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: 2 free hits.  First hit allows Rosamund to communicate in human language while transformed (a low, harmonic growl that other Awakened understand perfectly).  Second hit extends duration to the full session rather than just the scene.

## Matter — The Sphere of Substance and Form

Matter governs the inanimate: stone, steel, glass, water, air, and all non-living material. Where Life deals with the warm complexity of biology, Matter deals with the cold elegance of chemistry and physics. Matter mages transmute substances, reshape objects, create new materials, and understand the molecular structure of everything they touch.

Tradition Affinity: Sons of Ether. Technocracy Affinity: Progenitors, Iteration X. Order of Hermes approaches it through alchemical theory.

## Matter Dot Levels

### Matter 1 — Matter Perception

The mage reads materials: they know the composition, integrity, history of use, and structural properties of anything they examine. They can see through illusions of substance (fake walls, disguised metals) and detect hidden compartments or flaws.

**Passive Sense:**

The mage automatically knows the basic composition of any material they touch. They receive +1 die on Technology or Science rolls involving materials analysis.

#### Sphere Tricks — Matter 1

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Assay (1) | You know the exact chemical composition and structural integrity of any object you touch. |
| Detect Hidden (1) | You detect hollow spaces, hidden doors, and concealed objects within Close range (0–2m). No roll required; this is automatic perception. |
| Appraise (2) | You know the monetary value, age, and provenance of any object you examine. Replaces Culture or Larceny rolls for valuation. |

### Matter 2 — Alter Properties

The mage can now change the properties of existing materials without changing their fundamental composition. They can make metal flexible, glass unbreakable, wood fireproof, or water thick as honey.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Alter a material's hardness, flexibility, conductivity, or transparency. Coincidental if subtle; Vulgar if dramatic.
- Purify a substance — remove contaminants from water, strip poison from food. Coincidental.
- Shape soft materials (clay, mud, water, fabric) by will. Coincidental for subtle shaping.

#### Sphere Tricks — Matter 2

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Harden (1) | One object gains +2 Armor for this scene (its material becomes supernaturally tough). |
| Liquefy (2) | A solid object up to 50kg becomes liquid for one scene. The substance itself does not change (iron becomes liquid iron, still metallic and scalding). |
| Purify (1) | Remove all toxins, contaminants, or diseases from up to 1 liter of liquid or 1 kg of solid material. |
| Phase (2) | One object up to 50kg becomes temporarily intangible for one round — it can pass through a single solid barrier. |

### Matter 3 — Transmutation

The mage can change one substance into another. Lead into gold, wood into steel, sand into glass. The transmutation requires the mage to understand both materials' composition (Intellect-based knowledge) and can be permanent with sufficient hits.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Transmute up to 50kg of one substance into another. Vulgar if witnessed.
- Create a working object from raw material (a functional gun from scrap metal, a key from clay). Requires a Craft narrative justification.
- Dissolve an object completely into its constituent elements.

#### Sphere Tricks — Matter 3

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Transmute (2) | Change up to 50kg of one substance into another. Permanent with 3+ hits; temporary (1 scene) with fewer. |
| Fabricate (2) | Create a functioning mechanical or electronic object from raw materials. Quality is equivalent to professional manufacture. |
| Disintegrate (3) | One non-living object is dissolved into its base elements. Objects up to 100kg per Sphere level. Vulgar. |

### Matter 4 — Reshape Matter

At this level, the mage can reshape the molecular structure of matter at will, creating entirely new materials with properties that do not exist in nature. Substances harder than diamond, materials that absorb specific frequencies, composites that respond to magical command.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Create a novel material — one whose properties do not exist naturally. A substance that conducts cold instead of heat, a metal that becomes rigid only when stressed, glass that is opaque to radar but transparent to light.
- Reshape up to 500kg of any material into a complex three-dimensional form with tolerances tighter than machine manufacturing. This is Vulgar only if witnessed; shaping that happens "off-screen" is usually Coincidental.
- Disassemble and reassemble complex mechanisms at the molecular level — extracting a bullet from a sealed gun without opening it, separating alloys back to component metals, removing a specific molecule from a compound.
- Permanently alter an object's material properties: make steel as light as aluminum, give cloth the tensile strength of Kevlar, make a window bulletproof.

#### Sphere Tricks — Matter 4

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Exotic Material (2) | You create a small amount (~1kg) of a material with one property not found in nature (perfect thermal insulation, selective phase shifting, supernatural conductivity). Duration: permanent. |
| Sculpt (1) | Reshape up to 500kg of any material into any form at the rate of 10kg per round. |
| Molecular Lock (2) | One object becomes permanently indestructible by mundane means — only Matter 4+ magic or Aggravated damage can affect it. |

### Matter 5 — Matter Mastery

The mage commands matter absolutely. They can create matter from nothing (technically drawing on Quintessence — requires Prime 3 as a prerequisite), unmake matter entirely, and reshape geology on a significant scale.

#### Sphere Tricks — Matter 5

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Create Matter (4) | Conjure matter from Quintessence. Requires Prime 3 as a co-requisite to handle the released pattern energy. Creates up to [hits] kg of any material the mage understands at the molecular level. Paradox +3. |
| Unmake Matter (4) | Dissolve any non-living object entirely — it ceases to exist. No ash, no rubble, no residue. Volume limit: up to a small building. Requires Prime 3 to safely handle the released Quintessence. Paradox +4. |
| Geology Command (3) | Reshape stone and earth on a significant scale — redirect a river, raise a hill, open a chasm, seal a cave system. Duration: permanent. Paradox +3; Vulgar except in unpopulated areas. |
| Perfect Construct (3) | Create an object with three impossible properties simultaneously (frictionless, perfectly rigid, selectively phase-shifting). Duration: permanent without maintenance. |
| Pattern Lock (4) | Permanently freeze an object's molecular state — it cannot be altered, damaged, corroded, or destroyed by mundane or magical means below Matter 5 level. The object exists in a locked quantum state. |

### Worked Examples — Matter

> MATTER ● ROTE — MATERIAL SENSE (Sons of Ether)
> 
> Karl (Sons of Ether, Matter ●●●, Intellect ●●●●) is handed a counterfeit coin at a market stall.  He flips it once, catches it, and in the half-second of contact his monocle-mounted spectrometer-rote reads the alloy composition.
> 
> Pool: Matter ● (1) + Intellect ●●●● (4) = 5 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: highest dot 1 − 1 = 0, minimum 1).  Difficulty: 1.
> Roll: [10, 8, 5, 3, 2] = 3 hits, 2 net.  The rote returns a complete elemental breakdown: 83% zinc, 14% aluminium, trace copper.  Not silver.  Not even close.
> Result: Full elemental and structural analysis of any non-living material by touch.  Identifies composition, purity, structural integrity, and any non-obvious alterations (plating, veneers, internal voids).
> COINCIDENTAL — Karl's monocle prop makes this look exactly like a scientific instrument.  Zero Paradox risk.
> 
> At ● Matter, Karl cannot alter materials — only read them.  This rote is foundational to his practice: before he changes anything, he understands exactly what it is.

> MATTER ●● ROTE — STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (Sons of Ether)
> 
> Karl (Sons of Ether, Matter ●●●, Intellect ●●●●) analyzes a suspicious lock before attempting to bypass it.
> 
> Pool: Matter ●● (2) + Intellect ●●●● (4) = 6 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: 2 − 1 = 1).  Difficulty: 1.
> Roll: [9, 8, 6, 4, 3, 2] = 3 hits.  Karl reads the material composition, tolerances, and failure points instantly.
> Result: Storyguide reveals the lock type, material weakness, and exactly what tool would open or defeat it.
> COINCIDENTAL — reads as materials science expertise.  Karl has a scanning monocle that sells the effect completely.

> MATTER ●●● FREEFORM — SOFTEN METAL
> 
> Karl needs to escape from handcuffs without a key.  He transmutes the steel temporarily to a tin-like softness.
> 
> Pool: Matter ●●● (3) + Intellect ●●●● (4) = 7 dice.  SF: 3.  Difficulty: 3.
> Roll: [10, 9, 7, 5, 4, 3, 2] = 5 hits (10 = 2), 2 net.  The cuffs go soft.  He slips free.
> Result: One metal object (up to Small Group size) has its hardness and strength reduced to near-zero for [hits] rounds.
> VULGAR — metal visibly changing texture is not explainable.  With no witnesses: 1 Paradox die.  With Sleeper witnesses: full Paradox roll.

> MATTER ●●●● FREEFORM — TRANSMUTATION
> 
> Karl needs a significant quantity of platinum to build a critical component for his Etheric Resonance Coil.  Platinum is expensive and traceable.  He has a large bar of lead.  He intends to transmute it.
> 
> Pool: Matter ●●●● (4) + Intellect ●●●● (4) = 8 dice.  SF: 4 (freeform).  Difficulty: 4.
> Roll: [10, 9, 9, 8, 5, 3, 2, 1] = 6 hits (10 = 2), 2 net hits.  Karl reconfigures the atomic lattice of 500 grams of lead.  The bar's grey, dull surface becomes the bright, cool silver of platinum.  Density and tensile properties match perfectly.  A mass spectrometer would confirm: this is platinum.
> Result: Transforms one non-living material into another of comparable mass and volume — permanently.  The transmuted material is real in every measurable sense.  Default volume for Matter ●●●● = several kilograms.
> VULGAR — any witness sees lead become platinum.  Karl performs this in his locked laboratory with blackout curtains.  Even so, the Technocracy's Reality Anchor in the building's basement may register the resonance shift.  SF 4 makes this risky; Karl has prepared a Paradox sink (a Tass reserve he can expend if the backlash triggers).
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: 2 free hits.  First hit increases the transmuted mass to 2 kilograms.  Second hit refines the crystalline structure to aerospace-grade purity — better than anything commercially available.

> MATTER ●●●●● FREEFORM — UNMAKE
> 
> The cabal is trapped behind a solid steel blast door — 15 centimetres of hardened ballistic steel, magnetically sealed.  There is no time to cut it.  Karl places both palms flat on the surface and reduces its atomic bonds to nothing.
> 
> Pool: Matter ●●●●● (5) + Intellect ●●●● (4) = 9 dice.  SF: 5 (freeform).  Difficulty: 5.
> Roll: [10, 9, 9, 8, 7, 5, 3, 2, 1] = 7 hits (10 = 2), 2 net hits.  A 2-metre section of the blast door simply ceases to exist.  The remaining steel does not fall — there is no remaining steel at the edges of the removed section.  The metal is gone at the subatomic level.  No rubble.  No debris.  A clean rectangular void.
> Result: Permanently unmakes a volume of non-living material up to the size of a large vehicle (or roughly 3 cubic metres of solid matter).  The material does not move or transform — it ceases to exist.  This effect is instantaneous and irreversible.
> HIGHLY VULGAR — matter does not simply vanish.  Even Sleepers who are not present will notice the impossible-clean edge where the door was.  SF 5 is maximum Paradox pressure; Karl burns an Arete-fuelled Tass immediately after to absorb the worst of the backlash.  He walks through the void without looking back.
> 
> FREE HIT SPEND: 2 free hits.  First hit makes the effect silent (no implosive rush of air, no sound at all — the steel is simply absent without the universe noticing the gap).  Second hit extends the unmaking to a second adjacent section, creating a full double-width exit.

## Mind — The Sphere of Thought and Consciousness

Consciousness is not a product of the brain. The brain is an antenna. Mind mages learn to adjust the signal. They read thoughts, plant ideas, construct entirely false realities in other minds, and ultimately confront the terrifying question: if all of this is perception, what is real?

Tradition Affinity: Akashic Brotherhood, Cult of Ecstasy, Virtual Adepts. Technocracy Affinity: NWO (who call it "social engineering" and "memetic influence").

## Mind Dot Levels

### Mind 1 — Sense Thoughts

The mage perceives the surface emotional and intentional state of others. They cannot read specific thoughts, but they feel the emotional weather — anxiety, aggression, joy, deception.

**Passive Sense:**

The mage always knows the dominant emotional state of people they interact with. They cannot be surprised by emotional shifts — they felt them coming.

#### Sphere Tricks — Mind 1

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Truth Sense (1) | You know immediately when someone tells a significant deliberate lie. Purely perceptual. |
| Mood Read (1) | You know the detailed emotional state of up to [hits] people in your immediate vicinity — not thoughts, but feelings. |
| Intention Sense (2) | You sense the immediate short-term intention of one target. You know what they are about to do before they do it. +2 dice on defense against that target for one round. |

### Mind 2 — Read and Shield Minds

The mage can read surface thoughts (what a person is actively thinking), project simple impressions (a feeling, a one-word concept), and create mental barriers against other Mind effects.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Read the active thoughts of a target. Opposed: Mage's Mind + Manipulation vs. Target's Resolve + Composure.
- Project a single impression, emotion, or concept into a target's mind. Coincidental (they think they had the thought themselves) if subtle.
- Create a mental shield (Armor 2 against all Mind effects) that lasts for one scene.

#### Sphere Tricks — Mind 2

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Surface Read (1) | Read the active thoughts of a target for one round. They do not know you have done this. Difficulty 2 if they are actively trying to conceal their thoughts. |
| Nudge (1) | Plant one simple emotional state or impression in a target's mind. They believe they feel it naturally. Lasts one scene. |
| Mind Shield (1) | You (or a touched target) gain Armor 2 vs. all Mind effects for one scene. |
| Detect Mind (2) | You know if any Mind effects are currently active on a target, and roughly how powerful they are. |

### Mind 3 — Influence and Rewrite

The mage can directly impose specific thoughts, false memories, complex commands, and sustained emotional states. They can also build walled-off sections of mind (compartments that even the target cannot consciously access) and destroy mental blocks.

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Plant a false memory. Opposed roll. The memory feels genuine unless the target achieves a remarkable Enigmas + Resolve success (Difficulty 3).
- Command a target to perform a complex multi-step task. They believe they chose to do it.
- Erase a specific memory (a face, an event, a skill temporarily).
- Access unconscious knowledge the target does not know they have.

#### Sphere Tricks — Mind 3

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| False Memory (2) | Plant a specific memory in a target. They believe it is real. Resist: Enigmas + Resolve, Difficulty 3, one attempt per day. Vulgar if done rapidly and forcefully. |
| Command (2) | The target must perform a complex task (up to one day's duration of behavior). They believe they chose it. Resist: Resolve + Composure, Difficulty 3. |
| Mind Wipe (3) | Erase all memory of a specific event or time period from a target. Vulgar. The gap is noticeable — the target is confused about missing time. |
| Construct (2) | Build a mental construct (a hidden room, a locked memory, a mental companion) within a target's mind. You can access it later. |

### Mind 4 — Dominate Consciousness

The mage can fully dominate another consciousness, communicate across any distance mind-to-mind, create shared mental spaces, and project their consciousness into another body (leaving their own inert).

**Active Effects (Freeform):**

- Possession: project your consciousness into a target's body. Your body falls inert and vulnerable. You have full access to the target's motor functions and surface memories. Duration limited by hits. Contested: Resolve + Composure. Always Vulgar.
- Mind Bridge: establish a two-way telepathic communication link with any known target, anywhere in the world. No range limit; the connection is instantaneous and private.
- Shared Mental Space: create a mindscape — a shared interior environment — that you and up to [hits] willing participants can inhabit simultaneously. The mindscape is real to all participants's senses. Duration: one scene.
- Memory Architecture: restructure a target's mental organization — build hidden compartments, install compulsion loops, erase entire categories of memory. The target cannot consciously detect what has been done.

#### Sphere Tricks — Mind 4

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Possession (3) | Your consciousness inhabits a target's body. Your body falls inert. You have full access to the target's motor functions and surface skills. Duration: [hits] scenes. Resist: Resolve + Composure, Difficulty 4. Vulgar. |
| Mind Bridge (2) | Establish a two-way mental communication link with a known target anywhere in the world. No range limit. Duration: one scene. |
| Mental Maze (3) | Trap a target's consciousness in a labyrinthine mental construct. They are effectively unconscious and unresponsive for [hits] hours. Resist: Enigmas + Resolve, Difficulty 4. |

### Mind 5 — Mind Mastery

The mage can affect entire groups simultaneously, create entirely new consciousnesses (artificial minds — useful with Prime 3), or permanently alter the fundamental cognitive architecture of a target. At this level, the boundary between the mage's mind and the world becomes dangerously thin.

#### Sphere Tricks — Mind 5

| Trick (Cost) | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| Mass Mind Affect (3) | Your Mind Effects extend to any number of targets within line of sight simultaneously, with no additional casting roll required per target. One roll covers the group. Paradox scales with target count (+1 Paradox die per 5 additional minds).  |
| Create Consciousness (4) | Construct a new, fully functional mind and install it in a prepared vessel (a constructed body, an AI system, a prepared automaton). The new mind is independent, not controlled by the mage. Requires Prime 3. Paradox +4. |
| Cognitive Architecture Rewrite (4) | Permanently alter the fundamental cognitive structure of a target — change their core personality traits, erase a core value, install a new belief system at the bedrock level. The target does not know this has been done. Paradox +3. Extremely Vulgar; contested by Resolve + Composure. |
| Gestalt Mind (3) | Merge the consciousness of a willing group (up to [hits] members) into a shared hive-mind for one scene. The group shares sensory awareness, memories, and tactical information instantaneously. Communication is perfect within the gestalt. |
| Ego Erasure (4) | Destroy a mind's sense of self entirely — the target becomes a blank, passive state. They retain biological function but no personality, motivation, or autonomous agency. Reversible with Mind 4+. Paradox +4; always Vulgar. |

### Worked Examples — Mind

> MIND ● ROTE — EMOTIONAL READ (Akashic Brotherhood)
> 
> Sasha (Mind ●●●, Resolve ●●●●) sits across from a potential informant.  Before the informant says a word, Sasha wants to know his emotional baseline — is he frightened?  Lying to himself?  Resolved?
> 
> Pool: Mind ● (1) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 5 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote: highest dot 1 − 1 = 0, minimum 1).  Difficulty: 1.
> Roll: [9, 7, 5, 3, 2] = 2 hits, 1 net.  Sasha reads the informant's surface emotional state: low-grade fear, not directed at Sasha specifically — at something else.  Guilt, old and buried.  And underneath both: a thin thread of hope.
> Result: Reads the surface emotional state of any person within Short range (up to 20m).  Does not access thoughts, memories, or intent — only the emotional texture of this moment.
> COINCIDENTAL — reads as exceptional empathy or a trained mediator's intuition.  Akashic tradition teaches this as basic Do awareness: feel what the opponent feels before they act.

> MIND ●● ROTE — MIND'S EYE (Akashic Brotherhood)
> 
> Sasha (Mind ●●●, Resolve ●●●●) sweeps a room for active mental signatures before entering.
> 
> Pool: Mind ●●● at ●● application (2 dots' worth of effect) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 6 dice.  SF: 1 (Rote).  Difficulty: 2.
> Roll: [8, 7, 5, 4, 3, 1] = 3 hits, 1 net.  He senses the mental landscape of the corridor.
> Result: Sasha detects the number, emotional state, and rough location of all conscious minds within Short range (up to 20m).  He counts three Sleepers and one Awakened.
> COINCIDENTAL — framed as martial awareness and chi-sensitivity.  No Paradox.

> MIND ●●● FREEFORM — SURFACE READ
> 
> Sasha reads an NWO agent's surface thoughts during an interrogation — not a compulsion, just listening.
> 
> Pool: Mind ●●● (3) + Resolve ●●●● (4) = 7 dice.  SF: 3.  Difficulty: 3 (resisting target: Willpower 5 = Difficulty 3).
> Roll: [9, 8, 7, 6, 4, 3, 1] = 5 hits, 2 net.  The agent's surface thoughts become audible.
> Result: Sasha learns the agent's immediate intentions and one piece of information she is actively thinking about.
> BORDERLINE: Coincidental if Sasha frames it as reading body language; Vulgar if he acts on invisible information in ways the agent can notice.
> With Sleeper witnesses who observe no physical action but see Sasha inexplicably know things: VULGAR.

> MIND ●●●● FREEFORM — DEEP COMPULSION
>
