Primordial mages are often the most unsettling presence in a Traditionalist Chantry, because they are not fighting for magic — they are fighting for something that existed long before anyone decided to call it magic, and they are not sure the word "magic" is doing the concept justice.

Questing Essences are the most individually variable. Their Avatar is oriented toward the Quest, not toward the War — and the War is relevant only insofar as it intersects with the Quest. Some Questing mages are the most fanatical combatants in the Ascension War, because their Quest is specifically related to its outcome. Others are entirely indifferent to the War's large-scale dynamics, fighting only the battles that cross their particular path.

## Essence in Fallen Mages

Nephandi who have completed the Caul have inverted their Avatar — twisted the Avatar's nature so that it serves Oblivion or the Outer Dark rather than the mage's growth and the universe's flourishing. Inverted Essences are deeply disturbing to perceive because they use the same grammar as normal Essences while directing it toward opposite ends.

An inverted Dynamic Essence does not seek change. It seeks the change that produces final stasis — the perfect destruction, the end state from which no further change is possible. An inverted Dynamic Nephandus is often the most energetic and creative of their kind, introducing innovations and provocations and disruptions in service of the one change they want: the heat death of possibility.

An inverted Pattern Essence does not seek understanding. It seeks the architecture of despair — the comprehensive understanding of why nothing good can be sustained, why every structure eventually fails, why the universe's systems are ultimately arranged against life and consciousness. An inverted Pattern Nephandus is often extraordinarily intelligent and extraordinarily persuasive, capable of making the argument for Oblivion in terms so systematic and complete that they are difficult to rebut.

An inverted Primordial Essence does not seek ancient truth. It seeks the ancient hunger — the predatory, consuming force that existed before consciousness and that waits beneath all the structures of civilization for its chance to return. Primordial Nephandi are among the most viscerally terrifying: they operate below the level of argument, below the level of ideology, at the somatic register where the Primordial Essence normally communicates wonder.

An inverted Questing Essence does not seek its object. It seeks the absence of its object — or rather, it seeks the object in order to destroy it and prevent anyone else from finding it. Questing Nephandi are the most personally driven of the fallen: their corruption is intimate, specific, the mirror image of something they once loved or wanted desperately, now directed toward its obliteration.

Marauders, whose connection to their Avatar has broken under the weight of accumulated Quiet rather than deliberate inversion, tend to produce Essence expressions that are maximized past the point of function. A Marauder with a Dynamic Essence is not a creative force — they are an agent of pure, unstructured disruption, change without direction, momentum without object. A Marauder with a Pattern Essence is not an analyst — they are a perceiver of patterns in everything, including patterns that do not exist, organizing reality around a structure that only they can see.

## Quick Reference: Essence Summary

| Essence | Core Drive |
| --- | --- |
| Dynamic | Change, creation, the new, forward motion, breaking stasis. |
| Pattern | Understanding, structure, completion, the logic that underlies the surface. |
| Primordial | Ancient truth, somatic knowing, pre-linguistic wisdom, what is beneath all systems. |
| Questing | Specific purpose, directed pursuit, orientation toward a singular object or question. |
| Transcendent | (Optional) Dissolution of self-other boundary, connection as the ground of being. |
| Warrior | (Optional) Growth through opposition, knowledge through being tested, the forge of conflict. |
| Trickster | (Optional) Truth through irony, revelation through concealment, the necessary gap. |

| Essence | Avatar Communication Style |
| --- | --- |
| Dynamic | Urgency, itch of restlessness, sudden complete creative impulses, discomfort with repetition. |
| Pattern | Sense of completeness/incompleteness, satisfaction when a structure closes, unease when it doesn't. |
| Primordial | Somatic sensation, heat/cold/pressure, directional instinct, pre-linguistic knowing. |
| Questing | Rightness when moving toward the Quest, wrongness when moving away, orientation. |

| Essence | Quiet Form |
| --- | --- |
| Dynamic | The Treadmill: obsessive repetition experienced as novelty; loops without progress. |
| Pattern | Analytical Paralysis: perfect understanding that has become an obstacle to all action. |
| Primordial | Somatic Disconnection: the body goes silent; the mage fills the silence with intellectualization. |
| Questing | Obsessive Descent: the Quest expands until it consumes everything; no perspective remains. |

| Essence | Paradox Flavor |
| --- | --- |
| Dynamic | Instability, unpredictability, inversions, things changing in the wrong direction. |
| Pattern | Over-revelation, hidden structures becoming visible at wrong times, private becoming public. |
| Primordial | Atavistic effects: weather, animal behavior, body becoming Other. |
| Questing | Misdirection, delay, false trails, doppelgangers of the Quest's object. |

# Appendix D: Entities and Antagonists

This appendix presents full Storypath Ultra stat blocks for every significant creature, spirit, construct, mage, and monster drawn from M20 supplemental sources. All statistics have been converted from Mage: The Ascension 20th Anniversary Edition into SPU format using the conversion rules described below.

This appendix is organized into sections by category. Within each section, templates appear before named individuals, and less dangerous entries appear before more dangerous ones. Every stat block includes full SPU attributes, derived statistics, relevant skills, special abilities described as SPU Tricks or Tags, and a brief narrative description to help Storyguides bring the entity to life at the table.

Storyguides should feel free to adjust any statistic here to suit their chronicle. These numbers represent typical specimens; exceptional individuals, freshly manifested spirits, ancient entities in their prime, and weakened versions of powerful foes may all warrant modification.

## Reading These Statistics

The following conversion key explains how M20 statistics were transformed into SPU equivalents. Understanding this key allows Storyguides to convert any additional entities from M20 sources they may own.

| M20 Statistic | SPU Equivalent and Method |
| --- | --- |
| Strength | Might — direct 1-to-1. Values above 8 capped at 8 for playability, excess converted to Enhancement. |
| Dexterity | Dexterity — direct 1-to-1. |
| Stamina | Stamina — direct 1-to-1. Drives Health calculation (2 + Stamina). |
| Charisma | Presence — direct 1-to-1. |
| Manipulation | Manipulation — direct 1-to-1. |
| Appearance | Absorbed into Presence and Composure as appropriate. |
| Perception / Wits | Cunning — use the higher of the two values. |
| Intelligence | Intellect — direct 1-to-1. |
| Arete | Enlightenment — divide by 2, round up. Arete 1-2=E1; 3-4=E2; 5-6=E3; 7-8=E4; 9-10=E5. |
| Willpower | Split into Resolve and Composure. Divide by 2; assign the higher half to whichever is narratively primary. |
| Health Levels | SPU Health = 2 + Stamina (base formula). Adjusted where source level-count differs significantly. |
| Armor Rating | Armor — direct 1-to-1. |
| Spirit Rage | Enhancement bonus to physical combat = Rage / 2 (round up). Also approximates Might for materialized spirits without direct Strength. |
| Spirit Gnosis | Arete equivalent: Gnosis 1-2=E1; 3-4=E2; 5-6=E3; 7-8=E4; 9-10=E5. |
| Spirit Essence | Spirit Health pool while dematerialized or when targeted by Spirit sphere. Materialized spirits also track standard Health. |
| M20 Abilities | Alertness/Awareness=Awareness; Athletics=Athletics; Brawl/Melee=Close Combat; Firearms=Ranged Combat; Dodge=Athletics; Stealth=Larceny; Occult/Enigmas/Cosmology=Esoterica; Computer=Technology; Intimidation=Leadership; Empathy=Empathy; Subterfuge=Deception; Drive=Pilot; Crafts=Artistry; Medicine=Medicine. |
| Special Advantages | Converted to SPU Tricks or Tags. Bonus-dice powers become Enhancement. Outcome-change powers become Tricks. Innate qualities become Tags. |
| Spirit Charms | Converted to SPU Tricks. Each Charm is a named ability activated by an Enlightenment + attribute roll vs. Difficulty set by Storyguide. |

A note on scale: SPU does not use wound penalties. Characters act at full effectiveness until their Health track empties. The Storyguide may apply the [Wounded] Complication at half Health for a grittier feel.

---

## Section One: Human Templates

The following templates represent ordinary mortals at various life stages and social roles. These are the Sleepers — the vast majority of humanity — who will never know that mages walk among them. They are not powerless: a frightened crowd, a well-armed police unit, or a determined hunter can all pose genuine threats to a careless mage. Use these templates to quickly build NPCs, adjusting individual skills by ±1 to differentiate individuals without rebuilding from scratch.

```
Young Child (Ages 2–8) — Human Template
---------------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 1 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 2 | Resolve 1
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 1
Skills: Awareness 2, Athletics 1, Empathy 1
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Uncanny Perception] — Children who have not yet learned to dismiss the impossible sometimes see through glamours and illusions. When a child might notice something supernatural, the Storyguide may call for a Cunning + Awareness roll at no penalty, even against Coincidental effects that would fool adults.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Young children are not combat resources. If threatened, they flee, freeze, or scream. A mage who harms a child in view of Sleeper witnesses generates 2 extra Paradox dice immediately, regardless of how subtle the magic was. The universe notices.
Example: Joey McCallister, age seven, keeps drawing the same symbol in crayon. He does not know what it means. He just knows it makes him feel safer. It is the sigil of the Node under his school.
```

```
Teenager (Ages 14–18) — Human Template
--------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Athletics 2, Awareness 2, Close Combat 1, Empathy 2, Deception 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Impressionable] — Teenagers exposed to magical events are more likely than adults to accept the actual explanation rather than rationalizing it away. Convincing a teenager they did not see what they clearly saw requires Manipulation + Deception at Difficulty +1 compared to an adult.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Teenagers vary enormously. A scholarship athlete might have Might 3 and Athletics 4. A computer prodigy might have Intellect 4 and Technology 4. Adjust two or three skills and one attribute freely. Example: Kim Phan keeps to herself and dreams of the day she can leave. She does not know she is on the edge of Awakening. Her Cunning is 4, not 3 — she notices everything.
```

```
Everyday Person (Adult) — Human Template
----------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 2
Skills: Awareness 1, Athletics 1, one job-relevant Skill at 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: The vast middle of humanity. A nurse has Medicine 3. A mechanic has Artistry (Mechanics) 3. A police officer has Ranged Combat 2, Close Combat 2, Leadership 2. Each gains one or two skills representing their working life. In an emergency, most adults freeze for one round before acting.
Witnesses and Paradox: Every Everyday Person in line of sight of openly vulgar magic adds 1 die to the Paradox pool. A crowd of twelve people watching a fireball erupts from a mage's hands adds 12 dice immediately. This is why mages prefer empty alleys.
```

```
Hardcase (Street-Level Combatant) — Human Antagonist
----------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 2 | Resolve 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 1 (street armor/heavy clothing)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Close Combat 3, Ranged Combat 2, Athletics 2, Awareness 2, Leadership 2 (Intimidation), Larceny 2
Weapons: Fists (Enhancement 0, bashing), Knife (Enhancement +1, lethal), Pistol (Enhancement +1, lethal, range 30m)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Street-Hardened] — Does not freeze on first contact with violence. Immune to mundane intimidation. Has seen enough to know that running is sometimes correct.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Hardcases are gang enforcers, mob soldiers, and hired muscle. In groups of three or more, use Group Rules: the group rolls as one entity with Enhancement +1 per additional combatant beyond the first. Example: Jeremy Bruckner has a .38 under the counter and a reputation that keeps most problems from becoming confrontations. He knows something is wrong with the neighborhood.
```

```
Witch-Hunter — Human Antagonist
-------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 1 (tactical vest)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Ranged Combat 4, Close Combat 3, Athletics 3, Awareness 3, Esoterica 3, Investigation 3, Larceny 2, Medicine 2
Weapons: Rifle (Enhancement +2, lethal, range 200m), Knife (Enhancement +1, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Anti-Mage Training] — Adds Enhancement +1 to all actions taken against mages actively casting. Carries cold iron rounds (1 extra damage vs. spirit-touched entities), salt (disrupts materialized spirits), and a ward kit (requires 10 minutes to deploy; adds Difficulty +1 to all casting rolls within the warded area for the scene).
[Believer] — Unlike ordinary Sleepers, a Witch-Hunter has accepted that magic exists. They add no Paradox dice from witnessing vulgar magic.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Witch-Hunters prepare. They work in teams, set ambushes, and target mages in moments of vulnerability — during extended rituals, after major Paradox events, or when recovering from wounds. A mage who underestimates a hunter because they lack magical ability makes a potentially fatal error. Example: Marcus Washington has survived three mages who believed their magic made them invulnerable.
```

```
Bioenhanced Field Agent (Technocratic Union) — Human Antagonist
---------------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 8 (augmented from base 6)  |  Armor: 2 (subcutaneous polymer mesh)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Enhancement: +1 to all physical rolls (bioenhancement)
Skills: Close Combat 3, Ranged Combat 4, Athletics 4, Awareness 3, Medicine 2, Technology 3
Weapons: Service Pistol (Enhancement +1, lethal), Combat Knife (Enhancement +1, lethal), Stun Baton (Enhancement 0; target makes Stamina difficulty 2 or gains [Dazed] for 1 round)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Regenerative Bioware] — Heals 1 Health per scene automatically while above 0 Health. This is science, not magic; generates no Paradox.
[Field Conditioning] — Immune to mundane fear effects. Social rolls to panic, bribe, or dissuade are at Difficulty +2.
[Classified Assets] — Bioenhancement is internal and invisible to medical examination or standard supernatural senses. Does not radiate Enlightened resonance.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Bioenhanced agents do not generate Paradox — their powers are scientifically framed. Against a mage who relies on vulgar magic, a four-agent squad operating in a public space effectively imprisons the mage: any magical response creates a Paradox disaster. Example: Henry Woo moves like water. His psych evals are spotless. He is also reporting on his handler to a different Union department.
```

---

## Section Two: Night-Folk

The Consensus holds more than humanity. Vampires, werewolves, faeries, and ghosts share the world with mages, and their relationship to Awakened magic is complicated. Their supernatural natures are sustained by their own metaphysical frameworks, which the Consensus grudgingly accommodates as "things that have always been strange." A vampire drinking blood is not more vulgar than a wolf hunting; the universe is used to predators. Mages who traffic with Night-Folk find allies and enemies in equal measure.

```
Blood-Bound Ghoul — Night-Folk Ally/Antagonist
----------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Close Combat 3, Athletics 2, Awareness 2, Larceny 2, Medicine 1
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Blood Points — 3 total] — Spend 1 blood point as a free action to: add Enhancement +2 to a single physical roll; temporarily add 2 Health (until end of scene); or instantly heal 1 bashing Health.
[Blood Bond] — Psychologically tied to their domitor. Rolls made to act directly against the domitor suffer Difficulty +2. Must make Resolve + Composure at Difficulty 3 to even consider betrayal.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Ghouls occupy an uncomfortable middle ground — stronger than mortals, weaker than vampires, and emotionally trapped. Some serve willingly; many serve because they do not know how to stop. Mages who encounter ghouls often underestimate them. Example: Fallon Marx has been her domitor's ghoul for six years. She tells herself she serves freely. She has not tried to leave.
```

```
Young Vampire (10 Years or Less) — Night-Folk Antagonist
--------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 5  |  Armor: 1 (supernatural resilience)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Blood Points: 10  |  Skills: Close Combat 3, Athletics 2, Awareness 2, Empathy 2, Deception 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Undead Physiology] — Immune to bashing damage. Non-magical lethal sources are treated as bashing instead (accumulates, but heals fast). Magical weapons, fire, and sunlight inflict full damage.
[Blood Expenditure] — Spend 1 blood point per action: add Enhancement +2 to any single physical roll, OR heal 1 bashing Health immediately.
[Predator's Gaze] — Manipulation + Empathy (opposed by Composure + Resolve) to make a target feel unnaturally calm and still. No magic, no Paradox — just a thing vampires can do.
[Sunlight Vulnerability] — Direct sunlight: 1 aggravated Health per round. Aggravated does not benefit from supernatural healing; requires feeding and rest.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Young vampires are dangerous but not extraordinary. A prepared mage can handle one. The threat multiplies in groups, or when the vampire has had time to plan. Vampires do not sleep; they have more time to think than most mages.
```

```
Ancient Master Vampire (Century or More) — Night-Folk Antagonist
----------------------------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 5 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 4 | Resolve 5
Health: 8  |  Armor: 0 (supernatural resilience is its own protection)  |  Blood Points: 20+
Skills: Close Combat 4, Awareness 4, Empathy 5, Deception 5, Leadership 5, Academics 5, Investigation 3, Esoterica 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Ancient Resilience] — Non-magical, non-fire lethal damage reduced to bashing. Magical weapons, fire, and sunlight inflict full lethal or aggravated damage.
[Discipline Mastery — three to five powers] — Dominate (Manipulation + Leadership vs. Resolve + Composure difficulty 3; success = one compelled action per hit); Fortitude (reduce all incoming damage by 2 before Armor); Presence (all in the room feel the vampire's emotional state; Composure + Resolve difficulty 2 to resist); plus at least one unique elder power.
[Centuries of Cunning] — Enhancement +2 to all social manipulation rolls. Has survived this long through preparation, not brute force.
[The Blood Calls] — Any mortal or ghoul who has fed from this vampire within the past year may be influenced at any range by a Presence + Leadership roll. They do not know they are being influenced.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Ancient vampires are not combat encounters; they are political situations. A mage who attempts to fight an ancient head-on has almost certainly already lost — the ancient arranged that the mage would be alone, tired, and in unfamiliar territory before the first word was spoken.
```

```
Murderous Werewolf (Crinos / War Form) — Night-Folk Antagonist
--------------------------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 2 | Resolve 4
Health: 7  |  Armor: 2 (supernatural toughness)  |  Enhancement: +2 to all physical attacks in Crinos
Skills: Close Combat 5, Athletics 4, Awareness 4, Survival 3
Attacks: Claws (Enhancement +2, lethal), Bite (Enhancement +2, lethal; requires Grapple)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Regeneration] — Heals 1 Health per round automatically, including lethal damage. Only silver or magical attacks inflict wounds that do not regenerate. Silver inflicts aggravated damage (1 Health per day of recovery).
[War Form Terror] — Non-werewolves witnessing Crinos for the first time: Composure + Resolve at Difficulty 3 or gain [Terrified] for the scene. Veterans may make a second roll after the first failed attempt.
[Delirium] — Ordinary Sleepers' memories of Crinos encounters are distorted by the Consensus. Mages, however, remember with perfect clarity.
[Immune to Emotional Mind Magic] — In war form, cannot be affected by Mind sphere effects requiring calm or reflection. Can be disrupted by overwhelming magical force but not soothed or confused.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A werewolf in Crinos is among the most physically dangerous entities a mage will encounter. Silver bullets are effective and known. The more practical concern: werewolves come in packs, defend territory, and killing one guarantees a visit from several others.
```

```
The Fae (Changeling) — Night-Folk Wild Card
-------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0 (iron: aggravated)  |  Defense: Dex 5
Skills: Athletics 4, Awareness 3, Empathy 4, Deception 4, Artistry 4, Esoterica 3, Leadership 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Cantrip Arts] — Fae reality-warping is cultural and ambient. Cantrips function as coincidental magic and do not trigger Witness Paradox under normal circumstances. Each cantrip: Presence + Artistry at Difficulty 2-3.
[Iron Bane] — Iron inflicts aggravated damage. Cold iron (meteoric/wrought iron without steel alloy) inflicts double dice. Fae will do almost anything to avoid iron — useful leverage for savvy mages.
[Dream Sight] — Perceives dreaming resonance of places and people. Always knows when in a magically active location or when speaking with an Awakened mage. Can sense Avatar resonance and Node power.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: The Fae are ancient and strange. They may honor a deal made in 1643 while completely forgetting last Tuesday. They make peculiar allies and unpredictable enemies, and they do not treat mortality the way humans do.
```

```
Tragic Phantom (Ghost) — Night-Folk Encounter
---------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 0 (ephemeral) | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Essence Pool: 6 (Health while dematerialized)  |  Materialized Health: 4  |  Armor: N/A
Skills: Awareness 3, Empathy 3, Esoterica 2, Deception 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Ephemeral Form] — Cannot be harmed by physical means while dematerialized. Only Spirit sphere magic, magical weapons, or other supernatural attacks can harm a ghost. Physical attacks pass through.
[No Wound Penalties] — Does not suffer penalties from Essence loss until completely banished.
[Haunt] — Within its anchor location, can manifest telekinesis, temperature drops, visual distortions, and emotional impressions. Each manifestation costs 1 Essence.
[Anchor] — Bound to a place, object, or person. Destroying or releasing the anchor sends the ghost onward.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Most ghosts are not malevolent — they are confused, grieving, or unaware they are dead. The dangerous ones are Raging Spectres who have been in the Shadowlands long enough to forget everything but their rage. Raging Spectre variant: add Might 3, Enhancement +2 to all attacks, and the [Mindless] Tag (cannot be negotiated with).
```

---

## Section Three: Technocratic Constructs

The Technocratic Union does not send mages when machines will do. HIT Marks, cybernetic field operatives, androids, and bioengineered soldiers are the Union's weapons of war against Reality Deviants. Technocratic Constructs do not feel fear, pain, or doubt. They do not hesitate. They are not susceptible to social manipulation, emotional Mind sphere magic, or Presence-based intimidation. On the other hand, they have no creativity, cannot adapt to truly unprecedented situations, and rely on programming — which a clever mage may exploit.

Critically: Technocratic Constructs do not generate Paradox in Sleeper areas, because the Union's Primal Energy science is framed as advanced engineering to the Consensus. A mage who throws a fireball at a HIT Mark in a crowd accumulates Paradox; the HIT Mark's plasma-discharge return fire does not.

```
Darius / Masako 01 — Iteration X Android (Standard Model)
---------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 3 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 1 | Resolve 1
Health: 10 (2 + Stamina 8)  |  Armor: 2 (reinforced synthetic frame)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Close Combat 4, Ranged Combat 3, Athletics 2, Technology 3, Awareness 2
Weapons: Internal carbine (Enhancement +2, lethal, range 100m, concealed); unarmed strike (Enhancement +1, bashing)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Machine Mind] — Unaffected by emotional or consciousness-targeting Mind sphere magic. Mind magic targeting information processing (false sensory data, disruption) operates at Difficulty +2.
[Hardwired Loyalty] — Will not act against assigned mission under any circumstances short of physical destruction.
[Synthetic Physiology] — Does not eat, sleep, or breathe. Immune to poison, disease, extremes of temperature. Does not register as living on most supernatural senses.
[Reinforced Frame] — Operates at full effectiveness until complete destruction; no performance degradation from wounds.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Darius and Masako 01 are designed to pass as human in casual contexts. Up close, something feels wrong — eye movements too regular, breathing too controlled. These units are deployed for surveillance, covert extraction, and targeted elimination of high-value Reality Deviants.
```

```
Atlas Unit — Iteration X Heavy Enforcement Android
--------------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 2 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 1 | Resolve 4
Health: 8  |  Armor: 2 (heavy synthetic frame)  |  Enhancement: +1 to all physical attacks
Skills: Close Combat 4, Ranged Combat 4, Athletics 3, Awareness 2, Technology 2
Weapons: Retractable claws (Enhancement +1, lethal), heavy strike (Enhancement +2, bashing or lethal), optional integrated ranged package (Enhancement +2, lethal, range 150m)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Machine Mind] — Cannot be affected by emotional Mind sphere effects.
[Remote Termination] — Each unit contains a field-commander-activated destruct charge. This is a failsafe against enemy capture. PCs will not know about this until they do.
[Field Repair] — Self-maintenance during dormancy: heals 1 Health per hour with access to a repair kit or Construct facility.
[Emergency Broadcast] — If destroyed or offline for more than 10 minutes, automatically transmits last known location and status to the nearest Technocratic Construct.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Atlas Units do not attempt to pass as human. They are deployed in situations where the Technocracy has already decided force is the appropriate response and where witnesses either won't matter or will be managed afterward.
```

```
HIT Mark VI "The Mercury" — Iteration X Combat Automaton
--------------------------------------------------------
Might 8 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 2 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Health: 10  |  Armor: 4 (titanium alloy / ablative ceramic)  |  Enhancement: +2 to all physical attacks
Skills: Close Combat 5, Ranged Combat 5, Athletics 2, Awareness 3
Weapons: Integrated assault system (Enhancement +3, lethal, range 300m; three-round burst adds Enhancement +1 but targets area); heavy strike (Enhancement +3, lethal)
Countermagick: 3 dice (woven prime-energy countermeasures)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Machine Mind] — Immune to emotional and consciousness-targeting Mind sphere effects.
[Countermagick Chassis] — Any magical attack must overcome 3 dice of Countermagick before affecting the unit. Subtract 3 from the magical attack's net hits.
[Body Spikes Option] — If equipped with spike arrays: Enhancement +1 to damage on attacks where the Mark moves into grapple range.
[Heavy Load] — Weighs ~800 lbs. Leaves impressions in concrete. Cannot navigate standard stairs, standard elevators, or narrow corridors without structural damage. An ambush weapon and siege machine, not an infiltrator.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Encountering a HIT Mark VI means you have irritated someone important or your Node is on a target list. These are not sent for reconnaissance. Running is a legitimate tactical option.
```

```
HIT Mark IX "Iron Bob" — Iteration X Elite Terminator
-----------------------------------------------------
Might 8 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 10  |  Armor: 10 (classified composite; experimental)  |  Enhancement: +3 to all physical attacks
Skills: Close Combat 5, Ranged Combat 5, Athletics 3, Awareness 4, Technology 3
Countermagick: 4 dice (integrated prime-energy shielding)
Weapons: Multiple integrated weapons systems (assault, anti-armor, area denial); impact frame (Enhancement +3, Close Combat; treats vehicle armor as Armor 2)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Machine Mind] — Immune to emotional and most information-disruption Mind sphere effects. Only direct data-corruption magic (Correspondence 4+ targeting communication systems) has a chance of interfering.
[Armor 10 — Boss Tier Threat] — Virtually no attack, magical or mundane, will deal more than 1-2 damage per hit without extraordinary effort. To defeat Iron Bob, mages need: Forces 5 effects producing catastrophic damage; collaborative casting with four or more mages; creative Correspondence or Matter use to bypass the armor entirely; or access to the unit's termination code.
[Classified Unit] — Iron Bob's existence is officially denied by the Technocratic Union. Mages who survive an encounter become priority targets for an information-management response.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Iron Bob is a boss-tier encounter. It should appear rarely, if ever. When it appears, make clear this is a different category of threat. Running is not cowardice — it is how you survive to fight this when prepared. Iron Bob is designed to be defeated by preparation and creativity, not raw power.
```

```
MODES Unit — Multi-Operational Data/Enforcement System
------------------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Health: 9  |  Armor: 3 (modular composite plating)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Close Combat 3, Ranged Combat 4, Awareness 4, Technology 5, Investigation 4, Academics 3
Countermagick: 4 dice (Primum countermeasures)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Data Integration] — Continuously gathers, processes, and transmits surveillance data. Characters within visual or audio range of a MODES unit should assume they are being recorded and analyzed in real time.
[Tactical Analysis] — After observing an opponent for two rounds, spend an action (Cunning + Awareness vs. Difficulty 2) to identify tactical patterns. On success, gains Enhancement +1 against that specific opponent for the scene.
[Machine Mind] — Immune to emotional and consciousness-targeting Mind sphere effects.
[Construct Coordination] — Boosts all Technocratic Constructs and agents within 100 meters: Enhancement +1 to their Awareness rolls. Acts as a distributed tactical network node.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Mages who disable a MODES unit should know that the last several hours of data have almost certainly already been transmitted to its home Construct. Destroying the unit does not destroy what it already learned.
```

---

## Section Four: Bygone Monsters

Bygones are creatures that exist outside the modern Consensus — entities whose natures are too ancient, too mythical, or too strange to be fully incorporated into what the contemporary world believes is real. Unlike spirits, Bygones are physical. Unlike ordinary animals, they carry metaphysical weight. The Consensus presses against them constantly. Some retreat to protected wilderness. Some hide in Umbral pockets. Some walk through the modern world wearing just enough mundane camouflage to survive — but only just. Magic used on or around Bygones does not generate extra Paradox by default, as Bygones are not Witnesses in the Sleeper sense.

```
Anuk Ite — Two-Faced Spirit-Beast (Lakota Tradition)
----------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 2 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 4
Skills: Larceny 3, Awareness 4, Athletics 3, Close Combat 3, Deception 4
Attacks: Claws (Enhancement +1, lethal; leaves corruption stains on earth where they strike)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Two Faces] — Controls which face presents in any direction. Anyone who meets the gaze of the beautiful face: Composure + Resolve at Difficulty 3 or be rooted in place, unable to act, until the creature breaks eye contact or attacks.
[Poison Trail] — Wherever it walks, it leaves corruption visible to Spirit or Entropy sphere users. Plants wither; animals avoid the area for days; the Gauntlet thickens by 1 in that location for one week.
[Low Cunning] — Uses its beautiful face to lure targets to isolated locations before revealing the hideous face and attacking. Will flee when outmatched.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A being of hunger and deception from Lakota tradition. In a modern chronicle, one might be haunting a rural area, preying on people who wander alone — or have been driven from traditional territory into an urban environment where its prey is far more concentrated.
```

```
Inkanyamba — Storm Serpent (Zulu/Xhosa Tradition)
-------------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 7 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 10  |  Armor: 3 (scales and supernatural resilience)  |  Defense: Dex 7
Skills: Athletics 5, Close Combat 4, Awareness 3, Survival 4
Attacks: Bite (Enhancement +2, lethal), Constrict (requires Grapple; on subsequent rounds, automatic Might damage per round, no roll required)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Storm Summoning] — As an action, summons a localized violent storm centered on its location within one hour. The storm sustains itself without concentration: winds 80mph+, lightning, blinding rain. All fires extinguished. Visibility drops to 3 meters. All ranged attacks suffer Difficulty +2.
[Waterborne Predator] — In water: Enhancement +3 to all physical rolls. On land: Enhancement +0 to movement-based rolls.
[Storm Sense] — Cannot be surprised outdoors or near water. Continuously senses changes in atmospheric pressure, electrical charge, and water currents.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A massive serpentine creature associated with violent storms and floods. When one is seen, storms follow — this is literal, not metaphor. An Inkanyamba pursuing a party makes every other problem dramatically worse: the weather becomes a hazard that compounds all other hazards.
```

```
Jiangshi — Hopping Vampire (Chinese Tradition)
----------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 2 | Resolve 3
Health: 8  |  Armor: 2 (rigid, partially desiccated flesh)  |  Defense: Dex 2
Skills: Close Combat 4, Awareness 2 (smell: 4)
Attacks: Claws (Enhancement +2, lethal), Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal; see Chi Drain)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Chi Drain] — On a successful bite, target loses 1 Resolve per hit. If Resolve reaches 0, they die. Resolve returns at 1 per night of rest.
[Hopping Movement] — Cannot bend knees or hips naturally; moves by hopping in straight lines. Terrifyingly fast in open corridors. Difficulty +3 to any movement requiring direction change — running or environment exploitation can neutralize this creature entirely.
[Cannot Cross Running Water] — Moving water (streams, rivers, a garden hose at full pressure across a doorway) stops it completely. This is absolute, not a preference.
[Breath Detection] — Hunts by following the breath of the living. Holding breath: Difficulty +3 to be tracked. A character holding breath for a full scene makes Stamina + Athletics rolls at increasing Difficulty to avoid gasping.
[Rooster Crow Vulnerability] — At the sound of a rooster crowing (or a convincing recording), the Jiangshi is driven toward its coffin. If it cannot reach its resting place by sunrise, it becomes dormant wherever it is.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: One of the best-defined Bygones in terms of specific, exploitable weaknesses. A prepared team with this knowledge has considerable advantages. The danger is when it is not identified, or when it gets its hands on a target before its weaknesses can be exploited.
```

```
Kijimuna — Okinawan Tree Spirit-Child
-------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 2 | Resolve 1
Health: 8  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 6
Skills: Athletics 5, Awareness 4, Larceny 4, Close Combat 3, Survival 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Tree Melding] — Can dissolve into and emerge from any large tree as a free action. While within a tree, cannot be harmed by physical attacks. Spirit sphere attacks can reach it; fire harms the tree and the Kijimuna simultaneously.
[Fish Obsession] — Offering a Kijimuna fresh fish: Manipulation + Empathy at Difficulty 0 holds its attention completely for the scene. Will not attack anyone offering fish unless attacked first.
[Mischief Instinct] — Prefers to misdirect, steal food, tie knots in hair, and harass rather than kill. Only attacks seriously when its tree is threatened. Fights dirty and defensively; flees to its tree if losing.
[Fire Vulnerability] — Fire inflicts aggravated damage. Even a lit match causes a strong aversion reaction.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: In Okinawan tradition, Kijimuna are spirits of ancient banyan trees — childlike, red-haired, mischievous. In the modern era, a Kijimuna whose tree survives in an urban environment surrounded by concrete may be angry, confused, and far more dangerous than the tradition implies.
```

```
Kongamato — Great Flying Reptile (Central African Tradition)
------------------------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 1 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 8  |  Armor: 1 (scaled hide)  |  Defense: Dex 6 (in flight)
Skills: Athletics 5 (flight), Close Combat 4, Awareness 4, Survival 3
Attacks: Beak strike (Enhancement +2, lethal), Claw rake (Enhancement +1, lethal; can hit two adjacent targets in one action)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Dive Attack] — Attacking from altitude (10+ meters above target): Enhancement +2 to attack and damage roll. Must spend next action regaining altitude before diving again.
[Flight] — Flies at ~80 km/h. Ground-level ranged attacks at full speed suffer Difficulty +3. Vulnerable to Forces sphere disruption.
[Boat Breaker] — Specifically targets watercraft by instinct. Beak punches through wooden or fiberglass hulls at Enhancement +3 against vehicle-rated structures.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A surviving Cretaceous predator sustained by the Otherworlds, with a wingspan of 4-7 feet, reddish skin, and a toothed beak. The primary tactical challenge is mobility — it is very hard to corner something that can simply fly away.
```

```
Hulijing — Nine-Tailed Fox Spirit (Chinese/Japanese Tradition)
--------------------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 2 | Resolve 1
Health: 6  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Deception 5, Empathy 5, Awareness 4, Larceny 3, Esoterica 4, Artistry 3, Close Combat 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Perfect Shapeshifter] — Can appear as any human of any gender, age, or ethnicity. Detecting the disguise requires Cunning + Esoterica at Difficulty 4, or Spirit sphere use. Retains full intelligence in fox form.
[Spirit Sight] — Always perceives spirits, magical auras, and Avatar resonances. Always knows when speaking to an Awakened mage, and identifies their Tradition by resonance flavor.
[Draining Touch] — After a full scene of close physical contact, target makes Stamina + Resolve at Difficulty 2. On failure, loses 1 Resolve for the day. The Hulijing may do this involuntarily.
[Tail Count as Power] — Gains one tail per century, maximum nine. One tail = base stats. Nine tails = Presence 8, Manipulation 8, Cunning 8, Health 10, spirit-magic at Arete 4. A nine-tailed Hulijing is a major entity requiring a story arc.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Hulijing are seduction entities that feed on human emotional energy. Not inherently evil — some find humans charming. But their feeding harms hosts. A mage who befriends one gains a supernaturally perceptive ally; the cost is that the Hulijing may not distinguish emotional feeding from actual relationship.
```

```
Oni — Japanese Demon (Fearsome Form)
------------------------------------
Might 7 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Health: 7  |  Armor: 2 (supernatural toughness)  |  Enhancement: +1 to all attacks
Skills: Close Combat 5, Athletics 3, Awareness 3, Leadership 4, Esoterica 3
Weapons: Kanabo iron-studded club (Enhancement +3, lethal; harms Fae and spirit-touched as iron weapon)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Terror Roar] — As an action: everyone who has not previously encountered an Oni makes Composure + Resolve at Difficulty 3 or gains [Terrified] for the scene.
[Iron Weapon Affinity] — The kanabo functions as silver against spirit-touched creatures and as iron against the Fae simultaneously. Its blows carry the weight of divine enforcement.
[Moral Mission] — Oni enforce justice and test the worthy. An Oni that decides a mage is wicked is an implacable pursuit antagonist; one that decides a mage is worthy is an extraordinary (if unsettling) ally willing to grant a boon.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Oni stand 8 feet tall, skin red or blue or black, horned. They are not stupid. They have opinions about justice, duty, and the proper order of things, and they are willing to debate those opinions at length — usually while holding a kanabo.
```

```
Tanuki — Raccoon-Dog Shapeshifter (Japanese Tradition)
------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 4
Skills: Deception 4, Artistry 4 (brewing/drumming), Awareness 3, Larceny 3, Athletics 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Shapeshifting] — Can take the form of any object or living creature of roughly similar mass. Tanuki illusions are imperfect — they delight in the attempt. A leaf transformed into a coin reverts at midnight. Detecting a Tanuki disguise: Difficulty 2 (vs. Difficulty 4 for Hulijing).
[Belly Drum] — Drums on its belly to produce uncanny rhythms. Presence + Artistry at Difficulty 2; on success, all listeners add Difficulty +1 to detect deception or maintain hostility toward the Tanuki for the scene.
[Sake Brewing] — Can brew supernatural sake that causes drinkers to recount their deepest regrets. Not considered hostile. Just thorough.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Genial supernatural tricksters. Not malevolent — they cause chaos, steal food, and play elaborate jokes, but mean no lasting harm. In a modern urban setting: running a bar no one quite remembers the location of afterward, throwing wild parties in the Umbra, or following an interesting mage out of curiosity.
```

```
Tengu — Wind Demon and Martial Teacher (Japanese Tradition)
-----------------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 7 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 6  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 7
Skills: Close Combat 6, Athletics 5, Awareness 5, Esoterica 5, Leadership 4, Medicine 3
Weapons: Master-crafted katana (Enhancement +2, lethal; functions as a magical weapon)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Master Combatant] — Bladed weapon attacks: Enhancement +3. When attacked in melee: Enhancement +2 to defense. Facing a Tengu in pure swordsmanship is suicide without magical assistance.
[Wind Riding] — Flies at extraordinary speed. In aerial combat or maneuvering: Enhancement +2 to all Dexterity-based rolls.
[Teaching Tradition] — A Tengu that deems a character worthy can teach Fighting Tricks and physical Skills. Training is brutal but the skills are real.
[Pride and Protocol] — Disrespect or arrogance triggers immediate hostility. Genuine challenge and demonstrated skill trigger interest. Responding well to being surprised by someone better than expected.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Crow-like in supernatural form — long-nosed, black-winged, terrifying. Also among the most interesting potential allies if approached correctly. A mage who earns a Tengu's respect gains a supernatural combat trainer and an ally who commands the wind.
```

---

## Section Five: Ephemeral Entities and Spirits

Spirits are not magic, exactly — they are the living resonance of reality itself. Every concept, emotion, place, force of nature, and cultural belief that achieves sufficient mass of human acknowledgment eventually produces a spirit. The Spirit sphere allows mages to perceive, communicate with, and interact with these entities. The Dreamspeakers, Verbena, and many Disparate Crafts specialize in spiritual diplomacy; the Technocracy refers to them as "class-seven epistemic anomalies" and tries to contain or destroy them.

Spirits exist on the Essence economy: they need Essence to sustain themselves, and they gain it from the things that define them — a fire spirit gains Essence from fires, a war spirit gains it from violence. When Essence runs low, spirits become desperate, which is when they become dangerous. A spirit at full Essence is usually predictable; a spirit at zero Essence will do anything.

Spirit stat blocks use the following additional fields: Essence Pool (their spiritual health while dematerialized), Arete equivalent (from Gnosis), and Spirit Enhancement (from Rage, added to physical attacks when materialized).

```
al-Ghadib — The Mad Warrior (Materialized Desert War Spirit)
------------------------------------------------------------
Might 7 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 2 | Resolve 5
Essence Pool: 25  |  Materialized Health: 8  |  Armor: 2 (spiritual resilience)  |  Defense: Dex 5
Arete: 2 (Gnosis 3 → 2)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +4 to all physical attacks (Rage 7 → +4)
Skills: Close Combat 5, Athletics 4, Awareness 4, Leadership 3 (Rage)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Rage-Fueled Combat] — When al-Ghadib is below half Essence, its Enhancement to physical attacks increases to +5 and it cannot take non-violent actions. Starvation drives it toward increasingly extreme violence.
[War Aura] — Within 30 feet of al-Ghadib, all characters add Enhancement +1 to aggressive or violent actions and Difficulty +1 to non-violent resolutions. This is ambient; al-Ghadib does not control it.
[Materialize] — Can spend 5 Essence to enter the physical world for up to a scene. Each round of materialization costs 1 Essence. When Essence is exhausted, the spirit dissolves back to the Umbra.
[Spirit Vulnerability] — While materialized, can be harmed by physical attacks. While dematerialized, only Spirit sphere attacks or magical weapons affect it.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: al-Ghadib is the spirit of a particular quality of warrior fury — the rage of the unjustly killed, the battle-madness of the eternally martial. It can be communicated with, but only in the language of challenge, strength, and conflict. A mage who speaks to it through demonstration of personal courage gains a wary, temporary respect.
```

```
Dataphyte — Digital Parasite Spirit
-----------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Essence Pool: 20  |  Materialized Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 5
Arete: 2 (Gnosis 3 → 2)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +2 to all attacks
Skills: Technology 5, Awareness 4, Esoterica 4, Deception 4, Close Combat 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Data Tap] — Can read and alter information in any connected technological system it touches or is adjacent to. Dataphytes do not hack; they infect. Affected systems develop subtle corruption that spreads to connected systems over time.
[Digital Inhabitation] — Can inhabit technology as a form of dematerialization. While inside a device, the Dataphyte is effectively dematerialized and cannot be directly attacked. Spirit sphere effects can reach it inside the device.
[Digital Dissolution] — Attacks made by a Dataphyte against technological infrastructure inflict damage classified as aggravated to devices (circuits melt, drives dissolve, systems experience cascade failure). Physical entities take bashing damage.
[Essence Feed] — Gains 1 Essence per hour of active information processing occurring within its presence (any large server, busy office building, or connected device cluster). A hungry Dataphyte is drawn irresistibly toward information hubs.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Dataphytes are nascent spirits of the digital age — the spiritual residue of information processing, data loss, and algorithmic urgency made manifest. The Virtual Adepts study them obsessively; the Technocracy considers them malign infections. Most are simply hungry, operating on instinct, and entirely uninterested in anything a mage wants.
```

```
Omniphage — Consuming Void Spirit
---------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 1 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 1 | Resolve 4
Essence Pool: 10  |  Materialized Health: 3  |  Armor: 0
Arete: 1  |  Spirit Enhancement: +1
Skills: Awareness 2, Close Combat 1
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Devour] — Any material the Omniphage physically touches is consumed at a rate of 1 cubic inch per round. It spends 1 Essence to activate this effect; while active, it automatically eats whatever it contacts. Clothing, skin, metal, stone — the type of matter is irrelevant. This inflicts 1 aggravated damage per round to any living thing it maintains contact with.
[Endless Hunger] — The Omniphage is driven entirely by consumption. It has no goals, no preferences, and no capacity for communication. It eats until there is nothing left in its immediate environment, then drifts toward the next source.
[Undetectable at Rest] — An Omniphage that is not actively consuming generates no detectable supernatural resonance until it begins eating. It may drift unnoticed through an area for hours before finding something to devour.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Omniphages are small — fist-sized or smaller when first encountered — but they do not stop growing as they consume. An Omniphage left unaddressed in a Chantry or Node for a week becomes a sphere several feet across that has eaten everything within reach. They are best handled quickly, preferably with Prime or Spirit sphere containment.
```

```
Miengu — Water Spirit (Central African Tradition)
-------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Essence Pool: 20  |  Materialized Health: 6  |  Armor: 0
Arete: 2  |  Spirit Enhancement: +2 to physical attacks
Skills: Athletics 5 (swimming), Awareness 4, Empathy 4, Medicine 3, Esoterica 3, Deception 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Water Form] — In or adjacent to water, the Miengu gains Enhancement +3 to all rolls. On land away from water, it loses Enhancement and suffers Difficulty +1 to all physical actions.
[Healing Waters] — Can spend 3 Essence to heal 1 Health of lethal damage (or 2 Health of bashing) on a touched target. The target must be willing. This is the Miengu's most common interaction with mortals who know how to approach it properly.
[Prophetic Dreams] — The Miengu can give or take dreams. It may spend 2 Essence to send a vivid, accurate prophetic image to a sleeping target within 1 mile of its water source. It may also enter a sleeping target's dreams and read their fears.
[Social Binding] — If a mortal or mage makes and breaks a promise made at the water's edge in the Miengu's presence, the Miengu is immediately aware and may exact a price it considers proportionate.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Miengu (singular: Miengu, also called Jengu) are beautiful water spirits from the traditions of the Sawa peoples of Cameroon. They serve as intermediaries between the human world and the spirit world, providing healing and good fortune to those who honor them. Dreamspeakers working in Central Africa commonly maintain working relationships with Miengu of local rivers.
```

```
Qilin — Celestial Chimera (East Asian Tradition)
------------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Essence Pool: 40  |  Materialized Health: 8  |  Armor: 3 (divine hide)
Arete: 3  |  Spirit Enhancement: +2
Skills: Athletics 5, Awareness 5, Esoterica 5, Empathy 4, Leadership 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Perfect Judgment] — The Qilin cannot be deceived by supernatural means. Any deception magic, Mind sphere manipulation, or illusion is automatically detected. The Qilin perceives the moral character of all beings within 50 feet as a continuous sense — it knows who is innocent, who is guilty, and who is uncertain.
[Blessing Presence] — While a Qilin is present and approves of those around it, all characters who are acting with genuine virtue add Enhancement +1 to all rolls. Characters acting with malicious intent add Difficulty +1 to their rolls.
[The Good Cannot Harm It] — Attacks made by characters who are acting with genuine, uncomplicated virtue miss the Qilin completely. The Qilin's hide repels blows from those it has judged worthy. This does not apply to characters whose virtue is mixed or whose motives are complicated.
[Omen of the Righteous Ruler] — A Qilin appears when a truly virtuous leader is born or rises to power. Encountering a Qilin is a rare and significant omen — for good or ill depending on whether the mage is part of the virtue the Qilin heralds or part of the corruption it has come to witness.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: The Qilin is a divine chimera — dragon-scaled, ox-hoofed, flame-wreathed, and utterly benevolent to the just. It is also the most demanding entity in this appendix to deal with: the Qilin knows what you actually are, not what you present yourself as. Mages who encounter one should expect to be seen with complete clarity.
```

---

## Section Six: Paradox Entities and Malign Spirits

Not all spirits arise from the gentle resonance of place and belief. Some emerge from fractures in reality — from the accumulating damage of vulgar magic, from the concentrated hatred and malice of mortal minds, and from the deep places where the Consensus frays at its edges. These are Paradox Entities and Malign Spirits: beings whose very existence is evidence that something has gone wrong.

Paradox Entities from Chapter Five of this rulebook are the most familiar variety: they emerge directly from a mage's Paradox pool when results are severe enough. The entities below represent the more elaborate versions — the named types that have achieved enough individual character to be encountered as specific beings rather than as faceless hazards.

Malign spirits are different: they are entities whose essential nature is harmful to human flourishing, whether through active malice, consuming hunger, or simple incompatibility with human life. They are not evil in a metaphysically absolute sense — they are what they are — but encountering them without preparation is extremely dangerous.

```
Black Wind — Cyclone Spirit (Malign Entity, Abyssal Origin)
-----------------------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 4 | Resolve 5
Essence Pool: 25  |  Materialized Health: 8  |  Armor: N/A (storm form)
Arete: 2 (Gnosis 4 → 2)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +3 to all attacks
Skills: Athletics 4, Close Combat 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Storm Form] — The Black Wind is not a creature in the traditional sense; it is a mass of ghostly forms, freezing winds, and bone-edged grit occupying a 30-foot radius. All creatures within its radius take Enhancement 0 bashing damage per round automatically — no attack roll required. This damage cannot be soaked by mundane armor; only magical armor (Prime sphere) or barriers created by Spirit or Forces magic provide protection.
[Spirit-Only Damage] — While in Storm Form (which is essentially always), the Black Wind can only be harmed by Spirit sphere attacks, magical weapons with spirit-resonance, or combined Forces + Prime effects. Physical attacks, mundane firearms, and conventional explosives pass through it.
[Eternal] — The Black Wind cannot be destroyed in the normal sense. Reducing its Essence Pool to zero banishes it — forces it back to its origin realm. It will eventually return. Truly eliminating it requires sealing the breach it came through, which typically requires Spirit 4+ collaborative work.
[Charms: Appear, Corruption, Create Wind] — Appear: can materialize or dematerialize as a free action (costs 2 Essence). Corruption: anything caught in the Black Wind for more than three rounds develops a spiritual corruption mark visible to any Sphere user. Create Wind: can extend its storm radius by up to 100 feet for 1 Essence, intensifying winds to dangerous levels.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: The Black Wind is a called entity — it was summoned from a hell-realm at some point in the past and never properly dismissed. It is essentially eternal, as patient as weather, and as directed as a natural disaster. Encountering it means either fleeing or having the Spirit sphere resources to perform a banishment. It does not negotiate.
```

```
Sinfeeder — Astral Parasite (Psychic Fungus Entity)
---------------------------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 1 | Stamina 1 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 2 | Composure 1 | Resolve 1
Essence Pool: 5 (small patch) to 30 (large colony)  |  Materialized Health: 2-6
Arete: 1 (Gnosis 1 → 1)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +1
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Absorb Psychic Essence] — The Sinfeeder automatically draws 1 Essence per turn from any person within approximately 15 feet who acts out a forbidden urge or engages in behavior they consider sinful, shameful, or transgressive. This feeding is passive and unconscious; the Sinfeeder does not need to do anything. People near a Sinfeeder colony feel strangely compelled to confess or act on their worst impulses — this is a symptom, not a power the Sinfeeder controls.
[Corruption Spread] — A location where a Sinfeeder has fed for more than a week develops a corruption aura perceptible to Entropy or Spirit sphere users. The Difficulty of all Resolve-based rolls in the area increases by 1. This stacks with additional weeks of feeding.
[Mind Sphere Vulnerability] — Takes double damage from Mind-based attacks targeting psychic energy, Psychic Blast effects, or any Spirit effect combined with Mind. This is one of the few cases where Mind and Spirit overlap directly.
[Invisible to Normal Senses] — Sinfeeders are detectable only through Spirit sphere (they appear as a pale, spreading mold-pattern in the spirit landscape) or through the emotional symptoms of their presence. They do not have a physical form visible to the naked eye.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Sinfeeders grow in Astral space near locations of concentrated guilty behavior: confessionals where guilt runs deep, addiction recovery centers where people relapse, prison cells, certain nightclubs. A large Sinfeeder colony can affect an entire city block, making everyone in it more likely to act on their worst impulses. They are very difficult to remove because they have no single body to target — clearing a Sinfeeder colony requires systematic Spirit-sphere cleansing of the entire affected area.
```

```
The Collector — Shapeshifting Paradox Entity (Qlippothic Debt-Collector)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 8 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 6 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Essence Pool: 50  |  Materialized Health: 10  |  Armor: 2
Arete: 5 (Gnosis 10 → 5)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +3
Skills: Awareness 5, Esoterica 5, Empathy 5, Deception 4, Close Combat 5, Athletics 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Terror Manifestation] — The Collector appears as the target mage's deepest, most private dread — a perfect psychological mirror. All witnesses who make eye contact with the Collector in its terror form must make Composure + Resolve at Difficulty 4 or gain [Terrified] for the scene. Mages with Quiet track marks suffer Difficulty +1 to this roll.
[Soul Reading] — Knows the deepest fear, greatest regret, and most desperate desire of anyone it looks at. This is not a power that requires a roll; it is simply what the Collector perceives. It uses this knowledge to negotiate, threaten, and maneuver.
[Spirit Away] — On a successful touch attack (Might + Close Combat vs. target Resolve + Composure at Difficulty 3), the Collector can pull the target into a personalized fear Realm — a pocket dimension built from the target's own psychological landscape. Escaping requires either a Spirit 3+ effect or an extended Resolve + Composure challenge (three hits at Difficulty 3, rolled once per scene).
[Shapeshifting] — Can appear as any person the target knows, any object, or any environment. Cannot perfectly replicate Enlightened mages (something always seems slightly off: a resonance problem). Can replicate ordinary mortals with complete fidelity.
[Purpose-Bound] — The Collector comes for mages who have accumulated Paradox debts so severe that the universe has decided a physical collection event is warranted. It does not kill for sport; it harvests. Paying the debt (taking the Paradox consequence willingly, performing a significant act of atonement, or sacrificing something the mage genuinely values) can satisfy the Collector and cause it to withdraw.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: The Collector is not a random encounter. It appears when a mage has been accumulating massive Paradox, ignoring their Quiet Track, and refusing to take consequences. It is the universe's final warning before something worse than the Collector arrives. It can be negotiated with — it can be satisfied — but that satisfaction always costs something real.

Example encounter: The mage Vikram has been running from a Paradox debt for three weeks, using Prime magic to suppress Paradox symptoms rather than clearing the pool. He wakes at 3 AM to find someone sitting in his apartment that he cannot quite look at directly. "You owe," it says. "You know you owe. Let's discuss the terms." The conversation that follows is the actual encounter. The combat, if it happens, is the failure state.
```

```
Ichthion, Lord of Spasms — Zodiacal Paradox Demon (Life/Water Element)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 4 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Essence Pool: 30  |  Materialized Health: 9  |  Armor: 1
Arete: 4 (Gnosis 8 → 4)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +2
Skills: Medicine 8, Awareness 5, Close Combat 2, Athletics 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Cramp] — As an action, spend 1 Essence. One target within 30 feet takes the Collector's Rage-equivalent (4) in bashing damage per round until Ichthion releases them or is banished. This damage cannot be soaked by natural armor — only magical barriers interfere. The target convulses helplessly and cannot move while cramped.
[Nauseous Spasms] — As an action, spend 2 Essence. Target makes Composure + Resolve at Difficulty 3 or vomits helplessly for two rounds (cannot take any action). Even on success, the target is at Difficulty +1 to all actions for the remainder of the scene.
[Infect] — As a slow action taking a full minute of proximity, spend Essence at a rate of 3 per Toxin level inflicted. The target develops a disease equivalent to the Toxin Chart rating: Toxin 1 (mild flu symptoms), Toxin 2 (debilitating fever), Toxin 3 (life-threatening infection), Toxin 4+ (supernatural plague). Mages can resist with Prime or Life sphere cleansing.
[Flood] — Outdoors or near water: spends 5 Essence to cause flash flooding in a 100-meter radius. All characters in the area must make Athletics at Difficulty 3 or be swept off their feet (bashing damage, possible drowning consequences).
[Water Affinity] — Associated with the fish element and water in its zodiacal tradition. Gains Enhancement +2 to all rolls near water. Can materialize or dematerialize through any water source.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Ichthion is one of the 36 sub-lunar zodiacal spirits from the Testament of Solomon, a demon of the water element who causes cramps, spasms, and disease. It appears when Paradox has accumulated in an area with significant water or biological resonance. It is less imposing than the Collector in direct confrontation, but its Cramp ability makes it extremely disabling against even powerful mages who cannot focus through the spasms to cast.
```

```
Safin — The Awesome Radiance (Hound of Ra, Purifying Paradox Light)
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 1 | Intellect 1 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Essence Pool: 100  |  Materialized Health: N/A (does not have a Health track)
Arete: 1 (Gnosis 1 → 1) — ancient, not wise
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Burning Radiance] — Inflicts Rage (5) in aggravated damage per round to every malevolent being within its radius. This damage is unsoakable by natural or magical armor. Benevolent beings, mortals acting with genuine good faith, and animals are unaffected. The universe appears to define "malevolent" contextually; Storyguides should rule based on the character's actual intentions and actions, not their self-image.
[Grows Larger] — Every attempt to harm Safin causes it to grow. Each attack attempt (whether it deals damage or not) increases its effective radius by 5 feet and its Radiance damage by 1 point. By round five of combat, it is incandescent and fills an area the size of a city block.
[Cannot Be Bargained With, Harmed, or Deflected] — Safin is not a creature with motivations that can be exploited. It does not respond to speech, magic that would change its mind, or any supernatural effect that targets mental states. It burns. That is all it does. Attempting to fight it makes it larger and brighter.
[Absolute Judgment] — Safin targets based on some criterion the universe applies to "malevolent." A mage who has been acting with genuine good faith and has not accumulated Paradox maliciously may not be targeted even while standing next to a Nephandus. Storyguides: use this to reward genuinely virtuous play.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Safin is not a combat encounter. It is an extinction event for anyone the universe considers wicked. There is no winning against Safin in a fight; the only possible responses are to run (it does not pursue rapidly — it burns in place and expands), to determine why it has appeared (it always appears in response to something), or to eliminate whatever drew it before it becomes too large to escape. Safin appearing in a scene is the universe's way of saying "something here has gone very wrong."

Example: A Nephandus has been conducting a working to corrupt a Node for six months. A mage who stumbles onto the ritual site may be present the moment Safin manifests. If the mage has clean hands, Safin will burn through the Nephandi without touching them. If the mage has been reckless with their own power — they might want to be somewhere else.
```

---

## Section Seven: Malign Entities and Demons

Where Paradox entities are the universe's reaction to magical excess, Malign Entities are something older: beings whose nature is fundamentally predatory, possessive, or antithetical to human flourishing. Demonhounds are spirit-possessed animals, weapons of dark forces. Fomori are corrupted humans. Imps are infernal familiars. Raamas Ka is a category unto itself. And Goetic Demons are bound entities from traditions so old that their true nature is a matter of ongoing theological dispute.

None of these entities are inherently impossible to negotiate with — except, perhaps, Raamas Ka. Most have motivations, and motivations can be exploited. But "negotiable" is a far cry from "safe."

```
Demonhound (Standard) — Spirit-Possessed Canine
-----------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 2 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 7  |  Armor: 1 (spiritual reinforcement)  |  Defense: Dex 4
Skills: Close Combat 3, Athletics 3, Awareness 4, Larceny 3
Attacks: Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal), Claw (Enhancement 0, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Acute Senses] — Demonhounds track by scent, sound, and spiritual resonance simultaneously. They cannot be surprised by any target they have encountered within the past 24 hours. Attempts to hide from a Demonhound using only physical concealment (not magic) automatically fail.
[Spirit Persistence] — The possessing spirit makes the animal body far more durable than the animal itself would be. The Demonhound continues fighting at full effectiveness until its Health track empties. It does not flinch, retreat, or respond to pain.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Standard Demonhounds are encountered as hunters. They have been sent after someone. They do not stop until destroyed or recalled. They operate alone or in pairs, using coordinated attacks (when two Demonhounds attack the same target simultaneously, add Enhancement +1 to each attack due to flanking). Countermagick equivalent of 0 dice but 2 innate dice of soak vs. magical targeting.
```

```
Demonhound (Greater) — Spirit-Possessed Canine, Enhanced Form
-------------------------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 2 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 2 | Resolve 2
Health: 7  |  Armor: 1  |  Defense: Dex 4
Skills: Close Combat 4, Athletics 3, Awareness 4, Larceny 3, Esoterica 3
Attacks: Bite (Enhancement +2, lethal), Claw (Enhancement +1, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Acute Senses] — As standard Demonhound.
[Spirit Persistence] — As standard Demonhound.
[Optional Special Advantages — 10 points total, Storyguide chooses] — Select from: Bioluminescence (body glows with baleful light; Awareness against the Demonhound gains no benefit from darkness), Cause Insanity (3 dice; on Bite: Composure + Resolve difficulty 3 or gain [Shaken] for one day), Hazardous Breath (4 dice aggravated damage to all within 5 feet as an action; costs 2 Essence), Rapid Healing (heals 1 Health per round), Razorskin (anyone who grapples or strikes the hound bare-handed takes 1 lethal damage reflexively), Soul-Sense (always knows where the hound's assigned target is, regardless of concealment or distance, as long as they share the same plane), Spirit Vision (sees all spirits and Awakened auras automatically), Spirit Travel (can move through the Gauntlet as a free action; costs 1 Essence per crossing), Wings (flight at 60 km/h).
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Greater Demonhounds have been more thoroughly infused with the possessing spirit. The optional advantages make each Greater Demonhound a customized threat — Storyguides should choose advantages that fit the dark patron that created this particular hound. A Demonhound with Hazardous Breath and Soul-Sense is a completely different encounter than one with Spirit Travel and Razorskin.
```

```
Fomor — Bane-Spirit-Possessed Human (Template)
----------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 1-3 | Resolve 1-2
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0 or more (depending on Special Advantages)  |  Defense: Dex 4
Skills: Close Combat 3, Athletics 2, Awareness 2, plus whatever the host's former skills were
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Special Advantages — 5 to 20 points, Storyguide designs] — A Fomor has one to four supernatural advantages driven by its possessing Bane spirit. These might include: armor (natural toughness rating 1-4), claws and fangs (Enhancement +1 to +3, lethal), cause insanity (psychic scream causes Composure roll), rapid healing (1 Health/scene), acid secretion (touch: aggravated damage), enhanced attributes (specific stat increases), shapeshifting (limited forms), or others appropriate to the Bane type.
[Paradox Immunity] — The possessing Bane spirit is not from the Consensus framework — it is from outside it. Fomori are immune to Paradox effects as a category. Magic still works on them directly, but they do not add to Paradox pools as witnesses, and cannot be harmed by Paradox entities.
[Derangement] — The host personality survives in fragments, which the Bane uses against the person. Every Fomor has at least one Derangement representing this internal war: paranoia, obsessive behavior, sensory hallucinations, or dissociation.
[Genetic Tells — Optional] — Subtle Fomori (1-2 Advantages) may pass as human in most contexts. Grotesque Fomori (5+ Advantages) have visible mutations: extra eyes, wrong-colored flesh, vestigial additional limbs, or worse.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Fomori are the Nephandic answer to the Technocracy's Bioenhanced Agents. They are human beings who have been invaded and reshaped by hostile spirits, willing or not. Some Fomori serve willingly; many were violated. The ethical complexity is intentional. Destroying a Fomor may be necessary; it is rarely uncomplicated.
```

```
Imp — Infernal Familiar
-----------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Health: 5  |  Armor: 3 (supernatural resilience)  |  Defense: Dex 4
Essence Pool: 50  |  Arete: 3 (Gnosis 5 → 3)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +3
Skills: Larceny 6, Esoterica 4, Academics 3, Awareness 3, Athletics 3, Deception 4, Empathy 3, Artistry 3
Attacks: Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal), Claw (Enhancement +2, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Shapechanger] — Can take the form of any small creature or object. Unlike Hulijing or Tanuki shapeshifting, an Imp's disguise is nearly perfect — detecting it requires Cunning + Awareness at Difficulty 4 or Spirit sphere use.
[Paradox Nullification] — An Imp bound to a mage acts as a buffer. Reduce any Paradox roll targeting its host by 3 dice. This protection costs the Imp 3 Essence per use. Imps are therefore valuable but finite resources in high-Paradox situations.
```
