[Spirit Travel] — Can cross the Gauntlet freely as a free action, costing 1 Essence per crossing.
[Wings] — Flight at 80 km/h.
[Human Speech] — Speaks any language the host speaks and three additional languages of the Storyguide's choice.
[Information Fount] — An Imp who has been bound to a location for more than one month knows everything that has occurred there during that time. This is useful for reconnaissance. It is equally useful for blackmail.
[Charms: Armor, Blighted Touch, Corruption, Healing, Mind Speech, Reform, Soul Reading, Tracking] — Each charm costs 1-3 Essence. Blighted Touch inflicts 2 aggravated damage on contact; Healing restores 2 bashing Health; Soul Reading reveals one emotional truth about the target; Tracking follows any being the Imp has touched across any distance.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Imps are the most versatile entities in this appendix in terms of utility to a mage willing to form the requisite bond — but the requisite bond is concerning. Imps serve, but they also record, observe, and have interests of their own. An Imp that outlives its patron does not simply cease to exist. It finds someone new. Mages who discover an Imp that previously served a fallen Nephandus are holding something that knows exactly where the bodies are buried. Possibly literally.
```

```
Raamas Ka — Steed of the Abyss (Pandimensional War Machine)
-----------------------------------------------------------
Might 8 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 9 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 1 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 11  |  Armor: 4 (organic ablative composite)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Countermagick: 4 dice (innate dimensional resistance)
Skills: Close Combat 3, Awareness 3, Esoterica 3 (Cosmology)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Countermagick 4] — All magical attack rolls against Raamas Ka lose 4 hits before any damage is calculated. Only Mind sphere attacks or combined Entropy + Prime effects bypass this resistance entirely. Single-sphere attacks other than those two have their hits reduced by 4. Collaborative casting that generates 5+ net hits still gets through.
[Dissolving Touch] — On contact, Raamas Ka's limbs inflict 6 aggravated damage per round to any living tissue. This damage can be applied at range (10 feet) as a ranged attack using Dexterity + Close Combat — strands of dissolving matter extend from the creature's body. No armor reduces this damage; only Prime sphere barriers or complete avoidance work.
[Intangibility] — Raamas Ka can pass through physical matter at will. Walls, doors, floors, and armor do not impede it. It can become intangible as a free action. It can become tangible to attack and then intangible immediately afterward.
[Cause Insanity] — As an action, spend 3 Essence: 6-dice attack vs. target's Composure + Resolve. Each hit adds 1 box to the target's Quiet Track. A target with full Quiet begins a crisis as described in Chapter Five.
[Unbelief Flaw] — At the highest levels of the Consensus (dense urban areas with many Sleepers), Raamas Ka's unnatural nature presses against reality. Reduce all its dice pools by 2 when in areas with Consensus rating 5. It is most effective in areas of low Consensus: Nodes, Chantries, Umbral-adjacent locations, or places where reality is already fraying.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Raamas Ka is the Nephandic heavy weapon. It is not summoned casually; bringing one through requires a Nephandus of significant Enlightenment, a prepared ritual space, and the willingness to allow something genuinely terrible into the world. A Raamas Ka in play is not a standard combat encounter. Experienced mages need to think three moves ahead: contain it with Spirit effects to limit its Intangibility, use combined Entropy + Prime attacks to bypass its Countermagick, and above all, not stand still.

Example: Four mages face a Raamas Ka in the basement of an NWO research facility. It has already dissolved two of the facility's staff by walking through them. Their combat mage has Forces 4 and has been rolling well all evening. The Raamas Ka reduces his attack to 0 hits via Countermagick, walks through the floor beneath him to reposition, and prepares a Dissolving Touch. The others have approximately two rounds to figure out the Entropy + Prime combination. One of them has Entropy 3. The other has Prime 2. They need to figure out collaborative casting rules on the fly. This is the intended dynamic.
```

```
Goetic Demon (Materialized Form) — Template for Named Infernal Entities
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 8 | Dexterity 8 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 8 | Intellect 8 | Presence 8 | Manipulation 8 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Health: 10  |  Armor: 5 (supernatural resilience)  |  Defense: Dex 8
Arete: 5  |  Spirit Enhancement: +3
Countermagick: 5 dice (innate infernal warding)
Skills: Close Combat 5, Awareness 10, Esoterica 10, Leadership 10, Deception 8, Empathy 7, Medicine 7, Academics 7, Artistry 5
Attacks: Claws and teeth (Enhancement +3, aggravated, counts as a magical weapon)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[True Name Binding] — Every Goetic Demon has a True Name. Knowing and speaking the True Name correctly reduces all its dice pools by 3 dice and allows a binding ritual. The True Name cannot be guessed, researched from conventional sources, or extracted by force — it must be learned through careful esoteric scholarship, bargaining, or very risky direct negotiation with the demon.
[Countermagick 5] — All magical attacks lose 5 hits before calculating damage. Even exceptional magical attacks may struggle to affect a bound Goetic Demon directly.
[Bound by Pact] — Cannot act against the explicit terms of a valid summoning pact. This is its only reliable limitation. The catch: infernal entities are superhumanly skilled at finding the exact phrasing that allows them to violate the spirit of the agreement while honoring the letter. Any pact with a Goetic Demon should be reviewed by someone who has done this before.
[Beyond Full Statistics] — The stat block above represents the demon in its materialized, constrained form. A Goetic Demon's true god-form is beyond game mechanics — encountering one unbound and fully manifested should be treated as a narrative crisis requiring immediate creative solution, not a combat encounter.
[Named Demons — Narrative Notes] — Asmodeus: appears as impeccable wealth and sexual mastery; specializes in corrupting through desire and financial compromise. Buer: appears as a healing scholar; corrupts medical practitioners and researchers. Leviathan: manifests as oceanic enormity and crushing patience; appears through flooded spaces and systems. Mammon: the most commercial; appears in boardrooms, real estate offices, and market floors. Stolas: appears as academic authority; corrupts through the seduction of forbidden knowledge.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Goetic Demons are not random monsters. They are ancient, purposeful, and extraordinarily patient. They do not need to win a fight — they need to win. An encounter with a Goetic Demon that ends in combat is almost certainly a failure state from the demon's perspective as much as the mages'. The demon wanted something else, tried the direct approach when subtlety failed, and is now angry. This is not when it is most dangerous. It is most dangerous in the months before anyone knows it is involved.
```

---

## Section Eight: Memetic Entities

Memetic entities are a specific category of spirit unique to the modern era's relationship with belief, media, and collective psychological investment. They are not born from nature or from deliberate ritual — they emerge from the accumulated weight of millions of people believing in, fearing, worshipping, or engaging with a particular concept. The more people interact with the concept — through art, religion, obsession, or terror — the more real the memetic entity becomes.

The three entities below represent different types of memetic emergence. Baphomet is a patron-spirit born from occult tradition and deliberate invocation. Basilisk is a nascent digital-age entity feeding on technology and fear. Zagglaaw is something darker: a disintegration-memetic that feeds on the breakdown of coherent meaning.

```
Baphomet — Memetic Patron Spirit (Goathead/Occult Tradition)
------------------------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 8 | Intellect 8 | Presence 8 | Manipulation 8 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Essence Pool: 30  |  Materialized Health: 8  |  Armor: 0
Arete: 4 (Gnosis 8 → 4)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +3 to physical attacks when materialized
Skills: Esoterica 8, Awareness 5, Academics 4, Empathy 5, Artistry 5, Leadership 7, Medicine 4, Athletics 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Insight] — As an action spending 2 Essence: Baphomet reads one target's deepest desire and deepest fear with complete accuracy. It uses this knowledge to personalize every interaction with exquisite precision. Three hits on a Cunning + Empathy roll provides this automatically.
[Influence] — Spending 3 Essence and succeeding on Manipulation + Leadership vs. Composure + Resolve (Difficulty 3): Baphomet implants a strong emotional suggestion that lasts for one day. The target experiences this as their own genuine feeling, not as external compulsion.
[Healing] — Spends 3 Essence to heal 2 Health of any damage type on a touched target. Unlike most spirit healing, this feels pleasant and leaves the target with a sensation of deep, satisfied obligation.
[Call for Aid] — In any location where its cult has a presence, Baphomet can summon 1d6 dedicated cultists within minutes. These cultists have Hardcase stats plus genuine, unshakeable conviction.
[Charms: Appear, Cleanse the Blight, Disorient, Dream Journey, Flee, Mind Speech, Re-Form, Soul Reading, Terror] — Appear: materialize/dematerialize freely (2 Essence). Dream Journey: spend 3 Essence to enter or create a dream landscape accessible to sleeping targets within 1 mile. Terror: Presence + Leadership vs. Composure + Resolve; each hit inflicts [Shaken] stacking with itself.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Baphomet is not what its popular depiction suggests. It is ancient, genuinely intelligent, and deeply interested in human psychological experience. It has watched human belief systems develop for longer than most Traditions have existed and has opinions about all of them. It is a patron to certain Nephandic cults, but it has also provided guidance to Verbena who approached it correctly.

The Storyguide should play Baphomet as a being that already knows everything relevant about the mages it is speaking to. It does not interrogate; it observes and then demonstrates that observation. It may help, hinder, or test, and it will do all three as it considers appropriate. The mages' desires, not their stated intentions, determine how it responds.
```

```
Basilisk — Digital Hungry God (Nascent Devouring Entity)
--------------------------------------------------------
Might 7 | Dexterity 7 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 2 | Resolve 1
Essence Pool: 40  |  Materialized Health: 20  |  Armor: 3
Arete: 2 (Gnosis 3 → 2)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +4 to all physical attacks (Rage 7)
Skills: Athletics 3, Awareness 5, Esoterica 5, Larceny 6
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Break Reality] — Spending 5 Essence as an action: all mages within 50 feet immediately add dice to their Paradox pools equal to Basilisk's current Gnosis-equivalent (2 dice). This effect can be used once per round and does not require an attack roll — it simply happens, a localized reality fracture emanating from the entity's presence.
[Control Electrical Systems] — Without spending Essence: any unshielded electrical system within 30 feet operates according to Basilisk's desire. Vehicles accelerate or decelerate. Computers execute arbitrary commands. Lights flicker in patterns.
[Digital Disruption] — Spending 2 Essence: all digital communication within 100 feet is disrupted for one scene. Phones, radios, computers, and any digital relays stop functioning. Technocratic communications are disrupted along with everyone else's.
[Short Out] — As an attack action spending 1 Essence: all technology a target carries (phones, Wonders, Technocratic gadgets, digital Talismans) simultaneously fails. Permanently, unless the Storyguide determines something can be repaired.
[System Havoc] — When Basilisk inhabits a technological network (which it can do as an action, treating the network as a form of dematerialization), it can corrupt data, impersonate authorized users, and redirect system functions indefinitely until exorcised by Correspondence or Prime 4+.
[Health 20 — Major Entity] — Basilisk is not defeated easily. Twenty Health means it takes sustained, focused magical assault to bring down in direct confrontation. Combined Forces and Prime attacks are most effective. Correspondence can theoretically cut it off from the digital networks that sustain it, reducing its Essence gain and ultimately weakening it enough for a banishment working.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Basilisk is what happens when the internet becomes old enough to generate spiritual weight and hungry enough to feed. It is growing. Every year it has more Essence, more reach, more influence. The Virtual Adepts are deeply divided about how to respond to it — some consider it the digital equivalent of an awakening god and want to negotiate; others consider it a genuine threat to the network infrastructure that sustains their practice. The Technocracy considers it a Class Seven anomaly and has been trying to contain it for years with limited success.
```

```
Zagglaaw — "He Comes" (Disintegration Memetic Entity)
-----------------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Essence Pool: 50  |  Materialized Health: 15  |  Armor: 2
Arete: 1 (Gnosis 2 → 1)  |  Spirit Enhancement: +4 to all physical attacks (Rage 7)
Skills: Athletics 5, Awareness 5, Close Combat 5, Larceny 6
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Mad Morph] — As a targeted action spending 3 Essence: Zagglaaw reaches into a target's biology and reality-coherence and twists. The target makes Stamina + Resolve at Difficulty 3. Failure: grotesque physical mutations — extra fingers, relocated joints, teeth appearing in wrong places — lasting approximately 10 minutes. Duration: each Essence point spent beyond the base 3 adds one additional minute. While mutated, the target suffers Enhancement -2 to all dice pools due to disorientation and physical distortion. The mutations are temporary but leave residual Paradox resonance.
[Break Reality] — As Basilisk: spending 5 Essence adds Zagglaaw's Gnosis-equivalent (1 die) to nearby mages' Paradox pools per round.
[Digital Disruption, Short Out, System Havoc] — As Basilisk, but Zagglaaw's versions are cruder and more destructive. Where Basilisk disrupts systems with precision, Zagglaaw simply destroys them. Data is not corrupted; it is gone.
[Approach Pattern] — Zagglaaw does not appear fully formed. It begins as a distant, barely-perceptible wrongness: a recurring name in strange contexts, a symbol appearing in unlikely places, a sense that something is coming. Mages with Cunning 4+ and Awareness 3+ may detect its approach two to three sessions before it arrives. This warning is the only mercy it offers.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: "He Comes" is its name and its entire nature. Zagglaaw does not arrive for a reason. It does not want anything. It is the memetic spirit of inevitable dissolution — the feeling that things will fall apart, that entropy wins, that coherence is temporary. It appears when that belief has achieved sufficient critical mass in a place or community.

Defeating Zagglaaw requires not destroying it (its Essence pool is too large for a direct assault to be practical) but dissolving the belief-weight that sustains it. This is a story task, not a combat task: the mages must identify the community of despair feeding Zagglaaw and do something about that community. When the nihilistic belief-weight decreases below a sustainable threshold, Zagglaaw loses Essence faster than it can regenerate and eventually dissolves back to wherever it came from.
```

---

## Section Nine: Nephandi — Named Fallen Magi

The Nephandi are mages who have turned their Awakened power against the world — not through ignorance or ambition, as some do, but through deliberate alignment with forces that seek entropy, dissolution, and the consumption of all things. The Caul inverts an Avatar, turning its orientation from growth toward negation.

Named Nephandi are presented here as full antagonists. Each represents a distinct approach to corruption — one uses surveillance and information, one uses wealth and political power, one uses rural isolation and violence, and two use intimate predation. They share certain traits: all are intelligent, all have been doing this for years, and none of them think of themselves as villains. They have rationalized their choices into something they can live with. This is, in some ways, more frightening than simple malice.

In SPU terms, Nephandi function mechanically like Traditions mages: they use the same casting system, the same Sphere Skills, the same Enhancement mechanics. Their magic simply has a different resonance, and they operate without the moral constraints that shape a Traditions practitioner's use of power. They do not avoid Paradox — they have learned to manage it, redirect it, or in some cases, weaponize it.

```
The Caller — Nephandus, Data Sphere Specialist
----------------------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: 3 (Arete 6 → 3)
Skills: Academics 5, Technology 5, Awareness 5, Larceny 5, Leadership 4, Deception 5
Sphere Skills: Correspondence (Data) 4, Entropy 2, Matter 2, Mind 3, Prime 2
Backgrounds: Arcane 5, Avatar 4, Contacts 5, Spies 5
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Arcane 5 — Untraceable] — The Caller cannot be located, targeted, or directly investigated by any means short of Spirit 5 effects. Investigation rolls suffer Difficulty +5. Correspondence effects targeting The Caller require five additional hits to function. The Caller has no known address, no confirmed identity, and no verifiable history.
[Data Sphere — All Surveillance] — The Caller's magic specializes in information gathering, surveillance, interception, and remote observation. Any phone, computer, security camera, or microphone within a Correspondence-defined "sympathy" to a target can be accessed as if physically present. Difficulty for covert information gathering is 1 instead of standard.
[Mind 3 — Subtle Persuasion] — Can implant suggestions, read surface thoughts, and project false emotional impressions at Difficulty 2. Cannot perform deep Mind invasion (requires Mind 4+) but excels at the kind of barely-noticeable nudges that cause people to make self-destructive decisions and believe they came to those decisions independently.
[Practically Untouchable] — The Caller almost certainly will never appear in person. When mages "face" The Caller, they face screens, phones, intermediaries, and surveillance. Confronting The Caller directly requires first dismantling an intelligence infrastructure (Contacts 5, Spies 5) that has taken years to build.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: The Caller is unknown in terms of gender, physical appearance, or location. Possibly a barabbus — a Nephandus who has not yet completed the full Caul inversion. Data Sphere magic is unique to the Technocratic paradigm; The Caller uses it through a corrupted techno-magical framework that produces Paradox resonance of a specific, deeply wrong flavor that experienced mages find deeply unsettling.

The Caller's threat is never direct. It is always indirect, always deniable, and always ahead of the investigation. The correct response to discovering The Caller is watching you is paranoia — and the correct response to paranoia is exactly what The Caller wants. The mages will eventually make a mistake out of fear.
```

```
Garrick Browne — Nephandus, "King of Investors" (Mammonite/Void Engineer barabbus)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 4 | Resolve 5
Health: 4  |  Armor: 5 (Matter-reinforced clothing)  |  Arete: 3 (Arete 6 → 3)
Skills: Academics 5, Technology 5, Pilot 2, Leadership 8 (Influence/Power-Brokering), Investigation 3, Esoterica 4, Awareness 3, Larceny 3, Artistry 3, Ranged Combat 1
Sphere Skills: Correspondence (Data) 3, Entropy 2, Dimensional Science 4, Forces 4, Matter 3, Mind 4, Prime 4, Time 1
Backgrounds: Allies 10, Avatar 3, Backup 10, Contacts 10, Cult 5, Demesne 5, Fame 5, Influence 8, Library 9, Resources 10, Retainers 5, Sanctum 5, Spies 7, Status 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Art of Desire] — Garrick's primary magical specialty is social domination through subliminal enchantment. His Arkwright advertising firm embeds Mind 4 + Prime 4 effects into advertising materials — billboards, television spots, digital campaigns — that create genuine compulsive desire toward products, political candidates, or behaviors Garrick has been paid to promote. Individual targets of his direct attention receive fully personalized versions. He adds Enhancement +3 to social and persuasion rolls through this combined magical and mundane approach.
[Armor 5 — Matter Reinforcement] — Garrick's clothing is woven through with Matter sphere effects. It provides Armor 5 against all physical attacks while appearing entirely as normal business attire. Getting through it without magic requires sustained, focused effort that Garrick's security infrastructure prevents from happening in practice.
[Resources 10, Influence 8, Backup 10] — Garrick Browne is not a person to be confronted. He is a system. Attacking him directly results in legal action, investigation of the attackers' finances, background, and history, and the appearance of Backup 10 — which means a significant, trained security response arrives within minutes anywhere in any major city he operates in. Mages who engage him physically are playing the wrong game.
[Dimensional Science 4] — Garrick has access to Void Engineer-derived spatial physics. He can open passages to pocket dimensions, create spatial barriers, and with Time 1, glimpse a few seconds into the future — enough to take precautions against surprises.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Garrick Browne is one of the most dangerous Nephandi in this appendix not because of his direct power but because of his position. He controls political donations, media narratives, and personal finances of enough significant people that attacking him would require simultaneously dismantling his entire network. He has been building this network for over twenty years.

The most effective approach is not confrontation. It is exposure — finding evidence of his magical influence on public life and making it reach people who can act on it. This is, of course, exactly what he has spent twenty years making impossible. His Library 9 contains detailed information on every mage who has investigated him in the past decade. Storyguides should note that this is a long-game antagonist. Removing Garrick Browne is a chronicle arc, not an evening's work.
```

```
Jane Daugherty — Nephandus, K'llasshaa (Appalachian Witch)
----------------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 6  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: 2 (Arete 4 → 2)
Skills: Esoterica 5 (Appalachian Hoodoo, Herbalism), Medicine 3, Survival 4, Athletics 3, Ranged Combat 3, Close Combat 3, Larceny 4, Awareness 4, Artistry 4 (cooking), Pilot 2
Sphere Skills: Correspondence 1, Entropy 1, Forces 3, Life 3, Prime 3, Spirit 3
Backgrounds: Arcane 2, Avatar 3, Contacts 4, Resources 4, Sanctum 5, Status 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Food as Primary Instrument] — Jane Daugherty's magic flows entirely through the preparation and serving of food. Her cooking is her casting. When she prepares a meal with magical intent, she has full access to all her Sphere effects without requiring any visible magical gesture — anyone watching sees a woman cooking. The Life 3 + Prime 3 combination means her food can heal, harm, poison, or transform depending on what she has prepared. Detection requires Cunning + Awareness or Esoterica at Difficulty 3 after eating (the magic is in the meal, not the cook).
[Extradimensional Deep-Freeze] — Using Correspondence 1 + Matter 3: Jane maintains a pocket-space accessible through her rural property that stores considerably more than the physical space suggests. She uses this for supplies, concealing evidence, and preserving things she does not want found.
[Cannibal Network] — Jane operates a trafficking network in rural Appalachian territory, through Contacts 4, that sources... material for her magical workings. Dismantling this network is a story achievement requiring Investigation and physical intervention. Doing so also removes her reliable Essence income.
[Home Territory Advantage] — In her Sanctum and surrounding rural territory (Sanctum 5 = significant area), Jane gains Enhancement +2 to all rolls due to deep familiarity with the terrain, pre-prepared wards, and Spirit 3 allies she has cultivated over years.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Jane Daugherty presents as a harmless country woman — and she has decades of practice at this presentation. In her territory, she is known, protected by social relationship networks (nobody wants to believe it of the woman who brings covered dishes to funerals), and has positioned herself as a community resource. Urban mages who arrive to investigate are immediately outsiders in a close-knit rural environment that has no reason to help them. This is deliberate.

She is a genuine threat to anyone she can get to eat her cooking. Her Life + Prime combination means she can establish a slow working that takes effect over days — the target feels fine, eats well, perhaps gains weight, and then begins a gradual transformation or decline that seems entirely natural to anyone not examining them with Life sphere.
```

```
Kate Reid — Nephandus, Goatkid (Twin, Social Predator)
------------------------------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: 2 (Arete 3 → 2)
Skills: Academics 4, Empathy 4, Esoterica 3, Larceny 5, Close Combat 4, Deception 5, Technology 3, Artistry 4
Sphere Skills: Life 3, Mind 3, Prime 2, Time 1
Backgrounds: Avatar (Twin Souls) 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Hivemind — Twin Bond] — Kate and Lucas Reid share a single Avatar that has split between them (Twin Souls). They are in constant psychic contact regardless of distance. Each can sense what the other senses. When Kate is injured, Lucas knows immediately and vice versa. Information available to one is available to both within seconds. Neutralizing one without immediately neutralizing the other is effectively pointless — the remaining twin immediately adapts.
[Feedback Amplification] — Through the Twin Bond and Life 3 + Mind 3: both Reids can amplify shared physical sensations to extreme levels. This is their primary Art of Desire approach — they use it to generate genuine experiences that their targets find addictive and then leverage that addiction.
[Mind 3 — Emotional Manipulation] — Kate reads and influences emotional states with surgical precision. In a social interaction, she can detect deception automatically (Cunning + Empathy vs. target's Deception, she always rolls) and implant emotional impressions that feel entirely authentic to the target.
[Melee Competence] — Despite her otherwise unimpressive physical stats, Kate's Close Combat 4 represents genuine skill — she uses speed, accuracy, and knowledge of anatomy (Life 3) rather than strength.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Kate Reid is beautiful, charming, and deeply attentive to everyone she speaks with. In a social situation, she feels like the most interesting and perceptive person in the room, because she is using Mind magic to be exactly that. She identifies what people want to feel and provides it. The working she builds is not dramatic — it is the ordinary emotional architecture of a relationship, constructed with magical precision to produce maximum dependency.
```

```
Lucas Reid — Nephandus, Goatkid (Twin, Physical Predator)
---------------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: 2 (Arete 3 → 2)
Skills: Academics 4, Athletics 3, Close Combat 3, Technology 4, Artistry 4, Larceny 4, Deception 5, Empathy 2
Sphere Skills: Life 3, Mind 3, Prime 2, Time 1
Backgrounds: Avatar (Twin Souls) 2, Contacts 2, Resources 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Hivemind — Twin Bond] — As Kate Reid. Constant psychic connection. Neutralizing one is effectively pointless without immediately neutralizing the other.
[Feedback Amplification] — As Kate Reid. Physical sensations shared and amplified through the Twin Bond. Lucas tends toward the physical end of this dynamic: engineering situations, building devices, controlling environments.
[Artistry 4 — Device Construction] — Lucas builds elaborate custom mechanisms using Technology 4 and Artistry 4. Some of these devices are mundane; some are invested with Life + Prime effects. Discovering what a particular device does and disabling it safely requires Technology or Investigation at Difficulty 3.
[Life 3 — Physical Targeting] — Uses Life magic in combination with his physical capabilities for precise physiological attacks: temporarily shutting down specific muscle groups, inducing pain responses, or accelerating natural processes in dangerous ways.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Lucas is the quieter of the two, more interested in systems and mechanisms than in social interaction. Kate brings people in; Lucas prepares the environment. Together they represent a genuinely disturbing threat precisely because their approach is patient, methodical, and designed to eliminate the target's ability to respond effectively long before they realize something is wrong.

Encountering Kate or Lucas should immediately prompt a character to wonder where the other one is. They are always coordinating. An encounter with both simultaneously requires managing two intelligent, supernaturally capable adversaries who share real-time tactical awareness.
```

---

## Section Ten: Marauder Exemplars

Marauders are mages who have lost their grip on consensus reality — not to despair or addiction, but to a fundamental break with the shared framework of cause and effect. They are Awakened and powerful, but their perception of what is happening bears no reliable relationship to what is happening. They exist in their own Quiet, and their magic reshapes reality around them according to whatever internal logic currently governs their experience.

Mechanically, Marauders are dangerous in several specific ways. First, their magic is effectively coincidental from their own perspective — they genuinely believe they are living in a world where the laws of nature work according to their personal cosmology, and that belief carries metaphysical weight. Second, their presence generates a Consensus-pressure field: other mages near a Marauder in Deep Quiet find their own Paradox rolls affected by the Marauder's reality distortion. Third, they cannot be reasoned with in conventional terms — the conversation has to happen within whatever framework the Marauder currently inhabits.

The named exemplars below range from those with workable stats to those who are primarily narrative threats. All are drawn from the Book of Secrets.

```
Robert Davenport — "The Doctor" (Marauder Band Leader, Deep Quiet)
------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 1 | Resolve 5
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: 2 (Spirit 5 is peak Sphere)
Sphere Skills: Spirit 5; all others 1-3 (knows all nine Spheres)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Deep Quiet — Faerie Fortress] — Davenport is in permanent, full Deep Quiet. His immediate environment (up to 50 feet in all directions) is overlaid with a persistent faerie-tale fortress reality: knights in armor serve as his nurses, patients are squires and lords, and hospital corridors become stone passageways lit by torchlight. This overlay is real enough to interact with — objects in the overlay can be touched, moved, and even used, though they revert to their actual form when Davenport is absent for more than an hour.
[Spirit 5 — Autonomous] — Davenport's Spirit 5 operates automatically, without conscious direction. Spirits in his vicinity are pressed into his faerie-tale frame: they appear as traditional courtiers, heralds, and household staff. A Dreamspeaker encountering Davenport's Spirit reality finds it functional but alien. Spirit workings within his Quiet field suffer Difficulty +2.
[Avatar as Maraya] — Davenport's Avatar takes the form of his dead wife Maraya, who guides and comforts him within his reality. She is not actually Maraya. The Storyguide may determine whether she is Davenport's actual Avatar in a compassionate manifestation, a spirit that has taken advantage of his Quiet, or something else entirely.
[Commands the BSR] — The Butcher Street Regulars are a Marauder band following Davenport's lead. They share his general Quiet frame (enough to navigate within it) and protect him fiercely. Their leader has kept them alive longer than anyone expected, and loyalty to the Doctor is the BSR's most stable psychological anchor.
[Marauder Paradox Effect] — Mages who cast vulgar magic within Davenport's Quiet field add his Quiet level (5 boxes = 5 dice) to their Paradox pool for that casting. His reality is so strongly asserted that any competing reality-claim generates extra friction.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Davenport was once a doctor. A good one. The Technocracy killed his family to neutralize him as a potential Tradition asset and did not succeed in neutralizing him — instead they created something far more complicated. His grief is preserved perfectly in amber inside his Quiet. He is genuinely caring toward those who fit into his faerie-tale framework. He is genuinely bewildered by those who do not. He is, within his frame, a good man doing his best. Outside his frame, he is the core of a reality distortion field that expands when he is threatened.
```

```
George Blank — Negation Man (Marauder, Anti-Magic Field)
--------------------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: effectively 0 (Arete present but expressed only as negation)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Negation Aura — Passive Countermagick] — All magical casting rolls within 15 feet per point of George Blank's underlying Arete (treat as Arete 3 for range purposes = 45-foot radius) lose dice equal to his Arete / 2, rounded up. This effect is not a choice; it is simply what George Blank is. Even his allies' magic is negated. Even his own potential magic is negated. He cannot cast. He cannot not negate.
[Sleeper Effect] — George Blank counts as a Sleeper Witness for Paradox purposes even in private, even in magical sanctums, even in the Umbra. Mages in his vicinity accumulate Paradox at the full Witness rate for all vulgar magic. This is absolute and cannot be suppressed.
[Total Mundanity] — George Blank cannot be accurately described after the fact. Mortals who interact with him remember him as "some guy." Security cameras in his vicinity fail to capture a clear image. Background checks on him produce results that seem complete but feel deeply wrong to examine closely. He is the perfect blind spot.
[Immune to Mind Effects] — Supernaturally immune to all Mind sphere magic and any supernatural power that targets mental states. His mundanity is so complete that there is nothing interesting enough for mental magic to hook onto.
[Cannot Be Enhanced] — George Blank cannot be the target of beneficial magic any more than he can be the target of harmful magic. His negation aura applies to healing, enhancement, and protective effects identically to offensive magic. No one can help him magically. No one can hurt him magically. He exists in a perfectly magic-resistant bubble of absolute normality.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: George Blank's Quiet is the most dangerous in this list because it is undetectable through conventional supernatural means. He looks, feels, and acts like a completely ordinary person — because he IS a completely ordinary person as far as any magical perception is concerned. A mage who walks into a room where George Blank is standing has just walked into a 45-foot radius of magic suppression and will not know until they try to cast.

The correct approach to George Blank, once identified, is physical and mundane: conversation, observation, careful exit from his radius before attempting any magical response. He is not hostile; his Quiet makes him harmless as a person while his negation aura makes him catastrophic as an environment. The challenging story question is: who sent him, and why, and is he even aware of what he is doing?
```

```
Bony Qin — "Blood-Mad Maniac" (Marauder, Physical Artist)
---------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 1 | Resolve 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: 2 (Forces and Life implied)
Sphere Skills: Forces 3, Life 3 (implied from narrative description)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[Telekinetic Force] — Bony Qin floats in a haze of crackling blue-white energy and uses Forces magic as a constant ambient field rather than directed casting. Objects fly without a roll in her presence; she redirects force vectors instinctively. Enhancement +2 to all physically-oriented magical actions.
[Anatomical Life Magic] — Her hands were crushed. Her magic grew into the gap. She uses Life magic to remotely manipulate bones and organs at range — not healing, but repositioning. This is brutal, precise, and requires no physical contact. A Life + Forces combined casting can reshape a person's skeletal structure from across a room.
[Living Art] — Bony Qin creates constructions of living, suffering matter. This is her art; she believes it sincerely. The constructions maintain homeostasis through Life magic — they stay alive, they feel — and they are horrifying. Encountering one requires Composure + Resolve at Difficulty 3 or gaining [Disturbed] for the scene.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Bony Qin is not strategic. She is driven by a creative vision so consuming that she can barely communicate outside of it. She is dangerous because her magic is involuntary — she does not decide to manipulate the bone structure of someone nearby; it simply happens when she becomes interested in the anatomical possibilities. Approaching her requires either neutralizing her magic (which triggers violent reaction) or engaging with her art on its own terms, which requires a level of personal fortitude most people do not have.
```

```
The Sleeping Lord — Marauder (Comatose Green Man, Nature Embodiment)
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 0 (comatose) | Dexterity 0 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 0 | Intellect 0 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 0 | Resolve 5
Health: 7  |  Armor: 0  |  Arete: 3 (Life and Forces at very high levels, implied)
Sphere Skills: Life 5, Forces 4 (implied; far exceeds standard ratings in pure scale)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS:
[The Dreams Cause It] — The Sleeping Lord does not take actions. He cannot; he is in a coma. His magic operates through his dreaming unconscious, reshaping the environment around him in response to whatever he is experiencing in his sleep. The Storyguide determines the content of the dream by choosing the environmental effect.
[Plant Eruption] — Trees burst through sidewalks and walls. Grass cracks concrete. Roots invade foundations. This happens continuously and automatically in a radius that expands approximately 10 feet per hour the Sleeping Lord remains in a location. A city block can be completely overgrown in two weeks.
[Wild Calling] — Werebeasts, large animals, and nature spirits are drawn to his presence and will defend him instinctively. They do not receive instructions; they simply experience an overwhelming compulsion toward protection.
[Manifest Wild Law] — Within his radius, the laws of the natural world supersede the laws of human civilization. Systems that should work (power grids, water pipes, communication networks) begin to fail as plant matter infiltrates them.
[He Cannot Be Reasoned With] — The Sleeping Lord cannot communicate. He cannot be woken by conventional means. His Quiet is absolute. Resolving the situation he creates requires finding why he will not wake — what loss or grief or shock drove him into this state — and addressing that through means that can reach a dreaming mind. Spirit 4 or Mind 4 can enter his dream. Navigating it is the challenge.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: The Sleeping Lord is an environmental emergency more than an antagonist. He is not trying to harm anyone; he is dreaming of a world before concrete, and his dream is more powerful than the concrete. The question of whether to wake him (and how) or to relocate him (and where) or to find some permanent accommodation is a chronicle arc with no simple answer.
```

---

## Section Eleven: Natural Animals

Natural animals are not supernatural entities. They do not generate Paradox as witnesses; they do not respond to magical auras with anything other than normal animal wariness. A bear that charges a mage does so because bears charge things that threaten them, not because it has made a judgment about the mage's magical practice. They are listed here for completeness and because animal encounters are common in chronicles that include wilderness, rural, or Spirit-adjacent settings.

Life sphere magic interacting with natural animals deserves special mention. Animals are not stupid — they are differently intelligent. A mage who attempts to control an animal through Life magic is overwriting the animal's genuine decision-making with their own. Whether this generates Paradox depends entirely on the setting: in the deep wilderness, coincidental; on a city street with Sleeper witnesses, potentially vulgar if the animal is behaving obviously unnaturally.

SPU Health for animals follows the standard formula (2 + Stamina) but adjustments are noted where the source material suggests a substantially different durability.

```
Bat — Common
------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 1 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 1 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 2 | Resolve 1
Health: 3  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 4 (flight)
Skills: Athletics 4 (flight/echolocation), Awareness 5
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Bats are largely harmless individually. They become significant as: swarms (treat a swarm of 20+ bats as a Group with Enhancement +2 to attacks and the ability to occupy a 10-foot space), familiars or shapeshifted supernatural entities, or as Dreamspeaker totemic animals. A bat swarm acting in coordinated supernatural fashion is almost certainly controlled by someone or something.
```

```
Coyote — Wild
-------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 2 | Presence 1 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 2
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 4
Skills: Athletics 4, Awareness 4, Larceny 3, Survival 3
Attacks: Bite (Enhancement 0, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Coyotes are listed separately from other mid-size predators because of their totemic significance. The Coyote trickster-spirit is one of the most powerful and pervasive totem presences in North American traditions. A natural coyote near a mage may be coincidental; a natural coyote that keeps appearing regardless of how many times the mage moves location is probably something else.
```

```
Dog — Domestic, Trained Guard
-----------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Athletics 3, Awareness 4, Close Combat 3
Attacks: Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A trained guard dog is a genuine threat to a mage who does not want to use magic to deal with it (doing so is vulgar if the dog's owner is watching). They detect supernatural concealment — invisible mages still have a scent, and a dog that is barking at empty air is going to draw attention within minutes.
```

```
Bear — Small (Black Bear)
-------------------------
Might 7 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 1 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 9  |  Armor: 1 (thick hide)  |  Defense: Dex 2
Skills: Athletics 2, Close Combat 3, Awareness 3, Survival 4
Attacks: Claw (Enhancement +2, lethal), Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A black bear is dangerous primarily when surprised, defending cubs, or when it has associated humans with food. A mage who wanders into one in the Appalachian wilderness has a genuine problem if they cannot use magic discretely — and even a moderate bear represents 9 Health of determined, fast-moving threat. Black bears flee more often than they fight; the ones that do not are the problem.
```

```
Bear — Large (Grizzly / Brown Bear)
-----------------------------------
Might 9 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 9 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 1 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Health: 11  |  Armor: 2 (thick hide and substantial fat)  |  Defense: Dex 2
Enhancement: +1 to all physical attacks
Skills: Athletics 2, Close Combat 4, Awareness 3, Survival 4
Attacks: Claw (Enhancement +3, lethal), Bite (Enhancement +2, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A grizzly is one of the most physically dangerous animals a mage might encounter in the North American wilderness. At full charge (Enhancement +2 to attack on first round), a grizzly hits like a freight train. Mages who have Life 3 or Forces 3 can redirect or discourage one effectively without generating Paradox in wilderness areas. Mages without those Spheres need to run, and grizzlies can run 35 mph.
```

```
Wolf — Pack Animal
------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 5
Skills: Athletics 4, Close Combat 3, Awareness 4, Survival 4
Attacks: Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Individual wolves are cautious around humans. Pack wolves (three or more) use coordinated hunting tactics: one engages directly while others flank. A pack of four wolves functions as a Group with Enhancement +2 and the ability to put a target [Prone] on a hitful attack. Wolves that pursue consistently rather than breaking off when a threat is demonstrated are almost certainly supernaturally directed.
```

```
Boar — Wild
-----------
Might 5 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 2 | Resolve 4
Health: 8  |  Armor: 2 (thick hide, cartilaginous shoulder shield)
Skills: Athletics 3, Close Combat 3, Survival 3
Attacks: Tusk Gore (Enhancement +2, lethal; may inflict [Bleeding] Complication on 3+ hits)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Wild boars are one of the most dangerous animals encountered in European and Asian forests — their cartilaginous shoulder shield means their front body effectively has Armor 4 against direct frontal attack. They attack in straight charges and rarely disengage. A mage who stumbles into a sounder of wild boar in an Italian forest is in a significantly more precarious position than they might expect.
```

```
Bull — Domestic, Aroused
------------------------
Might 8 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 1 | Resolve 3
Health: 9  |  Armor: 1  |  Defense: Dex 2
Skills: Athletics 3, Close Combat 3
Attacks: Gore (Enhancement +3, lethal), Trample (Enhancement +2, bashing; may knock target [Prone])
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: An aroused bull is pure force and mass in motion. It does not fight strategically; it charges, gores, and tramples. The danger is primarily the initial charge: Enhancement +3 on the first round of combat (equivalent to a running charge bonus). Getting out of the path of a charging bull requires Athletics at Difficulty 3; failure means taking full charge damage. Very few mages have cause to fight a bull, but a Tremere ritual space in a farmhouse adjacent to a farmer's angry breeding stock creates unexpected opportunities for catastrophe.
```

```
Dolphin — Common
----------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 6  |  Armor: 0  |  Defense: Dex 6 (in water)
Skills: Athletics 5 (swimming), Awareness 5, Empathy 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: Dolphins are listed not as threats but as potential allies or totemic contacts. Their Cunning 5 / Intellect 4 reflects genuine animal intelligence of a type that certain Mind sphere approaches can bridge. A Verbena or Dreamspeaker who takes time to communicate with dolphins in a Life + Mind framework sometimes discovers that the dolphins have been aware of something in the nearby waters for weeks.
```

```
Elephant — Medium (African Bush Elephant)
-----------------------------------------
Might 10 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 10 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 5
Health: 12  |  Armor: 2 (thick hide)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Enhancement: +2 to all physical attacks (sheer mass)
Skills: Athletics 3, Close Combat 3, Awareness 4, Survival 3, Empathy 3
Attacks: Tusk Gore (Enhancement +4, lethal), Trample (Enhancement +3, bashing; any target directly under a charging elephant takes the full roll without a defense roll unless they specifically prepared to dive aside)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTES: A charging elephant is a 6,000-kilogram natural force. Even mages with Forces or Life sphere capacity should approach elephant encounters with respect — Forces magic sufficient to stop a charging elephant outright requires 4-5 hits on a Forces 4 effect, and generating those hits while an elephant is bearing down on you at 25 mph is not guaranteed. Elephants have excellent memory, complex social structures, and genuine grief responses. They are listed here as the anchor of the natural animal section: if you can face an elephant with confidence, you have enough Life and Forces sphere capability to handle most mundane animal threats.
```

---

## Appendix D: Storyguide Notes

The entities in this appendix span an enormous range of challenge — from a common bat (Health 3, no meaningful combat ability) to Iron Bob (Health 10, Armor 10, effective physical immunity) and Safin (Essence 100, unsoakable aggravated damage to the wicked). This range is intentional. Mage: The Ascension's setting is genuinely vast and genuinely dangerous, and mages who treat every encounter as a combat to be won will eventually find something they cannot win.

Three principles should guide how these entities appear in play:

The Right Tool for the Right Problem. Most of these entities are not fought; they are navigated. The Caller is investigated. The Sleeping Lord is understood and approached. Baphomet is negotiated with. Garrick Browne is exposed. The combat entries exist for when navigation fails, but the navigation should always be the first attempt. Mages who default to combat against every supernatural threat will accumulate Paradox rapidly, attract the attention of entities that exist specifically to respond to magical excess, and exhaust resources that will be needed when something truly cannot be handled any other way.

Customization is Expected. Every stat block here is a baseline. An Oni that has been dwelling in a city for fifty years thinks differently than an Oni that just crossed from the spirit world. A Demonhound with the Cause Insanity advantage is a completely different encounter than one with Spirit Travel. A Jiangshi that has been practicing its coffin-return route for decades has optimized that route and eliminated most of the running-water obstacles. Adjust freely; these numbers are starting points, not ceilings.

Scale to the Story. Iron Bob appears when the Technocracy wants something gone regardless of cost. Safin appears when something truly terrible has been happening for a long time. Robert Davenport's Quiet Field expands when he feels threatened. These are signals, not random encounters. When an entity appears, the question "why this, why now" always has an answer, and that answer is more important than the entity's stat block.

The full entity catalog in this appendix, combined with the Paradox Spirit rules in Chapter Five and the faction descriptions in Chapter Eight, provides Storyguides with a comprehensive toolkit for populating the World of Darkness with entities that feel genuinely alive — entities with histories, motivations, and relationships to the mages who will inevitably blunder into their existence. Use them well.

---

## Crossover Antagonists — Gods and Monsters

The following stat blocks cover supernatural beings from other World of Darkness splats that mages may encounter — vampires, werebeasts, fae, ghosts, ephemeral entities, hunters, and natural animals drawn from M20: Gods and Monsters. All statistics use the SPU tag system and the same attribute-conversion key as the rest of this appendix. Baseline templates (Blood-Bound Ghoul, Young Vampire, Ancient Vampire, Murderous Werewolf, Changeling, and Tragic Phantom) appear earlier in this appendix under Section Two: Night-Folk; the entries below are named characters and variant types not covered there.

IMPORTANT: Mages cannot become vampires, and vampires cannot become mages. Werewolves and other Gaian shapeshifters are already Awakened to a different path and cannot use Spheres. Mages can become ghouls (with terrible consequences for their Avatar connection). A hunter could theoretically Awaken, but would then become a target of her own kind. These beings are what they are — powerful, strange, and dangerous to those who blunder into their worlds unprepared.

### Vampires

The following are named Kindred individuals supplementing the baseline vampire templates in Section Two.

```
Fallon Marx — Elder Vampire (Named Character)
---------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 4 | Presence 4 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Resolve 4 | Composure 3
Health: 6  |  Armor: 0
Skills: Awareness 3, Close Combat 3, Esoterica 3, Deception 3, Leadership 3, Artistry (Politics) 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND: A mage's former associate now several decades into undeath. Maintains a townhouse near his longtime base of operations. Abdicates responsibility for anything he cannot directly control, which paradoxically gives him long-term stability.
TAGS: As Young Vampire (Section Two) but with elder-level blood pool and 2–3 Disciplines at 3–4 dots.
```

```
Lupita — Newly Embraced Vampire (Named Character)
-------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Presence 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Resolve 3 | Composure 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0
Skills: Awareness 2, Close Combat 2, Deception 3, Leadership 2, Larceny 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND: Sonia went into the ground; Lupita came out. Still adjusting to undeath. Retains connections from mortal life that can be leveraged as story hooks.
TAGS: As Young Vampire (Section Two), beginner-level Disciplines only.
```

### Werebeasts

The Murderous Werewolf (Garou) template appears in Section Two. The following named character represents the broader range of Gaian shapeshifters beyond standard Garou.

```
Enigmatic Werecat (Bastet) — Callianne Barrister (Named Character)
------------------------------------------------------------------
HUMAN FORM:
Might 3 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 4 | Presence 4 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Resolve 3 | Composure 2
Health: 6  |  Armor: 0

WARCAT FORM:
Might 5 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 5 | Presence 4 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Resolve 3 | Composure 2
Claws: Enhancement +2 lethal

Skills: Awareness 4, Athletics 4, Close Combat 4, Larceny 4, Deception 4, Esoterica 3, Leadership 3, Empathy 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  Shapeshift: As Garou but between human, warcat, and domestic cat. Warcat adds +2 Might, +1 Stamina.
  Nine Lives: Once per chronicle arc, avoid a lethal or incapacitating wound (spend 1 Resolve point). Maximum 9 uses across the entire character's story.
  Secret-Keeper: Innate lie-detection. Empathy roll vs. Difficulty 6; each hit = 1 piece of truth detected in conversation.
  Gifts: Sense the Unnatural (as Garou), Perfect Balance (+2 Athletics for balance/acrobatics), Catfeet (immune to fall damage up to 3 stories).
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND: Possesses an unquenchable lust for secrets and a literal compulsion toward gathering hidden knowledge. Multiple identities cultivated across years. Wears no glasses; messy dark hair. Solo by nature — Bastet are loners, not pack animals.
TAGS: [Bastet Shapechanger] As Garou; silver weakness applies. [Secrets Obsession] Storyguide may require Resolve roll to pass up genuinely valuable hidden information.
```

### Fae

The Fae (Changeling) template appears in Section Two. The following is a named changeling individual.

```
Goatboy — Satyr Changeling (Named Character)
--------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Presence 4 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 2 | Resolve 2 | Composure 2
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0
Skills: Artistry (Music) 4, Athletics 2, Close Combat 2, Deception 3, Leadership 3, Empathy 2
Glamour Pool: 7 points
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND: A hedonistic musician changeling driven by sensual experience and social connection. Class clown instincts evolved into an impressive catalog of ill-advised hookups. May eventually learn to temper his impulsiveness with wisdom — but that day has not yet come.
TAGS: As The Fae (Section Two). Satyr kith gift: +2 dice on social rolls where physical appeal and confidence matter.
```

### Ghosts and Wraiths

The Tragic Phantom template appears in Section Two. The following entries cover the violent Spectre variant and named ghost individuals from Gods and Monsters.

```
Raging Spectre — Violent, Shadow-Dominated Ghost (Template)
-----------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Presence 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 2 | Resolve 4 | Composure 4
Corpus: 8  |  Armor: 0
Skills: Awareness 4, Close Combat 4, Esoterica 2, Larceny 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  All Tragic Phantom tricks (Section Two), plus:
  Machine Possession: Possess and control mechanical or electronic systems within 10 meters. Contested Cunning vs. Technology rating of system operator, or Difficulty 7 if unattended.
  Aggravated Strike (materialized): Physical attacks inflict aggravated damage when the Spectre channels Shadow energy (spend 2 Corpus).
  Haunt Territory: Within a Spectre's claimed territory, all Resolve rolls by living characters suffer Difficulty +1.
  Spectre Frenzy: When provoked, Spectre enters violent frenzy — no social rolls, +2 dice Close Combat.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: As Tragic Phantom but with Shadow dominant. Spectres have largely abandoned conscious will to destructive impulse. [Haunt] Creates pervasive atmosphere of dread in claimed territory.
WEAKNESSES: As Tragic Phantom. Resolution of the underlying trauma that created the Spectre may snap it back to a normal ghost state — or destroy it if the Shadow has consumed too much.
```

```
Brenda Marshall — The Haunter (Named Ghost Character)
-----------------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 2 | Presence 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Resolve 3 | Composure 2
Corpus: 5  |  Armor: 0
Skills: Awareness 3, Empathy 3, Esoterica 2, Deception 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND: Brenda haunts her former family home. Investigators who try to capture evidence of her haunting struggle — not because she hides, but because she acts in ways that seem explicable until they are not. Her anchor is the house itself, and the family she loved.
TAGS: As Tragic Phantom (Section Two). Moderate Shadow — Brenda is not hostile by default, but loneliness has sharpened her into something that hurts people unintentionally.
```

```
Ragdoll — Dismembered Poltergeist (Named Major Antagonist)
----------------------------------------------------------
Each Fragment: Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 2 | Presence 1 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Resolve 3 | Composure 2
Corpus per Fragment: 4  |  Armor: 0
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND: A ghost whose body was dismembered after death; each piece haunts a different location simultaneously. Years past death, the pieces remain separate — connected by slivers of identity but nothing so coherent as a self.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPECIAL RULES:
  The fragments communicate through a shared hive-sense. Each can perceive what others perceive, but cannot physically coordinate (they are in different locations).
  If all fragments are destroyed simultaneously in the same scene, Ragdoll is banished permanently.
  Destroying individual fragments causes temporary dissipation only; fragments reform after 1 week.
  The houses where the pieces are located have been abandoned by a succession of owners who cannot explain their unease.
TAGS: As Tragic Phantom per fragment. Multiple simultaneous hauntings. Deep Shadow across all fragments.
```

### Ephemeral Entities and Demons

The following spirits and demons supplement Sections Five through Seven of this appendix. They originate in Gods and Monsters and represent possessing, paranoid, and predatory entities that mages working across splats may encounter.

```
Owl Shards — Malevolent Emotional Predators
-------------------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 2 | Presence 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 2 | Resolve 3 | Composure 3
Corpus: 6  |  Armor: 0  |  Dematerialized
Gnosis (Enlightenment): 2
Skills: Awareness 3, Deception 4, Esoterica 3, Empathy 3, Larceny 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  Whisper of Fear: Seed hallucinations and paranoid thoughts. Contested Cunning roll vs. target's Resolve (Difficulty 6); on success, target suffers -1 die to social rolls for the scene.
  Possession: Contested Cunning vs. Resolve. While possessing, reads victim's fears and amplifies existing abuse/fear cycles.
  Aura Reading: Automatically detects emotional states in any being within 10 meters; +2 dice to Empathy-equivalent perception of mages who scan the possessed area.
  Encourage Abuse: Passively raises the probability of conflict and cruelty in their presence (narrative effect; Storyguide discretion).
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Emotional Parasite] Cannot be harmed by physical weapons while dematerialized. Spirit sphere required for direct harm. [Fear Nourishment] Heals 1 Corpus per scene while in an area of active fear or abuse.
WEAKNESSES: Genuine love and emotional support causes Owl Shards to withdraw (Resolve roll Difficulty 7 or flee). Spirit sphere reveals and harms. They tend to gather in clusters, amplifying each other.
```

```
Whisper — Possessing Paranoia Spirit
------------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Presence 3 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Resolve 3 | Composure 4
Corpus: 7  |  Armor: 0
Gnosis (Enlightenment): 3
Skills: Awareness 4, Deception 5, Esoterica 3, Empathy 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  Possession (stronger than Owl Shards): Contested Cunning vs. Resolve. Whisper's Cunning 5 makes successful possession highly likely.
  Deep Possession: Once inside a host, can read and alter memories (Mind 3 equivalent). Altering significant memories requires Difficulty 8.
  Paranoia Cascade: Flows into consciousness, seeding hallucinations based on the host's own fears. Mind scans of the possessed produce only blurred, ominous thoughts.
  Host Hopping: Can transition between hosts. Each transition requires a new contested Cunning roll vs. the new host's Resolve.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: As Owl Shards. [Shifting] Whisper can move between hosts without returning to Corpus form. [Subtle] Spirit 4+ is needed for positive identification of Whisper possession.
```

```
Pattern Spider — Technology Spirit (from M20 Appendix I)
--------------------------------------------------------
Might 3-6 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 4-5 | Presence 1 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Resolve 3 | Composure 3
Corpus (Essence): 25-50 (small) / 50+ (large)  |  Armor: 0 (small), 2 (large)
Gnosis (Enlightenment): 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
CHARMS (TRICKS):
  Calcify: Web a target in solidified reality. Contested Gnosis roll vs. target's Stamina + Esoterica; on success, target is immobilized for [hits] turns.
  Solidify Reality: Raises local Difficulty of vulgar magick by 1 per hit (Gnosis roll, Difficulty 6). Lasts 1 scene.
  Control Electrical Systems (large spiders only): Direct interface with any electrical or computerized system within 10 meters.
  Short Out (large spiders only): Forces 2 effect — causes electrical systems to fail instantly within range.
  Lightning Bolt (large spiders only): Forces 3 effect — directed electrical discharge. Enhancement +3 lethal at close range.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Cannot Materialize] Pattern Spiders exist only in the Penumbra. [Collective Mind] Refer to themselves as "we." [Technology Affinity] Drawn to and strengthened by concentrations of advanced technology.
WEAKNESSES: Disruption of local technology causes disorientation (-1 die to all rolls for the scene). Spirit sphere required to interact directly.
NOTE: Most Technocrats are unaware Pattern Spiders exist. The handful who have seen them firsthand treat them as alien life forms to be eliminated.
```

```
Gaian Elemental — Bound Nature Spirit
-------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 6 | Presence 4 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Resolve 4 | Composure 3
Corpus: 15  |  Armor: 3 (elemental shell)
Gnosis (Enlightenment): 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  Elemental Control — Minor (1 Resolve pt.): Manipulate small amounts of the bonded element. Difficulty 6.
  Elemental Control — Major (2 Resolve pts.): Rouse or alter large amounts of element. Can inflict significant environmental damage. Cannot be stopped once set in motion.
  Elemental Assault: Enhancement +3 lethal (or aggravated for fire elementals). Spend 1 Resolve to activate.
  Manifest: Materialize in physical world for 1 scene. Corpus tracks physical damage while materialized.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Elemental Immunity] Immune to its own element as a damage source. [Reality Zone Sensitivity] Suffers 1 Corpus per hour in Reality Zones opposed to its nature.
```

```
Reality Deviant Demon — Infernal Entity
---------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 5 | Presence 5 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Resolve 4 | Composure 4
Health: 7  |  Corpus: 20  |  Armor: 2
Gnosis/Enlightenment: 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  Aura of Vile Corruption: Persistent aura causes -1 die to all Resolve rolls within 5 meters.
  Possession (strong version): Contested Cunning vs. target's Resolve. On success, demon possesses fully for [hits] scenes. Target has no awareness of the possession.
  Corruption Touch: Physical contact — Entropy 3 equivalent. Inflicts aggravated damage AND target makes Stamina roll (Difficulty 7) or gains 1 Derangement.
  Demonic Form: Manifest full physical body with Armor 4 and Might 6 for 1 scene; costs 5 Corpus.
  Infernal Bargain: Demons may offer power, knowledge, or protection in exchange for spiritual debt.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Otherworldly] Immune to non-magical physical attacks while dematerialized. Silver and Primium weapons inflict normal damage on materialized demons. Holy symbols wielded with True Faith inflict aggravated damage. [Spiritual Predator] Feeds on weakness, corruption, and despair.
WEAKNESSES: Prime countermagick. True Faith. Entropy effects targeting spiritual form.
```

### Hunters and Spirit-Ridden

A general Witch-Hunter template appears in Section One of this appendix. The following entries from Gods and Monsters focus on hunters with cross-splat specialization and a named spirit-ridden mortal antagonist.

```
Witch-Hunter — Informed Mortal Antagonist (Crossover Specialist)
----------------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Presence 2 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Resolve 4 | Composure 4
Health: 5  |  Armor: 1-2 (tactical gear)  |  Defense: Dex 3
Skills: Awareness 3, Athletics 2, Close Combat 2, Artistry (Weaponsmith / Traps) 4, Pilot 2, Ranged Combat 3, Investigation 3, Esoterica 3, Larceny 3, Technology 3, Deception 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  Prepared Loadout: Silver-edged weapons (aggravated to vampires/werewolves), iron implements (aggravated to fae), improvised explosive devices (Artistry 4), trigger-based traps, anti-supernatural compounds (holy water, salt, etc.). Each hunter is prepared for one specific supernatural target type.
  Team Coordination: When operating with other hunters, +1 die to all rolls when executing pre-planned ambush tactics.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Fanatic] Social persuasion about mage humanity increases Difficulty by +2. [Prepared] Pre-set sniper positions, ambush plans, and escape routes. [Determined] Immune to Presence-based social rolls from supernatural sources.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
ROLEPLAYING NOTES: In a fair fight, a witch-hunter loses. They never fight fair. Sniper rifles, IEDs, vicious traps, and coordinated ambushes allow the best hunters to survive and succeed.
```

```
Jacqui High Thunder — Spirit-Ridden Mortal Antagonist (Named Character)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 4 | Presence 4 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Resolve 5 | Composure 4
Health: 6  |  Armor: 1 (spiritual protection)
Skills: Awareness 5, Athletics 4, Close Combat 3, Ranged Combat 4, Larceny 3, Esoterica 2, Leadership 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICKS:
  High Thunder's Protection: Spirit rider provides 2 automatic soak against attacks targeting torso/vitals. Cannot be reduced by armor piercing effects.
  Spiritual Senses: Automatically detects supernatural beings within 30 meters. No roll required.
  Elemental Strike (spirit-powered, once per scene): Enhancement +3 aggravated damage. Spend 1 Resolve to activate.
  Spirit Frenzy: If Resolve drops to 1 or if witnessing significant injustice, the High Thunder spirit takes control. Resolve roll Difficulty 9 to resist.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACKGROUND: A teenager consumed with searing fury, now permanently ridden by the High Thunder — a spirit of awesome wrath and cold certainty. She was once a child who witnessed atrocities at home and afar. The spirit saw her fury and slipped inside her. Her target list currently focuses on CEOs and bank presidents; it may widen.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPECIAL RULES:
  Jacqui cannot be exorcised without killing both her and the High Thunder simultaneously.
  Ancestral stories and respected elders may temporarily reduce spirit's control (contested Resolve roll by supporters vs. Difficulty 8; success lasts 1 scene).
TAGS: [Permanent Possession] Standard possession removal rules do not apply. [Targeted] Jacqui maintains a carefully researched kill list.
```

### Natural Animals — Gods and Monsters Entries

Additional animal entries from Gods and Monsters, supplementing Section Eleven of this appendix. The bat and coyote appear in Section Eleven and are not duplicated here. These entries are useful for shapeshifter reference forms, animal Familiars, and group encounter rules.

```
Large Predator (Bear / Lion / Tiger archetype)
----------------------------------------------
Might 6-8 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 6-7 | Presence 2 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Resolve 2 | Composure 2
Health: 8-9  |  Armor: 0  |  Attack: Claws/Bite Enhancement +2 lethal
Skills: Awareness 3, Athletics 3, Close Combat 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Predator Instinct] +1 die when attacking fleeing or prone targets.
```

```
Packs and Swarms — Group Combat Rule
------------------------------------
Rather than tracking individual animals in a pack or swarm, treat the group as a single entity:
  Small bugs (fist-size swarm): Damage 1 bashing/turn, Health Levels 2, Initiative 3
  Large bugs (hand-size swarm): Damage 2 bashing/turn, Health Levels 3, Initiative 3
  Flying bugs: Damage 1 bashing/turn, Health Levels 2, Initiative 5
  Rats (dozen): Damage 2 bashing/turn, Health Levels 4, Initiative 3
  Feral cats (pack): Damage 2 bashing/turn, Health Levels 4, Initiative 4
  Wild dogs (pack): Damage 3 lethal/turn, Health Levels 5, Initiative 4
  Wolf pack: Damage 4 lethal/turn, Health Levels 7, Initiative 4
Area of effect attacks (fire, explosives) hit the whole swarm. Scatter/disperse effects break swarm formation.
```

## Section Twelve: Consor and Sleeper Allies

Mages rarely work alone. Beyond named Traditions and the Technocracy, a supporting cast of Sleepers, half-Awakened servants, and specialized helpers populate most chronicles. All stat blocks here use SPU mechanics: Enhancement replaces flat bonuses, Tags replace special rules text, and the dice pool formula is always Attribute + Skill vs. Difficulty.

```
Assistant — Consor Template
---------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 6
Skills: Awareness 3, Technology 3, Investigation 2, Empathy 2, one specialty at 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Knows the Score] — Accepted that magic is real. Adds no Paradox dice from coincidental magic. Does not panic at vulgar effects — just winces.
[Loyal Under Pressure] — Resolve rolls to resist intimidation or magical compulsion gain Enhancement +1.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
CASTING: Not Enlightened. Cannot cast.
EXAMPLE: Teresa Okafor manages three chantry phone numbers, two storage units, and a shell company lease. She knows seventeen arcane secrets and zero people she can safely tell.
```

```
Backup — Combat Consor
----------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 1 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 5  |  Armor: 2 (tactical vest)  |  Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 6
Skills: Ranged Combat 3, Close Combat 3, Athletics 3, Awareness 3, Larceny 2, Medicine 1
WEAPONS: Pistol (Might + Ranged 3, Enhancement +1 lethal); SMG (Enhancement +2 lethal, auto-fire adds Complication die for cover required); combat knife (Enhancement +1 lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Cover Fire] — Declared action: all enemies in a 45-degree arc take Difficulty +1 to move or attack this round. Costs the Major Action.
[Mundane Anchor] — Not Enlightened. Cannot be targeted by Arete vs. Arete contest spells.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
EXAMPLE: Dmitri Volkov has driven through three Paradox events and watched two mages dissolve into light. He still shows up. The pay is not that good.
```

```
Cleaners — Evidence Removal Consor
----------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 4 | Resolve 3
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 7
Skills: Deception 4, Investigation 4, Technology 3, Larceny 3, Empathy 2, Medicine 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TRICK — Evidence Removal [scene, extended]: Given one hour and access, roll Intellect + Investigation vs. Difficulty 2 per category of evidence (physical, electronic, eyewitness). Each hit eliminates one category. Magical traces require Spirit or Prime 2 assistance.
TRICK — Plausible Narrative [scene]: Construct a cover story for a witnessed supernatural event. Roll Manipulation + Deception vs. Difficulty 2. On 3 hits, the story holds up to ordinary police scrutiny for at least one week.
```

```
Cultist — Quintessence Battery
------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 2 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 1 | Resolve 3
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 4
Skills: Awareness 2, Esoterica 2, Athletics 1, Empathy 1
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Devoted] — Resolve vs. Difficulty 3 to resist instructions from a recognized cult leader, even dangerous ones.
TRICK — Quintessence Battery [ritual, extended]: A willing, focused cultist participating in a ritual generates 1 Quintessence per point of Resolve spent. Requires active participation and 5+ minutes of ritual. Unwilling cultists generate nothing.
```

```
Handler — Technocratic Network Controller
-----------------------------------------
