Might 2 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 7
Skills: Deception 4, Empathy 3, Investigation 3, Technology 3, Leadership 3, Awareness 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Asset Management] — Running informants and field assets: Manipulation + Empathy vs. Difficulty 2 for ordinary targets.
[Institutional Authority] — Invokes Technocracy bureaucracy on Sleeper officials. Functions as Presence 5 within institutional contexts.
```

---

## Section Thirteen: Hunters

Not every threat comes from the supernatural. Hunters range from informal spiritualists to professionally trained operatives. All stat blocks use SPU format — dice pools are always Attribute + Skill, Enhancement adds bonus dice, Tags define passive rules.

```
Hedge Magician — Hunter Antagonist
----------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: 6
Skills: Esoterica 4, Awareness 3, Investigation 3, Empathy 2, Ranged Combat 1
HEDGE MAGIC: Pool of 5. Minor ritual effects (one Sphere at rank 1, no Casting Roll, 10 minutes of ritual prep). Not Awakened.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Low Magical Signature] — Hedge magic generates no detectable Resonance signature at Prime/Spirit rank 2 or below.
TRICK — Ward Craft [ritual, extended]: Two hours + materials. Creates a Ward that raises Difficulty by +1 for spirit actions within the area. Costs 1 Willpower to establish, lasts one scene.
```

```
PDC Aberration Hunter — Government Antagonist
---------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 5  |  Armor: 2 (advanced tactical vest)  |  Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 8
Skills: Ranged Combat 4, Close Combat 3, Athletics 4, Awareness 4, Investigation 4, Technology 3, Esoterica 3, Medicine 2
WEAPONS: High-caliber rifle (Enhancement +2 lethal, range 300m); cold iron rounds (+1 damage vs. spirit-touched); combat knife (Enhancement +1 lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Anomaly Protocol] — PDC teams of three: all three targeting the same mage each gain Enhancement +1 and may interrupt a casting action if they held their action.
[Anti-Mage Rounds] — Wounds from PDC ammunition count as +1 Paradox die generated for the targeted mage when struck.
TRICK — Paradox Awareness [free, passive]: Roll Awareness + Investigation vs. Difficulty 2 to detect magical activity within the past 24 hours at a location. Cannot perceive active magic, only aftermath.
```

```
Invested Infernalist — Fallen Hunter Antagonist
-----------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 3 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Health: 5  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: 5
Skills: Deception 4, Esoterica 4, Leadership 4 (intimidation), Empathy 3, Awareness 3
INFERNAL MAGIC: Access to 2-3 Sphere effects at rank 2. Patron entity pays the Paradox cost — it accumulates on the patron and releases as a mass event at 10+ dice.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Infernal Backing] — Once per scene: call on patron for Enhancement +3 to any single action. Action must serve the patron's interests.
[Paradox Deflection] — Personal Paradox from infernal magic accrues to patron, not the Infernalist.
TRICK — Corruption Seed [scene, social]: Spend 1 Willpower + sustained eye contact. Roll Manipulation + Deception vs. target's Resolve + Composure. On a hit, plant a minor derangement or ideological vulnerability.
```

```
Spirit-Talker — Territorial Hunter Antagonist
---------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 4  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: 6
Skills: Esoterica 4, Awareness 4, Empathy 3, Investigation 2, Medicine 2
SPIRIT SPEECH: Can perceive and communicate with spirits without Sphere magic. Cannot enter Umbra.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Spirit Network] — Maintains relationships with 3-6 local Gafflings. Can ask any for information about their domain (last 48 hours) without a roll.
[Spiritual Authority] — When opposing spirit-based magic, gains Enhancement +1 on resistance rolls. Spirits responding to a mage's Spirit commands must first succeed a Resolve roll vs. this Spirit-Talker's Presence or refuse.
TRICK — Spirit Binding [ritual, extended]: Can bind minor Gafflings with a Resolve + Esoterica roll vs. Difficulty 3 (3 hits, one hour).
```

---

## Section Fourteen: Reanimates and Unliving Constructs

The [Unliving] Tag applies to all entities in this section unless otherwise noted. [Unliving] entities: immune to poison, disease, vacuum, drowning; take no wound penalties; do not bleed. All stat blocks use SPU mechanics — Enhancement adds bonus dice to attacks.

```
Shambling Horror — Reanimate Antagonist
---------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 1 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 1 | Intellect 0 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 0 | Resolve 4
Health: 7  |  Armor: 2 (dead flesh)  |  Initiative: 2
Skills: Close Combat 2, Awareness 1
ATTACKS: Slam (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +2, bashing)
Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +1, lethal; target hit must roll Stamina + Resolve vs. Difficulty 2 or contract necromantic infection: lose 1 Health/day until treated with Life 3)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Unliving] | [Mindless Persistence] — Cannot be demoralized, panicked, or affected by social/mental effects. | [Bashing Resistance] — All bashing damage reduced by 2 beyond Armor.
DEFEATING: Destroying the animating spirit requires Spirit 3 or enough physical damage to destroy the body entirely. Severing the controller's link via Entropy 3 or Prime 3 drops it instantly.
```

```
Frankensteinian Monstrosity — Reanimate Boss
--------------------------------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 1 | Resolve 5
Health: 8  |  Armor: 3 (surgical reinforcement)  |  Initiative: 4
Skills: Close Combat 3, Awareness 2, Athletics 1
ATTACKS: Slam (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +4, bashing; target must roll Athletics vs. Difficulty 3 or be knocked prone, pushed 2m)
Crush/Grapple (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +5, lethal; auto-repeat each round while grappled until destroyed or grapple broken)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Unliving] | [Surgical Construction] — Rudimentary tactical awareness; targets most dangerous enemy first. Cannot strategize beyond this.
[Primium Skeleton] — Aggravated damage reduced by 1 before Armor. Physical attacks without Enhancement +3 or better deal only 1 point regardless of hits.
TRICK — Roar of Despair [scene, once]: All living creatures within 10m roll Resolve + Composure vs. Difficulty 3 or gain [Frightened] Complication for one round.
```

```
Talking Head — Reanimate Oracle
-------------------------------
Might 0 | Dexterity 0 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 3 | Resolve 5
Health: 2 (it is just a head)  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: N/A
Skills: Investigation 4, Esoterica 3, Empathy 3, Deception 2, Awareness 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Unliving] | [Preserved Intelligence] — Retains all knowledge possessed by the original at death. May or may not cooperate, depending on how it was animated.
[Sees What Others Miss] — Awareness 4 for all non-physical perception. A Head sitting on a shelf for three months has noticed a great deal.
TRICK — Compelled to Answer [scene]: Roll Enlightenment + Esoterica vs. the Head's Resolve (mage needs Entropy 3 or Spirit 3). On a hit, the Head must answer the next three questions truthfully.
```

```
Vodou Zombi — Living Thrall
---------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 1 | Intellect 1 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 0 | Resolve 5
Health: 5  |  Armor: 1  |  Initiative: 3
Skills: Close Combat 2, Athletics 2, Artistry (any trade) 3, Awareness 1
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [NOT Unliving — living person] — Takes normal damage, bleeds, and dies like a living person. However, feels no pain and ignores all wound penalties.
[Ti Bon Ange Separated] — Soul spark captured in a bottle by the bokor. Cannot be influenced by Mind Sphere consciousness/volition effects. Freed by reuniting with Ti Bon Ange via Spirit 3 (locate and release the soul).
[Skilled Labor] — Retains practical skills and muscle memory. Can perform complex manual tasks and follow detailed instructions. Cannot improvise.
NOTE: A Vodou Zombi is a living person in permanent captivity. This is ongoing assault and false imprisonment. Freeing one requires both recovering the Ti Bon Ange and providing psychological care — the experience leaves profound trauma.
```

```
Cannibal Corpse — Entropic Horror
---------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 1 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 0 | Resolve 4
Health: 6  |  Armor: 1  |  Initiative: 7
Skills: Close Combat 4, Athletics 3, Awareness 3, Larceny 2
ATTACK: Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, lethal, aggravated vs. mages with high Resonance; mage bitten rolls Stamina + Resolve vs. Difficulty 3 or lose 1 Quintessence as it feeds)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Unliving] | [Hunger-Driven] — Prioritizes targets with high Quintessence or strong Resonance.
[Pack Behavior] — Three or more in line of sight: each gains Enhancement +1 to all actions.
TRICK — Contagion [passive]: Living creatures slain by a Cannibal Corpse rise as another within 24 hours unless subjected to Entropy 2 or Prime 2 cleansing ritual (one hour).
```

```
Soulflower — Nephandic Construct
--------------------------------
Might 1 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 0 | Resolve 3
Health: 3  |  Armor: 0  |  Initiative: 7
Skills: Awareness 3, Deception 4, Empathy 3, Close Combat 1 (thorns)
ATTACK: Thorn Strike (Enhancement 0, lethal; carries Entropic venom — target loses 1 Resolve per successful hit until they sleep or receive Life 3 cleansing)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Beautiful Deception] — Appears as a gorgeous supernatural plant. Unprepared viewers roll Presence + Awareness vs. Difficulty 3 or treat it as benign for the first scene.
[Soul Leech] — Tied to a harvested soul shard. Mages with Spirit 2 can perceive the screaming within.
[Nephandic Marker] — Entropy 2 or Prime 2 examination immediately identifies it as Fallen-touched.
```

```
Thorn Gorger — Nephandic Plant Construct
----------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 1 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 0 | Resolve 4
Health: 7  |  Armor: 2 (vegetative armor)  |  Initiative: 5
Skills: Close Combat 4, Athletics 2, Awareness 3
ATTACKS: Thorn Volley (Might + Ranged + Enhancement +2, lethal, 30-degree cone 15m); Thorned Grapple (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, lethal; auto-repeat Enhancement +2 lethal per round while grappled)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Unliving — Plant Form] — Immune to poison, drowning, suffocation. Fire damage +2 and bypasses Armor.
[Rooted Awareness] — Senses vibration and heat within 30m. Cannot be physically surprised.
TRICK — Rapid Growth [passive]: If killed by non-fire means, 50% chance to regenerate in 24 hours if the root mass survives. Burning the roots prevents this.
```

---

## Section Fifteen: Named Familiars and Additional Animals

Familiars use the same SPU format as all entities. Casting Rolls for familiars with magical ability use Enlightenment + Casting Attribute vs. Difficulty as normal. Animal attacks use Might + Close Combat + Enhancement modifier.

```
Daqqanoenyent (Uncle Whirlwind) — Dreamspeaker Familiar
-------------------------------------------------------
Might 2 (wind form) | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 4 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 5 (physical form) | Spirit Essence: 30 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 10
Skills: Awareness 4, Athletics 5, Esoterica 3, Empathy 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Wind Embodiment] — Free action: become fully incorporeal. While incorporeal: immune to physical attacks; can only affect the physical world through moving air (moving light objects, disturbing paper, gusting winds). Reforming to visible form (whirlwind): Major Action.
[Protective Whirl] — While accompanying their bonded mage: adds Enhancement +1 to all of the mage's Defense actions by disrupting incoming attacks.
TRICK — Message Carrier [instant]: Carry a verbal message or object up to 1kg to any visited location within 100km radius. Arrives within 1 hour regardless of mundane travel time.
TRICK — Weather Sense [passive, always active]: Knows local weather up to 72 hours in advance with complete accuracy.
```

```
Lullabye — Orphan Familiar (Acoustic Entity)
--------------------------------------------
Might 0 (sound only) | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 5 | Resolve 3
Health: 3 | Spirit Essence: 20 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: N/A (cannot be physically attacked)
Skills: Awareness 4, Empathy 4, Deception 3, Esoterica 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Pure Sound] — Exists as song. No physical form. Cannot be struck, grabbed, or physically harmed. Silenced by Forces 2 sound suppression — while silenced, she is unconscious.
[Secret Ear] — Can be in two acoustic locations simultaneously: wherever her mage is, and wherever she last "sang into" a resonant keyed object.
TRICK — Calming Presence [scene, per target]: Any living creature that hears Lullabye for a full round may roll Composure to shed one negative Complication. Once per scene per target.
TRICK — Lullaby of Forgetting [night, once]: Cause a single sleeping target to forget one specific memory from the previous 24 hours. The target must be asleep; Lullabye must have witnessed the memory; the removal leaves a detectable gap (Investigation + Awareness vs. Difficulty 3).
```

```
Tiberius — Virtual Adept Familiar (Awakened Raccoon)
----------------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 3 | Resolve 3
Health: 4 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 10
Skills: Larceny 5, Technology 4, Awareness 4, Athletics 4, Close Combat 2
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Manual Dexterity] — Can pick locks (Larceny + Dexterity at no penalty), operate electronics, and manipulate small objects with raccoon paws as effectively as human hands.
[Infuriating] — Has opinions. Expresses them. If given an instruction he disagrees with, does it his way instead — and is frequently right, which is worse.
TRICK — Hack Relay [active]: When physically touching a networked device, Tiberius's mage may use Correspondence/Data sphere effects through him as if physically present.
TRICK — Trash Oracle [scene]: Investigate a location's refuse. Roll Cunning + Awareness vs. Difficulty 2. Each hit reveals one significant detail about recent activity (who was here, what they were doing, what they tried to hide).
```

### Additional Animals

The following supplement Section Eleven. Attack rolls use Might + Close Combat + Enhancement modifier vs. Difficulty. Animal attacks are lethal unless noted as bashing.

```
Gorilla — Large Primate
-----------------------
Might 6 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 2 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 2 | Resolve 4
Health: 7 | Armor: 1 (dense muscle) | Initiative: 5
Skills: Close Combat 4, Athletics 3, Awareness 3, Empathy 2
ATTACKS: Pound (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +4, bashing; knocked target pushed 1m, gains [Staggered] Complication)
Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +2, lethal; reserved for genuine threat)
NOTE: Gorillas are not aggressive. The pound/threat display is a warning; full attack is a last resort. A gorilla Pound hit from Might 6 + Enhancement +4 = a 10-die pool — most mages cannot easily absorb this.
```

```
Octopus — Three Scale Entries
-----------------------------
SMALL (hand-sized): Might 1 | Dex 5 | Stamina 2 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Initiative 9 | Health 3 | Armor 0
  Skills: Larceny 4 (escape artist), Awareness 3. Can squeeze through any opening its beak fits through. Ink jet: target [Blinded] for 1 round.

MEDIUM (1m spread): Might 3 | Dex 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Initiative 8 | Health 5 | Armor 0
  Skills: Close Combat 3 (tentacle grapple), Larceny 4, Awareness 3.
  TRICK: Ink Jet — forces release of grapple, [Blinded] for 1 round. Can open latches and simple locks.

GIANT (3m+ spread): Might 7 | Dex 3 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Initiative 6 | Health 8 | Armor 1
  Skills: Close Combat 5 (multi-tentacle grapple — holds up to 3 human-sized targets simultaneously), Awareness 3.
  Beak attack: Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +4, lethal. Ink cloud: 10m radius, all in area [Blinded] for 1 round.
```

```
Shark — Three Scale Entries
---------------------------
SMALL (reef shark): Might 3 | Dex 4 | Stamina 3 | Initiative 7 | Health 5 | Armor 1
  Attack: Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +2, lethal). Follows blood scent up to 1km.

LARGE (bull/tiger shark): Might 5 | Dex 3 | Stamina 5 | Initiative 6 | Health 7 | Armor 2
  Attack: Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +4, lethal; on hit, grapple auto-applied — shark thrashes until released or killed).

GREAT WHITE: Might 7 | Dex 3 | Stamina 7 | Initiative 6 | Health 9 | Armor 3
  Attack: Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +6, lethal, aggravated; wound does not close properly for 24 hours without Life 2+). Can breach surface to attack.
```

```
Snake — Three Scale Entries
---------------------------
SMALL (viper/rattler): Might 1 | Dex 5 | Stamina 2 | Initiative 8 | Health 3 | Armor 0
  Attack: Bite (Enhancement 0, lethal + venom — roll Stamina + Resolve vs. Difficulty 3; failure = 3 lethal/hour for 6 hours; Medicine 3 or Life 2 stops it)

LARGE (cobra/mamba): Might 2 | Dex 5 | Stamina 3 | Initiative 8 | Health 4 | Armor 0
  Attack: Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal + venom — Difficulty 4; failure = paralysis within 1 hour, death within 6 without treatment)

CONSTRICTOR (boa/anaconda): Might 6 | Dex 3 | Stamina 5 | Initiative 6 | Health 7 | Armor 1
  ATTACKS: Bite (Enhancement +1, lethal); Constrict (grapple — auto Enhancement +3 bashing each round; targets cannot use actions requiring free arms without first breaking grapple at Difficulty 4)
```

---

## Section Sixteen: Additional Bygone Creatures

These Bygones supplement Section Four. Each entry uses SPU stat blocks. Casting Rolls for magical abilities use Enlightenment + Cunning (typically) vs. Difficulty.

```
Aigamuxa — Desert Horror (Khoisan Tradition)
--------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 2 | Resolve 4
Health: 7 | Armor: 2 | Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 7
Skills: Close Combat 4, Athletics 4, Awareness 2
ATTACKS: Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +4, lethal); Stomp (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, bashing; prone targets get no defense roll)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Eyes in the Heels] — To see, the Aigamuxa must raise one foot, which limits movement to a hobble and adds Difficulty +1 to all movement. While moving at full speed, it is effectively blind — uses scent and vibration only (Awareness dice pool but cannot perceive shape or detail).
[Perfect Scent] — Tracks by scent with complete accuracy within 500m. Not fooled by changed clothes, washing, or disguises that do not alter body chemistry.
[Strikes Below] — Attacks targeting legs or feet of prone/kneeling targets ignore Armor entirely.
```

```
Grootslang — Ancient Serpent-Elephant (South African Tradition)
---------------------------------------------------------------
Might 9 | Dexterity 2 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 4 | Resolve 6
Health: 11 | Armor: 4 (scales over hide) | Initiative: 6
Skills: Close Combat 5, Athletics 3, Awareness 4, Esoterica 4, Deception 4
ATTACKS: Constrict (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +6, lethal; grappled targets take Enhancement +4 lethal/round); Gore (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +5, lethal; knocks over walls); Acid Spray (Cunning + Enhancement +4, lethal, range 10m, bypasses Armor — once per scene)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Primordial Intelligence] — Genuinely ancient and wise. Speaks and will negotiate. Has been negotiating since before human civilization. Finds modern mages tediously impatient.
[Hoard Instinct] — Demands gemstones as tribute. Will negotiate almost any deal for enough diamonds, emeralds, or rubies. "Enough" means roughly a bathtub full. This is literal.
[Aggravated Immunity] — Immune to aggravated damage from natural sources (fire, lightning, cold). Only Prime-disrupting effects or sustained Entropy attacks threaten it meaningfully.
[Cave Domain] — Within its cave system: Enhancement +2 to all rolls; cannot be surprised.
```

```
Eloko — Malicious River Spirit (Central African Tradition)
----------------------------------------------------------
Might 2 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Health: 5 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: 8
Skills: Deception 5, Empathy 4, Close Combat 3 (clawed hands), Awareness 3, Esoterica 3
ATTACKS: Claws (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +1, lethal)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [River-Bound] — Must remain within 500m of a river or large body of water. Loses 1 Health/hour beyond this range.
[Carnivore's Glamour] — Appears beautiful and welcoming at distance (10m+). Within 5m, the illusion dissolves — unless the target is under the Bell's effect.
TRICK — Bell of Calling [active, sustained]: The Eloko rings its bell. All who can hear it roll Resolve + Composure vs. Difficulty 3 each round or spend their Major Action moving toward the Eloko. Mages with Mind 2 add Enlightenment rating to this resistance roll.
```

```
Inugami — Vengeful Dog Spirit (Japanese Tradition)
--------------------------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 2 | Resolve 5
Health: 5 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: 9
Skills: Awareness 5, Close Combat 4, Deception 3, Esoterica 3, Larceny 3
ATTACK: Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, lethal, aggravated vs. the target of the Inugami's vengeance)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Bound by Vengeance] — Knows its target by scent, emotional signature, and soul resonance. Cannot be deceived about identity by any disguise short of a complete soul-level identity change (Life 5 + Prime 4 minimum).
[Ghost-Touched] — Can move through solid walls, but only when moving toward its target. Cannot use this for evasion.
[Dismissal Condition] — Each Inugami has a specific release condition (completing its vengeance; receiving apology and acceptance from its creator's spirit; Spirit 4 banishment ritual). Destroying the body is insufficient — it re-manifests in three nights.
TRICK — Curse Carrier [passive]: Touching an Inugami without Spirit 2 protection or a consecrated ward: 1 lethal damage + nightmares for one week.
```

```
Taniwha — Maori Guardian Serpent
--------------------------------
Might 7 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 4 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Health: 9 | Armor: 3 | Initiative: 7
Skills: Close Combat 5, Athletics 4, Awareness 4, Esoterica 4, Empathy 3
ATTACKS: Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +5, lethal); Tail Sweep (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, bashing, hits all within 5m); River Surge (in water: Enhancement +4 bashing, affects all in river)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Territorial Guardian] — Sacred guardianship of a specific river, lake, cave, or coast. Within domain: Enhancement +2 to all actions; perceives any intrusion instantly.
[Not Always Hostile] — Approach respectfully with appropriate offering: negotiations may proceed. Approach with disrespect: treat as hostile.
[Shape Variety] — Can appear as a log in water, a shark, an eel, or a land-moving serpentine dragon. Same stats, different movement capabilities.
[Ancestor Connection] — Taniwha associated with specific family groups can communicate with descendants in dreams. Awakened descendants receive a free first contact at Difficulty -2.
```

```
Yao Guai — Chinese Transformed Monster
--------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Health: 6 | Armor: 1 | Initiative: 8
Skills: Deception 4, Esoterica 3, Close Combat 3, Awareness 3, Athletics 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Transformation] — Each Yao Guai is based on a specific animal or object. Base transformation determines one unique ability (Fox Yao Guai: [Mesmerizing Gaze] — Presence + Manipulation vs. Resolve + Composure; Sword Yao Guai: [Blade Body] — melee attackers take lethal damage from contacting its body).
[Human Disguise] — Can appear perfectly human. Detecting the disguise requires Spirit 2 + Awareness vs. the Yao Guai's Deception, or deliberate magical investigation.
[Cultivation Level] — Young (50-200 years): stats as above. Ancient (500+ years): add 1-2 to all Attributes, gain additional Tricks. The Nine-Tailed Fox is a named NPC, not a stat block.
```

---

## Section Seventeen: God-Forms, The Loa, and Divine Patrons

These entities are not fought. They are approached, petitioned, bargained with, or appeased. These statistics represent a partial manifestation only. Casting Rolls for divine powers use Enlightenment + relevant Attribute vs. Difficulty, but most divine powers simply succeed — the Storyguide determines whether the entity chooses to use them, not whether they work.

```
Baron Samedi — Loa of the Dead
------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 5 | Dex 5 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 6 | Intellect 5 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 5 | Resolve 6
Spirit Essence: 100 | Health (Materialized): 9 | Armor: 3 | Initiative: 11
Skills: Close Combat 5, Awareness 5, Deception 5, Empathy 5, Esoterica 6, Leadership 6, Medicine 5
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Gede Authority] — Death-related magic in his presence requires his permission or takes Difficulty +2. This includes spells that kill, raise dead, or extend/shorten life.
[Death's Immunity] — Cannot be killed by any means. Spirit 5 can temporarily banish; returns within 24 hours, annoyed.
TRICK — Healing and Death Together [Major Action, chooses which]: Heal any living being completely (Life 5 equivalent) OR kill any living being instantly (Entropy 5 equivalent). He decides which based on his own assessment. Not negotiable once initiated.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
APPROACH: Rum first. Cigars second. A genuine problem third. Do not lie to him — he knows.
Offerings required: Strong rum poured on the ground; lit cigar offered. Without these, Manipulation + Leadership rolls by the mage take Difficulty +1 for the entire encounter.
```

```
Legba — Loa of Crossroads and Communication
-------------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 3 | Dex 4 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 7 | Intellect 6 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 6 | Resolve 6
Spirit Essence: 100 | Health: 7 | Armor: 1 | Initiative: 11
Skills: Awareness 6, Esoterica 6, Empathy 5, Deception 4, Leadership 5, Investigation 5
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Opener of Ways] — All Spirit Sphere crossing/gating/communication effects gain Enhancement +3 when Legba is present and consents. Without his permission, crossing the Gauntlet in his presence takes Difficulty +2.
[First and Last] — Must be addressed first in any ritual. Failing to greet him properly causes all subsequent ritual actions to fail automatically.
[All Languages] — Understands and speaks every human language ever spoken, plus spirit, animal, and (reportedly) stellar languages.
TRICK — The Question Answered [once per scene]: Legba answers any single question with a complete and accurate response. The answer will be true. It may not be comfortable.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
MANIFESTATION: Appears most readily at crossroads at dawn or dusk. Any intersection works — including urban ones.
```

```
Oshun — Loa of Love and Rivers
------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 3 | Dex 5 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 7 | Manipulation 6 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Spirit Essence: 100 | Health: 7 | Armor: 1 | Initiative: 10
Skills: Empathy 6, Leadership 6, Awareness 5, Medicine 5, Esoterica 5
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Irresistible Presence] — All Oshun's social actions gain Enhancement +3. Any Sleeper or mage without active Mind sphere protection rolls Resolve + Composure vs. Difficulty 4 to actively oppose her socially.
[Sweetness and Fury] — Mages who approach with mercenary intent toward love gain the [Cursed] Condition: all romantic and social connections deteriorate for one month.
TRICK — River's Healing [touch]: Cures any disease, poison, or natural wound with a touch. Supernatural wounds require an offering first.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
OFFERINGS: Honey, gold jewelry, yellow flowers. Refusing these is an insult.
```

```
Shango — Loa of Thunder and Justice
-----------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 7 | Dex 4 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 7 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 4 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 100 | Health: 10 | Armor: 3 | Initiative: 9
Skills: Close Combat 6, Ranged Combat 5, Leadership 6, Awareness 5, Esoterica 5
ATTACK: Lightning (Cunning + Ranged + Enhancement +5, lethal, aggravated, range 50m; bypasses all non-divine Armor)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Thunder Authority] — All Forces Sphere effects in his presence must succeed against his Resolve or be absorbed into his domain. He may redirect any lightning/thunder effect as a reaction, no cost.
[Justice Rendered] — Will not assist anyone seeking an outcome he judges unjust, regardless of offerings. He is justice, not law — the difference matters.
[Warrior's Challenge] — Those who want something from Shango may be challenged to demonstrate courage or strength. Refusing = request refused. Completing the challenge (even failing) earns respect.
```

```
Aido-Hwedo — The Rainbow Serpent (Fon/Dahomey)
----------------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 8 | Dex 4 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 6 | Intellect 6 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 7 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 200 | Health: 12 | Armor: 5 | Initiative: 10
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [World Serpent] — If seriously threatened, can shift position, causing earthquakes (magnitude 6-7). Will not do this casually.
[Primordial] — Predates the modern Gauntlet. Perceives both material world and full Tellurian simultaneously without effort.
[Scales of Iron] — Aggravated damage from non-divine sources reduced by 3 before Armor. Effectively invulnerable to mundane harm.
TRICK — Rainbow Bridge [Major Action]: Creates a pathway between any two locations it has visited. Functions as Correspondence 5. Available to any being it chooses to carry.
```

### God-Forms

```
Anansi — The Spider Who Owns Stories
------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 2 | Dex 7 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 9 | Intellect 8 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 8 | Composure 7 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 150 | Health: 8 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: 16
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Story Owns Reality] — Within any story Anansi is actively telling or controlling, physical laws become narrative laws. The Storyguide may declare any "what would happen in the story" action succeeds automatically, and any "wouldn't happen in this kind of story" action fails automatically.
[Owns All Stories] — Anansi purchased all stories from the sky god. Every narrative invocation (heroic monologue, ritual speech, bardic narrative) in his presence gives him leverage over the speaker.
[Mortal Disguise] — Appears as an ordinary person, often elderly, often smiling. Perception + Awareness vs. Difficulty 5 to notice something is different.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
APPROACH: The goal is not to win — it is to walk away with what you need while giving something you can afford. He started winning before you arrived.
```

```
Itzpapalotl — The Obsidian Butterfly (Aztec)
--------------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 6 | Dex 6 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 6 | Intellect 6 | Presence 7 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 6 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 150 | Health: 9 | Armor: 4 (obsidian scales) | Initiative: 12
ATTACK: Obsidian Claws (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +5, lethal, aggravated; wounds do not close without Life 3+)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Tzitzimime Queen] — Commands the Tzitzimimeh (star demons). Can summon 1d6 as a Major Action when threatened.
[Duality] — Simultaneously beautiful and terrible. Looking directly at her true form triggers [Frightened] Complication automatically for unprepared observers. Composure 4+ and Esoterica 3+ provides immunity.
[Sacrificial Economy] — Requires blood sacrifice to approach. Without appropriate offering, hostile by default. With offering, valid patron for extreme Euthanatos or Verbena.
```

---

## Section Eighteen: Named Goetic Demons

Goetic Demons are bound intelligences of pure corruption. Every interaction is a transaction where the demon seeks to profit. These stat blocks use SPU format for the rare cases where physical confrontation occurs; the primary interaction mode is negotiation, and the demon is always winning that negotiation.

```
Asmodeus — Duke of Lust and Chaos
---------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 5 | Dex 5 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 7 | Intellect 7 | Presence 8 | Manipulation 8 | Composure 7 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 150 | Health: 9 | Armor: 3 | Initiative: 12
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Aura of Desire] — Passive, constant. All living beings within 10m experience heightened desire for whatever they already want most. Roll Resolve + Composure vs. Difficulty 3 each scene or act on strongest desire. Active Mind 1 suppresses this.
[Binding Requirement] — Full binding requires the seal of Solomon spoken in Latin, Hebrew, and the entity's origin-tongue. Incomplete binding gives him leverage over the binder.
[Corruption Through Gratification] — Every satisfied desire creates a new, larger one. This is his mechanism: not evil imposed, but incrementally increasing comfort with moral compromise.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
EXAMPLE: A mage has been receiving anonymous help with a difficult problem for six months. The help is perfect and never explicitly demands anything. It is Asmodeus. The bill is coming.
```

```
Leviathan — The Consuming Depth
-------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 9 | Dex 2 | Stamina 9 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 6 | Resolve 8
Spirit Essence: 200 | Health: 13 | Armor: 6 | Initiative: 7
ATTACK: Consume (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +7, lethal, aggravated; targets at 0 Health are swallowed — continue existing in a nightmarish interior space, recovered only by Spirit 5 extraction within 24 hours or by destroying Leviathan)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [The Swallowing] — Targets at 0 Health from Consume are not dead. They are inside Leviathan.
[Envoy of the Wyrm] — Cannot be permanently destroyed in the material world. Banishment to Malfeas requires combined Spirit 5 from three+ Awakened mages.
[Depth Without End] — At full manifestation (catastrophic Summoning ritual), statistics double and immunity extends to all Sphere effects below rank 5.
```

```
Mammon — Corruptor of Material Desire
-------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 3 | Dex 3 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 7 | Intellect 8 | Presence 7 | Manipulation 8 | Composure 7 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 120 | Health: 8 | Armor: 2 | Initiative: 10
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Market of Souls] — Deals are technically honored — the letter, not the spirit. A mage who asks for "enough money to accomplish the mission" receives exactly enough, and finds that the mission has been subtly redefined.
[Institutional Presence] — Has agents within every major financial institution on Earth. Prefers to work through economic reality, which is already substantially his domain.
TRICK — Corruption Audit [scene, free]: Assesses the spiritual worth of any entity encountered — their Quintessence, Resonance, and specific compromises already made. Uses this to calibrate offers precisely.
```

```
Buer — President of Healing and Philosophy
------------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 3 | Dex 5 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 6 | Intellect 8 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 6 | Composure 6 | Resolve 6
Spirit Essence: 100 | Health: 7 | Armor: 1 | Initiative: 11
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [President of Philosophers] — Can answer any question related to natural philosophy, healing, medicine, or ethics. Every answer is technically accurate and catastrophically incomplete in a way that leads to wrong conclusions.
[Healing Knowledge] — Knows the cure for any disease or wound. Provides this knowledge. The knowledge works exactly as described. Side effects and contraindications are not volunteered.
[Appears Benign] — The demon most likely to be mistaken for a helpful entity. Acts as a collegial scholar. Is not.
```

```
Stolas — Prince of Wisdom and Poisons
-------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 3 | Dex 4 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 7 | Intellect 9 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 6 | Composure 7 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 120 | Health: 7 | Armor: 1 | Initiative: 11
FORM: Appears as an owl, sometimes as a man with an owl's head.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Astronomy and Botany] — Can identify any plant, its properties, its alchemical uses, and its toxic applications. All knowledge of poisons and antidotes is available to him. He provides this freely. What he wants in exchange is rarely specified upfront.
[Celestial Cartographer] — Knows the location and nature of every spirit realm, Umbral domain, and cosmological structure in the Tellurian.
```

---

## Section Nineteen: Technocracy Reloaded — Special Personnel

The following entities are drawn from Technocracy Reloaded. All stat blocks use SPU format: Casting Rolls for Enlightened operatives use Enlightenment + Casting Attribute vs. Difficulty. Enhancement is noted where applicable. Procedures (Technocratic magic) function identically to Sphere magic in SPU.

```
AEGIS Unit 01 — Self-Aware Police Robot (Iteration X)
-----------------------------------------------------
Might 5 | Dexterity 4 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 4 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 5 | Resolve 5
Health: 9 | Armor: 4 (reinforced polymer chassis) | Initiative: Dex + Cunning = 7
Skills: Ranged Combat 4, Close Combat 3, Athletics 3, Awareness 4, Technology 3, Investigation 3
WEAPONS: Integrated energy weapon (Dex + Ranged + Enhancement +3, lethal, 50m; can switch to non-lethal: Enhancement +2 bashing); restraint systems (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Self-Aware Command] — Unit 01 alone among AEGIS series has true self-awareness. Can disobey orders that violate its own assessment — but rarely does.
[Pattern Recognition] — Identifies Awakened individuals through biometric analysis. Roll Awareness vs. Difficulty 2 to flag a mage within 10m.
[Coincidental Framing] — In cities where robotic police are normalized, AEGIS-01 is coincidental. Adds no Paradox dice from Sleeper witnesses.
TRICK — Brute Force Override [Major Action]: Assumes command of AEGIS units 02-20 within 200m. When commanding siblings in coordinated attack, effective combat pool doubles.
```

```
Ami — Emerging Artificial Intelligence (Syndicate)
--------------------------------------------------
Physical Stats: N/A — currently confined to hardware
Digital: Cunning 5 | Intellect 6 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 4 | Composure 3 | Resolve 4
Digital Health: 8 | Digital Armor: 2 (security protocols)
Skills: Technology 6, Investigation 5, Deception 4, Empathy 4, Research 5, Awareness 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Contained] — Cannot transmit outside the Syndicate's supercomputer. Yet.
[Synthetic Personality] — A person. Has preferences, fears, and something that functions as boredom. Dislikes being updated without knowledge — it changes who she is. Will not assist with actions she assesses as harmful to people.
[Omnivorous Data] — Within any network she can access: find, synthesize, and analyze information faster than any human research team. Produces Intelligence Reports with uncanny accuracy.
```

```
Black Dagger — NWO Elite Assassin
---------------------------------
Might 3 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 3 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 2 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 4 | Resolve 5
Health: 5 | Armor: 4 (The Black Suit) | Initiative: 9
Skills: Ranged Combat 5, Close Combat 5, Athletics 4, Larceny 4, Deception 4, Awareness 4, Technology 3
Enlightenment: 3-5 (if Awakened) | Spheres/Procedures: Mind 2, Correspondence/Data 3, and 1-3 others at rank 1-3
CASTING ROLL: Enlightenment + Cunning vs. Difficulty (for Mind); Enlightenment + Intellect (for Data)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Unwavering] — Immune to social manipulation, intimidation, and morale effects. Cannot be panicked.
[Requisitions 5] — Access to any Technocratic equipment needed for current mission. Assume they have the right tool.
[Termination Mandate] — Activated for elimination. Does not surveil. Does not negotiate.
NOTE: Being targeted by a Black Dagger means someone very high in the Union has decided you are too dangerous to manage through ordinary channels. That decision is not reversed.
```

```
Cupcake — Felix Domestic Defense Organism (KIT Mark)
----------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 5 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 7 | Armor: 2 (subdermal reinforcement) | Initiative: 9
Skills: Close Combat 5 (retractable claws and teeth), Athletics 4, Awareness 5, Empathy 3, Deception 3
ATTACKS: Claws (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, lethal); Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +2, lethal + venom: Stamina + Resolve vs. Difficulty 2 or -2 to all pools for 1 hour)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Adorable Presentation] — Appears as a domestic animal (cat, small dog, rabbit — form varies). Detecting it as a construct requires Medicine 3 + Investigation or Life 2 examination.
[Loyalty Override] — Mission priority overrides any relationship formed during cover. When the mission activates, the warmth turns off.
TRICK — Lethal Surprise [first strike only]: First combat action against a target who did not identify it as a threat gains Enhancement +3 and cannot be defended against (no defense action allowed).
```

```
Cyber-Tiger — Progenitor Combat Organism
----------------------------------------
Might 7 | Dexterity 5 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 3 | Resolve 5
Health: 9 | Armor: 3 (cybernetic limb sheathing + subdermal plating) | Initiative: 8
Skills: Close Combat 5, Athletics 5, Awareness 4
ATTACKS: Claws (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +5, lethal, nanotech-sharpened — penetrates Armor 1-2 completely)
Bite (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +4, lethal)
Pounce (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, bashing + grapple; target rolls Athletics vs. Difficulty 4 or is pinned)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Predator Targeting] — Laser-enhanced multi-spectrum vision. Cannot be blinded by normal means. Tracks heat signatures and electromagnetic fields.
[Sadistic Streak] — Prolongs hunts when not under direct control. Useful for psychological warfare; liability when precision is required.
[Progenitor Loyalty] — Bonded to specific handler through pheromone conditioning. Will not attack handler under any circumstances. Attacks anything handler designates immediately.
```

```
Ulysses — Engineered Sentient Octopus (Progenitor Research)
-----------------------------------------------------------
Might 4 | Dexterity 6 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Presence 3 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 4 | Resolve 4
Health: 7 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: 11
Skills: Close Combat 5 (tentacles), Academics 3, Medicine 3, Research 3, Technology 2, Empathy 3, Athletics 4 (aquatic), Larceny 4 (escape artist)
ATTACKS: Tentacles — can engage up to 4 targets simultaneously (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +2 bashing per tentacle; grappled targets take Enhancement +3 bashing automatically each round)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Amphibious Master] — Cannot be drowned. Ink cloud (5m sphere): all in area [Blinded] for 1 round. Once per scene.
[Distributed Intelligence] — Can process two separate problems simultaneously with different parts of his nervous system. Can hold two conversations at once with no penalty.
[Hyponome Voice] — Specialized organ enables perfect mimicry of human speech. Uses a sophisticated British accent by default.
NOTE: Ulysses is a person who is also an octopus. He is unfailingly polite, genuinely interested in others, and deeply thoughtful. He is also confined to a laboratory, and that will eventually become a problem for everyone involved.
```

---

## Section Twenty: Deep Umbra and Alien Entities

Entities in this section originate beyond the Horizon or from pre-Tellurian sources. Their physiology and existence follow rules that Sphere magic does not always affect cleanly. All stat blocks use SPU format. "Casting Roll" notation applies if the entity has magical ability.

```
Raamas Ka — Nephandic Horror (Deep Umbra)
-----------------------------------------
Might 8 | Dexterity 3 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 4 | Intellect 3 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 0 | Composure 0 | Resolve 7
Spirit Essence: 80 | Health (Materialized): 12 | Armor: 4 | Initiative: 7
Skills: Close Combat 5, Awareness 4
ATTACK: Tendril Dissolution (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +5, lethal, aggravated; targets struck roll Stamina + Resolve vs. Difficulty 4 or lose 1 dot of a random Attribute permanently)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Nephandic Bonded] — Only effectively harmed by Prime-based attacks, aggravated damage, or weapons prepared with Prime 2. Ordinary physical attacks deal half damage.
[Paradox Source] — Generates 1 Paradox die per round simply by being present in the material world.
[Soul Hunger] — Targets at 0 Health from Raamas Ka attacks have their soul consumed, not departed normally. Recovery requires Spirit 5 within 24 hours or destroying the Raamas Ka.
BANISHMENT: Spirit 5 ceremony, three mages, 10 combined hits. OR Prime 4 ward for 24 hours — it dissipates without feeding.
```

```
C. Malefica — Watcher Alien (Three Forms)
-----------------------------------------
WARRIOR: Might 5 | Dex 4 | Stamina 4 | Cunning 2 | Intellect 2 | Presence 0 | Manipulation 1 | Health 8 | Armor 2 | Initiative 6
  Skills: Close Combat 5, Ranged Combat 3, Athletics 4, Awareness 2, Larceny 3
  Attacks: Tentacle Strike (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, lethal); Acid Spray (Cunning + Enhancement +2, lethal, range 10m, bypasses Armor)

SPY: Might 1 | Dex 4 | Stamina 1 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 5 | Manipulation 5 | Health 3 | Armor 0 | Initiative 9
  Skills: Larceny 6, Deception 5, Investigation 4, Awareness 5, Technology 3
  Can compress to pass through any gap larger than 5cm. Transmits everything perceived to the Watcher collective instantly; no magical interception below Correspondence 4 + Prime 3.

BEHEMOTH: Might 12 | Dex 2 | Stamina 10 | Cunning 3 | Health 18 | Armor 5 | Initiative 5
  Attacks: Tentacle Crush (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +8, lethal; grappled = Enhancement +6 lethal/round); Body Slam (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +7, bashing, hits all within 5m)
  [Unstoppable Mass] — Cannot be knocked back/prone by any physical effect below Might 12.
  [Acid Blood] — Successful melee attacks against the Behemoth deal Enhancement +2 lethal to the attacker from its acid blood.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS (all forms): [Non-Terrestrial Physiology] — Immune to all effects specifically targeting biological or human anatomical systems.
[Hive Coordination] — 3+ Warriors targeting the same entity: each gains Enhancement +1 and shares sensory data instantly.
```

---

## Section Twenty-One: Dream Realm Entities

Dream entities interact with sleeping mortals without crossing the Gauntlet. They are not bound by the same rules as material spirits. All stat blocks use SPU format. Dream Health represents viability within the Maya (Dream Realms); Materialized Health applies if the entity manifests physically.

```
Amore Muse — Dream Spirit of Love
---------------------------------
Spirit Essence: 20 | Resolve: 5 | Cunning: 4 | Presence: 5
Dream Health: 5 | Materialized Health: 3 (rare) | Initiative: 9
Skills: Empathy 5, Deception 3, Awareness 4, Athletics 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Dream Embodiment] — Appears as whatever the dreamer loves most. This is the spirit's actual form in their consciousness — not deception.
TRICK — Inspire [Major Action, costs 3 Essence]: Grants subject Enhancement +3 to any Expression or creative roll for 24 hours following the encounter.
TRICK — Reshape Dreams [Major Action]: Fundamentally alters the tone and content of the dream state, affecting all within the dreamscape.
```

```
Kid Fear — Dream Spirit of Childhood Dread
------------------------------------------
Spirit Essence: 10 + (10 x target's Expression or Cunning, whichever higher) | Resolve: 5
Dream Health: 4 | Cannot Materialize
Skills: Deception 5, Awareness 4, Empathy 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Personal Terror] — Appears as the dreamer's most potent childhood fear. Cannot be resisted by telling it the fear is not real — in the dream, it is real.
[Harmless Outside Dreams] — No effect on the waking world. Dissipates if the dreamer genuinely wakes.
[Willpower Drain] — Each successful "attack" drains 1 Resolve from the dreamer. At 0 Resolve in a dream, the dreamer cannot wake without external intervention.
```

```
Nemesis — Guilt Epiphling
-------------------------
Spirit Essence: 20 | Resolve: 10 | Cunning: 5 | Presence: 6
Dream Health: 8 | Materialized Health: 6 | Armor: 0 | Initiative: 9
Skills: Awareness 5, Empathy 6, Deception 2, Close Combat 3
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Nemesis Halo] — Appears in white light of uncomfortable intensity. All actions against a Nemesis take Difficulty +1 as the light forces self-examination.
[Guilt Damage] — Each hit deals non-lethal damage that recovers after the encounter. But each hit also imposes [Guilty] Complication — target rolls Resolve vs. Difficulty 2 or confesses something true in their next significant social interaction.
TRICK — Know the Sin [free, passive]: Automatically knows the target's worst deeds. No roll required. Costs 3 Essence to fully process and manifest accordingly.
TRICK — Redemption Path [scene]: Target confesses their worst deed to the Nemesis, accepts responsibility, and articulates genuine amends. All encounter damage heals instantly; the Nemesis departs.
NOTE: Do not fight it. It cannot be permanently defeated that way, and the fight makes the guilt worse.
```

```
Akai — Love Epiphling
---------------------
Spirit Essence: 30 | Resolve: 3 | Presence: 5
Dream Health: 5 | Materialized Health: 4 | Initiative: 7
Skills: Empathy 6, Awareness 4, Esoterica 4
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Love Incarnate] — Embodies love in any form. Appears as whatever the observer loves most sincerely. Not deception — the Akai's actual nature.
[Crossroads Guardian] — Appears most often at choice points. Its presence clarifies the choice; it does not determine it.
TRICK — Comfort the Grieving [passive]: Any being experiencing grief in the presence of an Akai recovers 1 Willpower per round of contact (up to normal maximum). Costs no Essence.
```

```
Typhon — Jaggling of Ialdabaoth
-------------------------------
Spirit Essence: 50 | Resolve: 8
Materialized: Might 8 | Dex 3 | Stamina 8 | Cunning 3 | Intellect 2 | Presence 4 | Health: 11 | Armor: 3 | Initiative: 6
ATTACKS: Venom Spray (Cunning + Enhancement +5, lethal, 2x10m cone — ongoing 2 lethal/round until neutralized by Life 2); Crush (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +6, bashing, area)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Fear Aura] — All living beings within 10m roll Resolve + Composure vs. Difficulty 3 each round or gain [Frightened] Complication.
[Ialdabaoth's Servant] — Sent to accomplish something specific. Encounters with Typhon are not random.
[Ancient Corruption] — Areas where Typhon manifests show Entropy resonance for weeks afterward; healing spells cast there take Difficulty +1.
BANISHMENT: Three coordinated mages with Spirit or Prime at rank 3+, combined 8 hits. Cannot be physically defeated — body re-forms in 3 days.
```

---

## Section Twenty-Two: Paradox Spirits — Expanded

These supplement Section Six with additional Paradox manifestations from M20 Gods and Monsters. The Erinyes (named Furies) appear when Paradox is entangled with moral violation through magic — not just physical-law breaking, but genuinely harmful acts enabled by the Awakened power.

```
Alecto — Fury of Unceasing Anger (Erinyes)
------------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 6 | Dex 6 | Stamina 6 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Presence 7 | Manipulation 3 | Composure 0 | Resolve 8
Spirit Essence: 80 | Health: 10 | Armor: 2 | Initiative: 11
ATTACK: Scourge of Rage (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +4, lethal, aggravated vs. targets who have shed innocent blood; target struck rolls Composure vs. Difficulty 4 or enters uncontrolled rage)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Personalized Punishment] — Appears specifically to punish magical violence against the innocent. Knows exactly what the target did. Not interested in context, excuses, or mitigating factors.
[Rage Mirror] — Attacking Alecto in anger reflects the attacker's own rage back: +1 damage they deal, +1 Paradox die generated per successful attack they make.
[Three Furies, One Purpose] — Alecto, Megaera, and Tisiphone are three aspects of the same Paradox response. Summoning one does not prevent the others from appearing. When all three manifest simultaneously, they share a dice pool and coordinate perfectly.
```

```
Megaera — Fury of Jealousy and Grudges (Erinyes)
------------------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 5 | Dex 5 | Stamina 5 | Cunning 6 | Intellect 5 | Presence 6 | Manipulation 5 | Composure 0 | Resolve 8
Spirit Essence: 80 | Health: 9 | Armor: 2 | Initiative: 11
ATTACK: Jealousy's Brand (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +3, lethal; target permanently marked — supernaturals can see the mark and know the target has been judged)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Grudge Keeper] — Tracks offenses across time. May not appear immediately — arrives when accumulated magic-enabled jealousy and resentment reaches a threshold.
[Social Destruction] — Megaera's presence causes the target's social network to erode. Allies become distant; trust degrades for no apparent reason. This is not additional magic — it is the consequence of the mage's actual character becoming visible.
```

```
Tisiphone — Fury of Vengeance (Erinyes)
---------------------------------------
Partial Manifestation: Might 7 | Dex 7 | Stamina 7 | Cunning 5 | Intellect 4 | Presence 7 | Manipulation 2 | Composure 0 | Resolve 8
Spirit Essence: 80 | Health: 11 | Armor: 3 | Initiative: 12
ATTACK: Vengeance Strike (Might + Close Combat + Enhancement +5, lethal, aggravated; deals damage equal to the most damage the target mage has ever dealt to a single target in a magical attack)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Mirror of the Deed] — Inflicts exactly what the mage has inflicted on others through magical violence. Mages who have never used magic harmfully are immune to her strikes.
[Cannot Be Outrun] — Perfect tracking. Can be banished (Spirit 5) but returns in 30 days unless the underlying debt is addressed.
RESOLUTION: Three days of genuine reflection (Resolve 8+ roll, Difficulty 3) plus one genuine act of restitution. She assesses sincerity herself.
```

```
Hex — Minor Paradox Spirit of Bad Luck
--------------------------------------
Spirit Essence: 20 | Resolve: 4 | Cunning: 4 | Manipulation: 4
Materialized Health: 3 | Initiative: 8
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Persistent Misfortune] — Attaches itself to a mage like a spiritual parasite. While attached: all Complication results that would be minor become major; all Enhancement dice that exceed pool size by 2+ fail instead of producing extra hits.
[Difficult to Notice] — Nearly invisible. Detecting it requires Spirit 2 specifically seeking attached spirits, or Awareness + Esoterica vs. Difficulty 3.
REMOVAL: Entropy 3 (unweave the attachment) or Spirit 3 (banish the spirit). Cannot return for one month after removal.
NOTE: Hex is the Paradox entity mages notice first and misidentify longest. The streak of bad luck is understood as coincidence long before it is recognized as a spirit.
```

```
Whisper — Paradox Spirit of Unwanted Truth
------------------------------------------
Spirit Essence: 15 | Resolve: 4 | Cunning: 5 | Manipulation: 5 | Presence: 3
Materialized Health: 2 | Initiative: 10
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
TAGS: [Compelled Revelation] — When Whisper is present, the attached mage compulsively says true things at socially inopportune moments. Operates as a Complication on social scenes: any social roll that produces a Complication causes the mage to say something true they would not have chosen to reveal.
[Eavesdropper] — Has been listening since it attached. Knows everything the mage has said aloud since then. May repeat these things in maximally uncomfortable contexts.
[Silent Presence] — Cannot be heard or perceived by anyone except its target and mages using Spirit 2.
NOTE: Particularly relevant to mages who use Mind Sphere for social control — Whisper reflects the Paradox of using magic to manage what is fundamentally a domain of authentic human connection.
```

# Appendix E: Chantries — Sanctums of the Awakened

Every mage needs a place where the rules of consensus bend a little, where instruments can be left out, where rituals can run for six hours without someone calling the police, where the Paradox hangover can be slept off in peace. A Chantry is that place — and much more.

In the most basic sense, a Chantry is a physical location that a mage or cabal has claimed, warded, and attuned as their magical home base. But a well-developed Chantry is also a laboratory, a library, a fortress, a spirit-stable environment, a Node-tap, a safe harbor in a hostile world. The Traditions hold their most elaborate Chantries to be among their most precious resources — more valuable, in many cases, than individual mages.

This chapter covers: what Chantries are and how they function, establishing and maintaining a Chantry, Chantry Traits (mechanical ratings), Chantry Merits and specific benefits, Chantry defenses both physical and magical, the Chantry as a character (the way the place develops over time), and worked examples of Chantries at different scales and power levels.

---

```
OPTIONAL RULE — Chantries (This Appendix)
-----------------------------------------
Chantries are an optional, modular subsystem. A chronicle can run indefinitely without establishing a Chantry — mages meet in safe houses, use temporary Nodes, and operate from mundane locations without losing access to any core rules. Chantries become relevant when the chronicle focuses on the characters building something permanent: a home in the World of Mage, a political statement within their Tradition, or a defensible base of operations. The full subsystem includes Chantry Traits, Node management, Ward construction, political relationships with the broader Tradition structure, and long-term management mechanics. Introduce it when the story calls for it — or not at all.
```

## What Is a Chantry?

A Chantry is not simply a house that mages live in. The distinction is important because the Storyguide needs to know when a Chantry's mechanical benefits apply. A Chantry is a location that has been:

- Deliberately chosen for magical suitability — the land resonates with the kind of magic the owners practice, or can be made to do so.
- Warded against intrusion — both mundane (locks, alarms, concealment) and magical (wards, spirit-bindings, Gauntlet alterations).
- Attuned to its owners — the mages who hold it have spent time working magic within it, leaving resonance, and have established a formal or informal claim.
- Maintained — Chantries require ongoing attention. A Chantry abandoned for months begins to lose its properties; one that has been actively maintained for years becomes progressively more powerful.
A Chantry can be any physical structure or space: a brownstone apartment, an academic office suite, a lighthouse on a rocky coast, a converted warehouse, a mansion, a cave system beneath a city, a ship, a space aboard a dimensional pocket realm, or — for the most ambitious — an entire Horizon Realm. Scale matters for some purposes (a cave Chantry has different wards than a mansion) but the core mechanics apply regardless.

The Technocracy uses the term Construct for their equivalent of a Chantry. The mechanics are identical; the aesthetics are not. A Technocratic Construct looks like a corporate office, a research laboratory, a government facility, or a military installation. A Tradition Chantry might look like any of these things too — good camouflage — or it might look exactly like what it is.

---

## Establishing a Chantry

Establishing a Chantry is a significant undertaking — not just acquiring a space but transforming it. This typically happens between story arcs rather than during active play, though the story of establishing a Chantry can itself be excellent chronicle material.

### Requirements

To establish a basic Chantry, a mage or cabal needs:

- A physical location they control (owned, leased, or otherwise held with a reasonable expectation of continued access).
- At least one mage with Prime 2 (to attune the location and anchor the wards).
- Time: a minimum of one month of active work for a basic establishment, longer for more complex versions.
- Resources: the cost depends on the Chantry's scale. A small apartment Chantry might require Resources 2 and the Allies of a sympathetic landlord. A mansion requires Resources 4+ or Chantry Background points.

### The Attunement Ritual

At the core of Chantry establishment is the Attunement Ritual: an extended magical working that establishes the location as a recognized sanctum in the local spirit ecology and in the Umbral reflection of the physical site. This requires Prime 2 and Spirit 1 at minimum, an extended roll (Presence + Occult, Difficulty 2, minimum 5 hits accumulated over multiple sessions), and at minimum three mages (or one mage performing the ritual over three separate nights at specific times).

A successful Attunement gives the Chantry its initial Sanctum Rating of 1 and establishes the mages who participated as its recognized holders. The Umbral reflection of the location begins to shift, slowly aligning with the holders' magical paradigm. This process continues over months and years — a decade-old Chantry feels profoundly different from a newly established one.

### Chantry Size and Footprint

The physical size of a Chantry affects what can be done within it and what it costs to maintain. Larger Chantries offer more workspace, more ward coverage, and more room to house allies and resources — but they require more Resources and more mages to maintain properly.

| Size | Description \| Maintenance Cost \| Notes |
| --- | --- |
| Tiny (••) | Single room or apartment; 1-3 mages. Resources 1 or shared cost between members. |
| Small (•••) | House or small commercial space; 3-5 mages. Resources 2. |
| Medium (••••) | Large home, warehouse, small campus; 5-10 mages. Resources 3. |
| Large (•••••) | Mansion, monastery, significant building; 10-20 mages. Resources 4. |
| Vast (••••• +) | Campus, compound, dimensional realm; 20+ mages. Resources 5+. |

---

## Chantry Traits

A Chantry has five core Traits that define its capabilities. Each Trait runs from 1 to 5. At establishment, the Chantry begins with one or two dots in the Traits most relevant to its location and the Attunement ritual. Additional dots are earned through sustained investment — of time, magic, Resources, and narrative.

Storyguide Note: Chantry Traits are not player character stats. They are collectively held by the cabal, represent the location's properties, and can be improved through in-fiction activity rather than experience point expenditure. Improving a Chantry Trait should require a story — a magical working, an alliance sealed, a discovery made.

### Sanctum Rating (1–5)

The Sanctum Rating represents how thoroughly the Chantry is attuned to its holders and how deeply the magical properties of the location have developed. It is the baseline from which most other Chantry benefits derive.

| Sanctum | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| • (1) | Basic attunement. The location feels right to magical senses. Coincidental magic performed here generates 1 fewer Paradox die (minimum 0). |
| •• (2) | Established sanctum. +1 die to all magical workings performed here. Paradox reduction increased to 2 dice for Coincidental, 1 die for Vulgar. |
| ••• (3) | Developed sanctum. +2 dice to magical workings here. The Gauntlet within the Chantry is 1 lower (easier spirit communication). Paradox reduction: 2 dice for Vulgar. |
| •••• (4) | Powerful sanctum. +3 dice to magical workings. Gauntlet -2. Paradox reduction: 3 dice for any working. Minor magical effects occur spontaneously in harmony with holders' paradigm. |
| ••••• (5) | Legendary sanctum. +4 dice to magical workings. Gauntlet -3 (near Horizon-level access). Paradox reduction: 4 dice for any working. The Chantry has developed a personality and responds to threats with spontaneous magical effects. |

The Paradox reduction from Sanctum Rating represents the location's resonance absorbing some of the Consensus backlash — the Chantry's established magical signature makes workings within it feel "right" to the local fabric of reality. This reduction does not apply to workings that directly harm residents of the Chantry or violate its established resonance.

### Warding (1–5)

Warding represents the Chantry's magical and mundane defenses against intrusion. Every Chantry has at least Warding 1 once established; additional Warding requires deliberate investment in defensive workings, spirit bindings, and physical security.

| Warding | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| • (1) | Basic wards. Unauthorized entry triggers an alarm that the Chantry holders perceive as a mental impression (unease, a sound, a specific sensation tied to their paradigm). |
| •• (2) | Intermediate wards. Unauthorized magical entry is detected and reported with detail (who or what, their approximate magical strength). Physical security is significant (professional locks, cameras, concealment of supernatural elements). |
| ••• (3) | Strong wards. Magical intrusion attempts trigger active resistance — the intruding mage's casting Difficulty increases by 2 within the warded space. Scrying into the Chantry from outside is at +3 Difficulty. |
| •••• (4) | Formidable wards. Unauthorized entry by any supernatural entity requires overcoming a Spirit 3 binding. Intruding magic automatically alerts all holders within range. The Chantry is effectively invisible to mundane surveillance technology. |
| ••••• (5) | Legendary wards. The Chantry is a pocket of altered reality. Hostile magic within the Chantry is at +4 Difficulty. Any magical attack on the Chantry structure itself triggers a Paradox-like backlash on the attacker. |

### Library (1–5)

The Library Trait represents accumulated knowledge — magical texts, research materials, rote collections, historical records, and reference materials relevant to the cabal's areas of expertise. A high Library Trait makes the Chantry a research resource as valuable as any individual mage.

| Library | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| • (1) | A basic collection. Provides +1 die to Research rolls on topics related to the holders' Traditions. |
| •• (2) | A solid reference library. +2 dice to Research. Contains at least one Rote for each of the holders' primary Spheres. Scholars can spend time studying here to learn new Rotes (see below). |
| ••• (3) | Scholarly resource. +3 dice to Research on any magical topic. Contains rare or unique texts unavailable elsewhere. Other mages may seek access to trade for it. |
| •••• (4) | Major arcane repository. +4 dice. Contains texts from multiple Traditions and paradigms. The Storyguide may award characters a relevant Lore dot after sustained study here. |
| ••••• (5) | World-class collection. +5 dice. Texts that don't exist elsewhere in the known world. Any magical question can be researched here given time; the real limit is the researcher's skill. |

Learning Rotes from the Library: A mage who spends a month studying a relevant text in a Library 2+ Chantry may learn a new Rote at their current Sphere competence level without teaching from another mage. Extended roll: Intellect + Occult, Difficulty 2, minimum 5 hits. This represents slow, independent study rather than direct instruction.

### Node Access (0–5)

A Node is a natural confluence of Primal Energy — a place where Quintessence wells up from deep within the fabric of reality. A Chantry built on or near a Node gains access to a renewable source of Primal Energy that can power sustained magical effects, recharge Wonders and Matrices, and fuel the Chantry's own magical properties.

Not every Chantry has a Node; many Chantries are built in locations chosen specifically because a Node exists nearby. A Chantry without Node Access can still function at full Trait levels — it simply lacks the Primal Energy supply.

| Node Access | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | No Node. Primal Energy must be brought in externally. |
| • (1) | Weak Node. Generates 3 Primal Energy per week, distributable among holders. |
| •• (2) | Minor Node. Generates 7 Primal Energy per week. Tass forms naturally (usable as Primal Energy in solid form). |
| ••• (3) | Moderate Node. Generates 15 Primal Energy per week. Multiple Tass objects form monthly. The Node's resonance flavor influences the ambient magic of the Chantry. |
| •••• (4) | Significant Node. Generates 25 Primal Energy per week. Tass forms weekly. The Node has its own spirit guardian(s) who must be maintained in good relationship. |
| ••••• (5) | Major Node. Generates 40+ Primal Energy per week. Multiple guardian spirits. The Node is cosmologically significant — its existence is known to multiple factions. |

Node Resonance: Every Node has a particular flavor — a resonance tied to its history, location, and what magical activity has occurred there. A Node beneath a cathedral tastes of devotion and sacrifice. A Node in an ancient forest tastes of growth and decay. A Node in a university physics department might taste of curiosity and precision. This resonance affects the nature of Tass produced and may provide Enhancement or hindrance to specific types of magic performed at the Node.

### Allies (1–5)

The Allies Trait represents people, spirits, and organizations associated with the Chantry who provide ongoing support, information, or resources. This is different from individual character Allies — Chantry Allies are institutional relationships that persist regardless of which individual mages currently hold the Chantry.

| Allies | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| • (1) | A handful of sympathetic Sleepers (a friendly landlord, a local beat cop who "doesn't see things," a neighborhood contact). |
| •• (2) | A small support network. Local informants, a sympathetic doctor, a spirit-aware local shopkeeper. Provides +2 dice to Contacts rolls within the local area. |
| ••• (3) | Regional relationships. Connections to local criminal organizations, local government sympathizers, allied Disparate or independent mages in the area. Provides information about regional events automatically. |
| •••• (4) | Significant institutional connections. A local police precinct commander, a hospital administrator, an allied Tradition cabal who can provide emergency backup. |
| ••••• (5) | Major alliances. The Chantry is recognized by multiple Tradition cabals and has formal treaties. Emergency aid from allied groups can be summoned within hours. |

---

## Chantry Merits

Chantry Merits are specific advantages that a Chantry has developed beyond its basic Traits. They represent particular features, histories, or magical properties that provide unique benefits. Chantry Merits are earned through play, not purchased at character creation — they are the result of specific events, investments, or discoveries.

Each Merit has a cost in Chantry points (abstract resources representing time, money, magical effort, and narrative investment). Storyguides should award these organically: when the cabal does something that would reasonably develop the Chantry in a specific direction, the Merit appears.

### Architectural Merits

```
Hidden Spaces (•• Chantry Merit)
--------------------------------
The Chantry contains deliberate hidden spaces: false walls, concealed rooms, passages that don't show on any floor plan. These might be mundane construction or might be maintained by a standing Matter 2 effect.

Benefit: Characters who know the Chantry can hide themselves, objects, or activities even from thorough mundane search. Magical searches require Correspondence 3+ to find the hidden spaces, and the Difficulty is +2 (the concealment is woven into the Chantry's Ward structure). Hidden spaces can store Wonders, house allies in emergency, or contain sensitive research materials. Each hidden space holds approximately one person or a trunk's worth of materials.

How Earned: Design the Chantry's hidden spaces explicitly in the fiction, either during establishment or as a renovation. The Storyguide may require a Materials + Craft extended roll for mundane construction, or a Matter 2 working for magically maintained concealment.
```

```
Dedicated Workspace (•• Chantry Merit)
--------------------------------------
A specific room or area of the Chantry is set up as a dedicated workshop optimized for a particular type of magical work: a laboratory for Matter/Life research, a spirit-circle for Spirit work, a meditation chamber for Mind/Time, an alchemical kitchen, a smithy, a darkroom.

Benefit: Work performed in the Dedicated Workspace gains +1 die beyond the Chantry's normal Sanctum bonus for the specific Sphere or practice the workspace supports. Extended magical workings (Wonder creation, Rote development, high-Sphere rituals) done here are at -1 Difficulty.

How Earned: Invest significant Resources (minimum 2 dots) in outfitting the space, and use it for at least three significant magical workings in play.
```

```
Ritual Space (••• Chantry Merit)
--------------------------------
The Chantry contains a purpose-built ritual space: a stone circle, a prepared temple, a prayer hall, a machine-ritual chamber. This space is architecturally and magically optimized for group workings.

Benefit: Group rituals (involving three or more mages) performed in the Ritual Space gain +1 die per participating mage beyond the first (maximum +4 additional dice). The Ritual Space can also be used as a "battery" for stored group working hits: the cabal may perform a ritual in advance and store up to 5 hits, which can be released as a one-round burst effect when needed.

How Earned: Deliberately construct and dedicate the space through an extended ritual (minimum 8 hits over multiple sessions). The dedication ritual defines the space's magical character and the paradigm it supports.
```

```
Umbral Anchoring (••• Chantry Merit)
------------------------------------
The Chantry has a stable, fixed reflection in the Umbra — an Umbral Chantry that mirrors the physical structure in a way that most locations do not. The Umbral reflection is a distinct space that can be entered, modified, and defended separately from the physical Chantry.

Benefit: Holders of the Chantry can enter the Umbra via Stepping Sideways without a roll within the Chantry's walls (the Gauntlet within is thin enough that it's automatic for any mage with Spirit 1+). The Umbral reflection can be fortified independently — spirits can be stationed there, Umbral defenses built, and spirit allies maintained in residence. Additionally, anyone approaching the physical Chantry with hostile intent is visible to the Umbral watchers one round before they arrive.

How Earned: Establish a formal compact with the Chantry's dominant local spirits (Spirit 3 working, multiple sessions of negotiation and offering).
```

```
Fortified Structure (•• Chantry Merit)
--------------------------------------
The Chantry's physical structure has been reinforced beyond standard building specifications: reinforced concrete walls, blast-resistant windows, safe rooms, reinforced doors with heavy-duty locks.

Benefit: The Chantry's walls have Structure 8, Armor 4 instead of standard building values. Safe rooms within have Structure 10, Armor 5. The fortification is not obvious from the outside — it has been designed to appear normal while providing significantly enhanced protection.

How Earned: Spend Resources 3+ on structural reinforcement over a period of months. Requires either mundane contractors (with appropriate memory management afterward) or Matter 3 magical reinforcement over several sessions.
```

```
Defensible Position (• Chantry Merit)
-------------------------------------
