They do not claim to be the oldest magical tradition (the Dreamspeakers would contest that, and the Euthanatos have been around longer than memory) but they do claim to be the most honest about what magic actually is: a living relationship with the sacred power that runs through all biological existence.

The Verbena work blood and bone, seed and season, death and birth. Their magic is not polite. It involves knives and fire and blood freely given, the turning of the year in its full cycle including its dark and cold halves, and an unflinching acknowledgment that life and death are not opposites but partners in the same dance. This makes them difficult allies for Traditions that prefer cleaner aesthetics — and deeply trustworthy ones for Traditions that value honesty over comfort.

### Wonders

```
Candle of Communion (• Wonder)
------------------------------
Background Cost: 2 | Primal Pool: 5-15 | Enlightenment: 1-3

A glass-bound candle hand-painted by the Verbena practitioner, featuring a specific saint, holy woman, or sacred feminine figure appropriate to the witch's personal tradition — Mary, Mary Magdalene, Shekinah, or any number of blessed women of various faiths. Jewish Verbena create a candle symbolic of Shekinah; Muslim practitioners might honor blessed women of Islam. The symbols and sigils incorporated into the design carry personal meaning.

SPU Effect: When lit and accompanied by a standard Meditation roll (Composure + Resolve, Difficulty 1), a successful activation causes the Shroud around the witch to drop by an amount equal to the hits scored, to a minimum of 0. While the candle burns, the witch can see, hear, and speak with any ghosts in the immediate area, as well as sense other spirit presences within earshot. She may end the effect by extinguishing the candle. On a Complication (1 on the Paradox Dice when activated), the Shroud falls but unfriendly spirits are attracted to the opening.
```

```
Mama Cybele's Tea Collection (•• Wonder)
----------------------------------------
Background Cost: 4 | Primal Pool: 10-25 | Enlightenment: 2-5

A large spice rack of carefully labeled ceramic jars, each containing a different tisane blend. The labels name the blend and include sigils that are meaningless to anyone outside Mama Cybele's tradition. When steeped in just-below-boiling water according to the correct procedure, each blend activates its enchanted properties.

SPU Effect: The tea witch selects a jar and brews. The brew must be ingested for full effect (though throwing hot tea at an opponent as a desperation tactic applies only 1 hit of effect, not the full working). The effect depends on the jar selected:
- A Life jar applies a Life 3 healing effect: 3 hits = 3 boxes of healing, distributed across the track.
- An Entropy jar applies an Entropy 3 curse effect, adding +1 Difficulty to all of the drinker's rolls for a number of scenes equal to hits.
Multiple teas from the same collection cannot be stacked in the same scene.
```

```
Grand Book of Shadows (•••• Wonder)
-----------------------------------
Background Cost: 8 | Primal Pool: 10-25 | Enlightenment: 4-8

A lovingly maintained, leather-bound grimoire that serves as the living record of a coven's history, accumulated wisdom, and magical practice. Unlike most Wonders, ownership can be shared between the two highest-ranking coven members if the coven recognizes co-equal leaders. The Book has seen constant use since its creation and carries the accumulated intention of every spell and ritual recorded within.

SPU Effect: Multiple benefits apply to those who study the Book:
- All Intellect + Occult or Intellect + Academics research rolls related to occult subjects are at -1 Difficulty.
- If the research subject concerns the coven's historical allies, enemies, or territory, an additional -1 Difficulty applies.
- A coven member who spends one scene studying the Book gains a free specialty in a relevant Lore for the remainder of the story.
- All attempts to place wards on the coven's sacred spaces are at -2 Difficulty.
- The owners may use the Book as a Paradox buffer: when accumulating Paradox Dice, they may spend 2 Primal Energy from the Book to reduce the incoming Paradox by 1 die.
```

## The Virtual Adepts

The Virtual Adepts are the youngest Tradition on the Council — former Technocrats who defected in the early 1960s over the question of information freedom. Their founding position was simple and radical: information wants to be free, reality is information, and therefore the most profound magical act is to ensure that information flows unimpeded. What started as computer-oriented hacktivism has evolved into one of the most innovative magical traditions in the world, simultaneously pushing the boundaries of what "magic" can look like and what "reality" can be encoded to mean.

The Virtual Adepts of the 2020s are not a monolithic group. Old Guard hackers share space with NFT-mages encoding Tass as blockchain tokens, biotech pioneers blending Data and Life, digital-social activists using Mind and Correspondence to reshape consensus online, and deep-space data-archaeologists mapping the edges of the Digital Web. They argue constantly about methodology, ethics, and the proper relationship between the Virtual Adepts and both the Technocracy they left and the Council they've partly chosen.

### Rotes

#### Save State (Mind 3 / Time 2 / Prime 2)

Modern magical workings — particularly those involving digital systems — can require days or weeks of careful work. Any interruption risks catastrophic failure, causing accumulated hits to unravel. The Save State rote records the mage's exact magical configuration, the state of their working, and their internal alignment at a specific moment, encoding it as a file or set of notes that preserves their progress.

Mechanics: The mage rolls Wits + Technology at Difficulty 2. On success, the current state of any extended magical action is preserved. If the mage must interrupt the working, they may return to the exact state when they resume — the +1 Difficulty penalty normally imposed by interrupting an Extended Action is negated. On a Complication, the save state is imperfect: the mage loses their last session's hits but does not lose all progress.

#### NFTs — Non-Fungible Tass/Talismans (Mind 3 / Prime 2 + optional Time 4 / Entropy 3 + optional Data 2)

The Virtual Adepts developed this rote in response to a Council request for a way to track magical items — Infernalist texts, Tass from sensitive sources, contested artifacts — with a chain of ownership that could be verified by any mage. The solution is a low-level magical tag that announces the item's identity, provenance, and ownership history to any mage who focuses attention on it.

Mechanics: The base working (Mind 3 / Prime 2) requires 6+ hits to establish a permanent tag on an item. The tag is subtle enough that Sleepers feel "vibes" from the item but cannot consciously identify it. Any mage may investigate the tag by focusing on the object and rolling Intellect + Occult, Difficulty 0, receiving its description and ownership chain on a single hit.

The Time 4 / Entropy 3 option: The tag is now armed. If someone attempts to remove or alter the tag, the item self-destructs (damage appropriate to the item's physical nature).

The Data 2 option: The tag automatically updates a ledger on the Digital Web whenever the item changes hands, creating a permanent ownership chain accessible via the Web.

### Wonders

```
Rod Logic Computer (•• Wonder)
------------------------------
Background Cost: 3 | Primal Pool: 5-10

The Virtual Adept version of this device is functionally similar to the Etherite version (see Sons of Ether above) but aesthetically distinct: where Etherite models favor brass fittings and Victorian-era design language, Adept models tend toward minimalist organic design, bioluminescent displays, and materials that read as either ultra-futuristic or weirdly natural depending on the observer's paradigm.

SPU Effect: As the Sons of Ether entry above, but additionally grants +1 die to Mind and Data Sphere workings when used as a Focus, reflecting the Adept's integration of digital cognition with organic process. The computer's programs include several pre-loaded Adept rote frameworks.
```

---

The above entries cover the Nine Traditions' distinctive mechanical content as derived from Lore of the Traditions and converted to SPU. For their social relationships, political positions, and narrative roles in the Ascension War, see Chapter 8: Factions and the Council of Nine. For inter-Tradition rote-sharing rules, apply the standard Rote Learning rules from Chapter 4 — most Traditions freely share rotes with allied mages after a period of appropriate study.

# Appendix G: The Technocratic Union — Deep Operations

This appendix provides Storyguides with a comprehensive reference for the Technocratic Union as an institution, its five Conventions, their distinctive Procedures (the Technocracy's term for what the Traditions call rotes), their Hypertech Gear, and the philosophical and operational frameworks that make the Union one of the most formidable forces in the World of Darkness. Content here is derived from Technocracy Reloaded and converted to Storypath Ultra mechanics throughout.

The Technocracy is not simply the antagonist faction in a Mage chronicle. It is a complex institution staffed by human beings — many of them genuinely well-intentioned — who are pursuing a vision of humanity's future that differs from the Traditions' vision in ways that are sometimes reasonable and sometimes monstrous. The best Technocratic characters and antagonists embody this complexity.

## The Union: Philosophy and Structure

The Technocratic Union believes that reason, science, and systematic methodology will ultimately deliver humanity from the superstition, irrationality, and magical chaos that plagued earlier centuries. Where the Traditions see the magical world as a rich ecosystem to be preserved, the Technocracy sees uncontrolled magic as a threat to the stable consensus reality that allows ordinary human beings to live safe, predictable lives. They are, in their own view, the people who keep the lights on.

This does not make them good. The Union employs manipulation, Social Conditioning, institutional violence, suppression of inconvenient knowledge, and occasionally outright murder in service of their goals. Individual Technocrats run the full moral spectrum from idealistic true believers to cynical careerists to genuine monsters. The Technocracy's corruption is not a secret scandal — it is endemic to any institution that believes its goals justify its methods.

### Terranorming

Technocracy Reloaded introduces the concept of Terranorming: the systematic reshaping of reality-consensus in a given geographic area or cultural space to make it more hospitable to Technocratic paradigms and less hospitable to magical thinking. Terranorming is not simply propaganda — it is a magical working at scale, using a combination of Procedures, media manipulation, economic incentives, and social psychology to shift what the Masses believe is possible.

In SPU terms, a successful terranorming operation in an area adds 1 to the local Consensus Difficulty for any working that violates the established Technocratic paradigm in that space. A magically active location where the Technocracy has been terranorming for years might have an effective Consensus Difficulty of 4 or 5 for blatantly supernatural effects — not because the area is naturally hostile to magic, but because generations of managed consensus have made the local Masses deeply unreceptive.

Terranorming can be reversed, but it takes time and active effort. A Tradition cabal that moves into a terranormed area and begins performing covert magic, telling stories, and subtly introducing alternative frameworks to the local population can gradually reduce the Consensus Difficulty — one story arc per Difficulty point reduced, assuming sustained effort.

### Enlightenment in the Union

Technocrats do not use the word "Arete." They experience the same fundamental force as Tradition mages — the expansion of consciousness that allows a practitioner to perform Procedures and manipulate Primal Energy — but they frame it as Enlightenment in its scientific sense: the achieving of rational clarity, the ability to see past superstition to the underlying mechanisms of reality. In SPU terms, Technocratic Enlightenment is mechanically identical to Arete — the same 1-5 scale, the same mechanical effects, the same Sphere access — Arete ●–●●●●● caps all Sphere ratings.

The five Conventions each have a distinct philosophical relationship to Enlightenment:

- Iteration X pursues Enlightenment through upgrading and perfecting the human-machine interface, achieving a state of mathematical and mechanical precision that transcends biological limitation.
- The Progenitors seek Enlightenment through the evolution of biological design — the human body is the most complex system in known space, and understanding it completely is the path to transcendence.
- The New World Order pursues Enlightenment through the advancement of unified and controlled consciousness — a mind that fully understands the architecture of human thought can reshape reality through pure will.
- The Syndicate seeks Enlightenment through the mastery of economic and social systems — true power is influence, and influence flows through understanding how value, desire, and fear shape behavior.
- The Void Engineers seek Enlightenment through curiosity and revelation — the universe is vast and largely unknown, and exploring its frontiers is its own form of transcendence.

### Procedures vs. Rotes

A Procedure is the Technocracy's term for what the Traditions call a Rote: a pre-planned, repeatable magical working encoded in a specific method. The mechanics are identical — a Procedure is a rote, a rote is a Procedure. The difference is aesthetic and ideological. A Procedure is expressed through a technological or scientific framework: the mage is not casting a spell, they are running an algorithm, administering a compound, engaging an apparatus, or executing a protocol.

The key mechanical difference in play is that Procedures typically use Technology, Science, Medicine, or Engineering skills where Tradition rotes use Occult or Esoterica. A Technocrat rolling to heal someone might use Intelligence + Medicine rather than Intelligence + Occult, while achieving the same Life Sphere effect. Both approaches are legitimate under SPU's skill-flexible magic system.

## Iteration X

Iteration X — the "Clockwork Convention" — is dedicated to the proposition that the universe is a machine, and that understanding it completely requires becoming, in some sense, a machine. Their operatives combine the precision of engineers with the obsessive perfectionism of mathematicians, working toward a vision of humanity freed from the unreliability of biological cognition through seamless integration with technology. HIT Marks, cyborgs, neural interfaces, and tactical AI are Iteration X contributions to the Union's arsenal.

The Convention is organized around Methodologies — specialized research and operational divisions that each focus on a specific aspect of the human-machine integration project:

- BioMechanics: The biological-mechanical interface specialists. They design the cyborg Enhancements and research how organic tissue can be improved, augmented, or replaced by mechanical systems without losing the qualities that make it biological.
- Macrotechnicians: A newer Methodology focused on large-scale engineering projects — infrastructure, weapons systems, and the massive machines that reshape geography and resource flows.
- Statisticians: The mathematical heart of Iteration X. Every operation generates data; the Statisticians process it. They run probability projections, model social systems, and predict Reality Deviant behavior. Their calculations provide Iteration X with something approaching genuine prescience — limited, fallible prescience, but prescience nonetheless.
- Time-Motion Managers: Quality control and process optimization specialists. They study every aspect of an operation and find the inefficiencies, eliminating wasted motion, wasted resources, wasted time.

### Paradigm and Practice

Iteration X operates from a paradigm that could be summarized as Precision is Power. The universe follows rules; those rules can be known; once known, they can be exploited. What most people call magic is merely applied science that hasn't been explained yet. An Iteration X operative who appears to do the impossible has simply understood a principle that others haven't discovered yet.

Practice: Hypertech and Weird Science, with significant incorporation of Cybernetics. Instruments include: sophisticated machinery, cybernetic components, tactical AI systems, probability engines, nanite arrays, mathematical formulae and algorithms, blueprints and schematics, timing devices, and the HIT Mark command protocols.

### Procedures

#### Statistical Probability Adjustment (Entropy 2 / Mind 1)

The Statisticians have determined that what appears to be "luck" is in fact a measurable distribution of probability outcomes. By calculating the precise state of all relevant variables and applying pressure to specific probability leverage points, an operative can shift outcomes toward desired states. This does not feel like magic from the outside — it looks like the person making their shot, the lock malfunctioning, the witness glancing away at the critical moment.

Mechanics: The operative rolls Intellect + Science against Difficulty 2. Each hit adds 1 to the result of one die in any roll made by themselves or an ally this scene — specifically, after rolling, the operative may "promote" up to (hits) dice results by 1 point each (a 7 becomes an 8, a 9 becomes a 10). This can cause additional hits but cannot cause a 10 to become an 11. Looks entirely coincidental from the outside.

> Example: Agent Martinez needs to defuse a bomb. She rolls Intellect + Science and gets 3 hits on the Statistical Probability Adjustment. Then she rolls her actual defuse attempt (Dexterity + Technology, 4 dice) and gets results of 3, 7, 7, 9. She promotes the 7s to 8s, turning 0 hits into 2 hits. The bomb defuses. Structurally identical to an Entropy effect but framed as expert calculation.

#### Neural Overload Discharge (Mind 3 / Forces 2)

Using a combination of directional electromagnetic pulse emitters and calculated resonance frequencies, the operative discharges a targeted burst of energy that disrupts neural function in the target. High-end implementations use cybernetic transmitters; field operatives without enhancements use purpose-built devices that are legally classified as "experimental medical equipment."

Mechanics: The operative rolls Dexterity + Technology against Defense. Each excess hit inflicts 1 die of Mental Damage (Stun, then Disoriented, then Incapacitated) rather than physical damage. Targets with cybernetic or electronic components suffer additional effects: they must succeed on a Composure roll or have their integrated tech disrupted for (hits) rounds. The signature of this Procedure registers as technology to any mage scanning for it — explicitly not mystical.

### Enhancements (Cyborg Templates)

```
DEI — Digital Enhancement Implant (●●● Enhancement)
---------------------------------------------------
Background Cost: 6 | Enlightenment Required: N/A

The standard cybernetic implant throughout Iteration X, nearly ubiquitous in the Convention. The DEI is a neural-digital interface that connects the operative's nervous system directly to digital networks, providing continuous connectivity, enhanced processing, and seamless access to Union databases.

SPU Effects:
- Online Access: The operative can access all mundane internet functions, perform research and hacking, without external equipment.
- Digital Web Projection: Via a Data 2 Adjustment, the operative can project sensorially or physically into the Digital Web without external hardware.
- ES-Phone and PPP Integration: All Iteration X standard apps, tactical databases, and encrypted communication protocols are accessible.
- Processing Assist: +1 die to all Research rolls as the DEI cross-references relevant data continuously.
```

#### Automated Repair Nanites (●●● Invention)

Once programmed with an object's molecular structure, automated repair nanites maintain structural integrity continuously, eliminating wear and damage at the molecular level. The nanites are nearly invisible — they emit a faint bassy hum and a white glow at repair seams when actively working. Equipment treated with Automated Repair nanites never degrades from normal use and heals damage at 1 Structure per hour.

Mechanics: Matter 2 / Prime 2 Procedure, Enlightenment required to activate. Programmed object gains Regenerating Tag: heals 1 Structure per hour. Cannot restore destroyed objects (Structure 0), only maintain and repair.

#### Cognitive Attenuation Transmitter — CAT (●●● Invention)

A thin black disc that adheres to any surface when thrown, developed by Void Engineer research into extradimensional entity suppression. The CAT emits a field that disrupts cognitive processes and short-term memory encoding for all beings within its radius — on both sides of the Gauntlet. It is considered overkill by most operatives, which does not stop the Void Engineers from recommending it enthusiastically.

Mechanics: Entropy 5 / Dimensional Science 4 Procedure. On activation, the CAT imposes -X dice to all rolls by all beings (including the activating operative) within 100 feet, where X equals the hits on the activation roll. Duration equals hits in rounds. Anyone in the field when it deactivates loses their memory of the last 10 minutes (Composure + Resolve, Difficulty 2 to resist). An Enlightened operative can use a personal counter-procedure to protect themselves (Difficulty 1, any Sphere).

## The Progenitors

The Progenitors are the Technocracy's biological division — geneticists, biochemists, neuroscientists, medical researchers, and evolutionary biologists who pursue a vision of humanity improved from within. Where Iteration X integrates technology into the body, the Progenitors redesign the body itself. They create clones, splice genes, develop pharmaceutical interventions, engineer synthetic organisms, and — in their most ambitious projects — attempt to design the next stage of human evolution.

Despite their alarming methods, many Progenitors are also the Technocracy's most effective healers, disease researchers, and humanitarian field workers. A Progenitor at a crisis site can do more for injured civilians than any emergency room — the question is always what they're learning while they do it, and what they'll do with that knowledge afterward.

### Methodologies

- FACADE: Forensic Analysts and Data Compilation for the Advancement of Developmental Engineering. The forensic and observational wing, responsible for collecting biological samples, analyzing DNA from unusual sources, and cataloguing new life forms encountered in the field. Also handles medical cover stories.
- Genegineers: The pure research arm. They design the genetic modifications, the novel organisms, and the experimental protocols. Their work is decades (sometimes centuries) ahead of what civilian science will acknowledge.
- Pharmacopeists: Drug designers and biochemical specialists. They develop the compounds that suppress Awareness in Sleepers, the performance-enhancers used by Technocratic field agents, and the treatments that manage the side effects of cybernetic integration and magical exposure.
- Damage Control: The Progenitors who clean up the messes other Conventions make. When an experimental organism escapes, when a gene therapy goes catastrophically wrong, when biological evidence of Technocratic activity needs to disappear — Damage Control handles it.

### Paradigm and Practice

Progenitors work from a Medicine Work paradigm supplemented by Hypertech and Weird Science when the biology gets unusual enough. Their instruments are laboratory equipment, genetic sequencers, pharmaceutical compounds, medical procedures, surgical tools, and the organisms they create or modify. Many Progenitors are also skilled at Invigoration — the art of improving performance through biochemical and biological means.

The Progenitors' relationship to their own bodies is often fraught. They know better than anyone how malleable biology is, which means they also know how much of what people call "personality" or "identity" is encoded in biochemistry. Some Progenitors find this liberating; others find it deeply unsettling.

### Sample Procedures

#### Targeted Pathogen Induction (Life 3 / Entropy 2)

The operative designs or activates a targeted biological agent that specifically affects beings with a particular biological signature — a Werewolf's unique metabolic markers, a Vampire's unusual cellular chemistry, or a mage whose resonance has left measurable traces in their physiology. Completely ineffective against beings without the target signature.

Mechanics: Requires prior biological sample from target type (Difficulty 2 to obtain without detection). Operative rolls Intellect + Medicine against Defense. Each excess hit inflicts 1 Aggravated damage as the pathogen disrupts specifically targeted biological systems. The pathogen is selective — it will not affect Sleepers or other non-targeted beings even if they handle the agent. Duration without treatment: (hits) scenes.

#### Rapid Phenotypic Adjustment (Life 3 / Prime 2)

A biochemically induced physical transformation — temporary but dramatic. The Progenitor administers a compound (or activates a pre-installed genetic switch in an Enhanced subject) that causes rapid physical change: altered pigmentation, modified musculature, changed facial structure, or even altered apparent age. The Pharmacopeists developed the original compound; FACADE refined it for field use.

Mechanics: Operative rolls Intellect + Medicine against Difficulty 2. Each hit provides one significant physical change that lasts for a number of scenes equal to hits. The subject remains biologically themselves — DNA testing will still identify them — but visual identification is compromised. With 4+ hits, the change is severe enough that even close friends must roll Wits + Investigation at Difficulty 3 to recognize the subject.

## The New World Order

The NWO is the Technocracy's control architecture: the Conventions' intelligence services, propaganda arm, social management specialists, and psychological warfare division, wrapped in an organization that officially manages "information flow" for the benefit of humanity. They are the Black Suits, the Men in Black, the conspiracy theorist's nightmare — because they are, to a very significant extent, exactly what the conspiracy theorists believe them to be, except that their motivations are more complex than simple domination.

The NWO genuinely believes that carefully managed information — which necessarily means some suppression of inconvenient truths — protects ordinary people from a reality that would break their minds. They have seen what happens when Sleepers are exposed to uncontrolled magic and supernatural horror without preparation. They have case files. Their solution — control the narrative, manage the Consensus, keep people safe inside a curated reality — has costs that they acknowledge only in private.

### Methodologies

- Ivory Tower: The academic and institutional control arm. They manage curricula, fund research that supports Technocratic paradigms, defund research that doesn't, and ensure that the mainstream scientific consensus reflects what the NWO needs it to reflect.
- Operative: Field agents. Social manipulation, identity management, surveillance, and targeted intervention. The classic Men in Black are Operative methodology agents.
- Watchers: Intelligence gathering. They monitor Reality Deviants, track Tradition activities, and maintain dossiers on everyone of significance in the magical world. Their true power is information — they often know what's going to happen before it does.

### Social Conditioning — The NWO's Signature Technique

Social Conditioning is the NWO's version of Mind magic, but it is not simply Mind control. It is a systematic, multi-session process that reshapes how a subject thinks, what they believe, and what they are capable of believing. It is far more robust than a single Mind effect — harder to detect, harder to reverse, and capable of producing changes that persist even after magical scrutiny.

Mechanics: Social Conditioning is an extended action requiring a minimum of 3 sessions (scenes) with the subject. Each session requires the NWO operative to roll Manipulation + Persuasion or Intellect + Psychology against the target's Resolve + Composure. Accumulated hits function as follows:

- 3-5 hits: Phase One — Softening the Subject. The target becomes more open to alternative framings of their experiences. They begin to doubt minor details of what they witnessed or believed.
- 6-9 hits: Phase Two — Reinforcing the Programming. The target has adopted the new framework as default. They rationalize away evidence that contradicts it. Reversing this stage requires both magical assistance (Mind 3+) and the subject's active cooperation.
- 10+ hits: Phase Three — Deep Integration. The target's worldview has been fundamentally restructured. They are no longer capable of entertaining certain categories of thought without significant psychological distress. This level of conditioning can only be reversed with Mind 4+ and weeks of careful work.
Social Conditioning adds to the Paradox Pool of any Tradition mage who attempts to undo it — not because the conditioning is itself magical, but because it has reshaped the subject's consensus reality, creating effective "anti-magic" in the form of genuine disbelief.

### Sample Procedures

#### Consensus Engineering — Memetic Seeding (Mind 2 / Correspondence 1)

The operative disseminates carefully constructed informational content through media channels — news items, social media posts, academic papers, word-of-mouth — that subtly reshapes how a target population understands a specific phenomenon. This is terranorming at the granular level: not dramatic reality-reshaping, but steady, invisible adjustment of what people believe they know.

Mechanics: Extended action, Intellect + Persuasion, Difficulty 2, targeting a neighborhood or community (Difficulty 3 for a city, 4 for a region). Each hit seeded shifts one "belief" in the target population — moving them slightly closer to or further from belief in a specific proposition (magic is real, this organization is trustworthy, that phenomenon has a mundane explanation). Accumulate 5+ hits over multiple sessions to shift the local Consensus Difficulty by 1 for one type of working.

#### Sleepteacher Protocol (Mind 4)

During sleep, the target's critical faculties are suspended and their memory consolidation systems are operating at maximum efficiency. An NWO operative with the right equipment can use this window to perform memory editing, belief reinforcement, or targeted information implantation that integrates seamlessly with the subject's sense of continuous experience.

Mechanics: Requires the target to be asleep and the operative to be either physically present or connected via a neural interface device. Roll Intellect + Medicine against the target's Resolve (passive defense, not active). On success, the operative may: add or remove specific memory details (up to 3 per hit); reinforce Social Conditioning already in place (counts as a Social Conditioning session with bonus equal to hits); or implant a specific belief or instruction that the target will regard as self-generated upon waking. Memory editing of this kind is undetectable without Mind 4+. Note: The Sleepteacher Device (see Technocracy Reloaded — Advanced Devices section below) is a physical apparatus that automates and extends this Procedure for Skill impartation.

## The Syndicate

The Syndicate is the Technocracy's economic and influence arm — the Convention that understands that money is power in the most literal sense, and that the most effective form of control is the kind that the controlled population enforces on itself because they believe in the value being offered. They are bankers, media executives, hedge fund managers, lobbyists, arms dealers, celebrity handlers, and economic theorists, united by an understanding that Primal Energy flows through desire as naturally as it flows through any other human activity.

The Syndicate coined the term "Market Correction" for what the Traditions call Paradox. This is not purely semantic — it reflects a genuine paradigmatic framework in which the rules of reality are economic rules, and violations of those rules produce the same kind of correction that markets produce when someone overextends: a snapping-back toward equilibrium that punishes the overreach.

### Methodologies

- Controllers: Economic manipulation specialists. They move markets, manage currency flows, and ensure that economic pressure can be applied to any actor the Syndicate needs to influence.
- Enforcers: The muscle. Unlike other Conventions' combat operatives, Syndicate Enforcers often operate in legal gray areas — private security, debt collection that goes further than most debt collectors would go.
- Media Control: The narrative managers. They do not own media directly — they own the people who own media. Content shaping, story suppression, controlled leaks, and strategic information releases.
- Disbursement: The financial infrastructure of the entire Union. Every other Convention is ultimately funded through Disbursement's management of the Syndicate's vast accumulated wealth.

### Primal Utility — The Art of Desire

The Syndicate practices a unique approach to Prime manipulation called Primal Utility — the channeling of Quintessence through economic desire, the creation and management of value that carries genuine magical weight. In game terms, Primal Utility Procedures use the Syndicate's understanding of how desire, status, and value function in human psychology as instruments for Prime effects.

Any object that has been invested with genuine economic and social value — an expensive wine, a rare artifact, a piece of currency from a collapsed civilization — can serve as Tass for a Syndicate operative in a way that similar objects would not for other mages. The Syndicate can also suppress the value of objects, "deflating" their Quintessence by making them associated with contempt, failure, or social ridicule.

### Sample Procedures

#### Market Position Assessment (Correspondence 2 / Mind 1)

The operative rapidly assesses the social, economic, and power relationships in a room full of people — who wants what, who has leverage over whom, which alliances are unstable, which individuals are most susceptible to which approaches. This is what social intelligence looks like when enhanced by Enlightened perception.

Mechanics: Roll Cunning + Subterfuge against Difficulty 1. Each hit provides one piece of actionable social intelligence about the scene: an unstable alliance, a hidden desire, a secret resentment, a source of leverage. With 4+ hits, the operative also identifies any magical effects currently active in the room and their approximate nature.

#### Value Assertion (Prime 2 / Mind 1)

The operative asserts a specific valuation for something — an object, a person's reputation, a business opportunity — in a way that carries magical weight. Sleepers who come into contact with the assessed item feel a strong intuitive sense of its value (high or low, as the operative intends). This is the Syndicate's primary tool for moving Tass items through normal channels.

Mechanics: Roll Presence + Persuasion against Difficulty 1. Each hit shifts the perceived value of the target one step up or down on a five-point scale (worthless to invaluable). Sleepers interacting with the item feel the asserted value as a gut instinct. Mages perceive the Prime manipulation on an Intellect + Occult roll against Difficulty 2. Duration: one scene per hit, or permanent for an item that has been formally priced and publicized through legitimate-seeming channels.

## The Void Engineers

The Void Engineers are the strangest Convention — a group of dimensional explorers, deep-space scientists, extradimensional military corps, and reality-frontier researchers who believe that the greatest threat to humanity comes not from within but from beyond. They are right. The horrors that lurk in the Deep Umbra are real, and the Void Engineers are humanity's early warning system and first response force against entities that most mages — Traditions and Technocracy alike — are not prepared to face.

This mission has made the Void Engineers the Convention most in tension with the rest of the Union. They have seen things that have changed them. They know that the Technocracy's neat model of a comprehensible universe is inadequate. They know that the entities they fight are not simply hostile technology or Reality Deviants with extra steps — some of them are genuinely incomprehensible, genuinely malevolent on a scale that dwarfs any human concern. This knowledge has given the Void Engineers a kind of pragmatic flexibility that their colleagues find uncomfortable.

### Methodologies

- Border Corps Division (BCD): First-response military corps handling Gauntlet incursions, Umbral breaches, and interdimensional entity containment. The "ghostbusters and space marines" of the Union. Heavily armed, extensively trained in both conventional and supernatural threat response.
- Earth Frontier Division (EFD): Terrestrial exploration and environmental monitoring. Has become the Union's primary climate crisis response team — their data on environmental changes is more complete and more alarming than anything civilian science has produced.
- Neutralization Specialist Corps (NSC): Threat assessment and targeted response. They identify specific supernatural entities, research their capabilities, and develop targeted methods for dealing with them — capture when possible, neutralization when necessary.
- Pan-Dimensional Corps (PDC): Deep exploration and extradimensional research. The most affected by the Dimensional Anomaly (if it occurred in your chronicle) — formerly the most expansive Methodology, now either rebuilding or thriving depending on the timeline you're using.
- Research and Execution (RAE): Applied dimensional science. They develop the technologies and Procedures that allow Void Engineers to do what they do — Voidcraft navigation systems, dimensional stabilization arrays, extradimensional communication networks.

### Threat Null

Threat Null is the Void Engineers' designation for something they have discovered that they dare not tell the rest of the Technocracy: a faction or force of extradimensional entities that appears to have adopted Technocratic methods and aesthetics, but whose goals are fundamentally destructive to human existence. They look like Technocrats. They use procedures similar to Technocratic procedures. And they appear to be working toward an outcome that would be catastrophic.

Storyguides can develop Threat Null as they see fit — the sourcebook is deliberately vague on its precise nature to allow different chronicle directions. What matters mechanically is that Threat Null entities are treated as Technocratic operatives for all purposes of disguise and infiltration, but their Paradox interactions are different: they do not accumulate Paradox in the normal way, and effects directed at them by Technocratic weapons may be resisted differently than expected.

### Sample Procedures

#### Dimensional Mapping Survey (Correspondence 3 / Dimensional Science 2)

The VE operative deploys a suite of dimensional sensors and conducts a systematic survey of the immediate Umbral or extradimensional environment, mapping its boundaries, identifying potential hazards, locating entities, and assessing structural stability. This is standard operating procedure for any BCD or PDC unit entering unfamiliar dimensional territory.

Mechanics: Roll Intellect + Science against Difficulty 2. Each hit provides one piece of accurate information about the dimensional space: its approximate extent, its current structural stability (on a 1-5 scale), the location of any significant entities or features, its resonance type, or whether it connects to other known dimensional spaces. With 4+ hits, the mage can also identify whether any other Awakened beings have visited the space within the last month.

#### Gauntlet Lock — Dimensional Seal (Dimensional Science 4 / Prime 3)

A temporary but powerful reinforcement of the Gauntlet in a specific location, preventing Umbral crossings in either direction without specialized equipment. Used to contain supernatural incursions, protect sensitive locations, or prevent fleeing entities from escaping into the Umbra.

Mechanics: Extended ritual, Intellect + Technology, Difficulty 3, minimum 6 hits. The sealed area cannot exceed one city block per 2 hits beyond minimum. Duration: 8 hours per hit over minimum. Beings already in the Umbra when the seal is placed cannot cross back through the Gauntlet — they are effectively quarantined. Beings on the material side cannot step sideways. Breaking the seal from either side requires either a Prime 4 / Dimensional Science 3 working or physical destruction of the anchor devices (Structure 6, Armor 3).

## Technocratic Gear: The Q Division Catalog

The following entries represent standard and notable items in the Technocratic arsenal. The Union organizes its gear into categories based on complexity and the level of Enlightenment required to operate them effectively.

### Gadgets

Gadgets are simple, mass-produceable Technocratic tools that function without Enlightenment. They represent the cutting edge of mundane science applied with expert precision.

```
Pulse Dampener
--------------
A wristband device that monitors and suppresses magical resonance from the wearer. Sleepers cannot detect the wearer as supernatural through magical means. Mages who scan the wearer with magical perception see a "blank" — no resonance at all, which is itself suspicious to trained observers.

SPU Effect: +2 Difficulty to any magical attempt to detect, track, or locate the wearer through supernatural means. Duration: continuous while worn.
```

```
Neurosuppressant Aerosol
------------------------
A pressurized chemical compound that suppresses magical working by flooding the target's system with compounds that interfere with the neurotransmitter cascades associated with Enlightened perception.

SPU Effect: Target must succeed on a Stamina + Resolve roll (Difficulty 2) or their Enlightenment counts as 1 lower for all magical working for (hits) scenes. Delivered as a spray, gas, or injected compound. Does not affect Willpower, only the Sphere access point. Multiple doses can stack, but the target makes a new resistance roll for each additional dose.
```

### Trinkets

Trinkets are more sophisticated than Gadgets but still do not require Enlightenment — they are highly advanced technology that produces effects that would seem impossible to civilian scientists.

```
SPECM — Standard Primal Energy Containment Matrix
-------------------------------------------------
The Union's standard Quintessence battery, appearing as a dense crystal module in a protective housing. Used to power Devices and Inventions in the field.

SPU Effect: Holds up to 10 Primal Energy points. Can be recharged at Nodes or by Enlightened operatives with Prime 2+. Cannot be used directly as a casting instrument — it is a power source, not a focus.
```

```
Null-Sig Armor Weave
--------------------
Armored clothing that provides ballistic and supernatural protection while appearing to be normal business wear, casual clothing, or any other mundane apparel.

SPU Effect: Armor 3 against physical damage. Additionally, reduces Paradox-based damage to the wearer by 1 die. Appears completely mundane to visual inspection; X-ray or scanning technology can detect the armor weave, but standard magical perception sees only cloth.
```

### Devices

Devices require an Enlightened operative to activate their primary functions, though some secondary functions may be accessible to ordinary technicians.

```
AEGIS Unit (Iteration X Construct)
----------------------------------
Attributes: Might 5, Dexterity 3, Stamina 5, Presence 2, Manipulation 3, Cunning 3, Intellect 3
Health: 7 (Stamina 5 + 2 base) | Armor: 3
Primary Attack: Integrated weapon system, 5 dice, range 30m, Damage 3
SPU Tags: [RESILIENT] — Ignores wound penalties on first hit; [WIRELESS COMMANDER] — Can control any wireless-accessible device within 100m; [NETWORKED] — shares sensory data with all other AEGIS units in range; [NON-BIOLOGICAL] — Immune to Life magic, poisons, disease; Psychic attacks require Mind 3+ to affect
Special: Brute Force Override — Roll Intellect + Technology (Difficulty 2) to assume direct control of any networked device within 100m, including smartphones, vehicle systems, and security networks.
```

### Primal Energy Extractor (●●●+ Matrix)

An advanced inductive charging system for SPECMs and other Matrices. An Enlightened operative with Prime 3 can use the Extractor to harvest Primal Energy from a Node, charged material, or sacrificed Quintessence source, and channel it into a Matrix without the normal limitations on rate of transfer.

Mechanics: Roll Intellect + Technology against Difficulty 1 at a Node. Each hit transfers 2 Primal Energy into an attached SPECM (maximum SPECM capacity still applies). Higher-rated versions (●●●● , ●●●●●) can harvest from more distant sources, charge multiple Matrices simultaneously, or extract from sources that would normally require direct contact.

## Terranorming in Play

When the player characters interact with areas that have been terranormed by the Technocracy, the following rules apply:

Lightly terranormed areas — cities with significant Technocratic presence and standard media saturation — add 1 to the Consensus Difficulty for effects that violate established scientific consensus. This is already factored into the standard difficulty assumptions in Chapter 4.

Heavily terranormed areas — secure Technocratic facilities, locations under active PSYOP management, areas where the Masses have been systematically conditioned to disbelieve in magic — add 2 or even 3 to Consensus Difficulty. A Tradition mage in a Technocratic Construct performing vulgar magic faces Difficulty 4 or 5 on their Paradox rolls.

The inverse is also true: areas of high mystical activity, strong Tradition presence, or successfully maintained alternative consensus (a Dreamspeaker community with generations of shamanic practice, a city where Ecstatics have been openly weird for decades) can reduce Consensus Difficulty to 0, effectively making what would normally be vulgar magic coincidental within that community's shared reality.

This tension — the Technocracy pushing Consensus Difficulty up, the Traditions pushing it down, and the Masses responding to whoever makes the most compelling argument about what reality contains — is the Ascension War played out in every locale the characters visit. Track it. Let it shift. Let the player characters' choices matter.

---

For complete Technocratic character creation guidance, Convention-specific Merit lists, and detailed mission-structure rules, cross-reference Chapter 8: Factions and the Social Game, and the character creation guidelines in Chapter 3. The HOAR rating system described in the Technocracy Reloaded source material makes an excellent template for Storyguides to track how much the Technocracy cares about a specific Tradition mage or cabal — starting at rating 2 (neutral potential asset) and rising or falling based on actions taken in play.

## Operative Field Dossier — Adjustments, Procedures, and Devices

The following material is drawn from the M20 Operative's Dossier and covers the Union's field-ready operational toolkit in full: Adjustments (low-Sphere coincidental workings), high-value Procedures (demanding rotes for specific operational problems), a complete Device catalog with stat blocks, sensory bioware, MMDS prototype rules, and the Construct/Chantry creation system. All content has been converted to Storypath Ultra terminology: successes become hits, Quintessence becomes Primal Energy, and difficulty becomes Difficulty. Enlightenment is mechanically identical to Arete throughout.

### Adjustments — Field-Ready Procedures

Adjustments are the everyday workings of Technocratic operatives: low-SF effects that keep the mission running smoothly. Most are coincidental in appropriate contexts. Most rotes are cast with Resolve + relevant Sphere (or Cunning for subtle social effects, or Intellect for data and analysis).

#### Deviance Potential Evaluation (Life ●, Mind ●)

Sphere Skill: Life or Mind. SF: 1 (rote). Casting Pool: Intellect + Life or Mind.

Through Enlightened Science-enhanced psychological and cognitive testing, the operative detects whether a mortal subject has the potential to Awaken or manifest supernatural qualities. The operative spends several scenes in normal interaction, administering subtle tests. 1 hit: Determines if target has the potential to Awaken, become a shapeshifter variant, or manifest similar unusual qualities. 3 hits: Reveals if the target presents a recruitment risk for vampire factions. 5 hits: Indicates whether the target's emotional state makes Post-Life Entity manifestation likely after death. This Adjustment does not function on already-Awakened targets or those already manifesting supernatural qualities. Coincidental in professional counseling, medical, or psychological evaluation contexts; vulgar if conducted openly on an unwilling subject.

#### Distribution Mismanagement (Prime ●●●●, Entropy ●●●)

Sphere Skill: Prime. SF: 3 (rote). Casting Pool: Cunning + Prime.

A Syndicate specialty. The operative targets a rival organization's supply lines, financial infrastructure, or equipment access. The application of Prime disrupts the usefulness of the group's resources while Entropy ensures the disruption reads as an internal clerical error rather than external interference. On success, the next time the target organization engages in a significant conflict or operational activity, they cannot access their equipment, funding, or Requisitions for that encounter. The operative must have specific knowledge of the organization being targeted — this Adjustment cannot be broadcast blindly. The target organization cannot be larger than the operative's own primary organizational affiliation. Coincidental when executed through financial channels, digital audit manipulation, or logistics interference; vulgar if the effect manifests as obvious supernatural interference.

#### Ecological Harmonization (Life ●)

Sphere Skill: Life. SF: 1 (rote). Casting Pool: Resolve + Life.

The operative spends one round of focus attuning to nearby flora and fauna. On success, she gains complete awareness of every plant and animal within line of sight: their immediate needs, current states, and recent sensory impressions. Animals that have their needs met become cooperative and non-threatening. They cannot speak, but can convey impressions — the direction a sound came from, whether something passed recently, whether something made them afraid. Does not function on processed or dead organic material, weather phenomena, or intelligent spirit manifestations. Coincidental in outdoor contexts; potentially vulgar in environments where no animal contact would be expected.

#### Generate Emotional Field Array (Mind ●●●)

Sphere Skill: Mind. SF: 2 (rote). Casting Pool: Cunning + Mind.

The operative selects a base emotional state — indifference, calm, agitation, unease, or urgency — then disperses charged nanite aerosols through the target area. Affected mortals experience the chosen emotion as if it were organic. Area of effect: up to 1d10 mortal targets per Mind dot mastered, across roughly one city block. Targets affected with indifference reduce the Paradox risk of any Procedures performed nearby by 1 Paradox die, as the Consensus loosens its grip on the local area. Awakened targets and those with Resolve 4+ may resist with a Resolve + Composure check at Difficulty 3.

#### Predictive Analysis (Correspondence ●●●, Time ●●, Entropy ●, Prime ●)

Sphere Skill: Correspondence. SF: 2 (rote). Casting Pool: Intellect + Correspondence.

Using a large data set as Instrument — crime records, utility reports, traffic data — the operative projects probable supernatural events in the target area over the next thirty days. For each hit, the operative may ask the Storyguide one of the following: What category of supernatural entity is involved? Approximately how many? When will it occur? How long will it last? How likely is civilian harm? Which district? How powerful are the entities? Answers are probabilistic, not exact. Alternative use for hotspot searching: 1 hit identifies a general neighborhood with active supernatural presence; 5 hits provide a vague street address. Finding deeply embedded operatives requires 7+ hits.

#### Remote Access (Correspondence ●●, Forces ●●)

Sphere Skill: Correspondence. SF: 1 (rote). Casting Pool: Intellect + Correspondence.

Opens a persistent sensor connection to a specific location within the Digital Web or any networked device. The operative can observe the location and execute Adjustments and Procedures remotely without physically projecting into the Web. She cannot interact with physical objects or engage in combat through the connection — only information operations. One exception: she may attempt to open network backdoors through the Remote Access connection without being physically present in the target system. Coincidental in tech-saturated environments; vulgar if the observation window manifests visibly.

#### Save File Index (Correspondence ●●●, Time ●●, Prime ●)

Sphere Skill: Correspondence. SF: 2 (rote). Casting Pool: Intellect + Correspondence.

The operative permanently encodes specific Digital Web Icons or locations into their own Pattern, allowing instant recall. A stored Icon can be swapped for the current active Icon as a reflexive action (no roll required). A stored location can be used as a return point if the operative is de-rezzed within the Web. No limit to how many entries can be stored. If a stored Icon suffers Icon Death, or a stored location suffers Whiteout, that entry is permanently removed.

#### Schrodinger's Parcel (Matter ●●●●, Mind ●)

Sphere Skill: Matter. SF: 3 (rote). Casting Pool: Cunning + Matter.

The operative spends 5-10 minutes in conversation with a target, making a Cunning + Awareness read of what they most desire. She then activates this Adjustment on a prepared container. On success, the container produces one specific, mundane, replicable item the target covets: cash, a document, a keepsake. Cannot produce unique or non-reproducible items. With Correspondence and/or Time added, the item produced can instead be information: an address, a photograph, tomorrow's lottery numbers.

#### System Havoc (Forces ●●+, Correspondence ● optional)

Sphere Skill: Forces. SF: 1 (rote). Casting Pool: Intellect + Forces.

Directs electrical surge through a target machine or system, destroying it. 1 hit destroys a single device (smartphone, laptop, camera). Additional hits required for larger systems (bank of servers, integrated security network). With Correspondence added, the surge fires from range rather than requiring physical contact. Against individuals with cybernetic implants, use the standard damage track. In the Digital Web, System Havoc deals Aggravated damage to all Icons within 10 yards per hit, including the casting operative's own Icon. Icons may attempt Willpower-based soak against this damage (Difficulty 8). Using System Havoc in the Digital Web is always vulgar with witnesses and immediately triggers a Paradox roll at Difficulty 4.

#### White Out (Correspondence ●●, Entropy ●●)

Sphere Skill: Correspondence. SF: 1 (rote). Casting Pool: Cunning + Correspondence.

Applied to a Device or piece of information, White Out permanently obscures the trail of its origin. Hits scored are added to the Difficulty of any subsequent investigation roll attempting to trace the item to its creator, manufacturer, or point of origin. This is the standard operational security treatment applied to most Union Devices before field distribution.

### Alternate Perceptions — Sensory Bioware

The following sensory enhancements are permanent biomods or cybernetics available to Technocracy operatives through Requisition. They do not require activation rolls and do not cost Quintessence. Each adds specific Tag-based bonuses to relevant situations. No operative may have more than six senses total without suffering -1 die to all Perception rolls per sense beyond six.

```
Augmented Reality Vision — Background Cost 4
--------------------------------------------
Primary sense tied to the Digital Web's AR layer. Provides 360-degree awareness within 25 yards that passes through walls and floors — limited to what Web-connected devices can detect.
[AR Vision] — +2 dice on Investigation or Research rolls involving anything within range that has AR data attached. Provides instant biometric read on individuals in range with Web presence.
[Coverage Gaps] — +1 Difficulty to Perception rolls outside Constructs; +2 Difficulty in rural or remote areas with poor Web coverage.
```

```
Near-Infrared Vision — Background Cost 4
----------------------------------------
Perception in the 1000-3000nm light range. Sees IR security beams, targeting lasers, and subsurface biological features invisible to normal sight. No penalty in dim conditions short of total darkness.
[IR Band] — +1 die on Medical rolls, opposed Social rolls, and rolls to detect non-humans in disguise, within 10 feet.
[Colorblind] — Cannot perceive color normally. IR lights can illuminate dark areas without alerting targets who lack IR sensitivity.
```

```
Pulse Mapping — Sonar or Lidar variant — Background Cost 4
----------------------------------------------------------
Emits high-frequency pulses, building a 3D map of surroundings from reflected signals.
[Sonar variant] — Provides vision up to 25 yards, wrapping around corners and through open doors. Smoke does not obstruct. Closed windows do.
[Lidar variant] — Provides vision up to 200 yards. Transparent surfaces (including one-way mirrors) do not block. Smoke does.
[Mutual Detection] — Operators of the same variant (sonar or lidar) automatically detect each other within range.
```

```
Thermal Vision — Background Cost 4
----------------------------------
Perceives temperature variation in the 1000-15000nm range. Sees in all lighting conditions, including total darkness. Perceives heat signatures rather than reflected light.
[Thermal] — +3 dice on Perception rolls to notice hidden features or detect non-humans in disguise within 10 feet. Sees in total darkness.
[Reading Penalty] — +2 Difficulty to read conventional print or use standard computer screens. Requires specially made materials for normal document use.
```

### Procedures — High-Value Field Rotes

Procedures are more demanding workings requiring greater Sphere mastery. They often address specific operational problems the Union encounters regularly: vampire threats, forced form-change on supernatural infiltrators, large-scale data attack. Most are vulgar in ordinary contexts.

#### Anti-Hemophagic Saline (Life ●●●, Matter ●●, Prime ●●)

Sphere Skill: Life. SF: 2 (rote). Casting Pool: Resolve + Life.

A one-hour treatment that renders the operative's blood acutely toxic to vampires and similar hemophagic entities for 12 hours. Any hemophagic entity that drinks the treated blood takes Aggravated damage equal to the hits on the operative's original casting roll. The treated operative suffers no mechanical downside from the treatment during this period. Coincidental in a medical context.

> Example: Operative Chen, preparing for a confrontation with a vampire cell in Sao Paulo, administers Anti-Hemophagic Saline 90 minutes before entry. She rolls 4 hits on her Resolve + Life cast (SF 2, so 2 net hits). Any vampire that bites her will take 2 Aggravated damage — which most vampires will discover mid-bite, at the worst possible moment.

#### Atomic Override (Forces ●●●●, Matter ●●●●)

Sphere Skill: Forces. SF: 3 (rote). Casting Pool: Intellect + Forces.

Temporarily suspends or modifies a single scientific principle within roughly 1,000 cubic feet (a medium room) for one minute per hit. Common applications include suspended gravity, reversed electrical conductivity, suspended inertia, or altered friction. Intelligent beings retain basic kinetic control over their own forms even under altered laws. With 5+ dots in either Forces or Matter, the operative can edit the law rather than suspend it entirely: doubling gravity, reversing polarity on specific machines, making plastic conductive while metal becomes inert. If used as a targeted attack rather than an area effect, it deals damage per the standard scale. Almost always vulgar outside heavily warded Construct spaces.

> Example: Agent Reyes needs to stop three armored antagonists from pursuing her team through a parking structure. She casts Atomic Override, suspending gravity in the parking level directly behind them (5 hits). All three begin floating immediately. They can push themselves slowly along walls and ceilings but cannot move at running speed. Her team has 5 minutes to clear the building.

#### Force Compliance (Life ●●●●, Spirit ●● or Matter ●●)

Sphere Skill: Life. SF: 3 (rote). Casting Pool: Resolve + Life.

A compliance beam emitted from appropriate technology forces a supernatural entity to shed its non-human form and appear human for the duration. The entity retains all inherent abilities but loses any physical advantages granted by its alternate form. The Procedure resolves as an opposed roll against the target's Resolve. Sphere pairing depends on the target: Spirit pairs with Life for entities with spirit-based form changes (shapeshifters with spiritual connections); Matter pairs with Life for entities with purely physical transformation. The target may reassume its form immediately after the effect resolves, but doing so costs significant time and resources — which the operative should be ready to exploit.

#### Multipartite Host Infection (Life ●●●●, Entropy ●●●, Correspondence ●●, Forces ●●, Prime ●●; Time ●● optional)

Sphere Skill: Life. SF: 3 (rote). Casting Pool: Resolve + Life.

One of the most complex Union field Procedures: a living digital virus that crosses from a Digital Web Icon into the physical brain of the Icon's user. Correspondence allows the virus to cross from the Web to the physical target. Forces and Prime transmute it from digital corruption into something that attacks the user's living Pattern. Life attacks the brain directly. The virus takes approximately one week to fully infect the target's physical body, corrupting the Icon first and then following the sensory connection back to the user's neurons. For each hit on the casting roll, the operative may reduce one of the target's Intellect-equivalent Attributes or relevant Skills by 1 (minimum 1). Adding Time extends the duration by one day per hit, or accelerates onset by one day per hit. An infected Icon is contagious: any other Icon making direct data contact must pass a Stamina + Resolve check at Difficulty 4 or become infected. Decontamination requires Correspondence 3 + Life 3, minimum 3 hits. Vulgar with witnesses in virtually all contexts.

#### Pen Light (Forces ●●, Matter ●●, Life ●●, Prime ●●)

Sphere Skill: Forces. SF: 1 (rote). Casting Pool: Dexterity + Firearms (targeting the device as weapon).

A handheld device generating concentrated light that is lethal to vampires and similarly light-vulnerable entities. Against targets vulnerable to sunlight, each hit on the attack roll deals 1 Aggravated damage. Against targets without sunlight vulnerability, the Pen Light functions as a moderately bright flashlight with no special damage. The device draws from Primal Energy stores. Historical note: Agent Jacob Black pioneered this Procedure in the late 1990s and reportedly developed skin cancer from repeated use — a Paradox manifestation that engineers have since addressed in production models, though field reports suggest the issue is not entirely resolved.

#### Thanatos Project (Spirit ●●●●●, Correspondence ●●●, Life ●●●, Mind ●●●)

Sphere Skill: Spirit. SF: 4 (rote). Casting Pool: Intellect + Spirit.

A classified Iteration X system: quantum computers slaved to living human beings, processing and distributing information at the speed of thought across any distance, including through the Gauntlet and into Horizon Realms. The technology is invisible to conventional surveillance because it should not exist within the current Consensus. Connecting to the Project requires an Enlightenment casting roll.

```
Thanatos Project Access Results
-------------------------------
1-3 hits: Access to information equivalent to 4 hours of mundane research, delivered in 10 minutes.
4-6 hits: Instant access to information equivalent to 6 hours of mundane research.
7-10 hits: Access to non-physical data — memories, emotional records, psychic impressions — within 15 minutes.
11+ hits: Access to anything that can be known about the subject within 1 hour. No mundane encryption, wards, or conventional security is sufficient to deny this level of access.
```

The Project is vulgar virtually everywhere outside Ultima Thule (the Union's Antarctic station). Storyguides should treat access to this system as a chronicle-significant event, not a routine roll.

### Devices — Technocratic Wonders (Dossier Catalog)

Devices are Wonders: permanent or semi-permanent enchanted objects that store Primal Energy and produce specific effects on activation. Each entry includes Background Point cost (Requisition cost), Enlightenment rating (minimum required to operate), Primal Energy capacity, and the SPU Sphere basis of its effects. Activation roll: Resolve + relevant Sphere (using the Device's own Enlightenment rating if the operative's Enlightenment is lower than the Device rating).

#### Standard-Issue Devices (● rating)

```
30 Second Start — Background Cost 6, Enlightenment 3, Primal Energy 15
----------------------------------------------------------------------
A tripod-mounted early warning system that establishes a 30-second temporal surveillance loop on its surroundings. Takes 90 seconds to deploy.
Effect: Life 1/Mind 1/Prime 1 awareness perimeter. When hostile action is detected (weapons drawn, blood, rapid adrenaline spike, voice-command trigger), the device activates a Time 3 effect that rewinds local time by 30 seconds, giving the team advance warning of an incoming assault.
[Early Warning] — On activation, the operative's team may act in the rewound 30 seconds as if they had prepared: repositioning, readying weapons, or setting ambushes. The Device provides one free round of preparation before any detected hostile action begins.
Warning: Operatives attempting to loop the device using successive 30-second rewinds risk catastrophic Paradox accumulation. The Storyguide should treat looping as a catastrophic Paradox event.
```

```
Adaptive Combat Analytics Interface — Background Cost 7 per user, Enlightenment 3, Primal Energy 15
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Combat contact lenses that link the neural patterns of an Amalgam for real-time predictive combat coordination. Requires all users to be equipped.
Effect: Entropy 2/Mind 3/Time 2. Before combat begins, all equipped operatives roll the Device's Enlightenment at Difficulty 6. Hits form a communal pool distributable among Strength + Melee, Dexterity + Firearms, or Dexterity + Defense rolls during the next combat encounter within the following hour.
[Tactical Link] — Team members share sensory data in real time. An operative who has visually acquired a target shares that acquisition with all equipped teammates regardless of line of sight.
[Synchronized] — If one equipped operative gains the Flanked or Exposed tag on an enemy, all equipped teammates count the target as Flanked or Exposed for their next attack.
```

```
Brown Paper Bag — Background Cost 7, Enlightenment 3, Primal Energy 15
----------------------------------------------------------------------
A briefcase-sized container that renders its contents completely undetectable by any means, mundane or supernatural.
Effect: Data 3/Forces 3/Matter 3. On activation roll (Difficulty 6), hits are added to the Difficulty of any attempt to detect or investigate the bag or its contents. The bag blocks visual, thermal, electronic, arcane, and spirit-based detection equivalently.
[Operational Security Grade] — The contents are invisible to Prime-based aura reading, Correspondence location effects, and all sensor equipment.
Notable: Distribution has been tightly restricted since an operative named Samael Jaqueson used one to extract Union secrets for Nephandic contacts.
```

```
Conversation Emulator — Background Cost 6, Enlightenment 3, Primal Energy 10
------------------------------------------------------------------------
A USB-sized device that, when pointed at a target, predicts the likely arc of a future conversation between the operative and that target, including probable objections, emotional hot-points, and likely outcomes.
Effect: Entropy 2/Time 2. Hits on the activation roll add as bonus dice to all Social rolls made against that target during the following scene.
[Preview] — Before the conversation begins, the operative may ask the Storyguide one yes/no question about the target's likely response to one specific approach.
```

```
Field Medical Kit — Background Cost 6, Enlightenment 3, Primal Energy 15
------------------------------------------------------------------------
A handheld autodoc device. Press to wound site; automated processes assess and treat. Near-miraculous results in field conditions.
Effect: Life 3. On activation, the Kit heals one Aggravated or three Lethal damage over one hour, equal to the hits on the activation roll. Also reduces Difficulty on Intelligence + Medicine checks by 3 for the treating operative during the same session.
[Non-Invasive] — The device requires no surgical skill to operate. It will not fix structural damage without surgical follow-up, but it stops blood loss and begins tissue regeneration immediately.
```

```
Nanite Tags — Background Cost 2, Enlightenment 1, Primal Energy 5
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Microscopic tracking and recording nanites delivered by skin contact. GPS-enabled, audio/video capable, 7-day operational life.
Effect: Correspondence 1/Spirit 1. Record all movement, speech, and interactions of the tagged target for 7 days, relaying data to the operative's receiver in real time. Disperses in areas warded against Enlightened Science.
Removing the nanites requires Occult 3+ with appropriate specialization to detect, and Intelligence + Medicine to remove. At day 7, they disperse automatically and return to the operative's receiver for reuse.
```

```
Urban Navigation Tool — Background Cost 3, Enlightenment 2, Primal Energy 10
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Vehicle-mounted system that interfaces with urban infrastructure to clear the operative's path: lights switch green, other drivers yield, police assets redirect.
Effect: Correspondence 2/Entropy 2. While active, the operative's vehicle suffers no penalties from mundane traffic conditions. Cordoned areas, restricted access roads, and routine border checkpoints are navigated without incident.
[Counter-Manipulation] — If another entity is actively disrupting traffic to impede the operative, resolve as an opposed Enlightenment roll, with the Tool adding one bonus die.
```

#### Intermediate Devices (●●-●●● rating)

```
Augmented Reality Smart Glasses — Background Cost 4-8, Enlightenment 2-4, Primal Energy 10-20
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Available in three grades. Basic (●●): Projects the operative's AR interface to a companion, enabling shared data visualization and collaborative Procedures. Also falsifies the user's GPS signature.
Standard (●●●): All basic functions plus falsifies GPS of AR effects generated, allowing the operative to interact with objects in a remote location via AR projection without being physically present.
Advanced (●●●●): All standard functions plus creates solid-light projections of Virtual Assistants or remote colleagues. A colleague can be projected into a scene as a near-solid presence — interacting with physical objects at Difficulty +2 — without the physical hazard of teleportation or Correspondence travel.
```

```
Portable Perimeter Fence — Background Cost 5, Enlightenment 2, Primal Energy variable
------------------------------------------------------------------------
A collapsible tripod that appears as forensic surveillance equipment. Creates a perimeter that certain categories of supernatural entity are disinclined to cross. 1 Primal Energy per hour of operation.
Effect: Correspondence 1/Spirit 1/Life 1/Matter 1/Mind 2. Covers roughly 10x10 feet per unit; multiple units chain to cover larger areas or add additional categories of exclusion.
[Aversion Field] — Entities of the keyed category feel an inexplicable preference to be elsewhere. They do not experience the field directly.
[Willpower Override] — A determined entity may make a Willpower check to remain within the field for 5 minutes. Each additional 5 minutes requires another check. Committing a hostile act within the field requires an immediate check. On failure, the entity leaves by the fastest available route.
Mages aware of Technocratic methods (Occult 3+ or equivalent) may sense the presence of Enlightened Science, though not its precise mechanism.
```

```
DOUG (Direct Observation Utility Gear) Upgrade — Background Cost 3, Enlightenment 3, Primal Energy 15
------------------------------------------------------------------------
A nanite mesh worn over a Black Suit. The operative steps in, the mesh becomes invisible, and she appears to all social and most magical observation to be whatever type of supernatural entity she has specified.
Effect: Spirit 3/Prime 3. Superficial tests (sniff, blood-taste, visual inspection) succeed automatically. More intensive examination (aura reading, Mind-based investigation) requires an opposed Perception + Occult vs. the operative's Resolve roll.
[Expectation-Based] — The DOUG works on assumption: the operative must first introduce herself as the entity type she is impersonating. The DOUG provides the appearance but not the knowledge.
[Limitation] — Cannot replicate supernatural abilities. High-paranoia environments may see through the DOUG if the operative makes a significant social error.
```

```
Reverse Temporal Projector — Background Cost 5, Enlightenment 3, Primal Energy 15
------------------------------------------------------------------------
A sleek oblong device with a temporal camera and projector. Records ambient temporal resonance in a 10x10-foot area and replays recent events as a fully immersive 3D hologram visible to up to six people.
Effect: Time 2 (recording) + Correspondence 1/Spirit 1/Prime 1 (Reality Deviance detection). Scanning requires at least 3 rounds. Records up to 24 hours of past events; each additional Primal Energy point extends by 24 hours.
[Full Sensory Replay] — All present experience sight, sound, and smell of the recorded scene. Operatives can make minor interactive examinations: checking under tables, reading visible documents, examining objects. They cannot move major items or interact with persons.
[Deviance Recording] — The device automatically captures supernatural effects and events, including entities that attempted to conceal themselves during the recorded period. Concealment rotes scored during the recording period must re-contest the operative's casting hits to remain hidden in replay.
One analysis per device. Analysis can be uploaded to Union databanks for review by higher-level operatives.
```

#### Advanced Devices (●●●●-●●●●● rating)

```
Automated Construction Nanites — Background Cost 6, Primal Energy 10
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Briefcase-sized nanite colony that constructs objects from raw materials according to provided molecular schematics. Effectively an Enlightened 3D printer.
Effect: Matter 4/Prime 2. Can construct virtually any object that fits within the briefcase housing. Simple tools are fast; complex devices with moving or electrical components take proportionally longer. With access to sufficient Primal Energy, can craft Wonders (Matter 4/Prime 4). Wonder crafting produces an audible bass hum and visible white glow from the casing.
[Specifications] — Must be programmed with the target object's molecular structure and design schematic. The nanites cannot improvise; they build to specification.
```

```
Isolated Strand — Background Cost 8, Enlightenment 3, No Primal Energy
----------------------------------------------------------------------
A segment of the Digital Web that an administrator can lock, severing it from the rest of the Web and dropping it into the Umbral Realms. Perfect safehouse or prison.
Effect: When an administrator closes the Strand, all Icons present are locked inside. Escape requires either contesting the lock (opposed Correspondence + Computer vs. Difficulty 8, pool of 7 dice) or finding an Umbral exit. Disconnecting operatives save their minds but leave their Icons inside, vulnerable to identity theft and data extraction.
[Lockdown] — Locking the Strand is an administrative function, not a combat action. Locked Icons cannot use standard Web movement to exit.
```

```
Wormhole Generator — Background Cost 10+, Enlightenment 4-6, Primal Energy 20-30
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Needle-slim white obelisks developed for the Ultima Thule station. Coincidental at Ultima Thule; vulgar virtually everywhere else.
Open Transdimensional Gate (Spirit 4): Opens a gate into the Umbra for up to six personnel. 3-meter-tall black reflective surface. Stable for 72 hours for two-person teams; reduces by 12 hours per additional member.
Superimpose Alien Environment (Correspondence 5/Spirit 5): Connects a point in physical reality to a point in deep space or another dimension, imposing that environment on up to five square miles of physical territory for up to eight hours. Costs 10 Primal Energy. Requires prior scouting with beacon placement for reliable targeting.
```

```
Entropic Perception Field Alteration — Background Cost 11, Enlightenment 4, Primal Energy 15
------------------------------------------------------------------------
A dental implant. When bitten, triggers a 12-hour suspended animation that makes the operative appear spectacularly dead — seizure, hemorrhage, cessation of heartbeat and breathing.
```
