The technique is particularly effective against beings whose bodies have electromagnetic nervous systems — which is all mammals and most complex life forms. Beings without conventional biological nervous systems (certain spirits, machines, constructs) are largely immune to the neural component; the Life 3 pattern attack still affects them if they have Life Patterns.

Examples: With 2 hits (Forces 2 alone), the target experiences sudden painful muscle cramps and loses 2 dice from all physical actions for one scene. With 3 hits (Forces 2 + Life 3), the target suffers 3 dice of Lethal damage as their nervous system is directly disrupted. With 5 hits at full Life 3, the target suffers Aggravated damage from the direct Pattern assault and must resist with Stamina or lose consciousness.

Coincidental when the disruption appears as a sudden illness or nerve issue. Vulgar when the electrical component is visible or the attack is clearly supernatural.

### Drawing Upon the Weather

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers, Celestial Chorus, Order of Hermes. Spheres: Forces 4, Entropy 3. SF: 3. Difficulty: 4 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Forces. Tags: [Environmental], [Lasting], [Elemental].

Where Summoning Storms conjures weather from nothing (Vulgar), Drawing Upon the Weather works with existing atmospheric conditions — nudging probability (Entropy 3) so that existing storm systems move toward the mage's location, intensifying rain that was already falling, redirecting winds that were already blowing. This is the fundamentally coincidental approach to weather magic. The mage cannot create a blizzard on a clear summer day this way, but they can ensure that the overcast sky produces the thunderstorm it was already threatening, that the building wind becomes the gale the barometric pressure suggested, or that the scattered showers consolidate into meaningful rainfall.

The technique is substantially easier than conjuring weather from nothing: difficulty is lower, fewer hits are required, and Paradox generation is minimal because the mage is working within the bounds of what the weather was going to do anyway. Entropy 3 handles the probability steering — making it certain that the weather behaves as the mage needs rather than as raw probability would dictate. Forces 4 provides the control to shape the weather once the conditions are in place. Each hit on the casting roll represents a degree of weather-steering: 1 hit nudges a nearby storm closer; 3 hits reliably produces the effect the existing weather was threatening; 5 hits shapes an entire regional weather system for a scene.

Examples: With 2 hits on an overcast day, the mage ensures rain arrives within the hour rather than clearing. With 4 hits on a day with a distant storm system, the mage draws the storm front to their location, producing a genuine thunderstorm with lightning that provides cover for Forces-based workings (making Call Lightning entirely coincidental). With 5 hits in suitable conditions, the mage intensifies an existing storm to severe weather while remaining entirely coincidental.

Coincidental when existing weather conditions support the desired outcome. Vulgar when the mage attempts to create weather that has no existing meteorological basis.

### Banishing the Tempest

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers, Celestial Chorus, Order of Hermes. Spheres: Forces 4. SF: 3. Difficulty: 4 (Rote). Tags: [Environmental], [Counter].

The reverse of Summoning Storms — the mage disperses, calms, or redirects an existing storm. Calming a natural storm requires fewer hits than stopping a magically summoned one; a natural storm has no magical coherence to overcome, while a summoned storm has been anchored by another mage's working and must be contested. Natural storms require 3-5 hits to significantly calm; magically summoned storms require hits equal to those of the original summoning plus any maintained successes the original mage has applied to duration.

Forces 4 provides the control necessary to work at weather-system scale — redirecting storm systems, calming pressure differentials, dispersing cloud formations. The mage essentially applies a precise contrary force to the weather's existing momentum. Where Drawing Upon the Weather works with existing conditions, Banishing the Tempest works against them — this is more Paradox-generating when the weather being calmed has no natural reason to calm. The most coincidental application is calming a storm at its natural end-point — nudging it to conclude slightly earlier than it would have. The most Vulgar is instantly clearing a severe storm that has hours of natural momentum remaining. Against a magically summoned storm, the Paradox cost is lower because both the original summoning and the banishing are magical acts in acknowledged contest.

Examples: With 3 hits calming a storm at its natural waning stage, the mage accelerates the clearing by a couple of hours — entirely coincidental. With 5 hits against a mage-summoned storm, the mage engages in a magical weather contest; if their hits exceed the summoner's by 2 or more, the storm disperses rapidly. With 5 hits against a natural severe storm in full force, the mage significantly reduces its intensity but cannot eliminate it entirely.

Coincidental when calming weather that was naturally waning. Vulgar when instantly clearing a severe storm with no natural basis.

### Shaking the Earth

Traditions: Dreamspeakers, Order of Hermes, Verbena. Spheres: Forces 5, Matter 5, Entropy 3. SF: 4. Difficulty: 5 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Forces. Tags: [Environmental], [Damage], [Area].

The mage triggers or amplifies seismic activity — a working that requires exceptional Sphere mastery across three Spheres because earthquakes operate at the intersection of kinetic force (Forces), physical material behavior (Matter), and structural fault-line exploitation (Entropy). This is not a subtle working: it produces obvious, undeniable physical effects that affect everyone in the area without discrimination. Forces 5 manages the kinetic energy required to move tectonic material; Matter 5 controls how that energy propagates through different geological formations; Entropy 3 exploits existing structural weaknesses and fault lines to channel the effect rather than requiring the mage to generate all the seismic energy themselves.

Each hit on the extended casting roll determines the scope and intensity: 3 hits produces a localized tremor affecting a 30-meter radius (structures must resist with their Durability or suffer minor damage; all occupants must make Athletics rolls or be knocked down); 5 hits produces a significant quake affecting a block (buildings suffer meaningful structural damage; vehicles become inoperative; all occupants are knocked down); 10 hits produces a catastrophic quake affecting multiple blocks (buildings collapse without exceptional structural integrity; widespread casualties). This working cannot be made coincidental in an area without geological fault activity; even in earthquake-prone areas it produces effects well beyond normal seismic events. Extended ritual (not instant casting) is required for anything beyond a single tremor.

Examples: With 5 hits, the mage produces an earthquake that collapses a specific building while neighboring structures remain standing (Entropy 3 targeting the weakest points). With 8 hits, the mage creates a district-scale quake that disrupts an entire enemy operation. At 12 hits, the mage's working qualifies as a geological event that will be recorded in history.

Vulgar in all circumstances — generating earthquakes by magical will is not coincidental.

### Telekinesis

Traditions: Akashayana, Order of Hermes, Virtual Adepts, Sons of Ether. Spheres: Mind 3, Forces 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Mind. Tags: [Reaching], [Environmental], [Damage].

The mage moves objects without physical contact, channeling kinetic force through focused mental will. This is a cross-Sphere working: Mind 3 provides the mental interface, the focused will that determines what moves and how; Forces 2 provides the actual kinetic energy that accomplishes the movement. Without Forces, Mind 3 alone cannot move physical objects (the mind touches but cannot grasp the material world). Forces 2 without Mind cannot apply precise intentional control. Together, they produce the classic telekinetic effect.

A Willpower roll (difficulty 6) is required to maintain precise control while doing anything else; in combat, concentration is disrupted by damage or distraction. Forces 2 handles person-sized objects; Forces 3 handles heavy objects (furniture, large machines); Forces 4 handles vehicle-sized objects; Forces 5 handles massive objects (shipping containers, small structures). The telekinetic grip cannot achieve fine manipulation — threading a needle or picking a lock requires Life 2 to give the force biological precision. Combat applications are possible but suffer a +2 difficulty penalty due to the opponent's active resistance. Virtual Adepts call this technokinesis; Akashayana describe it as an expression of chi extending through space.

Examples: With 2 hits (Forces 2 + Mind 3), the mage lifts a person-sized object and moves it with authority. With 4 hits, the mage throws a heavy piece of furniture at an opponent for (3 + hits) dice of Bashing damage. With 5 hits, the mage engages in sustained telekinetic combat while still acting otherwise — though the sustained drag costs 1 die from all other pools.

Vulgar in all circumstances — objects moving without apparent cause are explicit impossibility.

### The Bullet-Catch

Traditions: Akashic Brotherhood. Spheres: Forces 2, Life 2, Time 2. SF: 1. Difficulty: 2 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Forces. Tags: [Protective], [Boost], [Sensory], [Reaching].

The legendary feat: a bullet fired at the mage at full velocity is caught between two fingers, slowed to nothing, and held. The Akashic Brotherhood encode this working as both a physical discipline and a spiritual proof — the demonstration that mastery of the living pattern extends to engagement with physical forces beyond mundane human capability. It was not originally developed against bullets; the Five Metal Dragons brought firearms to China and the Akashics were forced to adapt techniques originally designed for arrows, slingstone, thrown weapons, and other relatively slow-moving objects. Firearms made the discipline nearly impossible and then, as understanding of Forces deepened, merely very difficult.

Forces 2 operates on the bullet itself: it reads and partially counters the kinetic energy of the round, extending the time over which the bullet's momentum is transferred to the mage's hand. Without Forces, even a slowed bullet retains enough energy to damage; the Sphere is what makes the deceleration smooth rather than bone-shattering. Life 2 hardens the skin and underlying tissue at the catching point — not armor in the conventional sense, but a local pattern-reinforcement that prevents the round from penetrating even as its velocity falls to zero. Time 2 is the heart of the technique: it extends the mage's perception of the moment of impact into something experienced as several subjective seconds, allowing their reflexes to respond to an event that is, in objective time, occurring far faster than human neurology can process. The Time component also quickens their actual physical response — not slowing the bullet but accelerating the mage's engagement with it.

The working requires the mage to be aware that they are being fired upon — it cannot function against attacks the mage does not perceive. The mage must also have a hand free and the willingness to move toward the incoming round rather than away from it. Akashic training conditions this willingness through years of practice with progressively faster and harder projectiles; a new mage who knows the theory but lacks the training may find their instincts working against the discipline's requirements at exactly the wrong moment. On a botch, the bullet decelerates partially — enough to change it from instantly lethal to merely very damaging — and still strikes. Paradox for this technique depends entirely on where the mage is: in a traditional Chantry or among people who accept martial arts miracles, it might be coincidental; in a modern urban environment with Sleeper witnesses, it is spectacular vulgarity.

Examples: With 2 hits, the mage successfully catches a single bullet, taking no damage. With 4 hits, the mage can catch up to three rounds fired in the same action, catching each cleanly. With 5 hits, the mage returns the bullet to the shooter at the same velocity — a genuine feat that requires precise channeling of the recaptured kinetic energy back along the original vector.

Coincidental in environments that accept superhuman martial arts (dedicated Chantries, regions with martial arts paradigm). Vulgar with Sleeper witnesses in modern settings — no amount of training explains this.


### All Triggers Locked

Conventions: Iteration X, New World Order. Spheres: Forces 2, Matter 2. SF: 1. Difficulty: 2 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Forces. Tags: [Area], [Damage], [Control], [Controversial].

All Triggers Locked is named after a sequence from a popular action film that Iteration X armorer-operatives adopted as informal callsign for the Procedure, because the visual effect matches the scene with sufficient accuracy to be useful shorthand in field communications. It is a last-resort area-denial working that transforms every firearm in the immediate vicinity — regardless of who holds them — into elements of a single coordinated suppression field. The operative seizes all firearms within range via Forces 2, uses Matter 2 to temporarily fuse and orient them into a single synchronized line of fire, and simultaneously triggers every weapon at once. The result is a catastrophic volume of fire that covers the entire surrounding area and spares absolutely nothing within the radius.

The Procedure is brutally effective and almost equally dangerous to everyone present, including the operative's own allies. It requires three full turns to execute: the first turn deploys Forces 2 to seize and begin repositioning all firearms in range (up to about fifteen meters in any direction); the second turn applies Matter 2 to fuse the weapons into a unified fire-architecture — they are temporarily bonded into a single operating system while remaining physically distinct objects; the third turn fires all weapons simultaneously, emptying all available ammunition at once. Everything within the blast arc takes 7 dice of Lethal damage per turn for however many turns of ammunition remain, with no possibility of evasion beyond physical cover. Targets caught in the open with no cover simply absorb the full volume of fire.

The Procedure's operational limitations are severe. The three-turn execution time means it is impossible to use in a fast-moving firefight unless the operative can secure two turns of protected concentration before deployment — typically requiring allies to pin the enemy while the operative prepares. Any attack that interrupts the operative during the two preparation turns aborts the Procedure entirely, wasting the investment. The automatic nature of the fire means that it is equally effective at shredding the operative's own side, which is why All Triggers Locked is classified in NWO and Iteration X tactical doctrine as a "Mutual Assured Suppression Protocol" — it is formally authorized only when either (a) the operative has confirmed that no friendly assets are within the blast radius, or (b) the situation is so catastrophically compromised that reducing all parties to rubble is a more favorable outcome than the alternative. Veteran operatives who have used it report that (b) is uncomfortably easy to rationalize in the moment.

Matter 2 adds a secondary effect that is easily missed: the fusing of the weapons changes their material properties temporarily. Any weapon that was jammed, unloaded, or structurally damaged is made functional by the fusing process — Matter 2 normalizes the entire weapons-ensemble to operational readiness. This means that enemies who had thrown away guns, unloaded their clips, or were carrying inoperative weapons will find those weapons suddenly active and firing as part of the ensemble. The operative cannot discriminate about which weapons are brought into the system; the working grabs everything in range that qualifies as a firearm. A concealed holdout pistol in a pocket will be pulled toward the ensemble. A handgun left in a car outside the combat zone would be outside range, but a dropped weapon on the floor of the room is in range.

The Paradigm framing is straightforwardly Technocratic for both Conventions: Iteration X treats it as a kinetics-engineering field application, noting the precision of the Matter/Forces interface in achieving simultaneous cross-weapon synchronization. NWO field operatives are less interested in the mechanism and more in the outcome, typically framing it in after-action reports as "deployed area-suppression asset with high-collateral-damage profile." The working is explicitly Vulgar — there is no coincidental framing for every gun in a room simultaneously rising, turning, fusing, and firing with no human hands on them. The Paradox cost is significant, and the Union expects operatives who invoke it to have already accepted that cost as necessary.

Examples: With 3 hits on the execution roll (made at the end of turn 3 when the fire begins), the suppression field fires for one turn at 7 dice Lethal to everything in the fifteen-meter radius with no cover. With 5 hits, the ensemble fires for two turns; at 7 hits, three turns. With a failed roll (zero hits), the Matter fusing was insufficient — the weapons fire but in random directions, the force is uncoordinated, and the damage is halved and spread unpredictably, which may still be significant in a confined space. On a botch, one of the firearms explodes during the fusing process and the Procedure aborts with the operative as a potential target of the explosion.

Vulgar in all circumstances. There is no version of "guns assembling themselves and firing simultaneously" that reads as coincidental to any witness, Awakened or Sleeper.

### Look Away

Traditions: Nephandi, Hollow Ones. Spheres: Forces ●● (or Mind ●●, or Mind ●●● + Matter/Life ●●, or Life ●●–●●●); optionally with Forces ●● added to Mind variants. SF: 1 (Forces or Mind alone), 2 (combined variants). Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Forces. Tags: [Subtle], [Protective], [Control].

Misdirection is the Fallen practitioner's most fundamental operating principle. Look Away is the working that embodies it. In its simplest form, it creates a distraction — a sound from another direction, a flare of light, a subtle environmental pull — that draws attention away from the mage's position or activity. In more sophisticated forms, it installs a perceptual blindspot in the minds of observers: not invisibility, but an active deflection of attention that makes the mage's presence slide off conscious awareness like water off glass.

The Forces ●● variant manipulates existing environmental energies: a creak from another room, a ripple of shadow that draws the eye, a flicker of light across a reflective surface. The mage cannot generate forces that don't already exist in the environment — they work with what is there, redirecting what is present. This is purely mechanical misdirection. It is also the most reliably coincidental version of the working, since nothing produced is visually impossible.

The Mind ●● variant implants a brief "mood impulse" — a subliminal suggestion that nothing of interest is happening in the mage's direction. It is psychically more elegant than the Forces version and works regardless of whether the environment has useful existing forces to redirect. However, it is technically a Mind intrusion and generates correspondingly darker Resonance in the caster when used routinely.

The advanced variant — Mind ●●● plus Matter ●● (to lock the Effect on an object) or Life ●●/●●● (to lock it on a person, including the caster) — installs the Look Away deflection as a persistent, traveling effect. For the duration of the spell, the marked person or object projects a continuous psychic aversion: observers do not look at it unless they consciously work to do so. This functions, in game terms, as a temporary additional dot of Arcane (the Background that causes people's attention to slide away). Observers who are specifically and actively searching for the marked target must roll Perception + Awareness against Difficulty 4 to perceive them normally. Without directed searching, the target is simply not noticed.

The working cannot conceal vulgar magic. If the mage casts Look Away and then throws a fireball while looking away, witnesses will still register the fireball — the perceptual deflection applies to the mage's presence, not to the results of their actions. The working also cannot conceal a mage from electronic observation (cameras, sensors) unless the optional Forces ●● component is added to blur electrical recordings alongside the perceptual deflection.

SF: 1 (Forces ●● or Mind ●● alone). SF: 2 (Mind ●●● combined with Life or Matter). Casting pool: Dexterity + Forces (environmental variant) or Manipulation + Mind (psychic variant).

Example — 1 hit, Forces ●●: The mage, retrieving something from a locked cabinet, needs one second of unobserved access. Forces ●● rattles a window on the opposite side of the room. Every observer's head turns. The mage has the cabinet open and closed before attention returns to her direction.

Example — 3 hits, Mind ●●●, Life ●● (on self): The mage moves through a moderately occupied space — a library, an office building corridor — for the next two hours with the Look Away deflection active. Colleagues she passes experience a vague feeling they were about to say something but then let it go. No one can explain later why they didn't notice her. Security reviews the footage and finds her clearly visible on camera, which creates an interesting discrepancy between the camera record and human memory.

Example — 5 hits, Mind ●●● + Matter ●● (on an object): A Nephandic ritual site is marked with the advanced Look Away Effect. Even persons who know the site exists and are looking specifically for it find their attention sliding away as they approach. Reaching the door requires deliberate effort against a strong urge to be somewhere else. A Tradition mage with Prime ●● can perceive the Effect's presence; breaking it requires Mind ●●● in contest with the original casting hits.

Coincidental in all forms when the mage avoids any display of vulgar magic during its operation. Vulgar only if the Effect produces something visually impossible — a shadow moving against the light source, attention-deflection visible as a shimmer to Awakened senses.

---

### Shadow of the Black Wind

Traditions: Nephandi (Malfeans, K'llashaa). Spheres: Forces ●●●–●●●●●, Spirit ●●●, Mind ●●, Prime ●●. SF: 2 (Forces ●●●), 3 (Forces ●●●●), 4 (Forces ●●●●●). Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Forces. Tags: [Damage], [Environmental], [Aggravated], [Lasting], [Controversial].

One of the great environmental weapons in the Nephandic arsenal, and one that reveals the particular Malfean aesthetic most clearly: the cold, dark, howling storm as an expression of cosmic entropic principle made manifest. Where other factions call storms for dramatic effect or tactical advantage, Malfeans call *this* storm — because this storm contains something. Spirit ●●● draws minor Umbral entities into the wind's embrace, giving it a quality of inhabited malevolence that distinguishes it from any natural weather phenomenon. The wind does not merely blow. It carries something. Witnesses consistently report not the physical force of the gale but the sound within it: voices that cannot be parsed as language but convey, unmistakably, something about the nature of the person hearing them.

The Forces component determines the storm's scale. Forces ●●● produces a strong, localized wind in a confined area — enough to disrupt anything not fixed down, extinguish lights, and make normal human activity impossible within range. Forces ●●●● produces a genuine storm affecting a city block or a large building interior, capable of structural damage to improvised barriers and buildings not constructed for severe weather. Forces ●●●●● produces a major weather event with regional consequences — a supernatural tempest that grounds aircraft, floods streets, and makes emergency services prioritize the weather over whatever the mage was doing while the storm covered them.

Spirit ●●● calls the Umbral inhabitants. The minor spirits summoned into the storm are not given specific orders — they inhabit the wind and express their natures within it. Entropy-flavored spirits produce an intensified aura of despair and wrongness in the storm's area. Predatory Umbral entities hunt through the wind for any spiritual target they can find. The nature of the spirits summoned reflects the mage's Resonance and the local Umbral ecology; a Malfean working this in an already-corrupted Umbral zone will summon something far more purposeful and dangerous than one working in a neutral space.

Mind ●● adds the emotional surge: everyone within the storm's area feels fear, awe, and a visceral certainty that something vast and utterly indifferent to human life is present in the wind. This is not a subtle mood effect — it is the psychic signature of genuine Qlippothic resonance being channeled through weather.

Prime ●● provides the energy that sustains the storm at unnatural intensity. Without Prime, a Forces storm of this magnitude would dissipate naturally within minutes unless there were actual storm-level environmental preconditions to sustain it. With Prime, the mage is feeding Quintessential energy into the working, maintaining it for its full Duration.

Physical damage from the storm uses the Forces attack roll (hits scored provide damage dice for the storm's effects). At Forces ●●●, the wind inflicts Bashing in open areas; at ●●●●, it can inflict Lethal through flying debris; at ●●●●●, it can directly damage structures, inflicting aggravated damage to Pattern-level structure. The aggravated quality comes from the Spirit component — what attacks through this storm is not merely wind but something that resonates with the wound-frequency of spiritual injury.

SF: 2/3/4 (rote) depending on Forces level. Casting pool: Resolve + Forces.

Example — 3 hits, Forces ●●●: A confined ritual space is flooded with a howling cold wind bearing traces of Umbral sound. All light sources extinguish. Anyone not braced against a wall takes 1 Bashing per turn from the force of the wind. The sounds in the wind trigger Shock checks (Difficulty 2) for all witnesses. The mage is unaffected, having prepared a personal pocket of stillness at the working's center.

Example — 5 hits, Forces ●●●●: A city block enters a localized weather anomaly — temperatures dropping twenty degrees, winds exceeding hurricane levels in a four-block radius for thirty minutes while surrounding streets remain calm. The news covers it. Meteorologists are baffled. Within the storm's area, Spirit inhabitants driven by the working hunt for any spiritual anchor or vulnerable consciousness. Several witnesses suffer permanent Shock damage from what they experience during the thirty minutes.

Example — 7 hits, Forces ●●●●●: A regional catastrophe. The storm covers a significant urban area for two hours and causes the kind of damage that emergency services will be processing for weeks. The Nephandus responsible is in the eye — physically still, spiritually occupied directing the Spirit inhabitants toward specific targets. When the storm ends, the mage is three states away and the weather event is written up as an anomalous climate occurrence.

Vulgar in all circumstances. Generates significant Paradox (Forces ●●●●● alone generates catastrophic Paradox). The working is typically only attempted by arch-level practitioners who have prepared to absorb or redirect the Paradox load.


---

### Wave-Deflector Cloak

Traditions: Sons of Ether (Stealth-Field Generator), Virtual Adepts (EM Suppression Protocol). Conventions: Iteration X (Hypertech Variant — Technocracy NPC device). Spheres: Correspondence 2, Forces 2. SF: 1. Difficulty: 2 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Correspondence. Tags: [Protective], [Subtle], [Lasting].

This working combines spatial awareness (Correspondence 2) with electromagnetic and acoustic manipulation (Forces 2) to produce a moving envelope of invisibility and silence around a target. The Correspondence component maintains a continuously updated spatial model of the caster's surroundings, tracking incoming light and sound waves relative to the target's position. The Forces component bends electromagnetic radiation — including visible light — around the target and suppresses the acoustic signature of the target's movement, effectively rendering them optically transparent and acoustically silent to observers outside the envelope. Within the envelope, the target can see and hear normally; the effect is directional outward only.

The working does not make the target invisible to magical perception: Prime 1 reveals Quintessential resonance, Spirit 1 perceives the spiritual presence of the cloaked being, and any Sphere that specifically addresses the target's own Pattern (Life, Mind) operates normally through the envelope. Electronic sensors calibrated to non-visible wavelengths (infrared, ultraviolet, radar, sonar) require Forces 1 perception to defeat; the base working handles visible light and audible sound only. Against observers equipped with Correspondence-based spatial scanning, the envelope must achieve more hits than the scanning working to avoid detection as a spatial anomaly.

The working is eminently practical and sees use across Tradition, Technocracy, and Disparate practitioners alike. Sons of Ether build it into wearable "stealth field generators" — physical devices that house the working as a Wonder. Iteration X constructs similar devices as hypertech operatives' equipment, the collar worn by their familiar construct Tiberius being a documented example: a Correspondence 2 / Forces 2 wave-deflector Device with Enlightenment 4, granting not only invisibility and silence but allowing the wearer to sense surrounding spatial topology while cloaked. Virtual Adepts implement the working as an EM suppression program running on wearable hardware.

Duration scales with hits: each hit sustains the cloak for approximately ten minutes under conditions of active movement. Extended stationary concealment scales more favorably — a target who remains still counts as having double their hits for duration purposes. The working must be cast on a specific target; it cannot create a fixed zone of invisibility (that would require different Correspondence geometry). It can be cast on objects as well as persons, making it useful for concealing vehicles, equipment, and locations from visual and acoustic surveillance.

Practitioners who study Tiberius's collar specifically have noted that the Correspondence component in that device provides active spatial awareness rather than passive concealment — the cloaked target knows the layout of their immediate surroundings without requiring line of sight. Adding this active component requires Correspondence 3 rather than 2 and adds significantly to the working's complexity, but produces a cloaked target who can navigate with certainty in complete darkness and who cannot be surprised by threats approaching from outside their field of view.

Examples: With 4 hits, the mage cloaks themselves for forty minutes of active movement — sufficient for a standard extraction through a monitored facility. With 6 hits applied to a vehicle: the vehicle is invisible and acoustically silent to road-side observers, surveillance cameras, and bystanders for one hour. With 8 hits applied to a stationary observer position: the duration extends to several hours, providing a viable surveillance platform that leaves no optical or acoustic trace. With 4 hits of the Correspondence 3 variant (active spatial awareness included): the cloaked operative navigates a building's interior in complete darkness, aware of every room's contents and every person's location without needing to see them directly.

Coincidental in all circumstances — to observers, the cloaked target simply is not present. The absence of something is not, itself, detectable.


## Life — Rotes of Living Patterns

Life magic touches the biological patterns that distinguish living things from matter: the interlocking cascade of chemistry, electricity, and information that constitutes a living body. At lower ratings it reads and repairs; at higher ratings it rewrites the very blueprint of existence. Life mages include healers and torturers, shapeshifters and poison-crafters — the Sphere makes no moral distinctions.

### Sense Life

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers, Akashic Brotherhood. Spheres: Life 1. SF: 1. Difficulty: 1 (Rote). Tags: [Sensory], [Subtle].

The mage perceives the vital signatures of all living things in their vicinity — the warm glow of metabolic activity, the electrical hum of nervous systems, the subtle differences in pattern that distinguish human from animal, healthy from ill, magically altered from natural. Within (Arete/Enlightenment x 10) meters, the mage automatically knows: the number and rough location of living beings; their approximate health (fine / injured / critically hurt); their emotional state (the biochemical signatures of fear, rage, calm); and whether their Life pattern has been magically altered. Disguises that rely on concealing living tissue fail immediately. Those hidden by mundane darkness, camouflage, or illusion (but not Mind magic) are fully visible.

Coincidental in all circumstances.

### Knit the Flesh

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers, Akashic Brotherhood. Spheres: Life 2. SF: 1. Difficulty: 2 (Rote). Tags: [Healing].

The mage accelerates the body's natural healing mechanisms, compressing days of recovery into minutes. Each hit heals one level of Bashing damage immediately. To heal Lethal damage with this rote, the mage must achieve at least 2 hits per level healed, and each Lethal level converted becomes Bashing (which then heals normally over an hour of rest). Aggravated damage cannot be affected — it requires Life 4+ to address at all. The mage must touch the target and maintain concentration for the duration of the working (one full minute of uninterrupted casting for each Lethal level treated). The healing is visible if anyone is watching: wounds close, bruises fade, and color returns to the patient's face in real time.

Coincidental when the healing is subtle (small cuts closing, bruising fading gradually). Vulgar when dramatic healing (a closed wound, a set bone) occurs visibly in front of Sleepers.

### Body Armor

Traditions: Akashic Brotherhood, Verbena. Spheres: Life 2. SF: 1. Difficulty: 2 (Rote). Tags: [Protective], [Boost], [Lasting].

The mage reinforces the cellular structure of their own body (or a willing, touched target), thickening skin and musculature to absorb impacts that would normally penetrate or crush. Each hit adds 1 point of Soak against Bashing and Lethal damage. This reinforcement is visible as a subtle shimmer or faint luminescence on the skin under close examination — it passes casual scrutiny but would be noted by anyone actively looking for the supernatural. The enhancement lasts for one scene. It does not protect against Aggravated damage; the cells may be toughened, but they still burn and bleed from truly supernatural assaults.

Coincidental in dim conditions or when the mage can claim mundane toughness. Vulgar when the shimmer is clearly visible to a Sleeper who is paying close attention.

### Heal Another

Traditions: Verbena, Akashic Brotherhood, Celestial Chorus. Spheres: Life 3. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Tags: [Healing], [Targeted].

As Knit the Flesh (above), but applied to any person or animal the mage can touch. Each hit heals two levels of Bashing or one level of Lethal damage. The target feels the healing as a wave of warmth and the sudden absence of pain — jarring in its abruptness, but immediately welcome. The mage feels sympathetic exhaustion after the casting: they suffer 1 level of Bashing damage for each Lethal level they heal in another (the energy has to come from somewhere). This sympathetic cost cannot be soaked. Multiple castings can be performed in sequence; the mage simply continues accumulating Bashing damage. Clever mages carry Tass or have a second healer on hand.

Coincidental when healing is gradual and small-scale. Vulgar when a dramatic healing occurs visibly in front of Sleepers.

### Transform the Flesh — Shapeshifting

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers. Spheres: Life 3. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Tags: [Shapeshift], [Lasting].

The mage restructures their own biology entirely, assuming the form of any animal from a mouse to a large bear. The transformation takes one minute per hit to complete and involves genuine physical reorganization — bones crack and reform, organs shift, mass redistributes (the mage retains their total mass, which can make a "small" animal surprisingly heavy if the mage is large). In animal form: Physical Attributes change to appropriate ratings for the species (Storyguide determines based on animal type); Mental and Social Attributes remain the mage's own; the mage retains their Willpower, Arete, and all magical capabilities (casting is possible but may require appropriate voice and gesture). The form lasts until willingly ended. Clothing and small carried items are absorbed into the transformation; large items (armor, weapons) are left behind.

Vulgar with Witnesses — visible shapeshifting is explicitly supernatural.

### Venom

Traditions: Euthanatos, Verbena. Spheres: Life 3. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Tags: [Damage], [Lasting], [Targeted].

The mage alters the biochemistry of a target's blood and tissues, introducing a supernatural toxin that progresses independently once seeded. The mage must touch or have touched the target in the last scene (a handshake, a scratch, skin contact). Each round after the first, the target suffers (hits) dice of Lethal damage unless they resist with Stamina + Fortitude (Difficulty 3). The toxin is undetectable by conventional medical testing — blood panels show nothing abnormal; the damage appears as systemic organ failure of unknown cause. Only Life 2+ examination can identify the Venom as supernatural, and only Life 3+ magic can neutralize it. Natural recovery is impossible until the Venom runs its course (hits in days) or is magically removed.

Coincidental — the target just appears to fall ill. Vulgar only if the mage is visibly seen infecting the target through supernatural means.

### Regrowth

Traditions: Verbena. Spheres: Life 4. SF: 3. Difficulty: 4 (Rote). Tags: [Healing], [Lasting].

A profound healing working that regenerates lost, destroyed, or congenitally absent tissue — severed limbs, surgically removed organs, burned skin, destroyed eyes. The mage must maintain ritual contact with the target for the full duration of the working (one hour minimum). Each hit repairs one category of permanent physical damage, rebuilding the tissue from available biological resources. Full regeneration of a severed limb requires 4 hits and at least two days of bed rest before the limb is fully functional; destroying nerve damage or regenerating organs requires the same. Aggravated wounds from supernatural sources (fire set by a demon, claws of a Paradox spirit) require Prime 2 in addition to Life 4 and heal at half the normal rate. The mage must consume a point of Quintessence per hit to fuel the regeneration.

Coincidental only if the regrowth is disguised as normal healing over a plausible time period. Vulgar when regeneration is rapid and visible.

---

### Minor Shapechanging

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers, Akashayana. Spheres: Life 3. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Tags: [Transform], [Lasting], [Subtle].

The mage alters their own body's physical appearance — changing hair and eye color, adjusting facial features, modifying height and weight by ten to fifteen percent, or growing cosmetic features such as claws, minor horns, or altered skin texture. These are Pattern-level adjustments, not illusions: the changes are tactile, register on cameras, and persist until the mage reverts them or the Effect expires. One to three hits produce cosmetic alterations indistinguishable from natural variation; five or more hits produce significant changes that remain within the human species — a dramatically different face, a significantly different body type, retractable fighting claws. The mage cannot add entirely new organ systems or radically restructure anatomy (that requires Life 4-5), but within the human physical range, the alterations are complete. Verbena practitioners often use this for ritual purposes — taking on aspects of sacred animals or adopting the appearance of mythological figures during ceremony. Dreamspeakers use it for both practical disguise and spiritual transformation. Akashayana practitioners view it as an expression of the body's inherent flexibility under chi influence.

Coincidental when the alterations are subtle and performed gradually. Vulgar when dramatic visible transformation occurs in front of Sleepers.

### Impersonation

Traditions: Akashayana. Conventions: Progenitors, NWO. Spheres: Life 4, Mind 2. SF: 3. Difficulty: 4 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Transform], [Lasting], [Subtle].

The mage precisely replicates another specific person's physical appearance, including biometric details that survive modern verification — fingerprints, retinal patterns, and the unique biological signature that sophisticated Pattern-reading detects. Life 4 handles the detailed physical replication: every measurable aspect of the target's body is matched at the Pattern level rather than merely on the surface. Life 3 handles general appearance matching only; Life 4 adds the biometric depth. The optional Mind 2 component copies the target's behavioral patterns, vocal mannerisms, and surface-level social presentation — the way they gesture, their characteristic phrases, their resting expressions. Mind 2 does not copy memories (that requires Mind 4) and the impersonator may be caught by anyone who knows the target well and probes deeply. Duration: one scene by default, extended by additional hits. The NWO uses this technique extensively for deep-cover operations; Progenitors use it in classified research contexts where identity security is paramount.

Coincidental when the alteration is gradual and private. Vulgar when instant visible transformation occurs.

### Chi Healing

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena, Dreamspeakers. Spheres: Life 3, Prime 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Healing], [Lasting].

The mage channels chi — raw Quintessential life-force shaped by the Life Sphere — to accelerate the body's own healing processes, reinforcing damaged tissue and Pattern at the level of fundamental biological organization. Life 2 applies to the mage's own body; Life 3 extends the healing to another person. Each hit heals one level of Bashing or Lethal damage. Aggravated damage requires one Quintessence per level healed in addition to standard hits. The healing manifests as visibly accelerated recovery: a cut closes over in seconds, bruising fades, broken bones realign and knit. In combat, the mage can use this on themselves or an adjacent ally without ending their turn, but the rolls are made at +2 difficulty due to concentration required. Prime 2 provides the Quintessential fuel that actually drives the accelerated healing; without it, Life 3 alone can only stabilize damage rather than actively reverse it. Akashayana practitioners use acupressure and breathing techniques as foci; Verbena healers use herbs and touch; Dreamspeakers invoke healing spirits as part of the casting. Interactions: combined with Life 4, the rote can heal Aggravated damage without Quintessence cost but requires a full scene of uninterrupted attention.

Coincidental when the healing appears as exceptional first aid, meditation, or herbal treatment. Vulgar when wounds visibly close in seconds with no mundane explanation.

### Chi-Fire Punch

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena. Spheres: Prime 2. SF: 1. Difficulty: 2 (Rote). Tags: [Damage], [Aggravated].

The mage channels raw Quintessential chi directly through their physical strikes, infusing each blow with metaphysical force that bypasses ordinary physical resistance and inflicts aggravated damage — damage that heals slowly even for supernatural beings and cannot be soaked by conventional armor or Fortitude. The technique requires 1 point of Quintessence per strike, drawn from the mage's own reserves. The fist may or may not visibly manifest the chi: some practitioners' hands glow with visible fire or lightning when the technique is active; others show no external sign whatsoever. The visible manifestation determines coincidentality. This rote does not increase the raw physical force of the blow — it inflicts aggravated damage on top of the normal damage result, not instead of it. Combined with the mage's full unarmed combat dice pool, each chi-fire strike is treated as a separate Effect as well as a physical attack (one roll covers both). Akashayana masters of this technique describe the feeling as "reaching through the flesh to touch the Pattern beneath." Against beings that are immune to ordinary weapons but vulnerable to magical force — certain spirits that have partially materialized, Paradox-infused entities — the chi-fire punch is the primary combat answer available to an empty-handed martial artist.

Coincidental when the mage's hand shows no visible energy. Vulgar when chi flames or lightning visibly manifest around the striking hand.

### War-Power Chi Shout (Kiai)

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena, Dreamspeakers. Spheres: Life 3, Mind 2, Prime 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Damage], [Mental], [Knockback].

The mage releases a focused battle cry — kiai in the Akashayana tradition, war-shout in the Verbena — that simultaneously activates three overlapping Effects in a single instantaneous expression of will. Life 3 surges through the mage's body, temporarily boosting Strength and Stamina by (hits / 2, round up) dots and allowing the mage to soak Lethal damage with full Stamina for the remainder of the scene. Mind 2 projects a focused wave of psychic pressure outward, forcing all enemies within hearing to roll Willpower (difficulty = mage's hits) or be overcome with instinctive fear — hesitating for one turn, breaking contact if already fleeing, or suffering a -2 penalty to attack rolls against the mage for three turns. Prime 2 infuses the mage's next three physical strikes with aggravated damage potential (1 Quintessence per strike). All three Effects activate from the single shout. The combined working is dramatic and obvious; it is not suited to subtle operations. Used correctly in the opening instant of combat, the Kiai can completely reshape the tactical situation before opponents respond. Dreamspeakers use a version tied to invoking ancestral war-spirits, achieving the same mechanical effect through different paradigmatic framing.

Vulgar outside cultural contexts where a battle shout is paradigmatically appropriate. Coincidental within a dojo, ritual space, or combat context where extreme martial ability is expected.

### Soak Lethal and Aggravated Damage

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena, Euthanatos. Spheres: Life 3. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Tags: [Protective], [Lasting].

The mage reinforces their own biological Pattern, creating a state of enhanced physical resilience that allows them to withstand wounds that would cripple or kill a normal person. At Life 3, the mage soaks Lethal damage with their full Stamina rather than the halved amount that represents normal human biology, and gains the ability to soak Aggravated damage at all — normally impossible without supernatural traits. Each hit from the Effect roll adds one soak die to the mage's pool for the duration (typically one scene). The enhancement is invisible from outside — the mage's skin does not visibly harden, their body shows no external sign of the reinforcement. The effect is sustained at a slight ongoing metabolic cost: the mage will feel the full fatigue of the damage absorbed when the Effect ends, as the body presents the invoice for the protection. Combined with Life 4 or the Pain-Shrug rote (see below), the mage can become functionally immune to Bashing and Lethal damage while remaining coincidental throughout. Verbena use this as a pre-battle ritual; Akashayana cast it as part of the formal pre-combat meditative opening sequence.

Coincidental in all circumstances — no visible effect until someone witnesses the mage shrug off an injury they should not have survived.

### Battle Fury

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena, Dreamspeakers. Spheres: Life 3, Mind 3, Prime 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Boost], [Protective], [Lasting], [Damage].

The comprehensive sustained combat enhancement form — sometimes called the Full Warrior's Blessing or, in the Akashayana tradition, the Unfolded Form. Life 3 simultaneously boosts the mage's Strength, Dexterity, and Stamina by 1-2 dots each (depending on hits) and allows soaking of Lethal and Aggravated damage with full Stamina. Mind 3 grants berserker clarity — the mage is immune to fear-based effects, ignores all wound penalties, and cannot be dissuaded from combat by social or psychic means while the Effect is active. Prime 2 sustains the chi flow and infuses physical strikes with aggravated damage potential without requiring Quintessence expenditure per hit. The entire compound Effect is maintained simultaneously and costs one hit per turn of extension (the mage must score at least one hit on a subsequent casting roll each turn to sustain it, or it lapses). The optional addition of Time 3 grants extra actions; Spirit 2 extends the mage's physical attacks to harm ephemeral opponents. During Battle Fury, the mage does not experience pain, fear, or self-doubt. After the Effect ends, the deferred wound penalties and fatigue arrive simultaneously — many Verbena warriors describe waking from the Fury to discover injuries they felt nothing during.

Coincidental when the enhancement is subtle and not visually dramatic. Vulgar when the mage's boosted capabilities make it physically evident that something supernatural is occurring.


### Reading Biological Cues

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena, Euthanatos. Conventions: Progenitors. Spheres: Life 1. SF: 1. Difficulty: 1 (Rote). Tags: [Sensory], [Subtle], [Interaction].

The mage reads biological tells with superhuman precision — heart rate, hormone levels, micro-expressions, muscle tension, pupil dilation, skin flush and pallor, respiratory rhythm, and the subtle electrical changes that precede emotional reactions. This is essentially a magical polygraph combined with extraordinary medical assessment. The mage perceives information in the body's chemistry and electrical activity that no amount of social training can fully suppress because the mage reads the underlying physiology rather than the conscious presentation. The target is unaware of the reading unless they specifically shield their biological patterns (Life 3+ countermeasure or some Spirit/Mind defenses).

Each hit on the casting provides a specific category of information: 1 hit detects the presence or absence of significant physical deception (the body's stress response to lying); 3 hits identifies specific emotional states with precision (fear, excitement, disgust, arousal) and can distinguish between them when they are mixed; 5 hits provides a comprehensive biological portrait of the target's current physical and emotional state, including current health level, any ongoing chemical effects (drugs, poisons, disease processes), and the general trajectory of their recent physical experience (sustained fear for hours, recent exertion, sexual activity). Combined with Mind 1 (surface thoughts), this provides a complete picture of what a target is currently experiencing.

Examples: A Progenitor interrogator uses this to detect deception during questioning, noting which responses elevate cortisol; with 4 hits, she identifies that the subject is lying specifically about timing rather than about the identity of their accomplice. A Verbena healer reads a patient's biological state to identify the nature of their illness before prescribing treatment. An Akashayana practitioner reads an opponent's true combat readiness — the micro-expressions of hesitation and the muscle tension of genuine commitment — before deciding whether to engage.

Coincidental in all circumstances — appears as exceptional observational skill and intuition.

### Tweaking Chemistry — Self

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena. Conventions: Progenitors. Spheres: Life 2. SF: 1. Difficulty: 2 (Rote). Tags: [Boost], [Subtle], [Lasting].

The mage adjusts their own body's biochemistry to produce desired physical or emotional effects without direct magical energy expenditure — boosting pheromone production for social advantage, enhancing endorphin and testosterone levels to improve combat performance and pain tolerance, suppressing adrenaline for calm under pressure, altering neurotransmitter balance to achieve focused mental states, or accelerating metabolic processing of alcohol or toxins. This is the mage managing their own biology as a precision instrument rather than as a fixed system they must live within.

Each hit on the casting provides the magnitude of effect: 1 hit achieves minor adjustments (the mage is more relaxed or slightly more alert); 3 hits achieves significant changes (elevated combat hormones for a sustained engagement, or deep calm under interrogation that reads as genuine); 5 hits achieves dramatic biochemical alterations that would be medically impossible without intervention (suppressing shock response entirely, running on near-zero sleep without cognitive degradation, forcing complete neurochemical reset). The effects are temporary — the body compensates and returns to baseline when the Duration expires, sometimes with equal and opposite effects (a crash following sustained stimulation). The technique requires that the mage have internalized enough biochemical knowledge to know what they're adjusting; a Verbena with no medical training uses herbs and intuition; a Progenitor operant reads and adjusts their own biochemical readouts directly.

Examples: Before a tense negotiation, the mage adjusts their own biochemistry to project calm confidence; their body chemistry authentically matches the emotional state they're projecting, which makes it more socially credible. Before combat, the mage spikes their own combat hormones; Strength and Stamina effectively increase by 1 for the scene, but they suffer fatigue afterward. A mage who has been drugged uses this to accelerate clearance of the drug from their system.

Coincidental in all circumstances — the effects are physiologically real and indistinguishable from normal body function.

### Tweaking Chemistry — Others

Traditions: Akashayana, Verbena. Conventions: Progenitors. Spheres: Life 3. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Tags: [Control], [Subtle], [Lasting].

As Tweaking Chemistry (Self), but applied to another person's biochemistry. The mage floods the target's bloodstream with fear-chemicals (creating anxiety and hesitation without apparent cause), induces drowsiness (causing genuine biological sleep-pressure), triggers arousal (with all the social complications that entails), causes nausea (making the target physically ill), or drives anger responses beyond the target's conscious control. The target experiences these states as arising from within — this is not perceived as external compulsion, because it isn't. The mage is altering chemistry, not commanding behavior; the target's emotional responses are biologically real and arise through normal psychological mechanisms.

This makes the technique both more insidious and more limited than direct Mind control: more insidious because the target has no sensation of being influenced; more limited because crude emotional chemistry cannot produce fine behavioral control. The target can still choose how to respond to their emotions — the technique creates states, not commands. Resistance is by Stamina rather than Willpower (the body's physical resilience against biochemical disruption). Each hit determines the magnitude and precision of the biochemical adjustment; 3 hits is enough to reliably induce a specific strong emotional state; 5 hits can produce sustained extreme states that significantly constrain the target's behavior.

Examples: With 2 hits, the mage induces sufficient drowsiness that a guard starts nodding off at their post within a few minutes. With 4 hits, the mage floods a hostile negotiator's bloodstream with fear-chemistry before an important meeting, making them less aggressive and more conciliatory. With 5 hits against a specific target, the mage induces such extreme nausea that the target cannot function and must seek medical attention.

Coincidental in all circumstances — the effects appear as naturally occurring physiological states.

### Growing Wings — Biological Flight

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers. Spheres: Life 3, Forces 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Shapeshift], [Transport], [Lasting].

The mage grows functional biological wings capable of true powered flight — not the chi-flight technique (which is internally driven by Quintessential force), but actual physical flight structures that generate aerodynamic lift and thrust. Life 3 handles the biological transformation, growing wings of the mage's chosen type (feathered, membranous, or something more exotic) that integrate with the existing musculature and nervous system. Forces 2 handles the physics — actual wings of a size that could be grown on a human body would not provide sufficient aerodynamic lift under normal physics; Forces 2 adjusts the local aerodynamic relationships to make the wings function properly, producing actual flight rather than a cosmetic appendage.

At Life 3, the wings are self-only; extending the transformation to another person requires Life 4. Duration is one scene by default, extended by additional hits. The wings are biological reality — they register on cameras, cast shadows, can be damaged (they have Structure 4 each; destroying one prevents flight but is not immediately dangerous). During flight: movement at approximately running speed in any direction; maximum altitude as high as the mage is willing to go; maneuverability is good but not fighter-aircraft precision. The wings can be hidden when not in use by folding them close to the body (they compress significantly). The transformation is visible and undeniable — the mage is clearly not human while the wings are deployed.

Examples: With 2 hits, the mage grows functional wings and can fly for one scene, covering a few kilometers of travel. With 4 hits, the mage's wings are large enough to carry a passenger (one person their size or smaller) in addition to themselves. With 5 hits, the wings are permanent for the scene and the mage retains full combat mobility while airborne.

Vulgar in all circumstances — people do not grow wings.

### Cloning and Creating Organisms

Traditions: Verbena, Dreamspeakers. Conventions: Progenitors. Spheres: Life 3, Prime 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Lasting], [Craft].

The mage creates a genuine living organism from raw biological material — microbes, insects, simple plants, or a basic tissue/cell culture. The created life is genuinely alive and self-sustaining after creation. This is not animation of existing matter but actual creation of a new living pattern from Quintessential energy (Prime 2) shaped by Life Sphere mastery. Life 3 creates simple organisms with limited complexity; Life 4 creates complex organisms with full nervous systems; Life 5 with Mind 5 and Prime 2 creates higher organisms with cognition. The created organism has no soul (Spirit 5 is required for that) and exists as a biological construct only.

Each hit on the casting determines the complexity of the organism: 1 hit creates microbes or simple plants; 3 hits creates insects or small invertebrates; 5 hits creates small vertebrates (a mouse, a small fish, a sparrow). Creating higher organisms (anything above small vertebrate complexity) requires Life 4-5. The created organism is permanent once established — it is genuinely alive and will reproduce, eat, grow, and die as its biology dictates. Progenitors create organisms in laboratory settings, framing the creation as accelerated selective breeding or gene therapy achieving what would otherwise take millennia. Verbena create organisms through ritual invocations that call life into prepared biological substrate. The technique is used for creating specialized biological tools (organisms engineered to perform specific functions), research subjects, and in some traditions as a form of creation that has spiritual significance.

Examples: With 3 hits (Life 3 + Prime 2), the mage creates a small colony of specialized microbes that metabolize a specific toxin — a biological cleanup crew for a contaminated site. With 5 hits, the mage creates a functional sparrow that will live, reproduce, and remain healthy without further magical maintenance. With 7 hits using Life 4 + Prime 2, the mage creates a small mammal with a complete nervous system.

Coincidental in a proper laboratory setting. Vulgar when organisms materialize visibly from nothing in front of Sleepers.

### Reviving the Recently Dead

Traditions: Verbena, Celestial Chorus, Euthanatos. Conventions: Progenitors. Spheres: Life 4, Spirit 4, Prime 3. SF: 3. Difficulty: 4 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Healing], [Lasting].

The mage restores life to someone who has just died — with a strict window of approximately five minutes from the moment of death. Life 4 restores the failed biological systems; Spirit 4 prevents the soul from fully departing (catching it in the moment of crossing, before the tether to the living world becomes irrevocably severed); Prime 3 provides the Quintessential spark to restart the Pattern's fundamental processes. Beyond five minutes, the soul has moved on and this technique fails — Resurrection is then required, which is a far more demanding and morally complex working.

The techniques applies urgently and dramatically: the mage must complete their casting roll within seconds of the target's death (or maintain the casting begun at death through to completion). Each hit on the casting roll determines the quality of the revival: 1 hit restores minimal biological function (the target is alive but badly damaged, at one Health Level above Incapacitated); 3 hits restores the target to stable condition with full memories of events up to death; 5 hits restores the target to functional health (one-quarter of their total Health Levels). The revival does not heal existing damage — it restores life, not health. The target will need healing after revival. A botch on this roll may produce a failed revival, a partial revival with complications (vegetative state, persistent Spirit damage), or successful revival with the Spirit drawn into problematic relationship with the underworld.

Examples: With 2 hits applied within four minutes of death, the target is alive but barely — they need immediate medical attention. With 4 hits within two minutes of death, the target revives in stable if shaken condition, able to speak and act. Progenitors frame this as emergency resuscitation combined with proprietary Primal Flux cardiac stimulation; Verbena call it catching the departing spirit and binding it back to the flesh; Celestial Chorus regard it as refusing the divine appointment, an act requiring significant theological justification.

Coincidental in medical framing (defibrillation context, emergency revival). Vulgar when obviously dead people return to life without medical explanation.

### Resurrection

Traditions: Celestial Chorus, Verbena, Order of Hermes. Spheres: Life 5, Mind 5, Spirit 5, Prime 3. SF: 4. Difficulty: 5 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Healing], [Lasting].

The full restoration of life to a person who has been dead longer than five minutes — one of the most demanding workings in any tradition's repertoire. Life 5 rebuilds the biological Pattern (which has begun to deteriorate); Mind 5 restores memories and personality (which have been drifting away from the body-anchor); Spirit 5 recalls the soul from wherever it has traveled in the underworld and rebinds it to the physical form; Prime 3 relights the Quintessential flame that sustained the Pattern in life. For bodies that have begun to decompose, Matter 2-4 must be added to first rebuild the physical substrate before the other Spheres can work. All four Sphere ratings must be met; a deficiency in any one produces partial or failed resurrection at best.

Extended ritual work is required: the minimum threshold is 15 hits over a session of dedicated casting. Multiple mages in ritual cooperation can share the load; this is typically how Resurrection is accomplished in practice. The soul must agree to return — Spirit 5 calls and provides the opportunity, but it cannot compel. A soul that has found peace may decline; one with powerful unfinished business typically does not. The implications of this are philosophically significant: every successful Resurrection involves a judgment about the soul's willingness and readiness. Euthanatos practitioners are deeply reluctant to perform Resurrection, considering it a violation of the natural order; they are the tradition most likely to ask careful questions before attempting it.

Examples: With 15 hits over a four-hour ritual, the mage restores a colleague who died two days ago to full life; the colleague retains all memories up to death. With 20 hits, the mage resurrects a figure who has been dead for several weeks, requiring significant biological reconstruction in addition to the spiritual recall. A Celestial Chorus quartet performs the ritual over a full liturgical service, framing the resurrection as divine miracle; the Paradox is minimized by their tradition's alignment with the Consensus's residual belief in miraculous healing.

Vulgar in all circumstances — raising the dead is the paradigmatic supernatural act.

### Frankencritters — Chimeric Constructs

Traditions: Sons of Ether. Conventions: Progenitors. Spheres: Life 4, Prime 2. SF: 3. Difficulty: 4 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Craft], [Lasting], [Shapeshift].

The mage assembles a functional living being from biological components — either a Frankenstein-style chimeric creature composed of parts from multiple organisms, or an organism designed from theoretical scratch using biological knowledge rather than existing templates. Life 4 is required to maintain the coherence of a complex biological pattern across such fundamental discontinuities; Prime 2 provides the Quintessential animating force. Unlike Cloning (which creates simple organisms), this technique is aimed at creating complex, specialized biological constructs with specific capabilities determined by the mage.

The construction requires physical biological material to work from; the mage cannot conjure tissue from nothing without Prime 3 + higher Life ratings. Extended ritual work creates the construct step by step, with each phase of the casting adding complexity and capability. At minimum complexity (3 hits), the result is a functional organism with basic biological needs and behavior. At high complexity (8+ hits), the result is a sophisticated organism with specialized capabilities (designed for a specific purpose, with built-in enhanced abilities). The construct can potentially be given cognition if Mind 5 is also employed, and can be given genuine spiritual existence if Spirit 5 adds a soul — but these additions are separate major workings. Without them, the construct is biological but mindless (or animalistic in behavior). Adding Forces 4 to the construction allows electrical animation of an otherwise inert construct (the classic Frankenstein lightning bolt).

Examples: With 5 hits (Life 4 + Prime 2), the Progenitor creates a biological guard organism designed to respond to specific stimuli with specific protective behaviors. With 8 hits, a Sons of Ether researcher creates a chimeric organism combining the echolocation of a bat, the night-vision of an owl, and the physical durability of a badger — a biological sensor platform. With 10 hits over weeks of lab work, the Progenitor creates a functional biological duplicate designed for a specific covert purpose.

Vulgar in all circumstances.

### Hands of Death

Traditions: Akashic Brotherhood, Euthanatos, Virtual Adepts (cybernetic variant). Spheres: Life 3, Forces 2, Prime 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Damage], [Aggravated], [Boost], [Martial Arts].

The human hand is an inadequate weapon — soft tissue, fragile bone, no natural armament. The masters of Do disagree. Through precise cultivation of chi, anatomical knowledge, and the Sphere Arts, the initiated warrior converts the living hand into something the Laws of Physics politely refuse to endorse: a striking instrument capable of shattering stone, cracking steel, and punching through patterns that mundane force cannot touch. The Akashic Brotherhood developed this discipline over millennia, encoding it into Do under names that translate roughly as "iron palm," "death-touch," and "the finger that interrupts heaven." The Euthanatos practice a variant called the "thanatotic strike," calibrated to interrupt biological continuity at the cellular level — less martial arts, more precise biological assassination. Virtual Adepts have produced a cybernetic iteration where synthetic hand enhancements are charged with Primal Flux, producing functionally identical results through a distinctly unpoetic mechanism.

Life 3 restructures the body doing the striking: the bones of the hand are temporarily reinforced, tendons braced against the shock of impact, pain suppression engaged, and — in the Prime variant — Quintessential charge applied to the striking surface. Forces 2 converts the kinetic principle: the blow doesn't just carry the force of a human fist but concentrates and multiplies it through magically-enhanced mechanical advantage, producing terminal energy far beyond what the physical motion could otherwise generate. Prime 2 charges the strike with raw Quintessential energy, converting what would otherwise be exceptional physical damage into Aggravated damage — capable of harming beings whose supernatural resistance renders them immune to mundane lethal force. The combination makes the mage's bare hands as dangerous as heavy edged weapons, and more dangerous than either to beings with reinforced Patterns.

The Bullet-Catch and Hands of Death are related arts: both represent the Akashic conviction that mastery of the body's living pattern is mastery of physics. A master who catches bullets on one side of that practice has, in principle, already understood the mechanism that allows Hands of Death on the other. The discipline typically requires at minimum Do ••• as a focus instrument — the physical practice encodes the magickal principle. Akashic mages who lack the Do skill may know the theory without the physical proficiency; they find their casting difficulty increased and their body less willing to cooperate. Euthanatos practitioners typically substitute thanatology study for Do, working from anatomical knowledge of vulnerabilities rather than from general combat excellence.

Examples: With 2 hits, the mage's strike inflicts Aggravated damage at her normal Strength + 1 damage rating for one action. With 4 hits, she sustains the enhanced strikes for the scene, and her fists ignore the first 2 points of a target's armor or soak bonus. With 5 hits, she delivers a single stunning blow that inflicts Aggravated damage and leaves the target [Stunned] for one round on top of the injury — the chi strike disrupts their pattern's ability to maintain coordination in addition to damaging it.

Coincidental in dedicated martial arts training environments or traditional Chantries where the paradigm is established. Vulgar anywhere else — no training program produces fists that shatter reality.

### Talons

Traditions: Verbena, Akashic Brotherhood, Euthanatos, Order of Hermes. Conventions: Progenitors, Iteration X. Spheres: Life 3, Prime 2. SF: 2. Difficulty: 3 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Damage], [Aggravated], [Shapeshift], [Craft], [Lasting].

The Verbena call this the "witch's claw" — the extension of bone beyond the soft tissue of the fingertips, charged with Quintessential force, capable of tearing through patterns that mundane weapons slide off. It is one of the oldest combat workings in the mystic tradition: before steel, before the bow, the willworker who could transform their own hands into weapons had an enormous advantage. The Akashic Brotherhood integrate Talons into Do as an advanced discipline; the Euthanatos use a variant that targets the biological vulnerabilities of whatever they intend to strike; Iteration X produces functionally identical results through cybernetic claw-installation and Primal Flux charging, with the advantages of permanence and the disadvantage of being visibly inhuman.

Life 3 performs the physical transformation: the bones of the fingers elongate and reshape into functional talons, the skin toughens at the base of each, and the musculature of the hand and forearm reconfigures to deliver force through the new structure rather than through a fist. At Life 3, the mage can only work this transformation on themselves; Life 4 extends the capability to others. Prime 2 charges the resulting talons with Quintessential energy, changing the damage category from Lethal to Aggravated. Without Prime 2, the transformation produces functional but non-magical claws — devastating against mundane targets but no more effective than equivalent blades against beings with Pattern reinforcement. The Prime component creates a nimbus around the talons in paradigmatic traditions, though mages who wish to avoid the visual tell can suppress this to the cost of slightly reduced potency.

The transformation is not subtle. Talons that are large enough to be useful fighting tools are large enough to be visible — they are, after all, extensions of bone beyond the fingertips. Mages who want to use this working without revealing it must either keep their hands concealed or find social contexts where unusual hand morphology will not be scrutinized. The Euthanatos have developed a variant using Entropy 2 in place of Prime 2 that produces smaller, more concealable talons that bypass specific biological vulnerabilities rather than applying general Aggravated damage — this version is harder to spot but requires more precise strike placement. The transformation typically lasts a scene; extended durations require spending additional hits on Duration in the casting.

Examples: With 3 hits, the mage extrudes functional Aggravated-damage talons for one scene, usable for both combat and as tools for climbing or gripping. With 5 hits, the talons also grant a bonus to climbing and grip that ignores friction penalties, and can score through materials (wooden doors, light plaster walls) that mundane hands could not penetrate. With 7 hits (Life 4 version), the mage extends talons on an ally, granting them the same capability, while also sustaining their own.

Coincidental in no conventional circumstances — visible bone extensions are not explainable. Vulgar in all contexts with witnesses.

### The Notorious Vampiric Lawn Chair

Traditions: Order of Hermes (theoretical). Spheres: Life 5, Matter 5. SF: 4. Difficulty: 5 (Rote). Sig. Sphere: Life. Tags: [Transform], [Lasting], [Controversial], [Damage].

The rote whose name precedes it by reputation into every room it has ever been discussed in. The Notorious Vampiric Lawn Chair — also attributed, in various versions, to Caeron Mustai, Porthos Fitz-Empress, and a figure known only as the Hermetic Seventh who is mentioned in three separate chronicles without any other identifying information — is the complete transformation of a living (or unliving) being into inanimate matter: specifically domestic furniture. The name comes from the most famous documented application. Other purported applications include soap bubbles (disputed), decorative trees (implausible but persistent), household pets (which raises different questions about Life 5 entirely), and a flaming bag of excrement whose Sphere rating has not been satisfactorily established. The rote exists at the absolute edge of what is magically achievable, and its practitioners are correspondingly rare.

Life 5 is required to manage the transformation of a complex living Pattern across every level of biological organization simultaneously — the cells, the organs, the nervous system, the metabolic processes, the immune response, all of which continue attempting to assert themselves during a transformation of this scope, requiring constant override. For undead targets like vampires, the Life 5 requirement holds because the undead Pattern maintains a structure that mimics life closely enough to require the same Sphere rating for transformation; additionally, vampiric resistance to supernatural Pattern alteration (their own form of body-autonomy) adds significant difficulty. Matter 5 is required to specify the target form with the precision necessary to produce functional domestic furniture rather than a horrifying transitional state — Matter 3 might produce something that vaguely resembles a chair in the same way that evolution vaguely resembles design, and Matter 4 might produce something that collapses under weight. Only Matter 5 produces a chair.

The practical complications are enumerated fully in the theoretical literature and have prevented widespread adoption of this technique even among those few mages theoretically capable of executing it. The Effect is always vulgar — there is no coincidental reading of a person becoming a lawn chair. Paradox is therefore guaranteed and significant. The transformation must overcome the target's innate resistance to Pattern modification (which, for elder vampires, represents centuries of supernatural reinforcement). Even on complete success, the Duration is measured in hits spent — a successful transformation that lasts only a scene produces a chair that reverts to a very angry vampire when the Duration expires, having been unable to do anything for the Duration except experience furniture-consciousness and plan revenge. Truly extended Duration requires hitting numbers that are almost impossible to achieve without extended ritual collaboration, and the ethical questions about what it means to leave a sentient being in a state of furniture-consciousness for extended periods are not resolved by the magical literature.
