A bespoke suit — or dress, or business-appropriate equivalent — produced by the Falconi & Associates tailoring firm, a Technocratic front operation that has dressed New World Order operatives and Syndicate executives for decades. To an untrained eye, it is simply an exceptionally well-fitted, high-quality garment. To an Awakened analyst, it is a finely calibrated social instrument.

The Mind 2 Procedures woven into the fabric establish a low-level Empathic Bond with those in proximity, reading micro-expressions and subvocalizations and subtly feeding that data to the wearer's own decision-making. The Matter enhancements reinforce the materials to provide meaningful protection without compromising the suit's silhouette. The jacket conceals small items — a sidearm, an injector, a folded Device — against casual search. Optional: the Florian makeover add-on (Syndicate-applied social conditioning package) adds +2 Charisma dice at the cost of the wearer's fashion autonomy.

**Effect:** −3 Difficulty to Social rolls (Persuasion, Etiquette, Leadership, Negotiation) while worn. Provides 2 soak dice against bashing and lethal damage. Conceals items up to handgun size against casual search. With the Florian add-on: +2 additional dice to Presence or Manipulation-based rolls.

**Invoke:** [Social] when the wearer is attempting to establish dominance, credibility, or trust in a formal or professional environment; adds +2 dice.
**Compel:** [Social] when the wearer is in an environment where the suit is conspicuous — a combat zone, a working-class neighbourhood, an informal situation — drawing attention to their affiliations rather than smoothing them over.

**Crafting Note:** Mind 2 + Matter 2 + appropriate Technocratic Procedures. The tailoring Foundation roll uses Crafts (Tailoring) at Difficulty 7. The Florian add-on is a separate optional modification requiring Mind 3 and an additional 2 pts. Background Cost.

---

### Visual Data & Analysis Spectrum (VDAS)

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●● (6 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Mind 1 (to operate), Correspondence 1 or Data 1 (tracking and database access); Enlightenment 3–6 depending on configuration
**Quintessence Capacity:** None (battery or hardwired)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Perceptual], [Networked], [Technocratic]

The VDAS is the Technocracy's standard-issue enhanced intelligence platform — a heads-up display system that overlays real-time data analysis on the user's visual field. In its most common form it resembles smart glasses or a tactical visor; higher-end versions are cybernetic implants. The system continuously scans the environment, cross-references observations against Union databases, performs biometric identification, tracks tagged individuals, and flags anomalies.

In the modern era, VDAS functionality is substantially coincidental — it resembles commercial AR systems closely enough to pass in most Sleeper environments. Using the implant version while overt is coincidental in any sufficiently tech-saturated Reality Zone. Activating the full tracking suite (Correspondence/Data component) moves toward vulgar.

The system requires a Union database connection to function at full capability; isolated operation reduces it to local analysis only (removes database cross-reference functions).

**Effect:** The wearer can perform biometric identification, run background checks, cross-reference Union intelligence, and track tagged individuals within database range as Minor Actions. Full functionality requires Union network access. Local-only mode: biometric identification and real-time analysis still function.

**Invoke:** [Perceptual] when the wearer is attempting to identify a person, read an environment, or locate a target using the VDAS feed; adds +2 dice to Investigation or Awareness.
**Compel:** [Networked] when the VDAS connection reveals something Union command did not want the operative to see — an inconsistency in their orders, a flagged contact who is a registered ally, a classified data trail — creating a conflict between the operative's personal judgment and their institutional role.

**Crafting Note:** Enlightenment 3 minimum; Enlightenment 6 for the full implant version. The cybernetic implant version requires Skeletal Enhancement as a prerequisite (M20 p. 659) and the same installation procedure as other cybernetics. Data is from the Union's classified network — non-Technocratic users will find the database access returns null.

---

### Alanson R-25 Hardsuit

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●●● (8 pts. base; modular upgrades add 1–9 pts. each)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 3 (structural, armor), Life 2 (biometric integration); Enlightenment 4 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 20 (base; expanded by SPECM modules)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Armored (Heavy)], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Modular], [Conspicuous]

The R-25 is the Technocracy's standard heavy-engagement powered armour — the sort of thing that enters firefights with therianthrope packs, Reality Deviant incursion events, and other situations where standard Composite Armour is insufficient. It is not subtle. It is not designed to be subtle. Operatives deploying in an R-25 are communicating a clear message about what category of problem they have been sent to solve.

Base capabilities: 7 soak dice against all damage types (bashing, lethal, aggravated), +2 Might dice (Strength enhancement), −2 Dexterity dice (bulk penalty), integrated communication infrastructure (ICI: radio, internet, VDAS, GPS, phone, video uplink). Optional modular upgrades, each requiring separate Background Cost: CELSS life-support module (3 pts.: hazmat, underwater, extreme environment; 1 Quintessence/hour), ETC targeting enhancement (4 pts.: +4 dice to ranged attack after one targeting turn), Heavy Armor module (1 pt.: +3 soak for 10 total, −3 Dexterity, 10-yard run speed maximum), HIPAU plasma cannon (9 pts.: Forces 3/Prime 2, 6-die aggravated damage, 1 Quintessence/shot), NSRM self-repair nanites (3 pts.: Enlightenment roll at Difficulty 7 for temporary field repair), SCEMS life support (4 pts.: Life 2 Heal Self, 4-die pool at Difficulty 6, 1 Quintessence/use), SPECM power modules (5 pts. each: +10 Quintessence capacity, maximum 2 per suit).

**Effect:** 7 soak dice (all types), +2 dice to Strength-based physical actions, −2 dice to Dexterity-based actions. ICI grants communication and tactical network access. Each module adds its listed effect when installed.

**Invoke:** [Armored (Heavy)] when the suit's protection is directly relevant — soaking a major attack, absorbing an environmental hazard, breaching fortified structures; adds +2 dice to the relevant roll.
**Compel:** [Conspicuous] when the R-25's bulk, noise, and appearance creates social problems — it cannot pass through a civilian environment unnoticed, cannot be worn during infiltration, and draws Paradox attention the moment it enters a Sleeper-heavy area.

**Crafting Note:** Enlightenment 4 required to operate. Full manufacture requires Technocratic facilities; field modification of modular components is possible with appropriate tools and a Crafts (Engineering) roll. A solo mage cannot build an R-25 — it is an institutional product.

---

### DDGR Card

**Type:** Device / Gadget (single use)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (1 pt. per card)
**Sphere Requirements:** Data 2 / Forces 1 / Mind 2 (conjunctional Effect); Enlightenment 5 to craft
**Quintessence Capacity:** None (single-use; Quintessence is consumed in manufacture)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Single-Use], [Technological], [Technocratic]

The DDGR Card — full name classified — is a standard bank card that, when inserted into any ATM within 50 yards of a pre-identified corporate executive's electronic devices, executes a Data/Forces/Mind conjunctional Effect that scans accessible financial systems, cross-references unusual transaction patterns, and uploads the results to a specified secure drop. The card self-erases after one use. Any Paradox generated (Difficulty varies; 3 Paradox if witnessed by even one Sleeper who does not accept "it's a hacking tool" as an explanation) is suppressed by a built-in Technocratic Paradigm wrapper — barely.

These are used by Virtual Adept sympathisers and Syndicate internal affairs investigators. The latter use is not officially acknowledged. Each card is programmed at manufacture for a specific target category; generic versions exist but are less reliable.

**Effect:** Single-use Data Effect — retrieve financial transaction records and flag unusual patterns for a target within 50 yards. Self-erases on activation. Generates 3 Paradox if directly witnessed by a skeptical Sleeper.

**Invoke:** [Technological] when the investigative goal is financial — corruption, embezzlement, money laundering — and the target has left an electronic footprint; adds +2 dice to the relevant Investigation or Data roll.
**Compel:** [Single-Use] when the card has been used and the character needs it again — they have exhausted this resource and must find another approach.

**Crafting Note:** Enlightenment 5. Requires Data 2 / Forces 1 / Mind 2 and an extended Foundation roll (Crafts or Technology, Difficulty 8). Approximately 5 cards per extended crafting session. Each card is individually programmed — mass production is possible only with substantial Technocratic infrastructure.

---

### Kid Kimota's Jovian Thundergloves

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (9 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 3 / Prime 2 (lightning bolt), Correspondence 3 / Forces 2 (remote Tesla coil link); Enlightenment 6 to use
**Quintessence Capacity:** 30
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Dangerous], [Technological], [Weird Science], [Conspicuous]

A pair of heavy rubber-and-brass gauntlets with a distinctive crackling blue glow and a smell of ozone that never entirely goes away. Built by a Sons of Ether natural philosophy graduate student who has never fully answered the question of where "Kid Kimota" comes from or why it is engraved on the left gauntlet. The gloves tap a remote Tesla coil array (location: classified, theoretically mobile) via Correspondence 3 link and discharge accumulated electrical charge on contact or at short range.

In melee: the gloves add +3 bashing dice (electrostatic discharge on contact; the target is also Stunned if they fail a Stamina roll at Difficulty 7). In ranged mode: Forces 3/Prime 2 fires a 6-die aggravated lightning bolt at any target within line of sight, at a cost of 1 Quintessence per blast. After each blast, the gloves require 2 turns to recharge before another ranged attack is possible. The remote Tesla coil connection means the ranged attack works anywhere with a clear line to the sky — indoors or underground, it requires a Correspondence roll to maintain the link.

**Effect:** Melee: +3 bashing dice + Stun effect (Stamina roll, Difficulty 7, or stunned for 1 turn). Ranged: 6-die aggravated lightning bolt, 1 Quintessence/shot, 2-turn recharge between shots.

**Invoke:** [Dangerous] when the situation calls for overwhelming electrical force — breaching electronic security, disabling vehicles, fighting heavily armoured opponents; the raw damage output makes the situation qualitatively different.
**Compel:** [Conspicuous] when the glowing, crackling, ozone-reeking gloves make stealth impossible or attract exactly the kind of attention the character was trying to avoid.

**Crafting Note:** Enlightenment 6. The remote Tesla coil is a separate Device (approximately ●●● Device) that must be crafted and positioned first — the gloves are useless without it. Full construction: Forces 3 + Correspondence 3 + Prime 2 + extended Foundation roll (Crafts/Engineering, Difficulty 9). The two-turn recharge is not a flaw — it is a structural feature of the coil's capacitor cycle.

---

### Madness Grenade

**Type:** Fetish / Talisman (single use)
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (10 pts. per case of five)
**Sphere Requirements:** Entropy 5 (order-shattering), Entropy 2 (probability manipulation), Entropy 3 / Matter 3 (structural dissolution), Mind 4 (targeted psychosis)
**Quintessence Capacity:** None (Quintessence consumed in manufacture)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Dangerous], [Single-Use], [Area (20m)], [Vulgar], [Nephandic]

A hand grenade of conventional appearance — if one ignores the faint wrongness of its proportions and the way it seems to absorb rather than reflect light. On detonation, it produces no conventional explosion. Instead, within a 20-yard radius: Entropy 5 shreds orderly structures (physical and metaphysical), Entropy 2/3 skews probability sharply toward catastrophic outcomes for the next scene, Matter 3 causes non-biological materials to develop fractures and stress patterns, and Mind 4 induces acute psychosis in any living being who fails a Willpower roll (Difficulty 8). Five or more successes on the Mind Effect produce permanent insanity.

Madness Grenades are almost certainly of Nephandic manufacture — the specific combination of Sphere Effects and the nature of the Mind Effect (not confusion, but targeted disintegration of cognitive coherence) bears the signature of Malfean engineering. They are vulgar by definition regardless of Reality Zone. Paradox backlash on detonation is essentially guaranteed; any mage who activates one should have already said their goodbyes.

**Effect:** 20-yard blast radius. Entropy 5 Effect on all structures. Entropy 2/3 probability manipulation for remainder of scene. Mind 4 psychosis effect — Willpower roll at Difficulty 8; failure = incapacitation; 5+ successes = permanent insanity. Extremely vulgar; guaranteed Paradox.

**Invoke:** Not applicable — this item has no positive Invoke condition. It is a weapon of last resort and mass destruction.
**Compel:** [Nephandic] when the item's origin or presence becomes known to other mages or factions — carrying one implies either Nephandic connections or the willingness to use Nephandic tools, neither of which plays well in Tradition society.

**Crafting Note:** Entropy 5 + Mind 4 + Matter 3 + Prime 4. These items are almost certainly not crafted by player characters. Finding one is a story hook. Having one is a liability.

---

### The Rebooter Self-Retrieval Bio-Printer

**Type:** Device / Artifact
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (Not available for purchase; approximately 10 exist)
**Sphere Requirements:** Life 5 / Mind 5 / Prime 5 / Spirit 5 / Time 4; Enlightenment 8 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 40
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Dangerous], [Legendary], [Paradox Magnet], [Technocratic]

A Device the size of a large wardrobe that reconstitutes a mage from a saved biological and psychological snapshot — in essence, resurrection technology. The Rebooter continuously updates a stored Pattern of the owner: their complete biological template, the full state of their mind, and a partial imprint of their Avatar. When the owner dies, the Rebooter can print a new body using that saved snapshot.

Cost: 30 Quintessence for the mage's reconstitution, plus 10 for the operational overhead. The mage earns 1 permanent Paradox point per resurrection — the act of returning from death is explicitly anti-Consensus, and the Tapestry does not forget. A botch on the reconstitution roll permanently erases the saved identity from the Rebooter's memory — there is no recovery from a botched resurrection. The current snapshot is always 24–72 hours old; events and experiences since the last update are lost.

There are approximately ten of these in existence. Most are held by senior Technocratic personnel as institutional continuity assets. Several are rumoured to be in Tradition hands. Owning one is less a statement of power than a statement of priorities — it requires continuous maintenance, enormous Quintessence input, and the constant accumulation of Paradox that comes from using it.

**Effect:** Resurrects owner from saved snapshot. Cost: 40 Quintessence total. The resurrected mage gains 1 permanent Paradox. The snapshot is 24–72 hours old; recent memories are lost. Botch on the reconstitution roll permanently destroys the stored identity.

**Invoke:** [Legendary] when the character's near-invulnerability to permanent death is directly relevant to the scene — negotiations where death threats are meaningless, tactical situations that call for extreme personal risk.
**Compel:** [Paradox Magnet] when the accumulated permanent Paradox from multiple resurrections manifests — Paradox flaws, Quiet spirals, or spontaneous Reality distortions that follow the character through their impossibly long life.

**Crafting Note:** Life 5 + Mind 5 + Prime 5 + Spirit 5 + Time 4. These are not crafted by individuals — they are institutional projects requiring teams of Archmasters and decades of work. The Storyguide should not allow these into play as purchasable items.

---

### CLN-20 "Fun House Gas"

**Type:** Gadget (single use per grenade)
**Background Rating:** ●● (2 pts. per grenade)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 2 (optical refraction), Entropy 2 (probability adjustment for accidents); Enlightenment 3 to manufacture
**Quintessence Capacity:** 10 per grenade
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Single-Use], [Area], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Non-Lethal]

A grenade that releases a colourless gas producing optical sensor blindness within a 20-foot radius for 30–60 minutes. Forces 2 refracts light across the spectrum — cameras, LIDAR, IR sensors, UV systems, and standard visual perception all fail identically. No physical harm to living beings. Entropy 2 tilts probability toward minor accidents in the affected area: dropped equipment, misread instrument panels, tripped steps.

The gas is officially described as an "atmospheric refraction agent" in Union procurement documents. Q Division insists this is technically accurate.

**Effect:** 20-foot radius, 30–60 minutes duration. All optical sensors (visual, IR, UV, camera) non-functional in the cloud. Entropy 2 applies: one minor accident per person per scene in the affected area (Storyguide's call on specifics). No damage to living targets.

**Invoke:** [Non-Lethal] when the operational goal is to blind surveillance or create confusion without casualties; adds +2 dice to Stealth or Subterfuge rolls made while the gas is active.
**Compel:** [Area] when the gas blinds or trips the operative's own allies, catches a civilian in the affected zone, or persists in a ventilation system longer than expected.

**Crafting Note:** Forces 2 + Entropy 2 + Prime 2. Foundation roll: Crafts (Chemistry) or Technology at Difficulty 7. Manufactured in batches; shelf life of approximately 18 months before the refraction compound degrades.

---

### Bonding Facilitation Fluid (BFF)

**Type:** Gadget (consumable)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (3 pts. per 10-use tube)
**Sphere Requirements:** Matter 3 / Life 3 (molecular bonding of any surface); Enlightenment 3 to manufacture
**Quintessence Capacity:** 15 (per tube, distributed across uses)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Consumable]

A tube of clear gel that permanently bonds any two surfaces at the molecular level when applied. 6 square inches per application, per tube. The bond is immediate and permanent — no conventional solvent, mechanical force, or thermal process can break it. The gel becomes inert mundane adhesive if not applied within a number of turns equal to the activation roll's hits; the operator must apply immediately after breaking the seal.

Uses: sealing doors permanently (without obviously cutting through material), making field repairs to vehicles or equipment, jamming mechanisms, and one documented case involving a Progenitor's lab coat that Q Division still refuses to discuss officially.

**Effect:** Permanently bonds any two surfaces within one application (6 sq. in.). Bond is unbreakable by conventional means. Inert if not used within [hits] turns of activation.

**Invoke:** [Technological] when the goal is to permanently seal, jam, or adhere something in a way that cannot be explained away — stops a door, secures equipment, prevents mechanisms from operating; adds +2 dice to the relevant action.
**Compel:** [Consumable] when the tube is empty at a critical moment, or when the permanent nature of the bond creates an unanticipated problem — the operative has just permanently sealed the only exit.

**Crafting Note:** Matter 3 + Life 3 + Prime 2. Volatile — requires sealed storage. Foundation roll: Crafts (Chemistry), Difficulty 8. The Life component prevents the bond from failing at biological interfaces (skin, organic materials) — removing it requires Life 4 and surgery.

---

### HIPSTer / HIPSDTer

**Type:** Gadget (consumable, attaches to target)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (3 pts. per unit)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 2 / Correspondence 2 (IR trail, HIPSTer); adds Data 2 / Forces 2 (HIPSDTer variant); Enlightenment 3 to manufacture
**Quintessence Capacity:** 15
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Tracking], [Concealable]

A thumbnail-sized alloy disc that adheres to any vehicle surface and sheds IR-reactive flakes at a controlled rate, creating a thermal trail readable by Union tracking equipment. The HIPSDTer variant incorporates nanoprocessors that establish a Data 2 connection, allowing real-time GPS-level position updates to any authorised device.

Both versions require one action to apply to a target vehicle; this is typically done during traffic stops, while the target is parked, or by a second operative on a parallel intercept course. Detection requires a successful Awareness roll at Difficulty 8 and knowing what to look for.

**Effect:** HIPSTer: tracking rolls targeting the marked vehicle are at −2 Difficulty. HIPSDTer: additionally establishes a live Data connection for real-time positioning (3 free successes on any subsequent tracking action).

**Invoke:** [Tracking] when the operative is following the marked vehicle and environmental conditions (night, crowds, interference) would normally make tracking difficult; adds +2 dice or removes Difficulty modifier.
**Compel:** [Tracking] when the target vehicle is discovered, the tracker disc found and removed — or when the Data connection from the HIPSDTer is detected by a technologically savvy target who then uses it to set an ambush.

**Crafting Note:** Forces 2 + Correspondence 2 + Prime 2 (HIPSTer); add Data 2 for HIPSDTer. Foundation: Crafts (Electronics), Difficulty 7. Battery life: approximately 72 hours continuous operation.

---

### LIAM (Loitering Intelligent Assault Munition)

**Type:** Gadget (single use per grenade)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (2 pts. per grenade)
**Sphere Requirements:** Correspondence 2 / Forces 3 / Prime 2 (targeting + detonation); Enlightenment 3 to activate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 5
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Single-Use], [Dangerous], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Guided]

A baseball-sized guided grenade with integrated sonar and micro-thrusters. On activation, the operative designates a target; LIAM's Correspondence 2 component tracks the target's position through walls and around corners. When within detonation range, the Forces 3 / Prime 2 Effect produces an explosive discharge equal to the activation roll's hits in damage at the target's location. Blast radius scales with hits.

LIAM requires an activation roll (Correspondence 2 Procedure) to acquire and maintain a target lock. Loss of the target lock mid-flight produces a random-direction detonation — not a desirable outcome. Use against fast-moving or Paradox-shielded targets is not recommended.

**Effect:** Guided detonation — Correspondence lock required. Damage on impact: hits rolled = dice of aggravated damage. Blast radius extends to [hits] yards. Loss of lock = random detonation.

**Invoke:** [Guided] when the target is behind cover, around a corner, or in a location the operative cannot directly engage; LIAM bypasses the cover requirement and adds +2 dice to the attack.
**Compel:** [Dangerous] when the Correspondence lock breaks mid-flight — the Storyguide determines where LIAM goes when it loses guidance.

**Crafting Note:** Correspondence 2 + Forces 3 + Prime 2. Foundation: Crafts (Engineering), Difficulty 9. The micro-thruster fuel limits flight time to approximately 90 seconds from activation — if LIAM has not detonated within that window, it falls inert.

---

### Mightier Pen

**Type:** Gadget (single use; self-destructs)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (3 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 3 / Prime 2 (three selectable Effects); Enlightenment 4 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 5
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Concealable], [Single-Use], [Dangerous], [Technological], [Technocratic]

An ordinary-appearing pen (or wristwatch, or lighter) with a single-use Forces 3 / Prime 2 payload that can be directed in one of three ways at activation: (a) timed explosion — set a delay of 1 to 60 seconds, then detonation at a chosen location the device has been placed; (b) non-lethal force pulse — a concussive shockwave centred on the device affecting all targets within 5 yards (damage is bashing; no damage to the activating operative if they are outside range); (c) laser discharge — direct beam weapon targeting one specific creature, resolved as Dexterity + Energy Weapons vs. target. All three options use the same activation roll; the operator chooses the output before rolling.

The device is destroyed on activation regardless of option chosen.

**Effect:** Three selectable single-use Forces 3 / Prime 2 effects: timed explosion (lethal damage to all within blast radius), concussive pulse (bashing to all within 5 yards), or targeted laser (lethal to one target, Dex + Energy Weapons to hit). Device destroyed on activation.

**Invoke:** [Concealable] when the device's mundane appearance allows it to be carried through a search or into a secure environment that conventional weapons could not enter; narrative permission to have the weapon in hand when needed.
**Compel:** [Single-Use] when the operative has already used their Mightier Pen and faces a situation that calls for exactly its capabilities — they are empty.

**Crafting Note:** Forces 3 + Prime 2. Foundation: Crafts (Electronics) or Crafts (Toymaking — yes, this is in the Procedures manual), Difficulty 8. The miniaturisation requirements are substantial; mass production requires specialised Q Division equipment.

---

### Pre-Designed Proficiency Packages (PPP)

**Type:** Gadget (limited use)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (3–12 pts. depending on configuration)
**Sphere Requirements:** Mind 3 (temporary skill enhancement); Enlightenment 3–5 depending on depth of Effect
**Quintessence Capacity:** 5–25 (distributed across uses)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Concealable], [Consumable]

An unobtrusive module — most commonly a pair of glasses, a phone case, or a smartwatch — that delivers targeted cognitive enhancement for up to three Abilities simultaneously. The Mind 3 Procedure temporarily elevates the user's functional competence in the programmed skill set: a PPP pre-loaded with "Emergency Surgery," "Classified Communications Protocol," and "Mandarin" grants temporary competence in all three for the duration.

The enhancement lasts for a scene or up to the Base Damage/Duration chart duration (based on hits rolled). Each use consumes Quintessence. The module can be re-programmed at a Q Division facility; field reprogramming requires a Technology roll at Difficulty 8 and the appropriate Sphere Procedures.

Only one PPP can be active at a time per user. Attempting to stack two PPPs simultaneously generates a Paradox spike and a migraine.

**Effect:** +[hits] dice to up to 3 programmed Abilities for [duration]. Cannot exceed Ability 5 total including the bonus. Costs 1 Quintessence per activation.

**Invoke:** [Technological] when the operative needs competence in a skill they do not actually possess and the PPP is programmed for it; the bonus dice apply directly.
**Compel:** [Consumable] when the PPP's Quintessence is exhausted, when the module's programming does not match the current situation, or when the temporary enhancement expires at a critical moment.

**Crafting Note:** Mind 3 + Prime 3. Foundation: Crafts (Electronics) or Technology, Difficulty 7–9 depending on the number and depth of Abilities being programmed. Pre-loaded versions are available through Union supply chains; custom programming adds to cost and crafting time.

---

### TMBL-WD (Tumble-Weed)

**Type:** Gadget (limited use)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (5 pts. per unit)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 3 / Prime 2 (holographic projection); Enlightenment 5 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 15 (depletes at 1 per minute of active projection)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Concealment], [Consumable]

A fist-sized self-propelled drone designed to look like a piece of debris — a crumpled can, a ball of tape, a tumbling piece of litter — that projects high-fidelity holographic illusions up to human-sized, up to 70 decibels of audio, from any position within its movement range. Remotely operated; the TMBL-WD's Forces 3 / Prime 2 projection is substantially coincidental in modern urban environments (it resembles a sophisticated holographic advertising display), provided the observer does not try to touch the projection.

Uses: luring targets out of position, creating decoy targets, maintaining cover when surveillance is active, crowd management. Limitation: the Quintessence fuel runs at 1 per minute — a fully charged unit gives 15 minutes of continuous operation. When depleted, the unit self-destructs (small mechanical shredder; no fire hazard).

**Effect:** Projects a human-sized, fully audible holographic illusion from wherever the drone is positioned. Illusion is visually and aurally convincing but has no physical substance. 15 minutes maximum operation; self-destructs on depletion.

**Invoke:** [Concealment] when the TMBL-WD is being used to create a distraction, draw a target to a specific location, or maintain a false presence somewhere the operative is not; adds +2 dice to the Subterfuge or Persuasion roll.
**Compel:** [Consumable] when the unit runs out of power at the worst possible moment, or when the physical insubstantiality of the projection is discovered by a target who reaches for it.

**Crafting Note:** Forces 3 + Prime 2. Foundation: Crafts (Engineering), Difficulty 8. The self-destruct mechanism is a Q Division safety feature — recovering a malfunctioning TMBL-WD requires a Crafts roll at Difficulty 9 before the shredder activates.

---

### MP-34 Multipurpose Pacification Gas

**Type:** Gadget (single use per canister)
**Background Rating:** ●●●● (4 pts. per canister)
**Sphere Requirements:** None (not an Enlightened Effect — conventional chemistry)
**Quintessence Capacity:** None
**Tags:** [Concealable], [Single-Use], [Area (10m)], [Non-Lethal (primarily)], [Dangerous]

One of the few Q Division items that requires no Enlightenment to operate, and produces no supernatural effect — it is advanced conventional chemistry. A pressurised canister of garlic oil, aerosolised silver microparticles, iron powder, and high-concentration capsicum extract. Effective against: vampires (−2 dice all pools, garlic oil), Garou (−2 dice all pools, silver microparticles), some Fae (iron powder), and all living beings (−3 dice if eyes exposed, 2 bashing per turn if inhaled, capsicum).

Against ordinary humans it functions as an extremely potent pepper spray. Against Awakened targets it has no magical suppression effect — it is just an irritant. Against the supernatural targets it is specifically designed for, it is genuinely effective.

Optional use: the canister's contents are flammable. Opening the release valve without the diffusion cap and adding ignition produces a 4-die fire jet. This voids the warranty and the canister becomes a single-use flamethrower.

**Effect:** 10-yard cloud, 60 seconds airborne: −2 dice all pools (all targets), −3 dice if eyes exposed, 2 bashing/turn if inhaled. Effective against vampires and Garou per supernatural weaknesses. Flamethrower mode: 4-die fire, but destroys the canister.

**Invoke:** Not invokable as a Tag — this is a mundane weapon. Use standard combat rules for aerosol attacks.
**Compel:** [Dangerous] when the gas cloud catches allies, spreads into an unintended area, or when the flamethrower mode is tempting in a situation where fire would be catastrophic.

**Crafting Note:** No Sphere requirements. Standard chemistry lab and procurement channels. The silver microparticles are the expensive component; bulk orders require Technocratic supply authorization.

---

### Neuro-Optical Transmitter (NOT)

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●● to ●●●●● (6–15 pts. depending on scale)
**Sphere Requirements:** Mind 3 (standard) or Mind 4 (enhanced); Enlightenment 3 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 15–30
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Subtle], [Consensus-Protecting]

The standard New World Order memory management tool — colloquially "the Flashy Thing," though using that name in an official report earns a demerits notation. A pen, pocket torch, or phone app that produces a precisely tuned optical and sonic pulse erasing the last 30 minutes of memory in any un-shielded Awakened or Sleeper target within line of sight and replacing the erased window with a consensus-appropriate alternate explanation.

Three scale variants: standard NOT (pen or torch, one target, Mind 3, 6 pts.), LED spotNOT (handheld floodlight, dozens of targets simultaneously, Mind 3, 9 pts.), Vehicular Mounted NOTlight (vehicle-mounted, city-block scale, Mind 3/4, 15 pts.). The enhanced Mind 4 version can reach shielded or partially awakened targets. None of these work on Avatars — they erase episodic memory, not the soul's deeper knowledge.

**Effect:** Erases 30 minutes of episodic memory in target(s) and replaces it with a plausible alternate account. Standard version: one target. SpotNOT: dozens simultaneously. Vehicular: city block. Mind 4 variant: penetrates partial Awareness shields.

**Invoke:** [Consensus-Protecting] when the operative has just done something nakedly supernatural in front of Sleepers and needs a clean exit; narrative permission to erase the witnesses' memory of the specific Event.
**Compel:** [Subtle] when the NOT's effects are detected — a target who was shielded, an Awakened witness who resisted, or a third party who was watching from outside the effect radius and now has footage the NOT did not reach.

**Crafting Note:** Mind 3 + Prime 3. Foundation: Crafts (Electronics), Difficulty 7 (standard), Difficulty 9 (Vehicular Mounted). The vehicle mount version requires Technocratic engineering pool — not a solo project.

---

### PAWS Taser

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (8 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Dimensional Science 3 / Forces 3 / Life 4 / Prime 2; Enlightenment 5 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 15
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Dangerous], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Anti-Supernatural]

The Pandimensional Anomaly Waveform Shutdown device — a bulky, rifle-stocked taser with a distinctive purple charge glow — produces a targeted dimensional disruption pulse specifically calibrated to forcibly revert shapeshifting Dimensional Science-adjacent Deviants (Garou, Ananasi, Rokea, and related therianthropes) to their baseline human form, simultaneously delivering electrocution damage.

Against materialised spirits: the Forces 3 / Prime 2 component delivers aggravated damage equal to activation hits. Against therianthropes: the Life 4 component forces involuntary reversion to Homid form (contested roll: PAWS Enlightenment vs. target's Gnosis or Stamina) plus electrocution damage. Against ordinary human or mage targets: functions as a standard high-voltage taser (bashing damage, Stun effect).

The PAWS Taser is issued only to operatives with documented therianthrope or spirit engagement history. It is substantially coincidental in appearance — it looks like a heavy-duty taser, which is entirely plausible.

**Effect:** Against Garou/therianthropes: forced reversion to human form (contested roll) + electrocution damage. Against materialised spirits: aggravated damage = hits. Against other targets: bashing damage + Stun.

**Invoke:** [Anti-Supernatural] when the target is a therianthrope in non-human form or a materialised spirit; the specific calibration of the PAWS gives the operative a decisive tactical advantage.
**Compel:** [Dangerous] when the PAWS is deployed against a target who is not what the operative assumed — a mage with Life Effects active, a mundane person with unusual biology, or a being whose Dimensional Science signature the PAWS reads incorrectly and affects catastrophically.

**Crafting Note:** Dimensional Science 3 + Forces 3 + Life 4 + Prime 2. Foundation: Crafts (Engineering), Difficulty 10. Restricted procurement — requires documented threat assessment and supervisor authorisation.

---

### Universal Neutralizer Device (UND)

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●● (6 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Entropy 2 / Forces 2 / Matter 2 (disruption pulse); Enlightenment 4 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 20
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Anti-Technology], [Non-Lethal]

A handheld emitter that fires an invisible disruption pulse calibrated to disable mundane technology. Car-sized or smaller machines are shut down on a single success. Three or more successes may permanently destroy simple machines (jam a lock permanently, brick a phone, seize an engine). The pulse does not affect organic targets and generates no visible effect — making it substantially coincidental in most environments.

Against Wonders: the target gets a soak roll (Wonder's relevant Arete or Enlightenment rating). Non-mechanical Wonders (an enchanted athame, a spirit-bound object, a Talisman) are unaffected — the UND specifically targets mechanical and electronic systems, not metaphysical ones.

**Effect:** Disables car-sized or smaller machines on 1 success; may permanently damage simple machines on 3+ successes. Wonders get a soak roll. Non-mechanical Wonders are immune.

**Invoke:** [Anti-Technology] when the target's equipment or vehicle is a meaningful threat and the operative needs to shut it down quickly; adds +2 dice to the Enlightenment roll.
**Compel:** [Anti-Technology] when the UND's pulse affects something the operative needed — their own equipment, an ally's vehicle, a piece of infrastructure that was keeping something dangerous contained.

**Crafting Note:** Entropy 2 + Forces 2 + Matter 2 + Prime 3. Foundation: Crafts (Engineering), Difficulty 7. Standard Q Division procurement. Issued routinely to operatives.

---

### Neuro-Optical Transmitter NOT — Escalating Scale Variants

*(See the standard NOT entry above for base rules. This entry covers the scale variants only.)*

**LED spotNOT** (●●●●, 9 pts.): As standard NOT but affects dozens of targets simultaneously within a 30-yard cone. Requires the operative to be visible to all targets at the moment of activation.

**Vehicular Mounted NOTlight** (●●●●●, 15 pts.): Vehicle-mounted; covers a full city block. Requires a vehicle (SMC or equivalent), Enlightenment 4+ to operate, and a Union authorization code before activation. City-block scale memory erasure is an extraordinary use of resources and generates proportional Paradox risk if the witnesses include any Awakened individuals.

---

### Dimensional Backdoor

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (12 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Correspondence 4 / Spirit 5; Enlightenment 5 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 25
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Dangerous], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Dimensional Travel]

A backpack-portable Primium-alloy door frame that, when assembled and activated, opens a stable Correspondence 4 / Spirit 5 gateway to a partner gateway anywhere in the Tellurian. Assembly takes 10 minutes; the gate remains open until deliberately closed or until the Quintessence supply is exhausted. Both sides must have active Backdoor units for the connection to establish — the gateway does not function if the partner unit is inactive, destroyed, or unattended.

All standard Umbral travel rules apply. The gateway is a physical doorway — anything that fits through a standard door frame can pass through. This includes unwanted things moving in either direction. Dimensional Backdoor operatives are strongly advised to have security on both ends.

**Effect:** Creates a stable two-way physical gateway to a partner Backdoor unit anywhere in the Tellurian. Requires partner unit active. Assembly: 10 minutes. Costs 5 Quintessence to activate; 1 Quintessence per minute of continuous operation.

**Invoke:** [Dimensional Travel] when the operative needs to reach a location that is physically inaccessible by conventional means; the Backdoor bypasses travel time and physical barriers entirely.
**Compel:** [Dangerous] when something comes through the gateway that was not supposed to — a hostile entity, an unexpected pursuer, an Umbral hazard following the opening.

**Crafting Note:** Correspondence 4 + Spirit 5 + Prime 4. Foundation: Crafts (Engineering), Difficulty 10, requiring Primium alloy (restricted Technocratic material). Not a field-craftable item. Produced in Union secure facilities.

---

### NIMBY-50

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (13 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Correspondence 4 / Dimensional Science 4 / Entropy 2; Enlightenment 5 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 25
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Dangerous], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Banishment], [Anti-Supernatural]

The NIMBY-50 (Not In My Back Yard, 50-yard effective range) resembles a weathervane with a garden hose attached. On a successful attack (Dexterity + Energy Weapons, Difficulty 7), it dislocates the target dimensionally: targets with Gnosis or Pathos are sent to a random Umbral location (Correspondence 4 / Dimensional Science 4 / Entropy 2 Effect) and cannot step sideways for a number of turns equal to activation hits. Targets without Gnosis or Pathos (including mages without active Spirit connection) are teleported several miles away (Correspondence 4 / Entropy 2 Effect).

The NIMBY-50 is a defensive containment weapon — its purpose is to remove a threat from the immediate area, not to destroy it. The displaced target will presumably return eventually. Union doctrine on NIMBY-50 use emphasises "not in my back yard, not in anyone's back yard in this district."

**Effect:** Hit targets with Gnosis/Pathos: sent to random Umbral location + cannot step sideways for [hits] turns. Hit targets without Gnosis/Pathos: teleported [hits × 5] miles in a random direction.

**Invoke:** [Banishment] when the operative's priority is removing a supernatural threat from the area rather than destroying it; the NIMBY-50 solves the immediate problem decisively.
**Compel:** [Anti-Supernatural] when the NIMBY-50's effect sends something to a location where it becomes someone else's catastrophic problem, triggering a diplomatic incident, or when the "random Umbral location" turns out to be somewhere that makes things measurably worse.

**Crafting Note:** Correspondence 4 + Dimensional Science 4 + Entropy 2 + Prime 3. Foundation: Crafts (Engineering), Difficulty 10. Restricted. The random-location component is intentional — Union policy discourages operators from trying to program specific destinations, as the Entropy component introduces chaos that is considered a feature, not a bug.

---

### Sleepteacher

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (15 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Mind 3 / Time 2 (skill acceleration); Mind 4 (memory alteration variant); Enlightenment 5 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 25
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [Technocratic], [Educational], [Time-Sensitive]

A ritual device used during sleep to compress weeks of training into a single night. The operator runs an extended roll (one roll per 6+ hours of target sleep); successes needed equal the target's current Ability rating + 3. Surplus successes reduce the experience point cost of the Ability 1-for-1. The recipient has a window of weeks equal to total successes to complete the experience point purchase — after which, the neural pathway reinforcement degrades and the window closes.

The Sleepteacher can also function as the first phase of Social Conditioning (Mind 3 / Prime 2 Initial Processing), the Reinforcement phase (Mind 4), and a full memory alteration tool (Mind 4). The sleep requirement is non-negotiable — attempting to use the device on an awake subject causes extended unconsciousness and a Paradox spike.

**Effect:** Extended roll (one per 6+ hours): compress training into a purchase window. Hits needed = current Ability rating + 3. Surplus hits reduce XP cost. Window duration = [hits] weeks. Also usable for Social Conditioning phases.

**Invoke:** [Educational] when the character needs to demonstrate competence in a skill they have been trained in via the Sleepteacher and the circumstance calls for it; the compressed neural pathways are real even if the clock is ticking.
**Compel:** [Time-Sensitive] when the purchase window is about to close and the character has not completed the experience point expenditure — the training will fade, and the window may not recur.

**Crafting Note:** Mind 3 + Time 2 + Prime 3 (standard version); Mind 4 for conditioning variants. Foundation: Crafts (Engineering) or Medicine, Difficulty 9. Not available outside Technocratic channels. The conditioning variants are ethically controversial even within the Union.

---

### Dragon Pearls

**Type:** Periapt (personal Tass battery)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (6 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Prime 3 (to store/withdraw Quintessence); Prime 3 required to use active bonus
**Quintessence Capacity:** Variable — built up across the mage's history; effectively unlimited theoretically, but practically 10–30 for most bearers
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Resonant (personal)], [Concealable], [Tradition (Akashayana)]

Not manufactured but accumulated — Dragon Pearls are personal Tass batteries that form and mature across multiple reincarnations of an Akashic mage. The Pearl's form varies entirely by individual: some are literal pearl-like spheres, others are carved jade discs, others simple smooth stones worn to a particular finish by decades of handling. All are identifiable by Prime sense as extraordinary concentrations of carefully tended personal Quintessence.

The functional Periapt element is straightforward: Prime 3 activates storage or withdrawal. The additional effect is what distinguishes Dragon Pearls from ordinary Periapts: with a single action, the Pearl grants +1 die to Arete rolls for any Rote the mage already knows. This cannot push the roll above five dice and does not apply to spontaneous Effects — only to practised Rotes. The bonus represents the accumulated expertise of multiple lifetimes crystallised into the Pearl's resonance.

**Effect:** Functions as a Quintessence Periapt (Prime 3 to use). Additionally: +1 die to Arete rolls for known Rotes (maximum 5 dice total). Does not apply to improvised Effects.

**Invoke:** [Resonant (personal)] when the character is performing a Rote deeply connected to their Tradition practice and long history; the Pearl's stored lifetime-experience adds +2 dice to the roll.
**Compel:** [Resonant (personal)] when the Pearl's unique resonance signature draws attention from someone who recognises it — a rival Akashic, an ancestor's enemy, a spirit who has history with the mage's lineage.

**Crafting Note:** Dragon Pearls are not crafted — they accumulate. A new Akashic mage cannot acquire one at character creation; it is an earned Wonder that represents chronicle history. The Storyguide may allow a starting Dragon Pearl if the character's backstory includes a deep Tradition history.

---

### Antaratma

**Type:** Talisman (personal ward)
**Background Rating:** ●●● (8 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Prime 3 / Entropy 2 (jhor resistance, Morbidity ward); Arete 4 to activate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 10
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Bound (to bearer)], [Resonant (Euthanatoi)], [Tradition (Euthanatoi)]

A Talisman crafted specifically for a single student by their Acarya — teacher and spiritual guide. It manifests differently for each recipient: sometimes a tattoo, sometimes a carved amulet, sometimes a phone app with a peculiar icon that only the bearer and their teacher understand. Its function is intensely personal: it maintains the bearer's spiritual equilibrium against jhor (the corrupting accumulation of death-tainted acts) and all forms of Quiet.

When a Paradox discharge would push the mage into Quiet, the Antaratma activates automatically: the mage rolls its Arete (Difficulty 7) and reduces the Quiet severity by one level per two hits. This does not prevent Paradox — it buffers the specific consequence of Quiet onset. Against jhor accumulation, the Antaratma requires conscious activation (1 Quintessence) and imposes −2 Difficulty on any roll to resist or recover from jhor-related complications.

The Antaratma is bound to its bearer — it is non-transferable, loses function outside the bearer's possession, and cannot be recharged by anyone except the original Acarya.

**Effect:** On Quiet-triggering Paradox discharge: auto-activates; reduce Quiet severity by 1 level per 2 hits (Arete roll, Difficulty 7). Against jhor: active use costs 1 Quintessence, −2 Difficulty to jhor resistance rolls.

**Invoke:** [Bound (to bearer)] when the bearer is facing Quiet or jhor complications and the Antaratma's buffering is relevant; reduces the severity of a complication.
**Compel:** [Bound (to bearer)] when the Antaratma's Acarya connection creates a complication — the teacher is able to sense the bearer's Quiet state, or the bearer's actions have made the Acarya aware of something they disapprove of.

**Crafting Note:** The Antaratma is always commissioned and crafted by a senior Euthanatoi Acarya for a specific student. Player characters cannot craft their own — it is made for them, and the crafting process is part of the teacher-student relationship. Arete 4 required to activate primary function. Non-Euthanatoi mages who attempt to use a found Antaratma find it does not respond.

---

### Imphepho Wierook (Incense of Calling)

**Type:** Talisman / Consumable
**Background Rating:** ●●● (3 pts. per supply)
**Sphere Requirements:** Spirit 3 (Gauntlet reduction, spirit interaction); Prime 2 (Quintessence to sustain); 1 Quintessence per hour burned
**Quintessence Capacity:** 15 per prepared batch
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Consumable], [Resonant (Ancestor Spirits)], [Tradition (Order of Hermes — House Shaea / Ifá practitioners)]

Ritually prepared South African incense — imphepho, a native plant with a long history of ceremonial use — charged by an Awakened practitioner for two distinct but related uses: communion with ancestors and protection against hostile Umbrood.

When burned for summoning: reduces the local Gauntlet rating by 1 for the duration; all Awakened within the smoke can see and hear spirits (not just the mage); and all rolls to interact with willing Umbrood gain −2 Difficulty. When burned for protection: wards 500 square feet; hostile spirits find their Charm and Sphere rolls against those in the warded area gain +2 Difficulty; hostile Rage-based attacks lose 1 die.

The two functions cannot be used simultaneously — the same stick of incense cannot both summon and ward. The practitioner chooses intent at lighting.

**Effect:** Summoning mode: −1 Gauntlet, spirits visible to all Awakened present, −2 Difficulty to Umbrood interaction rolls. Protection mode: +2 Difficulty to hostile spirits' actions in warded area, −1 die from hostile Rage attacks.

**Invoke:** [Resonant (Ancestor Spirits)] when the mage is attempting to commune with, negotiate with, or draw information from ancestor spirits; the incense's resonance adds +2 dice to the Spirit roll.
**Compel:** [Consumable] when the supply is burned out during a critical ritual, or when the incense's distinctive scent draws attention from a party who associates it with a specific practitioner or practice.

**Crafting Note:** Spirit 3 + Prime 2. Foundation: Crafts (ritual preparation, 1-week process including harvest timing, prayer cycles, and infusion). The specific plant material matters — commercial imphepho incense produces no magical effect. The authentic material must be sourced and prepared ceremonially.

---

### Waidan Ding (Outer Alchemy Cauldron)

**Type:** Talisman
**Background Rating:** ●●●●● (5 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Prime 3 / Life 2 (alchemy, Willpower restoration); Prime 3 to activate; 1 Quintessence per use
**Quintessence Capacity:** 10
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Resonant (Wu Lung / Taoist)], [Ritual], [Tradition (Wu Lung / Order of Hermes — House Hong Lei)]

A three-legged bronze cauldron, roughly 18 inches across, with cast dragons climbing the exterior and interior inscriptions from Taoist alchemical texts. Attributed to House Hong Lei or the Wu Lung, this is an outer alchemy tool — it works on materials placed inside it, not on the body directly, and produces its effects through the mage's meditative engagement with the alchemy process.

Activation: place a small quantity of quicksilver inside, heat with coal for one hour while maintaining focused meditation. Roll Arete at Difficulty 5 (the low Difficulty reflects that the Waidan Ding is a refined, stable Talisman). On success: regain Willpower equal to hits, up to the mage's maximum. On full Willpower recovery (roll produces more hits than needed to fill the track): gain 1 temporary Willpower above the maximum, lasting until the next scene.

**Effect:** Extended ritual (1 hour): roll Arete at Difficulty 5. Recover [hits] Willpower (to maximum). Surplus hits: gain 1 temporary Willpower above maximum (lasts 1 scene). Costs 1 Quintessence per use.

**Invoke:** [Ritual] when the character has time for the full hour of alchemical meditation before a critical challenge and needs Willpower restoration; the Waidan Ding provides a reliable preparation ritual.
**Compel:** [Ritual] when the one-hour requirement is impossible given current circumstances — the Ding cannot be rushed, cannot be used in combat, and the mage who skips a step of the ritual gets nothing.

**Crafting Note:** Prime 3 + Life 2. Foundation: Crafts (Metalworking) or Crafts (Alchemy), Difficulty 8. The cauldron requires bronze — iron or copper alloys do not carry the alchemical resonance properly. The inscriptions must be done by an Awakened calligrapher; a skilled but Sleeper craftsperson produces a beautiful and entirely mundane object.

---

### Dümerang Blade

**Type:** Device (Etherite)
**Background Rating:** ●● to ●●●● (4–12 pts. depending on enhancements)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 2 (return-to-hand Effect); Matter 3 (indestructibility); to create: Etheric alloy sourcing + Forces 2 + Prime 4
**Quintessence Capacity:** None (Effects are passive)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Indestructible], [Returning], [Tradition (Sons of Ether)], [Weird Science]

A custom blade crafted from Hollow Earth Etheric alloy — a material that cannot be reshaped once forged, exists in whatever configuration it was first cast, and is indestructible by mundane means (equivalent to Primium in resistance). The weapon can be of any blade type — knife, short sword, machete, custom fantasy configuration — but will always be the weapon it was made as. No conventional sharpening, reshaping, or heat treatment will alter it.

The Dümerang Effect is automatic: when the blade is thrown, dropped, or knocked away, it rolls its Arete and returns directly to the wielder's grip on any success, regardless of distance or obstacles. This makes it functionally un-disarmable by conventional means. Additional magical or scientific properties can be layered over the base weapon at additional Background Cost (each property adds +1–4 pts.).

**Effect:** Deals aggravated damage (use base blade damage + enhancements). Indestructible by mundane means. Auto-returns to wielder's grip on any hit (Forces 2 Effect activates automatically when blade leaves hand).

**Invoke:** [Returning] when the blade has been disarmed, thrown, or dropped and needs to return at a dramatically appropriate moment; the auto-return adds +2 dice to the next attack.
**Compel:** [Indestructible] when a situation calls for the ability to destroy or alter the blade — and it cannot be destroyed. The blade is also identifiable as Etheric manufacture by anyone with relevant knowledge, creating problems in settings where Etherite association is politically inconvenient.

**Crafting Note:** Forces 2 + Prime 4. Foundation: Crafts (Smithing), Difficulty 9, using Hollow Earth Etheric alloy sourced from Etherite contacts or the Hollow Earth itself. The alloy cannot be replicated — it must be the genuine article. Once forged, the blade's form is permanent.

---

### Game of Senet

**Type:** Trinket
**Background Rating:** ●● (2 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** None to use; Time 2 / Entropy 1 (probability weaving); Arete requirement: both players must be Awakened
**Quintessence Capacity:** None
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Ritual], [Resonant (Egyptian/Hermetic)], [Tradition (Order of Hermes — House Shaea)]

An ancient Egyptian board game set, attributed to House Shaea, that functions as a divination and skill-sharpening ritual for two Awakened players. The game takes 30 minutes to complete; both players roll Arete at Difficulty 6 (reduced by 1 per dot in any gaming or strategy Ability, minimum Difficulty 3). The player with more hits gains a mechanical advantage that lasts until next dawn.

**Effect:** After a full 30-minute game (both players must participate), the higher-successes player gains −2 Difficulty to all Talent and Skill rolls until next dawn. Usable once per dawn. Both participants must be Awakened — Sleepers playing Senet against an Awakened opponent gain nothing and notice nothing unusual.

**Invoke:** [Resonant (Egyptian/Hermetic)] when the character has played Senet that day and is facing a situation where the dawn-long advantage applies; the −2 Difficulty is already active and the Invoke is narrative confirmation.
**Compel:** [Ritual] when the 30-minute game requirement interrupts the flow of events — the Storyguide can compel the character to insist on playing Senet before a critical action, losing tactically valuable preparation time.

**Crafting Note:** Time 2 + Entropy 1 + Prime 3. Foundation: Crafts (Woodworking/Painting), Difficulty 7. The game set must be made from appropriate materials (ebony and ivory are traditional; modern equivalents are acceptable if made with care). Mass-produced Senet sets are mundane board games and produce no effect.

---

### Candle of Communion

**Type:** Trinket / Talisman
**Background Rating:** ● to ●●● (2 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Spirit 2 (Shroud reduction, spirit communication); Arete 1–3
**Quintessence Capacity:** 5–15 (varies by dot rating)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Ritual], [Resonant (Ancestors/Death)], [Tradition (Verbena)]

A glass-encased candle with a hand-painted image of a saint, a holy woman, an ancestor figure, or a spirit patron on the exterior — the specific image reflects the spirit the candle was made to commune with. Lighting the candle and making a successful Meditation roll reduces the Shroud rating by 3 in the immediate vicinity; the mage (and any Awakened present) can see, hear, and speak with ghosts and the restless dead for the duration of the burn.

Extinguishing the candle ends the Effect immediately. A botch on the Meditation roll does not simply fail — it attracts hostile spirits who perceive an open connection and no adequate invitation.

The Verbena produce these commonly and distribute them freely within Crafts circles. Some have found their way into the hands of Hollow Ones, Euthanatoi, and a small number of spiritually curious Sleepers who discovered, to their considerable surprise, that they work.

**Effect:** Light candle + Meditation roll: reduce Shroud by 3 in immediate area. All Awakened present can see and communicate with ghosts. Extinguish to end. Botch: hostile spirit attraction.

**Invoke:** [Resonant (Ancestors/Death)] when the character is attempting to communicate with or learn from the dead; the candle establishes the connection, adding +2 dice to the Spirit or Awareness roll.
**Compel:** [Resonant (Ancestors/Death)] when the open Shroud connection draws something the character did not intend to contact — a hostile ghost, an Umbrood predator, or a spirit with its own agenda for the practitioners.

**Crafting Note:** Spirit 2 + Prime 2. Foundation: Crafts (Candlemaking + icon painting), Difficulty 6. The painted image must accurately represent the specific spirit or category of spirit the candle is intended for. A generic "ancestor" candle works as a broad invitation; a specific candle painted with an accurate ancestor portrait is far more reliable.

---

### Mama Cybele's Tea Collection

**Type:** Talisman (set of Charms/consumables)
**Background Rating:** ●● (4 pts. per spice rack)
**Sphere Requirements:** Life 3 (healing) or Entropy 3 (cursing); Arete 2–5 (varies by blend)
**Quintessence Capacity:** 10–25 (varies; distributed across jar collection)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Consumable], [Tradition (Verbena)], [Dual-Use]

A wooden spice rack of hand-labelled ceramic jars containing enchanted tisane blends — dried herbs, barks, and botanicals prepared with rigorous Verbena ritual. The collection is associated with Mama Cybele, a senior Verbena whose identity and location are deliberately obscure. The jars bear her handwriting and her distinctive wax-seal labels; determining her whereabouts from the collection alone would require Correspondence 4.

Each jar's contents produce either a Life 3 healing effect (administered as tea or thrown directly onto a target) or an Entropy 3 cursing effect (same delivery options). The witch selects intent at preparation — each jar is pre-loaded for one specific effect, and it cannot be changed after the fact. The effect activates on ingestion (tea form, 1 turn) or splash contact (thrown form, immediate). Thrown contact is substantially vulgar; tea form is coincidental if the target has accepted the drink.

**Effect:** Life 3 Effect on target (heals physical ailments appropriate to Life 3 scope) OR Entropy 3 Effect (curses target per Entropy 3 scope). Witch selects at jar-loading, not at activation.

**Invoke:** [Dual-Use] when the collection's flexibility — healing tea or curse weapon — creates a tactical option that a single-purpose Wonder would not; narrative permission to deploy it in a non-obvious way.
**Compel:** [Dual-Use] when a jar's loaded effect (healing or curse) is exactly the opposite of what the current situation calls for — the witch is carrying five curse jars into a situation that desperately needs healing, or vice versa.

**Crafting Note:** Life 3 or Entropy 3 + Prime 3. Foundation: Crafts (Herbalism), Difficulty 7–9 depending on the potency of the specific blend. Authentic Mama Cybele jars can occasionally be found in Verbena markets — collecting a full rack is a chronicle-scale acquisition project.

---

### Grand Book of Shadows

**Type:** Grimoire
**Background Rating:** ●●●● (8 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Prime 4 / Mind 3 (Grimoire creation); Arete 4–8 (embedded Effects)
**Quintessence Capacity:** 10–25
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Resonant (Coven)], [Tradition (Verbena)], [Communal], [Reference]

A leather-bound grimoire of substantial size — typically more than 200 pages — that records the accumulated magical knowledge of a Verbena coven. Ownership is shared between the two highest-ranking coven leaders, who alone may write in it; all coven members may read it and invoke its references. The specific contents vary by coven, but all Grand Books of Shadows provide consistent mechanical effects regardless of their specific Rote contents.

Effects (all coven members with access): −1 Difficulty to Research and Occult rolls (−1 additional for topics directly relevant to the coven's area of practice); 1 free Lore point per scene when the book is consulted; −2 Difficulty to any ritual establishing or reinforcing wards on the coven's sacred spaces. The Book can also function as a Familiar Background for coven members with the Storyguide's permission, distributing its Paradox buffer across all members.

Non-coven-members who attempt to use a Grand Book of Shadows find it unhelpful at best. The book is tuned to the coven's shared resonance; an outsider reading it gets historical context but no mechanical benefits.

**Effect:** Coven members: −1 Difficulty Research/Occult (−1 more for coven-specific topics), +1 free Lore/scene from consultation, −2 Difficulty to coven ward rituals. Optional: Familiar Background proxy for coven Paradox distribution.

**Invoke:** [Communal] when the coven is working together and the shared knowledge of the Book is relevant — a historical mystery, a Spirit's identity, a ritual the coven has performed before; +2 dice to the relevant roll.
**Compel:** [Communal] when the Book creates conflict within the coven — disagreement about who has authority to write in it, a new entry that contradicts existing doctrine, or the Book's presence revealing coven membership to an uninvited observer.

**Crafting Note:** Prime 4 + Mind 3. Foundation: Crafts (Bookbinding + Illumination), Difficulty 9. The Book requires continuous contributions from coven leadership to maintain its potency — a coven without active leadership finds the Book's effects gradually degrading. A Grand Book of Shadows that has been abandoned for more than a decade functions only as historical record.

---

### Rod Logic Computer

**Type:** Device / Trinket
**Background Rating:** ●● (3 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** None (operates by mechanical hypercomputation, no Sphere required to use); Data 1 optional (Digital Web access with modification)
**Quintessence Capacity:** 5–10 (if modified for Digital Web)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Technological], [EMP-Proof], [Tradition (Virtual Adepts)], [Concealable]

A mechanical hypercomputer using diamond rod logic, organoplastic membrane buffers, and ceramic processing layers — a physical computer operating at speeds that should be impossible for a mechanical system, powered entirely by the handling and manipulation of the rods (kinetic energy). The rod logic computer has no metal components and produces no electromagnetic signature. It cannot be detected by metal detectors. It is immune to EMP. It carries no battery and requires no external power.

Output is via a micromechanical colour display panel or a tactile patch for operators who need screen-free interfaces. It loads programs through physical rod-configuration processes; changing programs requires manual reconfiguration, which takes 1–10 minutes depending on complexity.

Downsides: it is slower than a modern electronic computer for raw throughput, cannot access electronic networks without modification, and requires operator familiarity (Technology specialty: Rod Logic Systems, or equivalent). Virtual Adepts who depend on it as their primary Focus find conventional computers feel crude by comparison.

**Effect:** Functions as a computer with full research, calculation, and program execution capability. Immune to EMP and electromagnetic interference. Produces no detectable electronic signature. Portable; power is user-supplied (handled movement). Cannot access electronic networks without a separate wireless module modification.

**Invoke:** [EMP-Proof] when electronic devices have been disrupted (Bafflejack in play, EMP attack, Faraday environment) and the operator needs a working computer; the rod logic computer is the only one still functioning.
**Compel:** [Technological] when the rod logic computer's mechanical nature creates limitations — it cannot plug into a network without modification, it requires familiarity that most tech-users lack, or its distinctive clatter and visual appearance identifies its user as someone unusual.

**Crafting Note:** No Sphere requirement for base function; the hypercomputation is achieved through physical engineering. Foundation: Crafts (Precision Mechanics), Difficulty 10 — building one is an extraordinary feat of craftwork. Available through Virtual Adept supply networks for those who know the right people.

---

### False Face

**Type:** Talisman or Device (hypertech variant)
**Background Rating:** ●● (6 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Life 2 (cosmetic transformation); Arete 4; 1 Quintessence per transformation
**Quintessence Capacity:** 20
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Concealable], [Subtle], [Dangerous (on botch)], [Dual-Type]

A thin translucent film mask — almost invisible when worn — that performs full-body cosmetic transformation to any essentially-human appearance. The Life 2 Effect reads the desired appearance from the wearer's mind on activation and restructures surface features accordingly. It does not change mass, height, or fundamental body structure — it works with what is there.

Success produces the desired look convincingly. Failure produces a version with noticeable errors (wrong eye colour, slightly off proportions, a visible seam where the mask did not set correctly). Botch produces a stuck horrific or absurd transformation for 1 hour per remaining Quintessence point — the wearer is locked in an appearance they cannot control or remove until the Effect expires.

Available as a hypertech Device (nanite-film version) or a mystic Talisman (enchanted organic membrane version). The Device version can be used by unAwakened individuals if given appropriate training. Minor facial changes (hair colour, minor feature adjustment) may be coincidental in tech-saturated Reality Zones.

**Effect:** Full-body cosmetic transformation to any human-baseline appearance. Success: desired look. Failure: obvious errors. Botch: stuck in uncontrolled transformation for 1 hour per remaining Quintessence. Costs 1 Quintessence per transformation.

**Invoke:** [Concealable] when the wearer needs to pass as a specific individual or adopt a new appearance entirely; the transformation is convincing and adds +2 dice to disguise-related Subterfuge or Stealth rolls.
**Compel:** [Dangerous (on botch)] when the mask has botched — the Storyguide imposes the worst possible transformation for the worst possible duration. The mask cannot be voluntarily removed while a botch Effect is active.

**Crafting Note:** Life 2 + Prime 4 (Talisman) or Matter 3 + Life 2 + Mind 1 (Device/hypertech). Foundation: Crafts (Biology) or Crafts (Nanofabrication), Difficulty 9. The Device version requires Technocratic manufacturing infrastructure; the Talisman version requires Verbena or Euthanatoi expertise in organic enchantment.

---

### Malign Cutlery

**Type:** Trinket or Talisman (optional active properties)
**Background Rating:** ●● base; ●●●+ with Talisman properties (4–12+ pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Forces 2 / Matter 2 (base, passive); optional Talisman properties each require additional Spheres and Quintessence
**Quintessence Capacity:** 10+ (if Talisman properties installed)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Brutal], [Two-Handed], [Conspicuous], [Nephandic (suspected)]

Grotesquely oversized weapons — titanic swords, massive axes, reaper scythes, wicked polearms, crushing mauls, grotesque spiked clubs — whose physics-defying proportions and behaviour are maintained by built-in Forces 2 / Matter 2 Effects. The base Trinket version keeps the material impossibly sharp and the physics of each swing correct regardless of the wielder's angle or strength; no Arete roll is needed for this passive function.

Damage values:

| Weapon | Difficulty | Damage | Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| Titanic Sword | 5 | Strength + 8 | Lethal |
| Massive Axe | 6 | Strength + 9 | Lethal |
| Reaper Scythe | 7 | Strength + 9 | Lethal |
| Wicked Polearm | 6 | Strength + 7 | Lethal |
| Crushing Maul | 7 | Strength + 10 | Bashing |
| Grotesque Spiked Club | 6 | Strength + 7 | Lethal |

All require two hands, minimum Strength 3, and cannot be concealed under any circumstances. Optional Talisman properties (each costs 1 Quintessence/turn when active): aggravated damage (Prime 2), energy discharge (Forces 3 / Prime 2), corrosive (Matter 3 / Prime 2), shadow extension (Forces 2), fear aura (Mind 3), Umbral cutting (Spirit 3), Quintessence drain on hit (Prime 3).

**Effect:** Passive: extreme lethal/bashing damage (see table). Active Talisman properties: 1 Quintessence/turn each, per listed effect.

**Invoke:** [Brutal] when the situation calls for extreme physical violence and the weapon's impossible damage output is the decisive factor; adds +2 dice to the damage roll.
**Compel:** [Conspicuous] when the weapon's size and appearance make any form of subtlety impossible — the character cannot enter a social environment, cannot explain the weapon's presence, and is immediately identified as an extreme threat by any observer.

**Crafting Note:** Forces 2 + Matter 2 + Prime 4 (base Trinket). Each optional Talisman property adds the listed Spheres to the crafting requirement. Foundation: Crafts (Smithing/Weaponry), Difficulty 8–10 depending on configuration. Origin is typically Nephandic manufacture or deep Umbral corruption — players who want one should expect a story attached.

---

### Gallu's Lash

**Type:** Talisman
**Background Rating:** ●●● (not for purchase; Storyguide asset)
**Sphere Requirements:** Entropy 3 (vulnerability seeking), Forces 2 (impact control), Life 3 (damage or healing), Mind 3 (psychological domination); optional Prime 3 (Quintessence drain)
**Quintessence Capacity:** 15
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Brutal], [Psychological], [Corrupting], [Nephandic], [Dual-Effect (damage or heal)]

A leather flogger — barbed or plain, in any style — whose enchantments go far beyond the physical. Entropy 3 finds and targets the most vulnerable point of any target on each strike, bypassing armor entirely. Forces 2 controls the physics of impact, allowing the wielder to calibrate damage from incapacitating to barely perceptible. Life 3 makes each strike simultaneously inflict aggravated damage and heal the wielder — or heal the target and harm the wielder, if the Talisman is being used in that mode.

Mind 3 produces the most dangerous effect: each successful strike builds a psychological bond with the target, elevating pain and pleasure responses simultaneously while bypassing consent and self-preservation instincts. The target may resist with a Willpower roll (Difficulty 5 + 1 per successful prior strike). Five or more strikes without a successful resistance roll produces a semi-permanent psychological bond that does not require the Lash's continued presence to maintain.

Optional Prime 3 Effect (separate activation): each strike transfers 1 Quintessence from the target to the wielder.

The Gallu's Lash is named for the servant-spirits of Ereshkigal — a deliberate association that indicates its intended use context.

**Effect:** Each strike: Entropy 3 bypasses armor; Forces 2 calibrates damage level; Life 3 inflicts aggravated damage to target or heals target (wielder's choice each strike). Mind 3: Willpower resistance (Difficulty 5 + 1/prior strike) or psychological bonding effect. Optional: 1 Quintessence transfer per strike.

**Invoke:** Not presented as a player-side Invoke — this is a Storyguide tool.
**Compel:** [Corrupting] when extended use of the Lash has established a psychological bond that begins affecting the wielder as well as the target — the empathic feedback of the Mind Effect is not one-directional.

**Crafting Note:** Entropy 3 + Forces 2 + Life 3 + Mind 3 (+ Prime 3 optional). Foundation: Crafts (Leatherworking), Difficulty 9. The crafting process requires something the Ars Goetia refers to as "willing receipt" — implications are significant. This item should not be craftable by player characters without substantial narrative justification and Storyguide approval.

---

### The Riotizer

**Type:** Charm / Gadget (single use per unit)
**Background Rating:** ●● (3 pts. per 10 units)
**Sphere Requirements:** Correspondence 2 / Entropy 2 / Mind 2; Arete 5 to craft
**Quintessence Capacity:** None (Quintessence consumed in manufacture)
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Single-Use], [Area (20m)], [Concealable], [Nephandic]

Single-use objects disguised as innocuous items — protest signs, bandannas, bottles, lapel buttons — that detonate near crowd flashpoints to tip uncertain situations into active violence. Correspondence 2 reaches across short distances to affect already-tense locations even if the activator is not present. Entropy 2 shifts probability sharply toward chaotic outcomes. Mind 2 amplifies violent emotions in a 20-foot radius.

Roll 5 dice at Difficulty 3 against an already-tense crowd (Difficulty 5–6 against a peaceful one). More hits produce proportionally more disorder. The Riotizer cannot create violence from nothing — it accelerates what is already present.

Designed for Nephandic destabilisation operations, agent provocateur work, and chaos sowing in urban environments. Finding a Riotizer means someone put it there deliberately — and that someone had access to an Awakened item-crafter.

**Effect:** Correspondence 2 remote reach; Entropy 2 chaos acceleration; Mind 2 violence amplification in 20-foot radius. Roll 5 dice at Difficulty 3 (tense crowd) or 5–6 (peaceful crowd). More hits = more violence.

**Invoke:** Not presented as a player Invoke — this is an antagonist/evidence tool.
**Compel:** [Nephandic] when evidence of the Riotizer's use (distinctive resonance signature, physical objects) links the incident to Nephandic activity and the player characters must now deal with the political fallout.

**Crafting Note:** Correspondence 2 + Entropy 2 + Mind 2 + Prime 2. Foundation: Crafts (fabrication of the disguise object), Difficulty 6. The crafting is not technically difficult — the moral weight is the barrier. These are manufactured in batches and distributed through Nephandic networks. Finding the source is a chronicle arc.

---

### Portable Hellgate

**Type:** Device
**Background Rating:** ●●●● (12 pts.)
**Sphere Requirements:** Correspondence 4 / Spirit 4 / Mind 4 / Prime 2 (gateway and summons); Matter 3 / Correspondence 3 (portable construction); Enlightenment 6 to operate
**Quintessence Capacity:** 30
**Tags:** [Enchanted], [Dangerous], [Dimensional Travel], [Nephandic], [Two-Way], [Paradox Magnet]

A suitcase, trunk, or backpack that unfolds into a metal summoning pentacle with an external power supply and a handheld computer pad loaded with what the documentation calls "summoning apps." The device establishes pre-negotiated gateways to specific Infernal or Labyrinthine Umbral locations — working relationships with demonic entities must be established before the Device can be used, requiring Correspondence 4 (reaching the quarry's home realm) + Spirit 4 (opening the passage) + Mind 4 (cooperative bond) + Prime 2 (power).

Each activation expends 5 Quintessence. The gateway is two-directional — what can come through can also leave through. Paradox backlash risk is extreme; on a botch, the activator is pulled through the gate they opened. The gate does not care about the hierarchy of whatever it is connected to.

The pre-negotiation requirement is not a minor inconvenience. Establishing a stable working relationship with an entity that warrants a Portable Hellgate is a chronicle in itself.
